39 9.4.1.6 Listen Interval field
40
41
Change the first paragraph as follows:
42
43
44 For non-MLO, theThe Listen Interval field is used to indicate to the AP how often an S1G STA with
45
dot11NonTIMModeActivated equal to false or a non-S1G STA in power save mode wakes to listen to
46
Beacon frames. It is also used to indicate to an AP the duration during which an S1G STA with
47
48 dot11NonTIMModeActivated equal to true is required to transmit at least one frame that is addressed to the
49 associated AP. This field is derived from the ListenInterval parameter when present as a parameter of an
50 MLME primitive. The value is in units of beacon interval if dot11ShortBeaconInterval is false and in units
51
of short beacon interval if dot11ShortBeaconInterval is true (see 11.1.3.10.2 (Generation of S1G Beacon
52
frames)).
53
54
55 For MLO, the Listen Interval field is used to indicate to the AP MLD how often at least a non-AP STA affil-
56 iated with a non-AP MLD wakes to listen to Beacon frames if all STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD are
57
in power save mode. This field is derived from the ListenInterval parameter when present as a parameter of
58
an MLME primitive. The value of this field is in units of the maximum beacon interval value (with respect
59
60 to the beacon interval corresponding to each of the links that the non-AP MLD intends to setup in the
61 (Re)Association Request frame).
62
63
The length of the Listen Interval field is 2 octets. The Listen Interval field is shown in Figure 9-88 (Listen
64
65 Interval field format carried in a non-S1G PPDU).

44 9.4.1.71 EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field
45
46
The EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field carries the average SNR of each spatial stream and com-
47
48 pressed beamforming feedback matrices V for use by a transmit beamformer to determine steering matrices
49 Q, as described in 10.34.3 (Explicit feedback beamforming) and 19.3.12.3 (Explicit feedback beamform-
50 ing).
51
52
53 The size of the EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field depends on the values in the EHT MIMO Con-
54 trol field. The EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field contains EHT compressed beamforming report
55 information or successive (possibly zero-length) portions thereof in the case of segmented EHT compressed
56
beamforming/CQI report (see 35.7.4 (Rules for generating segmented feedback)). EHT compressed beam-
57
forming report information is included in the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report if the Feedback
58
59 Type subfield in the EHT MIMO Control field indicates SU or MU.
60
61 The EHT Compressed Beamforming Report information contains the channel matrix elements indexed,
62
first, by matrix angles in order shown in Table 9-71 (Order of angles in the compressed beamforming feed-
63
back matrix when used in a non-S1G band), and second, by data and pilot subcarrier index from the lowest
64
65 frequency to the highest frequency (in case of MRU the lowest frequency stands for the lowest frequency of

1 first RU and the highest frequency stands for the highest frequency of the last RU) as shown in Table 9-127b

3
subblock are set to 1), Table 9-127c (Subcarrier indices when all bits in Partial BW Info subfield corre-

10
11
MIMO Control field, and Nr is the number of rows in a compressed beamforming feedback matrix deter-
12 mined by the Nr Index subfield of the EHT MIMO Control field.
13
14 The beamforming feedback matrix V is formed by the beamformee as follows. The beamformer transmits
15
an EHT sounding NDP with N spatial streams, where N takes a value between 2 and 8. Based
16 SS(cid:15)NDP SS(cid:15)NDP
on this EHT sounding NDP, the beamformee estimates the N (cid:117)N channel, and based on that
17 RX(cid:15)BFEE SS(cid:15)NDP
18 channel it determines a Nr(cid:117)Nc orthogonal matrix V, where Nr and Nc satisfy Equation (9-3) (see
19 9.4.1.49 (VHT Compressed Beamforming Report field)). N is the number of receiver chains used to
RX(cid:15)BFEE
20 receive the EHT sounding NDP at the beamformee.
21
22
Further restrictions on Nc are described in 36.2 (EHT PHY service interface). The angles are quantized as
23
24 defined in Table 9-74 (Quantization of angles) with b (bits for (cid:92)) and b (bits for (cid:73)) defined by the Code-
(cid:92) (cid:73)
25 book Information field of the EHT MIMO Control field (see 9.4.1.70 (EHT MIMO Control field)).
26
27
The EHT Compressed Beamforming Report information has the structure and order defined in Table 9-
28
91b (HE Compressed Beamforming Report information), where Na is the number of angles used for the
29
30 compressed beamforming feedback matrix (see Table 9-73 (Order of angles in the compressed beamforming
31 feedback matrix when used in a non-S1G band)).
32
33
In Table 9-91b (HE Compressed Beamforming Report information), Ns is the number of subcarriers for
34
which a compressed beamforming feedback matrix is sent back to the beamformer. A beamformer or beam-
35
36 formee, depending upon which of the two determines the feedback parameters, reduces Ns by using a
37 method referred to as grouping, in which only a single compressed beamforming feedback matrix is reported
38 for each group of Ng adjacent subcarriers. Ns is a function of the BW, Partial BW Info, and Grouping sub-
39
fields in the EHT MIMO Control field (see 9.4.1.70 (EHT MIMO Control field)).
40
41
42 Subcarrier indices scidx(cid:11)i(cid:12), i = 0(cid:15)1(cid:15)(cid:125)(cid:15)Ns–1, are a concatenation of the subcarrier indices for each
43 242-tone RU or 996-tone RU in the frequency order, identified by the Partial BW Info subfields together
44
with the BW and Grouping subfields. The subcarrier indices for each 242-tone RU or 996-tone RU are
45
defined in Table 9-127b (Subcarrier indices when not all bits in Partial BW Info subfield corresponding to
46
47 the 80 MHz frequency subblock are set to 1), Table 9-127c (Subcarrier indices when all bits in Partial BW
48 Info subfield corresponding to the 80 MHz frequency subblock are set to 1 for Ng = 4), and Table 9-127d
49 (Subcarrier indices when all bits in Partial BW Info subfield corresponding to the 80 MHz frequency
50
subblock are set to 1 for Ng = 16).
51
52
53 The Partial BW Info subfield values are set according to the bandwidth of the PPDU carrying the EHT NDP
54 Announcement frame and the RU or MRU in which the feedback is solicited, see Table 9-45b (Settings for
55 BW, Partial Bandwidth Info subfield in the EHT NDP Announcement frame).

9.4.1.72 EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field
18
19
20 The EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field carries explicit feedback in the form of delta SNRs. The
21
information in the EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field and the EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming
22
Report field can be used by the transmit MU beamformer to determine the steering matrices Q, as described
23
24 in 36.3.3.1 (DL MU-MIMO).
25
26
The size of the EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field depends on the values in the EHT MIMO
27
28 Control field. The EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field contains EHT MU Exclusive Beamform-
29 ing Report information or successive (possibly zero-length) portions thereof in the case of segmented EHT
30 compressed beamforming/CQI report (see 35.7.4 (Rules for generating segmented feedback)). EHT MU
31
Exclusive Beamforming Report information is included in the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report
32
(in addition to EHT compressed beamforming report information) if the Feedback Type subfield in the EHT
33
34 MIMO Control field indicates MU.
35
36
37 The EHT MU exclusive beamforming report information consists of Delta SNR subfields for each of the
38 spatial streams, 1 to Nc, of a subset of subcarriers typically spaced Ng apart, where Ng is signaled in the
39 Grouping subfield of the EHT MIMO Control field. The subset of subcarriers starts from the lowest fre-
40
quency subcarrier and continues to the highest frequency subcarrier. The subcarrier indices of the feedback
41
for each Delta SNR subfield are identical to the subcarrier indices for the compressed beamforming feed-
42
43 back matrix V.
44
45 NOTE—The feedback subcarrier spacings are mostly equal to Ng, but there are a few exceptions, generally around the
46 RU edge and the DC tone, where extra feedback subcarriers are added to improve the channel interpolation/extrapolation
47 quality.
48
49
No padding is present between (cid:39)SNR in the EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field, even if they
50 k(cid:15)i
51 correspond to different subcarriers. The subset of subcarriers included is determined by the values of the
52 Partial BW Info and Grouping subfields of the EHT MIMO Control field. For each subcarrier included, the
53 deviation in decibels of the SNR of that subcarrier for each column of V relative to the average SNR of the
54
corresponding spatial stream is computed using Equation (9-2) except that k is the subcarrier index in the
55
range scidx(cid:11)0(cid:12)(cid:15)(cid:125)(cid:15)scidx(cid:11)Ns–1(cid:12) and SNR is the average SNR of spatial stream i reported in the Aver-
56 i
57 age SNR of Space-Time Stream i field of the EHT Compressed Beamforming Report Information field.
58
59
The EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report information has the structure and order defined in Table 9-
60
61 125 (HE MU Exclusive Beamforming Report information).
62
63
In Table 9-125 (HE MU Exclusive Beamforming Report information), Ns and scidx() are defined in
64
65 9.4.1.71 (EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field).

1 9.4.1.73 EHT CQI Report field
2
3
The EHT CQI Report field carries the per-RU average SNRs of each spatial stream, where each per-RU
4
5 average SNR is the arithmetic mean of the SNR in decibels over a 26-tone RU for which the feedback is
6 being requested.
7
8
The EHT CQI Report field contains EHT CQI report information. EHT CQI Report information is included
9
10 in the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report if the Feedback Type subfield in the EHT MIMO Control
11 field indicates CQI feedback.
12
13
The EHT CQI Report field has the structure and order defined in Table 9-126 (HE CQI Report information).
14
15
16 Ncqi is the number of RU indices for which the CQI report is sent back to the beamformer. Ncqi equals 9
17 multiplied by the number of 1s from B1 to B8 of the Partial BW Info subfield when B0 is 0 and Ncqi equals
18
18 multiplied by the number of 1s from B1 to B8 of the Partial BW Info subfield when B0 is 1. The 26-tone
19
RU subcarrier indices for 20 MHz, 40 MHz, 80 MHz, 160 MHz, and 320 MHz are defined in Table 27-
20
21 7 (Data and pilot subcarrier indices for RUs in a 20 MHz HE PPDU and in a non-OFDMA 20 MHz HE
22 PPDU), Table 27-8 (Data and pilot subcarrier indices for RUs in a 40 M HE PPDU and in a non-OFDMA 40
23 MHz HE PPDU), Table 36-5 (Data and pilot subcarrier indices for RUs in an 80 MHz EHT PPDU),
24
Table 36-6 (Data and pilot subcarrier indices for RUs in a 160 MHz EHT PPDU), and Table 36-7 (Data and
25
pilot subcarrier indices for RUs in a 320 MHz EHT PPDU), respectively. 26-tone RUs that are not defined,
26
27 i.e., RU 19, RU 56, RU 93, and RU 130 (see 36.3.2.1 (Subcarriers and resource allocation in EHT PPDUs)),
28 are not included in the EHT CQI Report field.
29
30

31
32 mation) is a 6-bit 2s complement integer whose definition is shown in Table 9-127 (Average SNR of RU
33 index k for space-time stream i subfield).
34
35
The AvgSNR in Table 9-127 (Average SNR of RU index k for space-time stream i subfield) is found by
36 k(cid:15)i
37 computing the arithmetic mean of the SNR per subcarrier in decibels for spatial stream i over the subcarriers
38 in RU index k for which the feedback is being requested. The SNR per subcarrier calculation is defined in
39 9.4.1.71 (EHT Compressed Beamforming Report field).
40
41
42 Padding is not present between per-RU average SNRs of each spatial stream information, even if they corre-
43 spond to different RUs and spatial streams. If the size of the EHT CQI report information is not an integer

bits.

13 A non-AP MLD that supports enhanced multi-link single radio operation (see 35.3.17 (Enhanced multi-link
14 single radio operation)) sets the EMLSR Mode subfield to 1 to indicate that the EMLSR mode is enabled for
15
the non-AP MLD and to 0 to indicate that the EMLSR mode is disabled for the non-AP MLD. A non-AP
16
MLD that does not support enhanced multi-link single radio operation (see 35.3.17 (Enhanced multi-link
17
18 single radio operation)) sets the EMLSR Mode subfield to 0. The EMLSR Mode subfield is set to 0 if the
19 EMLMR Mode subfield is set to 1. An AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true sets
20 the EMLSR Mode subfield to the value obtained from the EMLSR Mode subfield of the received EML
21
Operating Mode Notification frame.
22
23
24 A non-AP MLD that supports enhanced multi-link multi-radio operation (see 35.3.18 (Enhanced multi-link
25
multi-radio operation)) sets the EMLMR Mode subfield to 1 to indicate that the non-AP MLD operates in
26
EMLMR mode and to 0 to indicate that the non-AP MLD does not operate in EMLMR mode. A non-AP
27
28 MLD that does not support enhanced multi-link multi-radio operation (see 35.3.18 (Enhanced multi-link
29 multi-radio operation)) sets the EMLMR Mode subfield to 0. The EMLMR Mode subfield is set to 0 if the
30 EMLSR Mode subfield is set to 1. An AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true sets
31
the EMLMR Mode subfield to the value obtained from the EMLMR Mode subfield of the received EML
32
Operating Mode Notification frame.
33
34
35 NOTE 1—The EMLSR Mode and EMLMR Mode subfields are used to enable or disable the EMLSR and EMLMR
36 modes, respectively. An EML Operating Mode Notification frame sets either of these subfields to a nonzero value only
37 when the corresponding mode is supported by the receiving MLD. An MLD indicates which mode(s) it supports in the
38 EML Capabilities field of the Basic Multi-Link element that it transmits (see 9.4.2.312.2 (Basic Multi-Link element)).
39
40 When the EMLSR Mode subfield is set to 1, the EMLSR/EMLMR Link Bitmap subfield is the EMLSR
41
Link Bitmap subfield. When the EMLMR Mode subfield is set to 1, the EMLSR/EMLMR Link Bitmap sub-
42
field is the EMLMR Link Bitmap subfield. When the EMLSR Mode subfield is set to 0 and the EMLMR
43
44 Mode subfield is set to 0, the EMLSR/EMLMR Link Bitmap is not present.
45
46
The EMLSR Parameter Update Control subfield indicates whether the EMLSR Parameter Update field is
47
present in the EML Operating Mode Notification frame. The EMLSR Parameter Update Control subfield is
48
49 set to 1 when the EMLSR Parameter Update field is present in the EML Operating Mode Notification frame,
50 and set to 0 otherwise. When the EMLSR Parameter Update Control subfield is set to 1, the EMLSR Link
51 Bitmap subfield of the EML Control field contains a different value than the EMLSR Link Bitmap value
52
contained in a previous EML Operating Mode Notification frame successfully transmitted by the non-AP
53
MLD. When included in a frame sent by an AP affiliated with an AP MLD, the EMLSR Parameter Update
54
55 Control subfield is set to 0.
56
57
The EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield indicates the subset of the enabled links that is used by the non-AP MLD
58
59 in the EMLSR mode. The bit position i of the EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield corresponds to the link with the
60 Link ID equal to i and is set to 1 to indicate that the link is used by the non-AP MLD for the EMLSR mode
61 and is a member of the EMLSR links; otherwise the bit position is set to 0. An AP MLD with dot11EHTEM-
62
LSROptionImplemented equal to true sets the EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield to the value obtained from the
63
EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield of the received EML Operating Mode Notification frame. The EMLSR Link
64
65 Bitmap subfield is present if the EMLSR Mode subfield is equal to 1 and is not present otherwise.

61 When the TIM is carried in a non-S1G PPDU, the traffic indication virtual bitmap, maintained by the AP, or
62
the mesh STA or the AP MLD that generates a TIM, consists of 2008 bits, and it is organized into 251 octets
63
64 such that bit number N (0 (cid:100) N (cid:100) 2007) in the bitmap corresponds to bit number (N mod 8) in octet number (cid:172)N
65 / 8(cid:188) where the low order bit of each octet is bit number 0, and the high order bit is bit number 7. When the
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 221
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 222 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 TIM is carried in an S1G PPDU, the traffic-indication virtual bitmap has the hierarchical structure shown in
2 Figure 9-152 (Hierarchical structure of traffic-indication virtual bitmap carried in an S1G PPDU). Each bit
3
in the traffic indication virtual bitmap corresponds to traffic buffered for a specific neighbor peer mesh STA
4
within the MBSS that the mesh STA is prepared to deliver1, or for a STA that is not affiliated with an MLD
5
6 within the BSS that the AP is prepared to deliver at the time the Beacon frame is transmitted, or for a non-
7 AP MLD that APs affiliated with the AP MLD are prepared to deliver at the time the Beacon frame is
8 transmitted. Bit number N indicates the status of buffered, individually addressed MSDUs/MMPDUs for the
9
STA or the non-AP MLD whose AID is N, or group addressed MSDUs/MMPDUs for the STAs whose
10
group AID is N. It is set as follows:
11
12 — If the STA is not affiliated with an MLD and is not using APSD, and any individually addressed
13
MSDUs/MMPDUs for that STA are buffered and the AP or the mesh STA is prepared to deliver
14
them, then bit number N in the traffic indication virtual bitmap is 1.
15
16 — If the STA is not affiliated with an MLD and is using APSD, and any individually addressed
17
MSDUs/MMPDUs for that STA are buffered in at least one nondelivery-enabled AC (if there exists
18
at least one nondelivery-enabled AC), then bit number N in the traffic indication virtual bitmap is 1.
19
20 — If the STA is not affiliated with an MLD and is using APSD, all ACs are delivery-enabled, and any
21
individually addressed MSDUs/MMPDUs for that STA are buffered in any AC, then bit number N in
22
the traffic indication virtual bitmap is 1.
23
24 — If all STAs affiliated with non-AP MLD are not using APSD and any individually addressed
25
MSDUs/MMPDUs for that non-AP MLD are buffered, then bit number N in the traffic indication
26
virtual bitmap is 1 (see NOTE below).
27
28 — If all STAs affiliated with non-AP MLD are using APSD and any individually addressed MSDUs/
29
MMPDUs for that non-AP MLD are buffered in at least one nondelivery-enabled AC (if there exists
30
at least one nondelivery-enabled AC in each of the affiliated STAs), then bit number N in the traffic
31
32 indication virtual bitmap is 1 (see NOTE below).
33
— If all STAs affiliated with non-AP MLD are using APSD whereas all ACs are delivery-enabled per
34
each affiliated STA and any individually addressed MSDUs/ MMPDUs for that non-AP MLD are
35
36 buffered in any AC, then bit number N in the traffic indication virtual bitmap is 1. (see NOTE below)
37
NOTE—The existence of individually addressed MSDUs/MMPDUs buffered for that non-AP MLD is based on the
38
rules defined in 35.3.12.4 (Traffic indication).
39
40 — Otherwise, bit number N in the traffic indication virtual bitmap is 0.

39 bandwidth including the primary channel without covering any punctured 20 MHz subchannel indi-
40 cated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the Bandwidth Indication subelement as
41 defined in 35.15.2 (Preamble puncturing operation), and
42
43 — the announced BSS bandwidth in the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch subelement is less than the
44 bandwidth in the Bandwidth Indication subelement and the corresponding BSS bandwidth shall not
45 be an 80+80 MHz channel.

48 9.4.2.22 Quiet element
49
50 Change the first paragraph as follows:
51
52
The Quiet element defines an interval during which no transmission occurs in the current channel from
53
54 STAs in the BSS with the exceptions stated in 35.8.5.2 (Quieting STAs during R-TWT SPs). This interval
55 might be used to assist in making channel measurements without interference from other STAs in the BSS,
56 or to protect channel access at the start of R-TWT SPs (see 35.8.5.2 (Quieting STAs during R-TWT SPs)).
57
The format of the Quiet element is shown in Figure 9-284 (Quiet element format).
58
59

61

63
which the next quiet interval starts. For an EHT AP (see 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures for channel switch-
64
65 ing, extended channel switching, and channel quieting)):

1 — the Quiet Count field is equal to the number of TBTTs until the beacon interval during which the

4 — the Quiet Count field minus 127 is equal to the number of TBTTs in the past to reach the beacon
5

1 The BSSMaxIdlePeriod parameter indicates the idle timeout limit, as described in 11.21.13 (BSS max idle
2 period management) and 35.3.12.3 (MLD max idle period management). The time period is specified in
3
units of 1000 TUs. The value of 0 is reserved. In a non-S1G STA, the Max Idle Period field is an unsigned
4
integer that contains the value of the parameter BSSMaxIdlePeriod. In an S1G STA, the two MSBs of the
5
6 Max Idle Period field contain the Unified Scaling Factor subfield and the remaining 14 bits contain the
7 Unscaled Interval subfield (see Figure 9-89 (Listen Interval field format carried in an S1G PPDU)). In an
8 S1G STA, the BSSMaxIdlePeriod parameter used by the MLME primitives is in units of 1000 TUs and is
9
equal to the value of the Unscaled Interval subfield, multiplied by the scaling factor that corresponds to the
10
value indicated in the Unified Scaling Factor subfield. The Unified Scaling Factor subfield encoding is
11
12 defined in Table 9-48 (Unified Scaling Factor subfield encoding).

36
37
38 The AP MLD ID subfield indicates the identifier of the AP MLD with which the reported AP is affiliated. If
39
the reported AP is affiliated with the same MLD as the reporting AP sending the frame carrying this ele-
40
ment, the AP MLD ID subfield is set to 0. If the reported AP is affiliated with the same MLD as a nontrans-
41
42 mitted BSSID that is in the same multiple BSSID set as the reporting AP (that corresponds to the transmitted
43 BSSID) sending the frame carrying this element, the AP MLD ID subfield is set to the same value as in the
44 BSSID Index field in the Multiple BSSID-Index element in the nontransmitted BSSID profile corresponding
45
to the nontransmitted BSSID. If the reported AP is affiliated with an AP MLD that is neither the AP MLD
46
with which the reporting AP is affiliated nor an AP MLD with which a nontransmitted BSSID that is in the
47
48 same multiple BSSID set as the reporting AP is affiliated, the AP MLD ID subfield is set to a value that is
49 unique for this AP MLD in frames sent by the reporting AP and that is higher than 0 and lower than
50 255 if no Multiple BSSID element is carried in the same frame or a value higher than 2n–1 and
51
lower than 255 if a Multiple BSSID element is carried in the same frame, where n is the value contained in
52
the MaxBSSID Indicator field in the Multiple BSSID element. The AP MLD ID subfield is set to 255 if the
53
54 reported AP is not part of an AP MLD, or if the reporting AP does not have information of that MLD.
55
NOTE 1—The AP MLD ID is used to identify the list of reported APs affiliated with the same AP MLD, especially
56
when APs from multiple AP MLDs are reported, and is assigned such that it is unique to an AP MLD only in the frames
57
which carries the Reduced Neighbor Report element describing reported APs affiliated with the AP MLD. Following the
58
rules to set the AP MLD ID field, another AP may use a different AP MLD ID for the same AP MLD.
59
60 NOTE 2—An AP MLD ID subfield set to 255 does not mean that the reported AP has BSSID index set to 255.
61
62
The Link ID subfield indicates the link identifier of the reported AP within the AP MLD with which the
63
reported AP is affiliated. The Link ID subfield is set to 15 if the reported AP is not part of an AP MLD, or if
64
65 the reporting AP does not have that information.

39 The Restricted TWT DL TID Bitmap and Restricted TWT UL TID Bitmap subfields specify the TID(s) that

41 streams in the downlink and uplink direction, respectively. A value of 1 at bit position k in the bitmap indi-
42
cates that TID k is classified as latency sensitive traffic stream. A value of 0 at bit position k in the bitmap
43
indicates that TID k is not classified as latency sensitive traffic stream.

46 9.4.2.217 Max Channel Switch Time element
47
48
49 Change the third paragraph as follows:
50
51
The Switch Time field is a 3-octet field. When the Max Channel Switch Time element is carried outside a
52
53 Basic Multi-Link element, the Switch Time field indicatesindicating the maximum time delta between the
54 time the last Beacon frame is transmitted by the AP in the current channel and the expected time of the first
55 Beacon frame in the new channel, expressed in TUs. When the Max Channel Switch Time element is carried
56
in a Basic Multi-Link element, in the Per-STA Profile subelement corresponding to a reported AP, the
57
Switch Time field indicates the maximum time delta between the time the last Beacon frame is transmitted
58
59 by the reported AP in its current channel and the expected time of the first Beacon frame in its new channel,
60 expressed in TUs, until the last beacon is sent on the current channel. After the last beacon is transmitted on
61 the reported link, the Switch Time field indicates the estimated time delta between the time the frame carry-
62
ing the Basic Multi-Link element containing the Max Channel Switch Time element is transmitted by the
63
reporting AP and the expected time of the first Beacon in the new channel by the reported AP (see 35.3.11
64
65 (Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting)).

43

45 present.
46
47
48 The Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield is a 16-bit bitmap where the lowest numbered bit corresponds to
49 the 20 MHz subchannel that lies within the BSS bandwidth and that has the lowest frequency of the set of all
50
20 MHz subchannels within the BSS bandwidth. Each successive bit in the bitmap corresponds to the next
51
52 higher frequency 20 MHz subchannel. A bit in the bitmap and that lies within the BSS bandwidth is set to 1
53 to indicate that the corresponding 20 MHz subchannel is punctured and is set to 0 to indicate that the corre-
54
sponding 20 MHz subchannel is not punctured. A bit in the bitmap that falls outside of the BSS bandwidth is
55
56 reserved.

subfield is set to 1 if the MLD supports the EMLSR operation; otherwise it is set to 0. For a non-AP MLD,
19

24

25
26 with an AP MLD, the EMLSR Padding Delay subfield is reserved. The EMLSR Padding Delay subfield
27 includes 3 bits and is set as defined in Table 9-401e (Encoding of the EMLSR Padding Delay subfield).

(cid:11)1(cid:100)n(cid:100)(cid:11)NSS_PE+1(cid:12)(cid:12). The NSS_PE subfield values shall be less than 8, and the values greater than or

52
53 401q (RU allocation index). The PPETmax and PPET8 subfields for RU allocation index k are present in the

55 olds field) is 1. For example, if B0 of the RU Index Bitmask subfield (B4 of the EHT PPE Thresholds field)

is 1, the PPETmax and PPET8 subfields are present in the PPE Thresholds Info field for the RU allocation

61 bit equal to 1. To indicate nominal packet padding values of 0 μs for all modes, the PPE Thresholds Present
62

1 vides the TID-to-link mapping information for frames transmitted on the uplink. It is set to 2 if the TID-To-
2 Link Mapping element provides the TID-to-link mapping information for frames transmitted both on the
3
downlink and the uplink. The value of 3 is reserved.
4
5
6 The Default Link Mapping subfield is set to 1 if the TID-To-Link Mapping element represents the default
7 TID-to-link mapping. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
8
9
The Mapping Switch Time Present subfield is set to 1 if the Mapping Switch Time field is present and 0 oth-
10
11 erwise.
12
13
The Expected Duration Present subfield is set to 1 if the Expected Duration field is present and 0 otherwise.
14
15
16 The Link Mapping Size subfield is set to 1 if the length of the Link Mapping Of TID n field is 1 octet and is
17 set to 0 if the length of the Link Mapping Of TID n field is 2 octets.
18
19
The Link Mapping Presence Indicator subfield indicates whether the Link Mapping Of TID n field is present
20
21 in the TID-To-Link Mapping element (i.e., it identifies the TID(s) for which the mapping is provided in the
22 element). A value of 1 in bit position n of the Link Mapping Presence Indicator subfield indicates that the
23 Link Mapping Of TID n field is present in the TID-To-Link Mapping element. Otherwise, the Link Mapping
24
Of TID n field is not present in the TID-To-Link Mapping element. When the Default Link Mapping sub-
25
field is set to 1, this subfield is not present.
26
27
28 The Mapping Switch Time field is present when the TID-To-Link Mapping element is transmitted by an AP
29 affiliated with an AP MLD in a Beacon or Probe Response frame and the indicated TID-to-link mapping is
30
not yet established; otherwise it is not present. The absence of Mapping Switch Time field in the TID-To-
31
Link Mapping element in a Beacon or Probe Response frame transmitted by an AP affiliated with an AP
32
33 MLD indicates that the indicated TID-to-link mapping is already established. The 2 octet Mapping Switch
34 Time field has units of TUs and is set to the time at which the new mapping is established using as a time-
35 base the value of the TSF corresponding to the BSS identified by the BSSID of the frame containing the
36
TID-To-Link Mapping element: i.e., bits 10 to 25 of the TSF.
37
38
39 The Expected Duration field indicates the duration for which the proposed TID-to-link mapping is expected
40 to be effective in units of TUs when the Mapping Switch Time field is present, and the remaining duration
41
for which the proposed TID-to-link mapping is expected to be effective in units of TUs when the Mapping
42
Switch Time field is not present. The Expected Duration field is present if the TID-To-Link Mapping ele-
43
44 ment is carried in a Beacon or a Probe Response frame transmitted by an AP affiliated with an AP MLD, and
45 is not present otherwise.
46
47
The Link Mapping Of TID n field (where n = 0(cid:15)1(cid:15)(cid:125)(cid:15)7) indicates the link(s) on which frames belonging
48
49 to TID n are allowed to be sent (i.e., carries a bitmap of the links to which the TID n is mapped to). A value
50 of 1 in bit position i (where i = 0(cid:15)1(cid:15)(cid:125)(cid:15)14) of the Link Mapping Of TID n field indicates that TID n is
51 mapped to the link associated with the link ID i for the direction as specified in the Direction subfield. A
52
value of 0 in bit position i indicates that the TID n is not mapped to the link associated with the link ID i.
53
When the Default Link Mapping subfield is set to 1, this field is not present.

1 — The User Priority subfield contains the user priority value (0–7) of the data frames that are described
2 by this element. The User Priority subfield is set to the value of the User Priority subfield in the
3
Intra-Access Category Priority element if the Intra-Access Category Priority element is present in the
4
SCS Descriptor element.
5
6 — The Presence Bitmap Of Additional Parameters subfield contains a bitmap where the i-th entry of the
7
bitmap is set to 1 if the i-th field starting from the Maximum MSDU Size field is present in this ele-
8
ment. For each field starting from the Maximum MSDU Size field, the value 0 is reserved.
9
10 — The LinkID subfield contains the link identifier that corresponds to the link for which the direct link
11
transmissions are going to occur. This field is reserved if the Direction subfield is equal to any value
12
but 2 (Direct link).
13
14
NOTE—The presence of the TID subfield is for any future expansion to enable carrying a TID value that is independent
15

17
18 The Minimum Service Interval field contains the following:
19
— If the Direction subfield is set to 0 (Uplink), the Minimum Service Interval field contains an
20
unsigned integer that specifies the minimum interval, in microseconds, between the start of two con-
21
22 secutive SPs that are allocated to the STA for UL frame exchanges and the value 0 is reserved.
23
— If the Direction subfield is set to 1 (Downlink), the Minimum Service Interval field contains an
24
unsigned integer that specifies the minimum interval, in microseconds, between the start of two con-
25
26 secutive SPs that are allocated for DL frame exchange sequences and the value 0 indicates that this
27 parameter is unspecified.
28
— If the Direction subfield is set to 2 (Direct link) the Minimum Service Interval field contains an
29
30 unsigned integer that specifies the minimum interval, in microseconds, between the start of two con-
31 secutive SPs that are allocated to the STA for direct link frame exchanges and the value 0 is reserved.
32
33
The Maximum Service Interval field contains the following:
34
35 — If the Direction subfield is set to 0 (Uplink), the Maximum Service Interval field contains an
36 unsigned integer that specifies the maximum interval, in microseconds, between the start of two con-
37
secutive SPs that are allocated to the STA for UL frame exchanges and the value 0 is reserved.
38
39 — If the Direction subfield is set to 1 (Downlink), the Maximum Service Interval field contains an
40 unsigned integer that specifies the maximum interval, in microseconds, between the start of two con-
41
secutive SPs that are allocated for DL frame exchange sequences and the value 0 indicates that this
42
parameter is unspecified.
43
44 — If the Direction subfield is set to 2 (Direct link) the Maximum Service Interval field contains an
45
unsigned integer that specifies the maximum interval, in microseconds, between the start of two con-
46
secutive SPs that are allocated to the STA for direct link frame exchanges and the value 0 is reserved.
47
48 — The value of this field is greater than or equal to the value of the Minimum Service Interval field.
49
50
51 The Minimum Data Rate field contains an unsigned integer that specifies the lowest data rate specified at the
52 MAC SAP, in kilobits per second, for transport of MSDUs or A-MSDUs belonging to the traffic flow
53 described by this element.
54
55 — If the Direction subfield is set to 0 (Uplink) or 1 (Downlink), the value 0 is reserved.
56 — If the Direction subfield is set to 2 (Direct link), the value 0 indicates that this parameter is unspeci-
57
fied.
58
59
60 The Delay Bound field contains an unsigned integer that specifies the maximum amount of time, in micro-
61 seconds, allowed to transport an MSDU or A-MSDU belonging to the traffic flow described by this element,
62
measured between the time marking the arrival of the MSDU, or the first MSDU of the MSDUs constituting
63
an A-MSDU, at the local MAC sublayer from the local MAC SAP and the time of completion of the suc-
64
65 cessful transmission or retransmission of the MSDU or A-MSDU to the destination. The completion time of
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 297
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 the MSDU or A-MSDU transmission includes the corresponding acknowledgment frame transmission time,
2 if present.
3
4 — If the Direction subfield is set to 0 (Uplink) or 2 (Direct link), the value 0 indicates that this parame-
5 ter is unspecified.
6
— If the Direction subfield is set to 1 (Downlink), the value 0 is reserved.
7
8
— The field is nonzero if the Burst Size field is present.

11 The Maximum MSDU Size field contains an unsigned integer that specifies the maximum size, in octets, of
12 MSDUs or A MSDUs belonging to the traffic flow described by this element.
13
14
The Service Start Time field contains an unsigned integer that specifies the anticipated time, in microsec-
15
16 onds, when the traffic starts for the associated TID. The Service Start Time indicates to the AP the time
17 when the STA expects to exchange frames corresponding to the TID specified in this element. The field rep-
18 resents the four lower order octets of the TSF timer associated to the link specified in the LinkID field at the
19
start of the anticipated SP.
20
21
22 The four LSBs of the Service Start Time LinkID field indicates the link identifier that corresponds to the link
23 for which the TSF timer is used to indicate the Service Start Time. The four MSBs are reserved. This field is
24
present only if the Service Start Time field is present.
25
26
27 The Mean Data Rate field indicates the average data rate specified at the MAC SAP, in kilobits per second,
28 for transport of MSDUs or A-MSDUs belonging to the traffic flow within the bounds of this element.
29
30
The Burst Size field is 4 octets long and contains an unsigned integer that specifies the maximum burst, in
31
32 octets, of the MSDUs or A-MSDUs belonging to the traffic flow that arrive at the MAC SAP within a time
33 duration specified in the Delay Bound field.
34
35
The MSDU Lifetime field contains an unsigned integer that specifies the maximum amount of time, in mil-
36
37 liseconds, since the arrival of the MSDU at the MAC data service interface beyond which the MSDU is not
38 useful even if received by the receiver. Therefore, the MSDU transmitter may consider discarding such
39 MSDU at the transmitter before it is transmitted over-the-air. The amount of time specified in this field is
40
larger than or equal to the amount of time specified in the Delay Bound field, if present.
41
42
43 The MSDU Delivery Info field contains the MSDU delivery information. The MSDU Delivery Info field
44 contains the MSDU Delivery Ratio subfield and the MSDU Count Exponent subfield that are defined as in
45 Figure 9-1002aw (MSDU Delivery Info field format):

5 9.4.2.318 AID Bitmap element
6
7
The AID Bitmap element is used to signal a list of AIDs in a frame transmitted by an AP. The format of this
8
9 element is shown in Figure 9-1002ay (AID Bitmap element format).
10
11
12 Element ID Length Element ID Partial AID Bitmap Control Partial AID
Extension Bitmap Length Bitmap
13
14
Octets: 1 1 1 1 1 0–251
15
16
Figure 9-1002ay—AID Bitmap element format
17
18
19
The Element ID, Length, and Element ID Extension fields are defined in 9.4.2.1 (General).
20
21
22 The Partial AID Bitmap Length field is a one octet field and carries the length of the Partial AID Bitmap
23
field.
24
25
26 The Bitmap Control field is a single octet. Bit 0 of the field is reserved. The remaining 7 bits of the field
27 form the Bitmap Offset as shown in Figure 9-1002az (Bitmap Control field of the AID Bitmap element).
28
29
30 B0 B1 B7
31
32
Reserved Bitmap Offset
33
34
Bits: 1 6
35
36
Figure 9-1002az—Bitmap Control field of the AID Bitmap element
37
38
39
An AID bitmap refers to a bitmap consisting of 2008 bits where a bit position k is set to 1 if AID k is a mem-
40
ber of the signaled list of AIDs and otherwise is set to 0. The AID bitmap is organized into 251 octets such
41
42 that bit number N (cid:11)0(cid:100)N(cid:100)2007(cid:12)in the bitmap corresponds to bit number (N mod 8) in octet number
43 N(cid:101)8 where the low order bit of each octet is bit number 0, and the high order bit is bit number 7.
44
45
The Partial AID Bitmap field consists of octets numbered N1 to N2 of the AID bitmap, where N1 is the larg-
46
47 est even number such that bits numbered 1 to (cid:11)N1(cid:117)8(cid:12)–1in the AID bitmap are all 0 and N2 is the smallest
48 number such that bits numbered (cid:11)N2+1(cid:12)(cid:117)8 to 2007 in the AID bitmap are all 0. The Bitmap Offset sub-
49 field value contains the number N1(cid:101)2, and the Partial AID Bitmap Length field is set to (cid:11)N2–N1(cid:12)+3.
50

9.6.8 FT Action frame details
25
26
27 9.6.8.1 General
28
29
Change the first paragraph as follows:
30
31
32 Four Action frame formats are defined to support fast BSS transitions over the DS, which are initiated
33
through the currently associated APfast BSS transition originator (FTR). The FT Action frames are sent over
34
the air between the STAfast BSS transition originator (FTO) and the current APFTR. The Action frame is
35
36 used as a transport mechanism for data that are destined for the target APFTR. An FT Action field, in the
37 octet immediately after the Category field, differentiates the FT Action frame formats. The FT Action field
38 values associated with each FT Action frame format are defined in Table 9-481 (FT Action field values).
39
40
41 9.6.8.2 FT Request frame
42
43
Change the first paragraph as follows:
44
45
46 The FT Request frame is sent by the STAFTO to its associated APFTR to initiate an over-the-DS fast BSS
47 transition.
48
49
50 Change the sixth paragraph as follows:
51
52
The Target AP Address field is set to the BSSID value of the target APMAC address of the target FTR.

is shown in Figure 9-1151 (BSS Transition Management Query frame Action field format).

1 9.6.13.9 BSS Transition Management Request frame format
2
3
Change the first paragraph as follows:
4
5
6 The BSS Transition Management Request frame is transmitted by an AP or an AP affiliated with an AP
7 MLD in response to a BSS Transition Management Query frame, or autonomously. The format of the BSS
8 Transition Management Request frame Action field is shown in Figure 9-1152 (BSS Transition Manage-
9
ment Request frame Action field format).
10
11
12 Change the fourth, fifth and sixth paragraphs, including Figure 9-1153 (Request Mode field
13 format), as follows:
14
15
The Dialog Token field is defined in 9.4.1.12 (Dialog Token field). It is the nonzero value received in the
16
17 BSS Transition Management Query frame if the BSS Transition Management Request frame is being trans-
18 mitted in response to a BSS Transition Management Query frame. If the BSS Transition Management
19 Request frame is being transmitted other than in response to a BSS Transition Management Query frame,
20
then the Dialog Token field is a nonzero value chosen by the AP or AP MLD sending the BSS Transition
21
Management Request frame to identify the request/response transaction.
22
23
24 The Request Mode field is defined in Figure 9-1153 (Request Mode field format).
25
26
27 B0 B1 B2 B3 B4 B5 B5B6 B7
28
29 Preferred BSS ESS Link
Disassociation
30 Candidate List Abridged Termination Disassociation Removal Reserved
Imminent
31 Included Included Imminent Imminent
32
33 Bits: 1 1 1 1 1 1 32
34
35 Figure 9-1153—Request Mode field format
36
37
— The Preferred Candidate List Included (bit 0) field indicates whether the BSS transition candidate
38
list included in this frame is a preferred candidate list or a list of known BSS transition candidates.
39
40 The Preferred Candidate List Included bit set to 0 indicates that the receiving STA or non-AP MLD
41 can ignore the BSS Transition Candidate List Entries field (see 11.21.7.3 (BSS transition manage-
42 ment request)). The Preferred Candidate List Included bit set to 1 indicates that the sender expects
43
the receiving STA or non-AP MLD to process this frame.
44
45 — The Abridged (bit 1) field indicates to the recipient of the frame the intended treatment of all
46 BSSIDs or AP MLDs not listed in the BSS Transition Candidate List Entries field. The AP or AP
47
MLD sets the Abridged bit in the Request Mode field to 1 when a preference value of 0 is assigned
48
to all BSSIDs or AP MLDs that do not appear in the BSS Transition Candidate List. The AP or AP
49
50 MLD sets the Abridged bit in the Request Mode field to 0 when the AP or AP MLD has no recom-
51 mendation for or against any BSSID or AP MLD not present in the BSS Transition Candidate List
52 Entries field.
53
— The Disassociation Imminent (bit 2) field indicates whether the STA or the non-AP MLD will be
54
55 disassociated from the current AP or AP MLD. The value 1 in the Disassociation Imminent bit in the
56 Request Mode field indicates that the STA or the non-AP MLD is to be disassociated from the cur-
57 rent AP or AP MLD, while the value 0 indicates that disassociation from the AP or AP MLD is not
58
imminent.
59
60 — The BSS Termination Included (bit 3) field indicates whether the BSS Termination Duration field is
61 included and indicates the following:, the BSS is shutting down and the STA will be disassociated.
62
The AP sets the BSS Termination Included bit in the Request Mode field to 1 to indicate that the
63
BSS is shutting down. The BSS Termination Included bit is 0 if no BSS Termination Duration infor-
64
65 mation is included in the BSS Transition Management Request frame.

1 • For an AP that is not affiliated with an AP MLD, it indicates that the corresponding BSS is shut-
2 ting down and that the STA associated with the AP will be disassociated.
3
• For an AP MLD that operates with only one affiliated AP, it indicates that the AP MLD and the
4
BSS are shutting down and that the STA and the non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD or
5
6 the corresponding affiliated AP will be disassociated.
7 • For an AP MLD that operates with more than one affiliated AP,
8
• If the Link Removal Imminent field in the Request Mode field is equal to 0, it indicates that
9
the AP MLD and all its BSSs are shutting down and that the STA associated with any affili-
10
11 ated AP and the non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD will be disassociated.
12 • If the Link Removal Imminent field in the Request Mode field is equal to 1, it indicates that
13
the BSS whose AP transmits this BSS Transition Management Request frame is shutting
14
down, the STA associated with the AP or the non-AP MLD that has set up only this link will
15
16 be disassociated and the non-AP MLD that has set up more than this link remains associated
17 to the AP MLD with the remaining setup link(s).
18
— The ESS Disassociation Imminent (bit 4) field indicates that the Session Information URL field is
19
included, and that the STA or non-AP MLD will be disassociated from the ESS. The value 1 in the
20
21 ESS Disassociation Imminent bit in the Request Mode field indicates that the STA or the non-AP
22 MLD is to be disassociated from the ESS, while the value 0 indicates that disassociation from the
23 ESS is not imminent. When the ESS Disassociation Imminent bit value is 1, a Session Information
24
URL field is included in the BSS Transition Management Request frame.
25
26 — For an AP MLD that operates with more than one affiliated AP, it sets the Link Removal Imminent
27 field (bit 5) to 1 to limit the scope of the BSS termination to the link on which the request is being
28
transmitted if the BSS Termination Included field (bit 3) is set to 1 (see 35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affili-
29
ated APs)), and otherwise, it sets the field to 0. If a receiving STA is not affiliated with a non-AP
30
31 MLD, it will ignore the Link Removal Imminent (bit 5) field. When the Link Removal Imminent
32 (bit 5) field is set to 1,
33
• If a receiving STA is affiliated with a non-AP MLD that has set up only this link, then the non-
34
35 AP MLD will be disassociated (see 35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affiliated APs)).
36 • If a receiving STA is affiliated with a non-AP MLD that has set up more than this link, and the
37
AP MLD is announcing that the AP will be removed according to procedures defined in
38
35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affiliated APs), then the non-AP MLD is still associated to the AP MLD
39
40 with the remaining setup link(s).
41 • If a receiving STA is affiliated with a non-AP MLD that has set up more than this link, and the
42 AP MLD is advertising that the AP link will be disabled for all associated non-AP MLDs accord-
43
ing to procedures defined in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe
44
Response frames), then the non-AP MLD may ignore the BTM request and remains associated to
45
46 the AP MLD on all currently setup link(s) while the link is disabled and re-enabled in a future
47 time.
48
The Link Removal Imminent (bit 5) field is reserved if one of the following conditions is met:
49
50 • when theThe transmitting AP is not affiliated with an AP MLD.
51
• The transmitting AP is affiliated with an AP MLD that operates with only one affiliated AP.or
52
when the BSS Termination Included field is zero, and is ignored by a receiving STA that is not
53
54 affiliated with a non-AP MLD or when the BSS Termination Included field is zero. The field is
55 set to 1 to limit the scope of the BSS termination to the link on which the request is being trans-
56 mitted, and is set to 0 otherwise.
57
• The BSS Termination Included field is set to zero.
58

61 STA or the AP affiliated with the AP MLD sends a Disassociation frame to this non-AP STA affiliated with
62
this non-AP MLD. The Disassociation Timer field contains the number of beacon transmission times
63
(TBTTs) until the AP sends a Disassociation frame to this STA or the AP affiliated with the AP MLD sends
64
65 a Disassociation frame to the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD. Setting the field to 0 indicates

15

17 Change the first paragraph as follows:
18
19
The BSS Transition Management Response frame is optionally transmitted by a STA or a non-AP STA
20

21
22 the BSS Transition Management Response frame Action field is shown in Figure 9-1156 (BSS Transition
23 Management Response frame Action field format).

Change the fifth to eighth paragraphs as follows:
26
27
28 The BTM Status Code field contains the status code in response to a BSS Transition Management Request
29 frame as defined in Table 9-511 (BTM status code definitions). If the STA or non-AP MLD will transition to
30
another BSS or the non-AP MLD will transition to another AP MLD, then the status code is 0 (i.e., Accept).
31
If the STA or non-AP MLD intends to retain the association with the current BSS or AP MLD, the status
32
33 code is one of the “Reject” status codes.
34
NOTE—For a non-AP MLD, it can transition from the current AP MLD to either another AP MLD or another AP that is
35
not affiliated with an AP MLD.
36
37
38 The BSS Termination Delay field is the number of minutes that the responding STA or non-AP MLD
39 requests the BSS or AP MLD to delay termination. This field is reserved if the Status code field value is not
40 set to 5.
41
42
The Target BSSID field is the BSSID of the BSS that the non-AP STA or non-AP MLD transitions to or the
43
44 MLD MAC address of the AP MLD that the non-AP MLD transitions to. This field is present if the Status
45 code subfield contains 0, and not present otherwise.
46
47
The BSS Transition Candidate List Entries field contains zero or more Neighbor Report elements described
48
49 in 9.4.2.36 (Neighbor Report element). The Neighbor Report elements are collected by the STA or non-AP
50 MLD as part of its scanning procedures and provided to the AP or AP MLD as described in 11.21.7.4 (BSS
51 transition management response) and 35.3.23 (BSS transition management for MLDs). The length of the
52
BSS Transition Candidate List Entries field in a BSS Transition Management Response frame is limited by
53
the maximum MMPDU size (see 9.3.3.1 (Format of (PV0) Management frames)

38 9.6.35.2 TID-To-Link Mapping Request frame format
39
40
41 An MLD uses the TID-to-link Mapping Request frame to negotiate a TID-to-link mapping for setup links
42 with a peer MLD. The Action field of the TID-to-link Mapping Request frame contains the information

39

9.7.1 A-MPDU format
41
42
43 Change the fourth paragraph as follows:
44
45
The EOF Padding field is shown in Figure 9-1204 (EOF Padding field format). This is present only in a
46
VHT, EDMG, S1G, or HE, or EHT PPDU.
47
48
49 Change the sixth paragraph as follows:
50
51
In a VHT, EDMG, S1G, or HE, or EHT PPDU, the following padding is present, as determined by the rules
52
53 in 10.12.6 (A-MPDU padding for VHT, HE, EHT or S1G PPDU):
54
— 0–3 octets in the Padding subfield of the final A-MPDU subframe (see Figure 9-1205 (A-MPDU
55
subframe format)) before any EOF padding subframes. The content of these octets is unspecified.
56
57 — Zero or more EOF padding subframes in the EOF Padding Subframes subfield.
58
— 0–3 octets in the EOF Padding Octets subfield. The content of these octets is unspecified.
59
60

62
63

64
65 MPDU in a DMG PPDU is 262 143 octets. The maximum length of an A-MPDU pre-EOF padding in a

23 9.7.3 A-MPDU contents
24
25 Change the first paragraph as follows:
26
27
In a non-DMG PPDU, an A-MPDU is a sequence of A-MPDU subframes carried in a single PPDU with one
28
29 of the following combinations of RXVECTOR or TXVECTOR parameter values:
30
— The FORMAT parameter set to VHT
31
32 — The FORMAT parameter set to HT_MF or HT_GF and the AGGREGATION parameter set to 1
33
— The FORMAT parameter set to S1G, S1G_DUP_1M, or S1G_DUP_2M and the AGGREGATION
34
parameter set to 1.
35
36 — The FORMAT parameter set to HE_SU, HE_MU, HE_TB, or HE_ER_SU.
37
38 — The FORMAT parameter set to EHT_MU or EHT_TB.
39
40 Change the sixth paragraph as follows:
41
42
The Duration/ID fields in the MAC headers of all MPDUs in an A-MPDU carry the same value. The Dura-
43
44 tion/ID fields in the MAC headers of the MPDUs in the A-MPDUs carried in a VHT MU PPDU, and an HE
45 MU PPDU, and an EHT MU PPDU carry the same value.
46
47
Change the tenth paragraph as follows:
48
49
50 A VHT MU PPDU, S1G MU PPDU, and HE MU PPDU, and EHT MU PPDU do not carry more than one
51 A-MPDU that contains one or more MPDUs soliciting an immediate response if the immediate response is
52 carried in a PPDU that is not an HE TB PPDU and is not an EHT TB PPDU. An HE MU PPDU and an EHT
53
MU PPDU can carry more than one A-MPDU each of which contains one or more MPDUs soliciting an
54
immediate response if the immediate response is carried in an HE TB PPDU or an EHT TB PPDU.
55
56
57 Change the 12th paragraph as follows:
58
59 NOTE 4—If a STA supports A-MSDUs of 7935 octets (indicated by the Maximum A-MSDU Length field in the HT
60 Capabilities element or in Reconfiguration Multi-Link element with operation update type equal to 0), A-MSDUs trans-
61 mitted by that TA within an A-MPDU carried in a PPDU with FORMAT HT_MF or HT_GF or within an MPDU carried
62 in a non-HT PPDU are constrained so that the length of the QoS Data frame carrying the A-MSDU is no more than 4095
63 octets. The 4095-octet MPDU length limit does not apply to A-MPDUs carried in VHT, HE, EHT or DMG PPDUs. The
64 use of A-MSDU within A-MPDU might be further constrained as described in 9.4.1.13 (Block Ack Parameter Set field)
65 through the operation of the A-MSDU Supported field.

1 10. MAC sublayer functional description
2
3
4
10.1 Introduction
5
6
7
Change as follows:
8
9
10
The MAC functional description is presented in this clause. The architecture of the MAC sublayer, including
11
the distributed coordination function (DCF), the hybrid coordination function (HCF), the mesh coordination
12
13 function (MCF), the triggered UL access (TUA), and their coexistence in an IEEE 802.11 LAN are intro-
14 duced in 10.2 (MAC architecture). These functions are expanded on in 10.3 (DCF), 10.23 (HCF), 10.24 (Mesh
15 coordination function (MCF)), and 26.2 (HE channel access), 35.2 (EHT channel access), and 35.3.15
16
(Multi-link operation group addressed frames). Fragmentation and defragmentation are defined in
17
10.4 (MSDU and MMPDU fragmentation) and 10.5 (MSDU and MMPDU defragmentation). Multirate sup-
18
19 port is addressed in 10.6 (Multirate support). A number of additional restrictions to limit the cases in which
20 MSDUs are reordered or discarded are described in 10.7 (MSDU transmission restrictions). Operation across
21 regulatory domains is defined in 10.22 (Operation across regulatory domains). The block ack mechanism is
22
described in 10.25 (Block acknowledgment (block ack)). The No Ack mechanism is described in 10.26 (No
23
Acknowledgment (No Ack)). The protection mechanism is described in 10.27 (Protection mechanisms).
24
25 Rules for processing MAC frames are described in 10.28 (MAC frame processing).

1 10.3 DCF

10.3.2 Procedures common to the DCF and EDCAF

6 10.3.2.9 CTS and DMG CTS procedure
7
8
Insert the following two paragraphs as the first and second paragraphs of the subclause:
9
10
11 In this subclause, a STA is NSTR limited if all of the following conditions are true:
12
— the STA is affiliated with an MLD that has at least one NSTR link pair
13
14 — the STA has received the RTS on a link that is a member of one or more of the MLD’s NSTR link
15 pairs
16
— another STA affiliated with the same MLD is a TXOP holder or TXOP responder on one of the other
17
18 links that is a member of at least one of the NSTR link pairs of which the link on which the RTS was
19 received is a member
20
21
If at least one of the above conditions is not true, then the STA is not NSTR limited.
22
23
24 Change the now-shifted third paragraph as follows:
25
26
A STA that receives an RTS frame addressed to it considers whether the STA is NSTR limited and considers
27
the NAV in determining whether to respond with CTS, unless the NAV was set by a frame originating from
28
29 the STA sending the RTS frame (see 10.24.2.2 (EDCA backoff procedure)). In this subclause for a non-S1G
30 STA, “NAV indicates idle” means that the NAV count is 0 or that the NAV count is nonzero but the
31 nonbandwidth signaling TA obtained from the TA field of the RTS frame matches the saved TXOP holder
32
address. In an S1G STA, “NAV indicates idle” means that both NAV and RID counters are 0 or that either
33
NAV or RID counter is nonzero but the TA field of the RTS frame matches the saved TXOP holder address.
34
35
36 Change the now-shifted fourth and fifth paragraphs as follows:
37
38
A VHT STA that is not an EHT STA and is addressed by an RTS frame in a non-HT or non-HT duplicate
39
PPDU that has a bandwidth signaling TA and that has the RXVECTOR parameter
40
41 DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT equal to Static behaves as follows:
42 — If the NAV indicates idle and CCA has been idle for all secondary channels (secondary 20 MHz
43
channel, secondary 40 MHz channel, and secondary 80 MHz channel) in the channel width
44
indicated by the RTS frame’s RXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT for a
45
46 PIFS prior to the start of the RTS frame, then the STA shall respond with a CTS frame carried in a
47 non-HT or non-HT duplicate PPDU after a SIFS. The CTS frame’s TXVECTOR parameters
48 CH_BANDWIDTH and CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT shall be set to the same value as the
49
RTS frame’s RXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT.
50
51 — Otherwise, the STA shall not respond with a CTS frame.
52
53
A VHT STA that is not an EHT STA and is addressed by an RTS frame in a non-HT or non-HT duplicate
54
PPDU that has a bandwidth signaling TA and that has the RXVECTOR parameter
55
56 DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT equal to Dynamic behaves as follows:
57 — If the NAV indicates idle then the STA shall respond with a CTS frame in a non-HT or non-HT
58
duplicate PPDU after a SIFS. The CTS frame’s TXVECTOR parameters CH_BANDWIDTH and
59
CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT shall be set to any channel width for which CCA on all
60
61 secondary channels has been idle for a PIFS prior to the start of the RTS frame and that is less than
62 or equal to the channel width indicated in the RTS frame’s RXVECTOR parameter
63 CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT.
64
65 — Otherwise, the STA shall not respond with a CTS frame.

1 10.3.2.14 Duplicate detection and recovery

10.3.2.14.2 Transmitter requirements
4
5
6 Change the first paragraph as follows:
7
8
A STA maintains one or more sequence number spaces that are used when transmitting a frame to determine
9
10 the sequence number for the frame. An MLD maintains one or more sequence number spaces that are used
11 when a STA affiliated with the MLD transmits an individually addressed QoS Data frame to a STA affiliated
12 with an associated MLD to determine the sequence number for the frame. An MLD with dot11QMFActivated
13
equal to false maintains a single sequence number space that is used when the MLD transmits through a
14
STA affiliated with the MLD an individually addressed Management frame (except for a frame that is
15
16 excluded as defined in 35.3.14 (Multi-link device individually addressed Management frame delivery)) to a
17 STA affiliated with another MLD to determine the sequence number for the frame. When multiple sequence
18 number spaces are supported, the appropriate sequence number space is determined by information from the
19
MAC control fields of the frame to be transmitted. Except as noted below, each sequence number space is rep-
20
resented by a modulo 4096 counter, starting at 0 and incrementing by 1, for each MSDU or MMPDU transmit-
21
22 ted using that sequence number space. If dot11MACPrivacyActivated is true, the counter in each sequence
23 number space shall be set to a random number modulo 4096 when the STA’s MAC address is changed.
24
25
Change the fourth paragraph as follows:
26
27
28 A transmitting STA shall support the applicable sequence number spaces defined in Table 10-5 (Transmitter
29 sequence number spaces). An MLD shall support the applicable sequence number spaces defined in
30
Table 10-5 (Transmitter sequence number spaces) with the Status indicated as Mandatory. A STA affiliated
31
with an MLD shall use SNS9 in Table 10-5 (Transmitter sequence number spaces) maintained by the MLD
32
33 to determine the sequence number of an individually addressed QoS Data frame that is transmitted to a STA
34 affiliated with another MLD. A STA affiliated with an MLD shall use SNS10 in Table 10-5 (Transmitter
35 sequence number spaces) maintained by the MLD to determine the sequence number of an individually
36
addressed Management frame (except for a frame that is excluded as defined in 35.3.14 (Multi-link device
37
individually addressed Management frame delivery)) that is transmitted to a STA affiliated with another
38
39 MLD. An AP MLD shall use SNS11 in Table 10-5 (Transmitter sequence number spaces) maintained by the
40 MLD to determine the sequence number of a group addressed Data frame that is transmitted by an AP affil-
41 iated with the AP MLD so that the same group addressed Data frame transmitted over multiple links by the
42
AP MLD uses the same sequence number for transmission on each link. Applicability is defined by the
43
Applies to column. The Status column indicates the level of support that is required if the Applies to column
44
45 matches the transmission. The Multiplicity column indicates whether the sequence number space contains a
46 single counter, or multiple counters and in the latter case identifies any indexes. The Transmitter require-
47 ments column identifies requirements for the operation of this sequence number space. The referenced
48
requirements are defined at the end of the table.

10.3.2.14.3 Receiver requirements
55
56
57 Change the first paragraph as follows:
58
59
60 A STA and an MLD maintains one or more duplicate detection caches. Table 10-6 (Receiver caches) defines
61 the conditions under which a duplication detection cache is supported and the rules followed by the receiver for
62
the cache. When a Data, Management or Extension frame is received, a record of that frame is inserted in an
63
appropriate cache. That record is identified by a sequence number and possibly other information from the
64
65 MAC control fields of the frame. When a Data, Management or Extension frame is received in which the Retry
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 335
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 336 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 subfield of the Frame Control field is equal to 1, the appropriate cache, if any, is searched for a matching frame.
2 In DMG, when a group addressed frame is received the appropriate cache is searched for a matching frame.
3
When a PV1 Data frame or PV1 Management frame is received, the appropriate cache is searched for a
4
matching frame, regardless of the presence of the Retry subfield of the Frame Control field. If the search is
5
6 successful, the frame is considered to be a duplicate. Duplicate frames are discarded.

11
12 A receiving STA shall implement the applicable receiver requirements defined in Table 10-6 (Receiver

14 ments defined in Table 10-6 (Receiver caches) with the Status indicated as Mandatory. All STAs affiliated
15
with an MLD shall use RC14 in Table 10-6 (Receiver caches), where the duplicate detection cache is main-
16
tained by the MLD, to assist the MLD in discarding duplicate individually addressed QoS Data frames
17
18 belonging to a TID without BA negotiation that are transmitted from the STAs affiliated with another MLD.
19 All STAs affiliated with an MLD with dot11QMFActivated equal to false shall use RC15 in Table 10-6
20 (Receiver caches), where the duplicate detection cache is maintained by the MLD, to assist the MLD in dis-
21
carding duplicate individually addressed Management frame (except the frames that are excluded in 35.3.14
22
(Multi-link device individually addressed Management frame delivery)) that are transmitted from the STAs
23
24 affiliated with another MLD. An MLD shall implement RC16 in Table 10-6 (Receiver caches) maintained
25 by the MLD to discard duplicate group addressed Data that are delivered from the associated MLD. A dupli-
26 cated group addressed Data frame received on any link shall be discarded. The method used to handle the
27
sequence number wrap around for duplicate detection is implementation specific. A receiving STA should
28
implement the applicable receiver requirements defined in Table 10-6 (Receiver caches) with the Status
29
30 indicated as Recommended. A receiving STA and a receiving MLD may implement the applicable receiver
31 requirements defined in Table 10-6 (Receiver caches) with Status indicated as Optional. Applicability is
32 defined by the Applies to column. The Status column indicates the level of support that is required if the
33
Applies to column matches the received frame. The Multiplicity / Cache size column indicates the indexes
34
that identify a cache entry and the number of entries that shall be supported. The Receiver requirements col-
35
36 umn identifies requirements for the operation of this cache. The referenced requirements are defined at the
37 end of the table. The requirements relate to caching information that identifies a cache entry and discarding
38 duplicate MPDUs.
39

## 第 345 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 10.6.11 Non-HT basic rate calculation
2
3
4 Change as follows:
5
6
This subclause defines how to convert an HT-MCS, a VHT-MCS, or an HE-MCS, or an EHT-MCS to a non-
7
8 HT basic rate for the purpose of determining the rate of the response frame. It consists of two steps as follows:
9
10 a) Use the modulation and coding rate determined from the HT-MCS (defined in 19.5 (Parameters for
11 HT-MCSs)), or VHT-MCS (defined in 21.5 (Parameters for VHT-MCSs)), or HE-MCS (defined in
12 27.5 (Parameters for HE-MCSs) or EHT-MCS (defined in 36.5 (Parameters for EHT-MCSs)) to
13 locate a non-HT reference rate by lookup into Table 10-10 (Non-HT reference rate).1 In the case of
14
an MCS with UEQM, the modulation of stream 1 is used.
15
16
b) The non-HT basic rate is the highest rate in the BSSBasicRateSet that is less than or equal to this
17
non-HT reference rate.
18
19
20
21 Table 10-10—Non-HT reference rate
22
23
24 Coding rate Non-HT reference rate
Modulation
25 (R) (Mb/s)
26
27 BPSK 1/2 6
28
29 BPSK 3/4 9
30
QPSK 1/2 12
31
32
QPSK 3/4 18
33
34 16-QAM 1/2 24
35
36 16-QAM 3/4 36
37
38 64-QAM 1/2 48
39
64-QAM 2/3 48
40
41
64-QAM 3/4 54
42
43 64-QAM 5/6 54
44
45 256-QAM 3/4 54
46
47 256-QAM 5/6 54
48
1024-QAM 3/4 54
49
50
1024-QAM 5/6 54
51
52 4096-QAM 3/4 54
53
54 4096-QAM 5/6 54
55
56
57
58 NOTE 1—The selection of a non-HT basic rate for the frame sent in response to an HE or EHT PPDU is not influenced
59 by DCM encoding in the HE or EHT PPDU.
60
61
NOTE 2—In a TVWS band, the non-HT reference rate is scaled as described in 22.2.4 (Support for NON_HT and HT
62
formats).
63
64
65 1 For example, if an HT PPDU transmission uses 64-QAM and coding rate of 3/4, the related non-HT reference rate is 54 Mb/s.

27 10.23.2 HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)

29
10.23.2.2 EDCA backoff procedure
30
31
32 Change the fourth paragraphs as follows:
33
34
The backoff procedure shall be invoked by an EDCAF if any of the following events occurs:
35
36 a) An MA-UNITDATA.request primitive is received or the transmit queues associated with that AC
37 have become nonempty due to the conditions in 35.3.16.4 (Nonsimultaneous transmit and receive
38 (NSTR) operation), where that causes an MPDU corresponding to the EDCAF’s AC to be queued
39
for transmission such that all of the following are true:
40
41 1) one of the transmit queues associated with that AC has now become non-empty
42
2) any other transmit queues associated with that AC are empty
43
44 3) the backoff counter has a value of 0 for that AC
45
4) the medium is busy on the primary channel as indicated by any of the following:
46
47 — physical CS
48
— virtual CS
49
50 — a nonzero TXNAV timer value
51
— for a mesh STA that has dot11MCCAActivated true, a nonzero RAV timer value.
52
53 b) For the EDCAF that is the TXOP holder, the transmission of the final PPDU transmitted by the
54 TXOP holder during the TXOP has completed and the TXNAV timer has expired, the PPDU does
55
not solicit an HE TB PPDU.
56
57 c) For the EDCAF that is the TXOP holder, the transmission of an MPDU in the initial PPDU of a
58 TXOP fails, as defined in this subclause, the PPDU does not solicit an HE TB PPDU.
59
d) A transmission attempt by the EDCAF collides internally with another EDCAF of an AC that has
60
61 higher priority, that is, two or more EDCAFs in the same STA are granted a TXOP at the same time.
62
e) The transmission of at least one MPDU in the final PPDU transmitted by the TXOP holder during
63
the TXOP for that AC has completed, the PPDU contains an MPDU that solicits an HE TB PPDU
64
65 and the TXNAV timer has expired.

This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 354 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 f) The transmission of all MPDUs in the initial PPDU of a TXOP fails, as defined in this subclause,
2 and the PPDU contains an MPDU that solicits an HE TB PPDU.
3
4 g) If explicitly indicated, such as in 26.17.2.3.3 (Non-AP STA scanning behavior).
5
h) If explicitly indicated as in 35.3.16.4 (Nonsimultaneous transmit and receive (NSTR) operation).
6
7
8 In addition, the backoff procedure may be invoked by an EDCAF if:
9
i) For the EDCAF that is the TXOP holder, the transmission by the TXOP holder of an MPDU in a
10
non-initial PPDU of a TXOP fails, as defined in this subclause, and an MPDU in the non-initial
11
12 PPDU does not solicit an HE TB PPDU.
13
14 j) The transmission by the TXOP holder of all MPDUSMPDUs in a non-initial PPDU of a TXOP fails,
15
as defined in this subclause, and the PPDU contains an MPDU that solicits an HE TB PPDU.
16
17
NOTE 1—If the transmission by the TXOP holder of an MPDU in a non-initial PPDU of a TXOP failed, the STA can
18
perform either a PIFS recovery, as described in n (Transmit a punctured non-HT duplicate PPDU if all of the 20 MHz
19
subchannels that are not punctured were idle during an interval of PIFS immediately preceding the start of the TXOP.),
20
perform a backoff as described in item e)i) above, or wait for the TXNAV timer to expire and invoke the backoff
21
procedure per item b) above. How it chooses among these options is implementation dependent.
22
23
A STA that performs a backoff within its existing TXOP per item e)i) above shall not extend the TXNAV timer
24
25 value (see n (Transmit a punctured non-HT duplicate PPDU if all of the 20 MHz subchannels that are not
26 punctured were idle during an interval of PIFS immediately preceding the start of the TXOP.)).
27
28 NOTE 2—In other words, the backoff is a continuation of the TXOP, not the start of a new TXOP.
29
30
If the backoff procedure is invoked for reason a) or h) above, CW[AC] and QSRC[AC] shall be left
31
unchanged.

33 ack agreement, the originator”):

35
In a block ack agreement between two EHT STAs, the buffer size is indicated based on the Buffer Size sub-
36
37 field (of the Block Ack Parameter Set field) together with the Extended Buffer Size field (when ADDBA
38 Extension element is included). During a block ack agreement establishment, the buffer size indication is
39 advisory in an ADDBA Request frame.
40
41 NOTE 1—The ADDBA Extension element is optionally present in an ADDBA Request or ADDBA Response frame
42 (see 9.6.4 (Block Ack Action frame details)). When block ack agreement is negotiated between two EHT STAs, if
43 ADDBA Extension element is present, then the total buffer size is computed as described in 9.4.2.139 (ADDBA Exten-
44 sion element).

originator may change the size of its transmission window (WinSize ) such that:
51 O

O
53 ADDBA Response frame if either STA is a non-EHT STA.
54

56 frame if both STAs are EHT STAs.
57

60

62 frame are HE STAs.
63
— The transmission window is not greater than 1024 if the sender and receiver of the ADDBA
64
65 Response frame are EHT STAs.

10.25.7 Protected block ack agreement
16
17
18 Change the first paragraph as follows:
19
20
A STA indicates support for protected block ack by setting the MFPC subfield in the RSN Capabilities field
21
to 1 (see 9.4.2.24.4 (RSN capabilities)) and the PBAC subfield in the Extended RSN Capabilities field to 1
22
23 (see 9.4.2.241 (RSN Extension element (RSNXE))). Such a STA is a PBAC STA; otherwise, the STA is a
24 non-PBAC STA. All STAs affiliated with the same MLD advertise the same PBAC capability (see 12.6.2
25 (RSNA selection) and 12.6.3.1 (General)). An MLD whose affiliated STAs indicate support for protected
26
block ack is a PBAC MLD; otherwise the MLD is a non-PBAC MLD. A block ack agreement that is suc-
27
cessfully negotiated between two PBAC STAs or between two PBAC MLDs is a protected block ack agree-
28
29 ment. A block ack agreement that is successfully negotiated between two STAs when either or both of the
30 STAs is not a PBAC STA is a block ack agreement that is not a protected block ack agreement. A block ack
31 agreement that is successfully negotiated between two MLDs when either or both MLDs is non-PBAC MLD
32
is a block ack agreement that is not a protected block ack agreement.
33
34
35 Change the now-shifted fifth paragraph as follows (not all items are shown):

39
control during full-state operation) to 10.25.6.6 (Receive reordering buffer control operation):
40

42
frame or an MU-BAR Trigger frame or a GCR-MU BAR Trigger frame for the purposes of updating
43
the value of WinStart and WinStart . If the Block Ack Starting Sequence Control subfield value is
44 B R
45 greater than WinEnd B or less than WinStart B , dot11PBACErrors shall be incremented by 1. If, for a

50 10.27 Protection mechanisms
51
52
Insert the following subclause at the end of 10.27 (Protection mechanisms):
53
54
55 10.27.8 Protection rules for EHT STAs
56
57
An EHT STA operating in the 2.4 GHz band is subject to all of the rules for HT STAs that apply to that
58
59 band, except that a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to EHT_MU or EHT_TB may be
60 substituted for a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to HT_MF.
61
62
An EHT STA operating in the 5 GHz band is subject to all of the rules for VHT STAs that apply to that
63
band, except that a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to EHT_MU or EHT_TB may be
64
65 substituted for a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to VHT.

10.28.5 Operation of the Dialog Token field
7
8
9 Change as follows:
10
11 A dialog token is an integer value that assists a STA or an MLD in grouping Management frames sent or
12
received at different times as part of the same dialog. The algorithm by which the integer value for the dialog
13
is selected is implementation specific, but should be selected in a manner that minimizes the probability of a
14
15 frame associated with one dialog being incorrectly associated with another dialog between two STAs or two
16 MLDs.

affiliated with the MLD.

Change NOTE 4 as follows:

7
8 NOTE 4—Modification of an element means that at least one field in the element is changed, although not all fields in
9 an element can be changed (e.g., the fields that advertise the basic MCS sets in HT Operation, VHT Operation, and HE
10 Operation elements do not change). Inclusion of an element means that the element is included in a Beacon frame. The
11 insertion of an element means that the element was not present in the previous Beacon frame, is present in the current
12 Beacon frame, and will be carried in the next Beacon frame. Inclusion/modification of an element for a nontransmitted
13 BSSID is done in a Beacon frame transmitted by the transmitted BSSID either by including/modifying the element in the
14 nontransmitted BSSID profile of the Multiple BSSID element or by including/modifying the element for the transmitted
15 BSSID if that element is inherited for the nontransmitted BSSID (see 11.1.3.8.4 Inheritance of element values).

18 11.3.1 General
19
20
Insert the following two paragraphs as the first two paragraphs of the subclause:
21
22
23 In 11.3 (STA authenticationAuthentication and association), a reference to a “STA” means that the “STA” is
24 not affiliated with an MLD unless specified otherwise.
25
26
In 11.3 (STA authenticationAuthentication and association), when referring to authentication,
27
28 deauthentication, (re)association, disassociation, or 4-way handshake between MLDs, a reference to “SME”
29 means the entity that manages the MLD.
30
31
11.3.2 State variables
32
33
34 Insert the following paragraph after the now-shifted third paragraph (“A STA (local) for which
35 dot11OCBAActiviated ...”):
36
37
An MLD (local) keeps an enumerated state variable for each MLD (remote) with which direct
38
communication between two MLDs through one STA affiliated with the local MLD to another STA
39
40 affiliated with the remote MLD via the WM is needed. In this context, direct communication between two
41 MLDs through one STA affiliated with the local MLD to another STA affiliated with the remote MLD refers
42 to the transmission of any Class 2 or Class 3 frame with an Address 1 field that matches the MAC address of
43
the STA affiliated with the remote MLD and an Address 2 field that matches the MAC address of the STA
44
affiliated with the local MLD.
45
46
47 Insert the following paragraph after the now-shifted seventh paragraph (“For nonmesh STAs,
48 this state variable ...”):
49
50
For MLDs, this state variable expresses the relationship between the local MLD and the remote MLD. It
51
52 takes on the following values:
53 — State 1: Initial start state for MLDs that perform IEEE 802.11 authentication. Unauthenticated and
54
unassociated.
55
56 — State 2: Authenticated but unassociated.
57
— State 3: Authenticated and associated (Pending RSNA Authentication). The IEEE 802.1X Controlled
58
Port is blocked.
59
60 — State 4: Authenticated and associated (RSNA Established or Not Required). The IEEE 802.1X
61
Controlled Port is unblocked, or not present.
