Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 479 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3 Multi-link operation
2
3
35.3.1 General
4
5
6 An EHT AP shall set dot11MultiLinkActivated to true and shall be affiliated with an AP MLD. An AP MLD
7 and all of its affiliated AP(s) shall follow the rules defined in 35.3 (Multi-link operation).
8
9
A non-AP EHT STA with dot11MultiLinkActivated equal to true shall be affiliated with a non-AP MLD. A
10
non-AP MLD and all of its affiliated non-AP STA(s) shall follow the rules defined in 35.3 (Multi-link
11
12 operation).
13
14 A non-AP EHT STA with dot11MultiLinkActivated equal to false shall not be affiliated with any non-AP
15
MLD.
16
17
18 MLO enables a non-AP MLD to discover, authenticate, associate, and set up multiple links with an AP
19 MLD. Each link enables channel access and frame exchanges between the non-AP MLD and the AP MLD
20 based on the supported capabilities exchanged during association. A non-AP MLD may establish a single
21
link TDLS direct link with another non-AP MLD or a non-AP STA as defined in 35.3.21 (TDLS procedure
22
in multi-link operation).
23
24
25 Each STA affiliated with an MLD may select and manage its capabilities and operating parameters
26 independently from the other STA(s) affiliated with the same MLD, unless specified otherwise.
27
28 NOTE 1—For example, each AP affiliated with an AP MLD can independently select, disable, and update its BSS color
29 (see 26.17.3 (BSS color)) for its BSS
30
31
NOTE 2—Examples of operating parameters that are selected at the MLD level (i.e., not independently selected by
32
affiliated STAs) are the listen interval (see 35.3.12.6 (Operation for MLD listen interval)) and the WNM sleep interval
33
(see 11.2.3.1 (General)).
34
35
Each STA affiliated with an MLD shall follow the procedures in 11.1.3 (Maintaining synchronization). An
36
AP MLD shall correct the clock drift to be within ±30 μs between TSF timers of any two APs affiliated with
37
38 it.
39
40 NOTE 3—An AP affiliated with an AP MLD provides TSF offset in the complete profile of a reported AP (see
41 9.4.2.312.2.4 (Link Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element)). A non-AP MLD can determine the TSF information of
42 all the APs affiliated with an AP MLD when it receives a frame carrying TSF of any one AP affiliated with that AP
43 MLD (also see 35.3.12.2 (Basic BSS operation)) and use that information to maintain TSF timer for each non-AP STA
44 per 11.1.3 (Maintaining synchronization).
45
46 NOTE 4—All APs affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD have the same TSF timer (see 35.3.19 (NSTR mobile AP
47 MLD operation)).
48
49 An AP MLD shall operate with one or more affiliated APs.
50
51
A non-AP MLD shall operate with one or more affiliated non-AP STAs.
52
53
NOTE 5—An AP MLD might operate with a single affiliated AP, for example, after removing some of its affiliated APs.
54
Similarly, a non-AP MLD might operate with a single affiliated non-AP STA, for example, after the associated AP MLD
55
has removed some of its affiliated APs. See 35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affiliated APs).
56
57
The MAC address of a non-AP EHT STA with dot11MultiLinkActivated set to false shall be set to the MLD
58
MAC address of the non-AP MLD that the non-AP EHT STA is affiliated with when the non-AP EHT STA
59
60 has dot11MultiLinkActivated set to true. See 4.5.3.2 (Mobility types).
61
62 NOTE 6 —A non-AP EHT STA might set dot11MultiLinkActivated to true or false.
63
64 NOTE 7—The (Re)Association Request frame sent by a non-AP EHT STA with dot11MultiLinkActivated set to false
65 does not include the Basic Multi-Link element (see Table 9-62 (Association Request frame body) and Table 9-64

## 第 480 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 (Reassociation Request frame body)). The (Re)Association Response frame sent in response to a (Re)Association
2 Request frame without including the Basic Multi-Link element does not include the Basic Multi-Link element (see
3 Table 9-63 (Association Response frame body) and Table 9-65 (Reassociation Response frame body)).
4
5 All APs affiliated with the same AP MLD are members of the same ESS and are connected to the same DS.
6
As a result, all APs affiliated with the same AP MLD shall advertise the same SSID.
7
8
9 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall not support TIM Broadcast and shall set to 0 the TIM
10 Broadcast field of the Extended Capabilities elements that it transmits.
11

35.3.2 Multi-link device addressing
13
14
15 An MLD uses an MLD MAC address that singly identifies the MLD.
16
17 STAs affiliated with an MLD shall use different MAC addresses.
18
19 NOTE 1—The MLD MAC address of an MLD might be the same as the MAC address of one affiliated STA or might be
20 different from the MAC address of any affiliated STA.
21
22 For an individually addressed frame sent on a link between two MLDs, the following applies:
23
— the value of the Address 2 (TA) field (if present) in the MAC header of the frame that is not a Probe
24
25 Response frame shall be the MAC address of the transmitting STA affiliated with the MLD
26 corresponding to that link except for the Individual/Group bit, which is set to 1 when the TA field
27 value is a bandwidth signaling TA and set to 0 otherwise.
28
29 — if the individually addressed frame is a Probe Response frame and the AP operating on the link is an
30 AP affiliated with the AP MLD
31
• and the AP does not belong to a multiple BSSID set or corresponds to the transmitted BSSID in
32
a multiple BSSID set, then the value of the Address 2 (TA) field in the MAC header of the frame
33
34 shall be set to the MAC address of the AP.
35 • and the AP corresponds to the nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, then the value of
36 the Address 2 (TA) field in the MAC header of the frame shall be set to the transmitted BSSID in
37
the multiple BSSID set (see 11.1.4.3.4 (Criteria for sending a response)).
38
39 — the value of the Address 1 (RA) field in the MAC header of the frame shall be the MAC address of
40 the receiving STA affiliated with the MLD corresponding to that link.
41
— if the individually addressed frame is a Management frame, the value of the Address 3 field in the
42
43 MAC header of the Management frame shall be set based on 9.3.3.1 (Format of (PV0) Management
44 frames).
45
— if the individually addressed frame is a Data frame, the value of the Address 3 field and the
46
47 Address 4 field (if present) in the MAC header of the Data frame shall be set based on Table 9-
48 58 (Address field contents for Data frames transmitted by nonmesh STAs) and the settings of the To
49 DS and From DS bits in the MAC header of the Data frame, where the BSSID is the MAC address of
50
the AP affiliated with the AP MLD corresponding to that link.
51
52 NOTE 2—For frames sent over a direct path in a single link TDLS direct link, by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-
53 AP MLD, the value of the Address 2 (TA) field is set to the MLD MAC address of the non-AP MLD as described in
54 35.3.21.2 (TDLS direct link over a single link).
55
56
For a frame sent by a STA affiliated with the MLD with Address 1 field set to a group address (if allowed as
57
described in 9.3.1 (Control frames), 9.3.2 (Data frames), and 9.3.3 ((PV0) Management frames)), the value
58
59 of the Address 2 field, the Address 3 field (if present), and the Address 4 field (if present) in the MAC
60 header of the frame shall be set as defined in 9.3.1 (Control frames), 9.3.2 (Data frames), and 9.3.3 ((PV0)
61 Management frames), where the BSSID is the following:
62
— if the STA is an AP, then the BSSID is the MAC address of the AP
63
64

7 35.3.3 Advertisement of multi-link information in Multi-Link element
8
9
35.3.3.1 General
10
11
12 The requirements for including a Basic Multi-Link element in a Beacon frame or in a Probe Response frame
13 are described in 35.3.4 (Discovery of an AP MLD). The requirements for including a Basic Multi-Link
14 element in a (Re)Association Response frame, in a (Re)Association Request frame, or in an Authentication
15
frame are described in 35.3.5 (Multi-link (re)setup).
16
17
18 The requirements for including a Reconfiguration Multi-Link element in a Beacon frame and Probe
19 Response frame are described in 35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affiliated APs).
20
21 NOTE 1—The Probe Response frame referred in the above paragraphs can be a multi-link probe response or not.
22
23 The requirements for including a Priority Access Multi-Link element in EPCS Priority Access Enable
24 Request/Response frames are described in 35.16 (EPCS priority access).
25
26
The requirements for including of a Probe Request Multi-Link element in a Probe Request frame are
27
28 described in 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe request and response).
29
30 The requirements for including a TDLS Multi-Link element in frames exchanged during TDLS discovery
31
and setup are described in 35.3.21 (TDLS procedure in multi-link operation).
32
33
34 The value carried in the Link ID subfield of the Per-STA Profile subelement carried in a Basic,
35 Reconfiguration or Priority Access Multi-Link element identifies a reported AP affiliated with that AP MLD
36 (see 35.3.3.2 (Link ID)). The value carried in the Link ID subfield of the Common Info field of the Basic
37
Multi-Link element identifies the link ID of the transmitting AP.
38
39
40 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD may include the Link Info field (see Figure 9-1002e (Multi-Link element
41 format)) in the Basic Multi-Link element that it transmits to carry complete or partial profile as defined in
42 35.3.3.3 (Advertisement of complete or partial per-link information) of another AP that is affiliated with the
43
same AP MLD as the transmitting AP. An AP corresponding to the transmitted BSSID may include Link
44
Info field in the Basic Multi-Link element that it transmits to carry complete or partial profile of another AP
45
46 that is affiliated with an AP MLD with which an AP corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID in the same
47 multiple BSSID is affiliated.
48
49 NOTE 2—In a Beacon frame or a Probe Response frame that is not a multi-link probe response transmitted by the
50 transmitted BSSID, the Basic Multi-Link element carrying information of an AP MLD with which an AP corresponding
51 to a nontransmitted BSSID is affiliated with is contained within the nontransmitted BSSID profile of the Multiple
52 BSSID element in a Beacon frame (see 35.3.20 (Multi-link operation in a multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set)).
53
54 NOTE 3—In a multi-link probe response sent by the transmitted BSSID in response to a multi-link probe request
55 directed to an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID, the Basic Multi-Link element carrying information of an
56 AP MLD with which an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID is affiliated with is contained outside the Multiple
57 BSSID element (see 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe request and response)).
58
59 NOTE 4—Also see 35.3.6 (Multi-Link reconfiguration) for inclusion of Reconfiguration Multi-Link element within a
60 nontransmitted BSSID profile of the Multiple BSSID element carried in a Beacon frame or Probe Response frame
61 transmitted by the transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set.
62
63
NOTE 5—A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD does not include Link Info field in a Basic Multi-Link element
64
when carried in the Authentication frame and includes Link Info field containing complete profile of the non-AP STA
65

## 第 482 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 that is operating on a requested link in a (Re)Association Request frame that it transmits during multi-link (re)setup.
2 Also see 35.3.5 (Multi-link (re)setup).
3
4 The Basic Multi-Link element when carried in the Neighbor Report element shall not include a Link Info
5
field, except as described in 35.3.23 (BSS transition management for MLDs).
6
7
8 A STA affiliated with an MLD that receives a frame carrying a Multi-Link element shall determine the
9 presence of subfield(s) carried in the Common Info field based on the subfields of the Presence Bitmap field.
10
11 NOTE 6—A receiving STA determines the end of the Common Info field based on the Common Info Length subfield
12 and skips information that it cannot decode.
13
14 A STA affiliated with an MLD that receives a frame carrying a Multi-Link element that carries a Per-STA
15 Profile subelement shall determine the presence of subfield(s) carried in the STA Info field based on the
16
subfields of the STA Control field.
17
18 NOTE 7—A receiving STA determines the end of the STA Info field based on the STA Info Length subfield of the STA
19 Info field in the Per-STA Profile subelement and skips the information it cannot decode.
20
21
35.3.3.2 Link ID
22
23
24 A link ID is a numeric value that corresponds to a tuple consisting of Operating Class, Operating Channel,
25 and BSSID of the AP affiliated with the AP MLD. Each AP affiliated with an AP MLD has a link ID that is
26 smaller than 15. An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not have the same link ID as another AP affiliated
27
with the same AP MLD. The link ID shall not change for the lifetime of the BSS that is set up on the link
28
associated with the link ID nor for the lifetime of any BSS of the other AP(s) affiliated with the same AP
29
30 MLD.
31
NOTE 1—The Link ID subfield in Reduced Neighbor Report element is set to 15 to indicate an AP that is not affiliated
32
with an AP MLD or if the reporting AP does not have information of the reported AP (see 9.4.2.170.2 (Neighbor AP
33
Information field)). Therefore, an AP MLD does not assign link ID value 15 to any of its affiliated AP.
34
35
36 NOTE 2—Since a link ID identifies an affiliated AP’s BSS and does not change throughout the lifetime of the BSS, the
37 link ID remains unchanged if the AP moves its BSS to a different channel by performing channel switch procedure
38 described in 11.8.8 (Selecting and advertising a new channel) or 11.9 (Extended channel switching (ECS)). In addition,
39 the link ID for an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is the same across all non-AP MLDs.
40
41 NOTE 3—A non-AP MLD includes the link ID obtained during discovery for identifying an affiliated AP to be
42 requested as part of the multi-link setup with the AP MLD.
43
44 35.3.3.3 Advertisement of complete or partial per-link information
45
46
If a reporting STA that is affiliated with an MLD transmits a frame that carries a Basic Multi-Link element,
47
48 which includes a Per-STA Profile subelement that carries the complete profile for a reported STA, then the
49 reporting STA shall set the Complete Profile subfield of the STA Control field in that Per-STA Profile
50 subelement to 1. Otherwise, the reporting STA shall set the Complete Profile subfield of the STA Control
51
field in the Per-STA Profile subelement to 0 and the profile of the reported STA is defined as partial profile.
52
53
54 The complete profile of a reported STA consists of all the elements and fields (subject to inheritance rules
55 defined in 35.3.3.6.1 (Inheritance in the per-STA profile of Basic Multi-Link element) and exceptions
56 specified in 35.3.3.4 (Fields and elements not carried in a per-STA profile) that would be included in a
57
Management frame, that is of the same subtype as that transmitted by the reporting STA carrying the Basic
58
Multi-Link element, if the reported STA were to transmit the frame.
59
60
NOTE 1—Only Management frames belonging to subtypes (Re)Association Request or (Re)Association Response can
61
include the complete profile of a reported STA (see 35.3.5.4 (Usage and rules of Basic Multi-Link element in the context
62
of multi-link (re)setup, authentication, and FT action frame exchange between two MLDs)), with the exception that a
63
multi-link probe response can also include the complete profile of a reported AP (see 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe
64
request and response)).
65

28 Figure 35-3—Example of Basic Multi-Link element in an Association Request frame

30
31
32 In Figure 35-3 (Example of Basic Multi-Link element in an Association Request frame), a non-AP STA
33 affiliated with a non-AP MLD transmits an Association Request frame which includes a Basic Multi-Link
34
element that carries the complete profile of two other non-AP STAs affiliated with its non-AP MLD (non-
35
AP STA x and non-AP STA y). The figure expands the Per-STA profile for non- AP STA x. The Type
36
37 subfield of the Multi-Link Control field is set to 0 to indicate that the Multi-Link element is a Basic Multi-
38 Link element. The Common Info field carries information that applies to the MLD level as described in
39 9.4.2.312.2 (Basic Multi-Link element). In this example, only the Common Info Length and MLD MAC
40
Address fields are shown. However, there can be other fields present in the Common Info field whose
41
presence is signaled via the subfields carried within the Presence Bitmap subfield of the Multi-Link Control
42
43 field. Each Per-STA Profile subelement in the Link Info field carries the complete profile, with inheritance
44 applied, of a reported non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD. Each Per-STA Profile subelement
45 carries the STA Control field followed by the STA Info field and the STA Profile field. In this example, only
46
the STA Info Length and STA MAC Address fields are shown. However, there can be other subfields
47
present in the STA Info field whose presence is signaled via the subfields in the STA Control field. The STA
48
49 Profile field carries variable number of fields and elements in the order defined in Table 9-62 (Association
50 Request frame body) with inheritance applied (see 35.3.3.6 (Inheritance in a per-STA profile)). The Non-
51 inheritance element (if present) lists the elements that are not inherited by the reported STA.
52
53 NOTE 4—Listen interval is not included within a STA Profile field (see 35.3.3.4 (Fields and elements not carried in a
54 per-STA profile)). Therefore, Capability Information field is the only field carried in the STA Profile field and is
55 followed by elements applicable to the reported STA.

57 35.3.3.4 Fields and elements not carried in a per-STA profile
58
59
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not include a Timestamp field, a Beacon Interval field, an AID field,
60
61 a BSS Max Idle Period element, a Neighbor Report element, a Reduced Neighbor Report element, a
62 Multiple BSSID element, TIM element, Multiple BSSID-Index element, Multiple BSSID Configuration
63 element or another Multi-Link element in the Per-STA Profile subelement of the Basic Multi-Link element.

1 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not include SSID element in the Per-STA Profile subelement of the
2 Basic Multi-Link element for a reported AP unless both of the following conditions are satisfied for the
3
element:
4
5 — The element carries complete profile of the reported AP
6
— The element is contained in a multi-link probe response transmitted by the transmitted BSSID in a
7
multiple BSSID set in response to a multi-link probe request directed to the nontransmitted BSSID in
8
9 the same multiple BSSID set.
10
NOTE 1—For an NSTR mobile AP MLD, only the AP on the primary link transmits a Beacon frame. In addition, the
11
TSF timer of the nonprimary link is the same as that of the primary link (see 35.3.19 (NSTR mobile AP MLD
12
operation)). For an AP MLD that is not an NSTR mobile AP MLD, the Timestamp field is specific to each link and the
13
value for each can be obtained on the respective link (i.e., Beacon frame includes Timestamp field and TIM element and
14
Probe Response frame includes Timestamp field). The content of the TIM element for a non-AP MLD are consistent
15
across all links. Beacon Interval field is an explicit subfield in STA Info field for the reported AP. AID field and BSS
16
Max Idle Period element apply at the MLD level and have the same value for all links.
17
18
19 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall not include a Listen Interval field, a Current AP Address
20 field, an SSID element or another Multi-Link element in the Per-STA Profile subelement of the Basic Multi-
21 Link element.
22
23 NOTE 2—Listen Interval field and Current AP Address field apply at the MLD level and have the same value for all
24 links.
25
26 NOTE 3—All APs affiliated with the same AP MLD advertise the same SSID (see 35.3.1 (General)) and therefore, the
27 same (SSID) value applies to a reported (AP or non-AP) STA.
28
29
A STA affiliated with an MLD shall not include FTE and MDE for each reported STA in the reported STA’s
30
Per-STA Profile subelement of the Basic Multi-Link element carried in a (Re)Association Request frame or
31
32 a (Re)Association Response frame that it transmits. Also see 13.4.2 (FT initial mobility domain association
33 in an RSN) and 13.7 (FT reassociation).
34
35 NOTE 4—There is no RSNE/RSNXE included in the Basic Multi-Link element carried in a (Re)Association Request
36 frame because there is only one RSNE/RSNXE provided by non-AP MLD during multi-link (re)setup. See 12.6.3.1
37 (General). An AP MLD can have different MFPR carried in RSNE for each of its affiliated APs and in such case, the
38 (Re)Association Response frame includes RSNE in the corresponding Per-STA Profile subelement of Basic Multi-Link
39 element. See 12.6.2 (RSNA selection).
40
41 35.3.3.5 Processing of Per-STA Profile subelement of Multi-Link element
42
43
A non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1) affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall follow the procedures (if any) that are
44
applicable to a field carried (directly or within an element) in a Management frame received on another link,
45
46 from an AP (AP 2), as if it (non-AP STA 1) had received that field in the corresponding frame transmitted by
47 a reported AP (AP 1) operating on the same link as the non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1), if all of the following
48 conditions are satisfied:
49
— The transmitting AP (AP 2) is affiliated with the same AP MLD as the reported AP (AP 1).
50
51 — The field is carried within the STA Info field or STA Profile field of a Per-STA Profile subelement
52
of a Multi-Link element, corresponding to the reported AP (AP 1).
53
54 — The corresponding frame is received by another non-AP STA (non-AP STA 2) that is affiliated with
55 the same non-AP MLD as the non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1).
56
— One of the conditions is true:
57
58 • The Management frame is a Beacon frame, a Probe Response frame or a (Re)Association
59 Response frame and the transmitting AP (AP 2) does not belong to a multiple BSSID set or is the
60
transmitted BSSID in the multiple BSSID set
61
• The Management frame is a (Re)Association Response frame, and the transmitting AP (AP 2)
62
63 corresponds to a nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set.
64

1 NOTE—In a multiple BSSID set, an AP corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID responds to a (Re)Association
2 Request frame by transmitting a (Re)Association Response frame that does not include the Multiple BSSID element.
3
4 A non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1) affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall follow the procedures (if any) that are
5
applicable to a field carried (directly or within an element) in a Beacon frame or a Probe Response frame
6
received on another link, from an AP (AP 3), as if it had received that field in the corresponding frame
7
8 transmitted by a reported AP (AP 1) that is operating on the same link as the non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1),
9 if all of the following conditions are satisfied:
10
— The transmitting AP (AP 3) is the transmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set as an AP
11
(AP 2) that corresponds to a nontransmitted BSSID and the AP (AP 2) is affiliated with the same AP
12
13 MLD as the reported AP (AP 1).
14
— The corresponding frame is received by another non-AP STA (non-AP STA 2) affiliated with the
15
same non-AP MLD as the non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1).
16
17 — The field is carried within the STA Info field or STA Profile field of a Per-STA Profile subelement

19

An AP (AP 1) affiliated with an AP MLD shall follow the procedures (if any) that are applicable to a field
21
22 carried (directly or within an element) in a (Re)Association Request frame received on another link, from a
23 non-AP STA (non-AP STA 2), as if it (AP 1) had received that field in the corresponding frame transmitted
24 by a reported non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1) operating on the same link as the AP (AP 1), if all of the
25
following conditions are satisfied:
26
27 — The transmitting non-AP STA (non-AP STA 2) is affiliated with the same non-AP MLD as the
28 reported non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1).
29
— The corresponding frame is received by another AP (AP 2) affiliated with the same AP MLD as the
30

33
of a Multi-Link element, corresponding to the reported non-AP STA (non-AP STA 1).
34
35
36 35.3.3.6 Inheritance in a per-STA profile
37
38
35.3.3.6.1 Inheritance in the per-STA profile of Basic Multi-Link element
39
40
41 It is possible for STAs affiliated with an MLD to have similar capabilities and operational parameters on
42 different links. As a result, an element that is applicable to a reported STA might have the same value as the
43 corresponding element applicable to the reporting STA and carried in the frame outside the Basic Multi-Link
44
element. To reduce the frame size, when a Per-STA Profile subelement carries complete profile for a
45
reported STA, it inherits the elements from the reporting STA based on the rules defined in this subclause.
46
47
48 The inheritance mechanism described in this subclause shall apply only when the Per-STA Profile
49 subelement of the Basic Multi-Link element carries complete profile of the reported STA (i.e., the Complete
50
Profile subfield in the STA Control field of the subelement is set to 1). When a Per-STA Profile subelement
51
of the Basic Multi-Link element carries partial profile for a reported STA, the transmitting STA shall not
52
53 apply the inheritance mechanism described in this subclause.
54
55 A STA that transmits a Management frame carrying the Basic Multi-Link element shall include an element
56
that is specific to the reported STA in the complete profile of the reported STA carried in the Basic Multi-
57
Link element. An element, identified by an Element ID and Element ID Extension (if applicable), is
58
59 considered specific to a reported STA if any of the following conditions are satisfied:
60 — at least one element with the same Element ID and Extended Element ID (if applicable) is present in
61
the frame that carried the Basic Multi-Link element but the contents of the Information field is not
62
the same for the reported STA if the reported STA were to transmit the same Management frame
63
64 subtype.
65
486 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 487 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — the reported STA satisfies the condition for that element to be included in the same Management
2 subtype as the frame that carries the Basic Multi-Link element while the reporting STA does not
3
satisfy the corresponding condition.
4
5 NOTE 1—For example, when there exists one or more Vendor Specific elements carried in a Management frame that
6 includes the Basic Multi-Link element containing a per-STA profile for a reported STA, and the contents of the
7 Information field for at least one of the Vendor Specific elements are not the same as that of at least one Vendor Specific
8 element that applies to the reported STA, then each Vendor Specific element that applies to the reported STA is included
9 in its Per-STA Profile subelement.
10
11
NOTE 2—The conditions to include an element in a particular Management frame are as specified in 9.3.3 ((PV0)
12
Management frames). For example, Table 9-63 (Association Response frame body) specifies the conditions for an
13
element to be included in an Association Response frame.
14
15
If an element, identified by an Element ID and Element ID Extension (if applicable), is carried in a
16
17 Management frame transmitted by a reporting STA and the element is outside the Basic Multi-Link element,
18 and there is no element having the same Element ID and Element ID Extension (if applicable) in a complete
19 profile of a reported STA carried in the Basic Multi-Link element, then the element is considered to be part
20
of the reported STA’s profile and the value to use is the same as that of the corresponding element carried in
21
the reporting STA’s frame unless any of the following conditions are true, in which case the element is not
22
23 considered to be part of the reported STA’s profile:
24 — the complete profile carries the Non-Inheritance element (see 9.4.2.240 (Non-Inheritance element))
25
and the element is listed in the Non-Inheritance element.
26
27 — the element is excluded from being included in the Per-STA Profile subelement as described in
28 35.3.3.4 (Fields and elements not carried in a per-STA profile).
29
30
If an element, identified by an Element ID and Element ID Extension (if applicable), is carried in a
31
32 Management frame transmitted by the reporting STA and the element is outside the Basic Multi-Link
33 element, and there is an element having the same Element ID and Element ID Extension (if applicable) in a
34 complete profile of a reported STA carried in the Basic Multi-Link element in that frame (i.e., the element is
35
specific to the reported STA), then the STA receiving the Management frame shall consider the element
36
received in the Management frame outside of the Basic Multi-Link element to not be part of the reported
37
38 STA’s profile.
39
40 When an AP corresponding to a transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set transmits a multi-link probe
41
response in response to a multi-link probe request directed to an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted
42
BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set (see 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe request and response)) and the
43
44 Basic Multi-Link element corresponding to the AP MLD of the nontransmitted BSSID carries complete per-
45 STA profile of the requested AP(s), then the inheritance (or non-inheritance) for each per-STA profile is with
46 respect to the elements carried in the Probe Response frame that are outside the Multiple BSSID element.
47
48 NOTE 4—The Multiple BSSID element and Basic Multi-Link element are not inherited by the profile for a reported AP.
49
50 A Fragment element (see 9.4.2.188 (Fragment element)) is considered under the same context as the element
51
that is being fragmented. Therefore, when an element that is fragmentable (see Table 9-128 (Element IDs)
52
and 10.28.11 (Element fragmentation)) is inherited (or not inherited), the Fragment element(s) (if present)
53
54 corresponding to that element shall also be inherited (or not inherited).
55
56 Figure 35-4 (Example of inheritance in a complete per-STA profile) illustrates inheritance when a per-STA
57
profile carries complete profile. The example shows a Management frame transmitted by a reporting STA
58
that is affiliated with an MLD. The Management frame carries several elements with their corresponding
59
60 element IDs shown in parentheses. The frame also carries a Basic Multi-Link element which is carrying two
61 Per-STA Profile subelements corresponding to STA 1 and STA 2. In this example, the profile for STA 1,
62 which is a complete profile is expanded to show the details of inheritance. The contents of the profile for
63
STA 2 are not shown in this illustration. The per-STA profile for STA 1 includes element with ID B since the
64
element has a value different from the corresponding elements carried in the frame. The profile also includes

34 35.3.3.6.2 Inheritance in the per-STA profile of Probe Request Multi-Link element
35
36
When a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD requests a partial profile for another AP (AP 1)
37
affiliated with the same AP MLD as an AP (AP 2) receiving the multi-link probe request (see 35.3.4.2 (Use
38
39 of multi-link probe request and response)) and the same partial profile for the AP (AP2), the non-AP STA
40 may include the (Extended) Request element only in the Probe Request frame body, and this element will be
41 inherited for the other requested AP (AP 1) even if it is not carried in the Per-STA Profile subelement
42
corresponding to the other requested AP (AP 1), following the rules defined in 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link
43
probe request and response).
44
45
46 Figure 35-5 (Example of inheritance in a Request element for multi-link probe request) illustrates a multi-
47 link probe request transmitted by a non-AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD. The non-AP STA
48
requests partial profile for three APs and complete profile for one AP, where all APs are affiliated with the
49
same AP MLD. The non-AP STA includes a Request element in the Probe Request frame body requesting
50
51 the element with element ID “a” for the AP to which the Probe Request frame is sent. The frame carries a
52 Probe Request Multi-Link element that includes three Per-STA Profile subelements requesting information
53 for AP x, AP y, AP z.
54
55
For AP x, the non-AP STA requests the element with element ID “a”, which is the same as the element
56
57 requested for the AP receiving the Multi-Link Probe Request frame. Hence, the Complete Profile Requested
58 subfield for the per-STA profile x is set to 0 and the per-STA profile does not include the Request element in
59 the STA Profile field by inheritance rule. For AP y, the non-AP STA requests the element with element ID
60 “b”, which is different from the requested element for the AP (i.e., element ID “a”) receiving the Multi-Link
61
Probe Request frame. Hence, the Complete Profile Requested subfield for the per-STA profile y is set to 0
62
and the per-STA profile includes the Request element in the STA Profile field that indicates element ID “b”.
63

35.3.3.7 Subelement fragmentation in the Link Info field of a Multi-Link element
32
33
34 This subclause describes the procedure for splitting the contents of a subelement that is carried within a Link
35 Info field of a Multi-Link element, across multiple subelements, when the length of the content of the
36
subelement exceeds 255 octets. The procedure is described with respect to the Per-STA Profile subelement
37
of the Basic Multi-Link element. However, the same procedure applies to any subelement except the
38
39 Fragment subelement (see Table 9-401c (Optional subelement IDs for Link Info field of the Multi-Link
40 element)) and to any variant of Multi-Link element.
41
42 NOTE 1—When the length of the subelement is less than or equal 255 octets but exceeds the remaining size of the
43 Multi-Link element, the subelement is not fragmented. Instead, the Multi-Link element is fragmented by following the
44 procedure described in 10.28.11 (Element fragmentation), and the subsequent Fragment element (see
45 9.4.2.188 (Fragment element)) carries the portion of the subelement that exceeded the remaining size of the element.
46
47 If the length of a Per-STA Profile subelement for a reported STA exceeds 255 octets, the transmitting STA
48 shall fragment the contents across a series of subelements consisting of the Per-STA Profile subelement
49 (Subelement ID set to 0 as shown in Table 9-401c (Optional subelement IDs for Link Info field of the Multi-
50
Link element)), immediately followed by one or more Fragment subelements (Subelement ID set to 254 as
51
shown in Table 9-401c (Optional subelement IDs for Link Info field of the Multi-Link element)) as
52
53 illustrated in Figure 35-6 (Per-STA Profile subelement fragmentation). All the information for a fragmented
54 subelement shall be carried across the same Basic Multi-Link element and its Fragment element(s). A Per-
55 STA profile subelement shall not be fragmented if the length of the Data field of the subelement is less than
56
255 octets. A Fragment subelement shall not be the first subelement or the only subelement within a Link
57
Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element.
58
59
NOTE 2—When the length of the Per-STA Profile subelement is greater than 255 octets, the length of Basic Multi-Link
60
element that carries the subelement would exceed 255 octets. As a result, the element is fragmented by following the
61
62
63
64
65

37 SubEID:(cid:3)Subelement(cid:3)ID(cid:3)of(cid:3)Per(cid:882)STA(cid:3)Profile(cid:3)subelement(cid:3)(=0) FragID:(cid:3)Subelement(cid:3)ID(cid:3)of(cid:3)the(cid:3)Fragment(cid:3)subelement(cid:3)(=254) L:(cid:3)Total(cid:3)size(cid:3)(octets)(cid:3)of(cid:3)fragmented(cid:3)subelement

40 Figure 35-7—Per-STA Profile subelement fragmentation within a fragmented Multi-Link ele-
41
ment
42
43
44 The information to be fragmented is divided into P + Q portions, where:
45
— P is L(cid:101)255 .
46
47 — Q is equal to 1 if (L mod 255) > 0 and equal to 0 otherwise.
48
— L is the size of the information in octets.
49
50
51 The Per-STA Profile subelement into which the information does not fit is filled with the first segment of
52 information. This subelement is immediately followed by P – 1 Fragment subelements, each containing the
53 subsequent segments of 255 octets of information. If Q = 1, these subelements are immediately followed by
54
another Fragment subelement containing the remaining segment of information. The length of this last
55
Fragment subelement shall be (L mod 255).
56
57
NOTE 3—A Fragment subelement never follows a subelement with fewer than 255 octets of information.
58
59
A Per-STA Profile subelement that has its information fragmented shall be followed by one or more
60
61 Fragment subelements. To reconstruct the original information, the portion of information from the Per-STA
62 Profile subelement shall be concatenated, in order, with the portions of information from the series of
63 Fragment subelements that follow it. The defragmentation procedure shall complete when any subelement
64

1 other than a Fragment subelement is encountered or the end of the last Fragment element of the Basic Multi-
2 Link element is reached.
3
4 NOTE 4—The receiving STA follows the procedure defined in 10.28.12 (Element defragmentation) to defragment the
5 Basic Multi-Link element.

7 35.3.4 Discovery of an AP MLD
8
9
35.3.4.1 AP behavior
10
11
12 If an AP is affiliated with an AP MLD and does not correspond to a nontransmitted BSSID, then the Beacon
13 and Probe Response frames transmitted by the AP shall include a TBTT Information field in a Reduced
14
Neighbor Report element with the TBTT Information Length field set to 16 or higher (see 9.4.2.170.2
15
(Neighbor AP Information field)), for each of the other APs (if any) affiliated with the same AP MLD. A
16
17 FILS Discovery frame transmitted by the AP may include a Reduced Neighbor Report element with the
18 same information.
19
20
If an AP (AP 1) is affiliated with an AP MLD (AP MLD 1) and corresponds to a nontransmitted BSSID,
21
then the Beacon and Probe Response frames transmitted by the AP (AP 2) corresponding to the transmitted
22
23 BSSID of the same multiple BSSID set as the AP (AP 1) shall include a TBTT Information field in a
24 Reduced Neighbor Report element with the TBTT Information Length field set to 16 or higher (see 9.4.2.170.2
25 (Neighbor AP Information field)), for each of the other APs (if any) affiliated with the same AP MLD (AP
26
MLD 1). A FILS Discovery frame transmitted by the AP (AP 2) may include a Reduced Neighbor Report
27
element with the same information.
28
29
NOTE 1—We use the terms AP 1 and AP 2 and AP MLD 1 and AP MLD 2 in this paragraph with the sole purpose to
30
differentiate the APs and the AP MLDs and ease the understanding of the sentences.
31
32
If all the following conditions are true:
33
34 — a reporting AP is affiliated with an AP MLD (AP MLD 1) and is in the same co-located AP set as
35 APs affiliated with another AP MLD (AP MLD 2)
36
37 — the other AP MLD (AP MLD 2) has no affiliated APs operating on the same channel as the reporting
38 AP
39
— an AP affiliated with the other AP MLD (AP MLD 2) is in the same multiple BSSID set as an AP
40
41
affiliated with the AP MLD (AP MLD 1) of the reporting AP
42 — the APs affiliated with the other AP MLD (AP MLD 2) are not already reported in Beacon frames
43
and broadcast Probe Response frames transmitted by an AP in the same cohosted AP set as the
44
reporting AP and operating on the same channel as the reporting AP.
45
46
47 then each AP of the other AP MLD (AP MLD 2) shall be reported in a TBTT Information field with the
48 TBTT Information Length field set to 16 or higher (see 9.4.2.170.2 (Neighbor AP Information field)) in the
49
Reduced Neighbor Report element that is included in the Beacon frames and broadcast Probe Response
50
frames transmitted by the reporting AP and may be reported in a FILS Discovery frame that includes a
51
52 Reduced Neighbor Report element transmitted by the reporting AP.
53
54 If a reporting AP reports an AP affiliated with an MLD in a Reduced Neighbor Report element with the
55
MLD Parameters subfield present in the TBTT Information field for that AP, then the reporting AP shall set
56
the AP MLD ID, the Link ID, and the BSS Parameters Change Count subfields as described in 9.4.2.170.2
57
58 (Neighbor AP Information field). The AP MLD ID of an AP MLD shall not be set to 255 and an AP
59 affiliated with an AP MLD shall not have a BSSID index set to 255.
60
61 NOTE 2—The AP MLD ID subfield in the Reduced Neighbor Report element is used to determine the AP MLD with
62 which the reported AP is affiliated, especially when multiple AP MLDs are reported in the same frame.
63
64
65

## 第 492 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 The TBTT offset between two APs affiliated with the same AP MLD shall never be larger than 254 TUs. An
2 AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not have the Neighbor AP TBTT Offset subfield set to 255 for an AP
3
affiliated with the same AP MLD, except under the rules defined in 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures for
4
channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting) and except if the link on which the
5
6 reported AP is operating is disabled as advertised according to the procedures in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-
7 to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames).
8
9
35.3.4.2 Use of multi-link probe request and response
10
11
12 A multi-link probe request allows a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD to request an AP affiliated
13 with an AP MLD to include the complete or partial set of capabilities, parameters and operation elements of
14 the AP(s) affiliated with the targeted AP MLD in the response frame. The complete profile and partial
15
profile of a requested AP are defined in 35.3.3.3 (Advertisement of complete or partial per-link
16
information).
17
18
NOTE 1—If an AP MLD has only one affiliated AP, a multi-link probe response will not provide additional information
19
compared to a Probe Response frame that is not a multi-link probe response.
20
21
An MLD SME may generate a multi-link probe request by calling MLME-SCAN.request with the ScanType
22
23 set to MULTI_LINK PROBE. A multi-link probe request is a Probe Request frame that is sent as a non-
24 scanning probe request transmission (see 11.1.4.3.8 (Non-scanning probe request transmission)). The Probe
25 Request frame shall be formatted as follows:
26
— either with the Address 1 field set to the broadcast address and the Address 3 field set to the BSSID
27
28 of an AP, or with the Address 1 field set to the BSSID of an AP’s BSS.
29
— with the AP MLD ID subfield (if present in the Probe Request Multi-Link element) set to the AP
30
MLD ID that identifies the targeted AP MLD with which the requested AP(s) are affiliated.
31
32 — including a Probe Request Multi-Link element defined in 9.4.2.312.3 (Probe Request Multi-Link
33 element).
34
35 — If a non-AP MLD is sending a multi-link probe request, it shall follow the rules defined in 9.3.3.9
36 (Probe Request frame format) regarding the inclusion of the SSID element, the SSID List element,
37 the rules defined in 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe request and response) regarding the inclusion
38
of the Request element, the Extended Request element, and the Probe Request Multi-Link element,
39
and shall follow the rules for sending a Probe Request frame outside the context of active scanning as
40
41 defined in 35.3.4.5 (Probe Request frame content for a non-AP EHT STA) regarding the inclusion of
42 the other elements.
43
44
If either the Address 1 field or the Address 3 field of the multi-link probe request is set to the MAC address
45
of the AP affiliated with an AP MLD that corresponds to the nontransmitted BSSID, then the AP MLD ID
46
47 subfield shall not be present in the Probe Request Multi-Link element of the multi-link probe request and the
48 AP MLD is the targeted AP MLD.
49
50
If either the Address 1 field or the Address 3 field of the multi-link probe request is set to the MAC address
51
of the responding AP that operates on the same link where the multi-link probe request is sent, then the AP
52
53 MLD ID subfield shall be present in the Probe Request Multi-Link element of the multi-link probe request
54 and the targeted AP MLD is identified by the AP MLD ID subfield, which is set to the same AP MLD ID
55 value as the one used by the AP that is addressed by the multi-link probe request to identify the AP MLD in
56
the Beacon and Probe Response frames that it transmits.
57
58
59 If the Probe Request Multi-Link element in the multi-link probe request does not include any per-STA
60 profile, then all APs affiliated with the same AP MLD as the AP identified in the Address 1 or Address 3
61 field or AP MLD ID of the multi-link probe request shall be requested APs.
62
63
If the Probe Request Multi-Link element in the multi-link probe request includes one or more per-STA
64
65 profiles, then only APs affiliated with the same AP MLD as the AP identified in the Address 1 or Address 3
492 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 493 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 field or in the AP MLD ID subfield (if present) of the multi-link probe request and whose link ID is equal to
2 the value in the Link ID field in a per-STA profile in the Probe Request Multi-Link element in the multi-link
3
probe request shall be requested APs.
4
5
6 The partial profile of a requested AP sent by a reporting AP consists of one or more elements that are
7 requested in the (Extended) Request element carried in the multi-link probe request.
8
9
A multi-link probe request allows a non-AP STA to request an AP to include the partial profile for a
10
requested AP affiliated with the targeted AP MLD if the Probe Request Multi-Link element carries a Per-
11
12 STA Profile subelement for the requested AP to retrieve partial profile. To do so, the STA shall include the
13 (Extended) Request element in the frame body of the multi-link probe request and/or in a Per-STA Profile
14 subelement in a Probe Request Multi-Link element carried in the multi-link probe request, and:
15
— the Complete Profile Requested subfield of the STA Control field in the Per-STA Profile subelement
16
17 shall be set to 0.
18
— the (Extended) Request element, if carried in the per-STA profile corresponding to the requested AP,
19
specifies the partial profile.
20
21 — the (Extended) Request element, if not carried in the per-STA profile corresponding to the requested
22 AP that requests the same partial profile as the AP addressed by the multi-link probe request can be
23
inherited from the (Extended) Request element in the frame body, subject to the rules defined in
24
35.3.3.6.2 (Inheritance in the per-STA profile of Probe Request Multi-Link element).
25
26
27 A multi-link probe request allows a non-AP STA to request an AP to include the complete profile of all
28
requested APs affiliated with the targeted AP MLD if the Probe Request frame does not include the
29
30 (Extended) Request element in the frame body and the Probe Request Multi-Link element in the Probe
31 Request frame does not include any per-STA profile.
32
33
A multi-link probe request allows a non-AP STA to request an AP to include the same requested partial
34
profile for all requested APs affiliated with the targeted AP MLD if the Probe Request frame includes the
35
36 (Extended) Request element in frame body and the Probe Request Multi-Link element in the Probe Request
37 frame does not include any per-STA profile.
38
39
A multi-link probe response is a Probe Response frame:
40
41 — that is transmitted in response to a received multi-link probe request
42
— and that includes Basic Multi-Link element which can carry complete or partial profile(s), based on
43
the soliciting request, for each of the requested AP(s) affiliated with the targeted AP MLD.
44
45
46 If an AP that is affiliated with an AP MLD receives a multi-link probe request from a non-AP STA affiliated
47 with a non-AP MLD requesting complete profile for a requested AP, possibly among other requests for other
48
requested APs, it shall respond with a multi-link probe response that includes a Basic Multi-Link element
49
with a per-STA profile with complete profile for the requested AP subject to the rules defined in 11.1.4.3.4
50
51 (Criteria for sending a response). If it receives a multi-link probe request from a non-AP STA affiliated with
52 a non-AP MLD requesting partial profile for a requested AP, possibly among other requests for other
53 requested APs, it shall respond with a multi-link probe response that includes a Basic Multi-Link element
54
with a per-STA profile with at least the elements requested from the (Extended) Request element for the
55
requested AP, unless the elements requested are not part of the complete profile for the requested AP and
56
57 subject to the rules defined in 11.1.4.3.4 (Criteria for sending a response).
58
59 If an AP that is affiliated with an AP MLD receives a multi-link probe request requesting complete profile
60
and responds with a multi-link probe response (per 11.1.4.3.4 (Criteria for sending a response)), the
61
Address 1 field of the Probe Response frame may be set to the broadcast address.
62
63
NOTE 2—An AP operating in the 6 GHz band is allowed to set the Address 1 field of the Probe Response frame to
64
broadcast address as defined in 26.17.2.3.2 (AP behavior for fast passive scanning).
65

## 第 494 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An AP corresponding to the transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set shall transmit a multi-link probe
2 response in response to a multi-link probe request that is soliciting information of an MLD with which an
3
AP corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set is affiliated. Such a multi-
4
link probe response shall carry a Basic Multi-Link element containing information of the solicited AP MLD
5
6 and one or more APs affiliated with it. The Basic Multi-Link element shall be carried in the frame body of
7 the multi-link probe response, whose location is outside of the Multiple BSSID element carried in the frame
8 and the AP MLD ID Present subfield of the Presence Bitmap subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element shall
9
be set to 1. The AP MLD ID subfield of the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element shall be set
10
to the same value as the BSSID Index subfield of the Multiple-BSSID Index element carried in the
11
12 Nontransmitted BSSID Profile subelement of the Multiple BSSID element.
13
NOTE 3—A multi-link probe request can only solicit information of only one AP MLD and one or more APs affiliated
14
with that MLD.
15
16
17 NOTE 4—A multi-link probe response carries complete information for only one AP MLD and one or more APs
18 affiliated with that MLD.
19
20 NOTE 5—A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that receives such a multi-link probe response identifies that
21 the Basic Multi-Link element in the frame, outside the Multiple BSSID element, corresponds to an AP MLD with which
22 the AP corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID is affiliated with based on the presence of the AP MLD ID subfield.
23
24 35.3.4.3 Non-AP MLD behavior
25
26
A non-AP MLD discovers an AP MLD and its affiliated APs when a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP
27
28 MLD receives using one or more of the following:
29 — a Basic Multi-Link element carried in a Beacon frame or Probe Response frame, that is not a multi-
30
link probe response, transmitted by an AP affiliated with the AP MLD or by the AP corresponding to
31
the transmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set as at least one of the APs affiliated with the
32
33 AP MLD.
34
— a multi-link probe response from an AP affiliated with the AP MLD or the AP corresponding to the
35
transmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set as at least one of the APs affiliated with the AP
36
37 MLD carrying a Basic Multi-Link element with a complete profile of the reported AP.
38 — either a Beacon, Probe Response or FILS Discovery frame transmitted by an AP (reporting AP) and
39
the frame carries a Reduced Neighbor Report element that includes the MLD Parameters subfield in
40
the TBTT Information field corresponding to the reported AP. A non-AP MLD infers the
41
42 relationship between the reported AP and the reporting AP by decoding the AP MLD ID subfield of
43 the MLD Parameters subfield in the Reduced Neighbor Report element and following the rules
44 described in 35.3.4.1 (AP behavior).
45
46 — a Management frame and the frame carries a Neighbor Report element. A non-AP MLD determines
47 that two or more APs reported in different Neighbor Report elements that include the Basic Multi-
48 Link subelement are affiliated with the same AP MLD. The reported APs are affiliated with the same
49
AP MLD if the values carried in MLD MAC Address field of the Common Info field of the Basic
50
Multi-Link element of the reported APs are the same.
51
52
53 A non-AP MLD can use the information it gathers from a Reduced Neighbor Report element and a Basic
54 Multi-Link element to decide whether to perform multi-link setup with an AP MLD.
55
56
A non-AP MLD can use the information it receives from a Neighbor Report element to make a decision on
57
58 performing multi-link (re)setup (see 35.3.5 (Multi-link (re)setup)) or BSS transition (see 4.5.3.2 (Mobility
59 types) and 35.3.23 (BSS transition management for MLDs)).
60

62
63
64

1 35.3.4.4 Multi-Link element usage rules in the context of discovery
2
3
If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is not in a multiple BSSID set or the AP corresponds to a transmitted
4
BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, hen the AP, in a Beacon frame and a Probe Response frame that is not a
5
6 multi-link probe response that it transmits,
7 — shall include the Multi-Link Control field and the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link
8
element for the AP MLD as defined in 9.4.2.312.2 (Basic Multi-Link element)
9
10 — shall not include the Link Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element for the AP MLD unless
11 conditions in 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and
12
channel quieting) are satisfied
13
14 — may include a Reconfiguration Multi-Link element as defined in 9.4.2.312.4 (Reconfiguration Multi-
15 Link element) and 35.3.6 (Multi-Link reconfiguration).
16
17
If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD corresponds to a nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, then
18
the AP that corresponds to the transmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set, in the nontransmitted
19
20 BSSID profile corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID in a Beacon frame and a Probe Response frame
21 that is not a multi-link probe response that it transmits,
22
— shall include the Multi-Link Control field and the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link
23
24 element for the AP MLD as defined in 9.4.2.312.2 (Basic Multi-Link element)
25 — shall not include the Link Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element for the AP MLD unless
26
conditions in 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and
27
channel quieting) are satisfied
28
29 — may include a Reconfiguration Multi-Link element as defined in 9.4.2.312.4 (Reconfiguration Multi-
30
Link element) and 35.3.6 (Multi-Link reconfiguration).
31
32 NOTE—The conditions for the presence of subfields in the Common Info field are defined in 9.4.2.312.2.3 (Common
33 Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element).
34
35
A Probe Request frame that is not a multi-link probe request shall not include any variant Multi-Link
36
element.
37
38
39 A multi-link probe request shall include a Probe Request Multi-Link element and shall not include any other
40 variant Multi-Link element.
41
42
The value of the Link ID field of the Per-STA Profile subelement of the Probe Request Multi-Link element
43
44 identifies the AP affiliated with an AP MLD that is requested and shall be set to the link ID that is assigned
45 to this AP (see 35.3.3.2 (Link ID)).
46
47
35.3.4.5 Probe Request frame content for a non-AP EHT STA
48
49
50 An EHT AP shall follow the rules defined in 11.1.4.3.4 (Criteria for sending a response) when receiving a
51 Probe Request frame addressed to it even if the frame does not contain some of the elements described in
52 Table 9-66 (Probe Request frame body) when the soliciting non-AP STA follows the rules described in this
53
subclause.
54
55
56 If a non-AP EHT STA is sending a Probe Request frame that is not an multi-link probe request:
57 — it shall follow the rules defined in 9.3.3.9 (Probe Request frame format) regarding the inclusion of
58
the SSID element, the Supported Rates and BSS Membership Selectors field, the Request element,
59
the Extended Supported Rates and BSS Membership Selectors element, the DSSS Parameter Set
60
61 element, the SSID List element, the Extended Request element, the FILS Request Parameters
62 element, the Short SSID List element, Vendor Specific elements, and the Known BSSID element,
63
— it may omit other elements listed in 9.3.3.9 (Probe Request frame format) and may disregard the
64
65 normative requirements in 9.3.3.9 (Probe Request frame format) for these other elements.

1 If a non-AP EHT STA is sending an multi-link probe request:

3

15 35.3.4.6 Frame exchange sequences during MLO discovery and multi-link setup
16
17
A non-AP MLD is expected to discover an AP MLD and affiliated AP(s) of interest before initiating a multi-
18
link setup with the AP MLD. The non-AP MLD can use one or a combination of the following methods for
19
20 discovering the AP MLD and affiliated AP(s) of interest:
21
— Through each of its affiliated STAs, perform passive scanning by following the procedure defined in
22
11.1.4.2 (Passive scanning) or active scanning by following the procedure defined in 11.1.4.3
23
24 (Active scanning and probing procedures).
25 — Through one of its affiliated STAs, transmit a multi-link probe request on any link that the AP MLD
26
is operating on, with the frame addressed to the affiliated AP operating on that link, to obtain
27
information about the AP MLD and its affiliated AP(s) by following the procedure defined in
28
29 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe request and response).
30
31 The combination that the non-AP MLD selects to gather information is implementation dependent and can
32
be based on criteria such as power consumption, single radio operation, reachability, etc. The non-AP MLD
33
follows all the probing rules for the channel the Probe Request frame is sent on in the context of active
34
35 scanning. For example, when performing active scanning on 6 GHz channels, it follows the rules specified
36 in 26.17.2.3.3 (Non-AP STA scanning behavior).
37
38 NOTE 1—A non-AP MLD can discover basic information of an AP MLD or that of an AP affiliated with an AP MLD
39 via other means such as BSS transition management (see 35.3.23 (BSS transition management for MLDs)). The frame
40 exchange for gathering information of the AP MLD and its affiliated one or more APs, and for performing multi-link
41 setup with the AP MLD will be the same as that described in this clause.
42
43 Figure 35-8 (Possible frame exchange sequences during MLO discovery and multi-link setup when the AP
44 operating on the channel does not correspond to a nontransmitted BSSID) shows a possible frame exchange

36

38 — There are three BSSIDs active in the multiple BSSID set: transmitted BSSID (index 0) and two

40

44
operating on a different link.

47

48
corresponding to an AP that is operating on a different link and affiliated with a different AP MLD.

16 35.3.5 Multi-link (re)setup
17
18 35.3.5.1 Multi-link (re)setup procedure
19
20
The multi-link (re)setup procedure sets up link(s) between a non-AP MLD and an AP MLD and is
21
22 completed through the exchange of (Re)Association Request and (Re)Association Response frames. The
23 non-AP MLD and AP MLD shall follow the (re)association procedure between MLDs as described in 11.3
24 (STA authenticationAuthentication and association).
25
26 NOTE 1—Prior to utilizing (Re)Association Request/Response frame exchange to perform multi-link (re)setup with an
27 AP MLD, the non-AP MLD and AP MLD follow the authentication procedure between MLDs as described in 11.3 (STA
28 authenticationAuthentication and association).
29
30 A non-AP MLD may initiate a multi-link setup with an AP MLD to (re)set up one or more links with AP(s)
31
affiliated with the AP MLD. When a non-AP MLD initiates a multi-link (re)setup with an AP MLD, a STA
32
that is affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall transmit an (Re)Association Request frame on the link that it
33
34 desires to use as part of the multi-link (re)setup.
35
36 A (Re)Association Request/Response frame exchange is for a multi-link setup only if both the
37
(Re)Association Request frame and the (Re)Association Response frame include a Basic Multi-Link
38
element.
39
40
41 In the (Re)Association Request frame, the non-AP MLD indicates the link(s) that are requested for (re)setup
42 and the capabilities and operational parameters of the non-AP STA(s) affiliated with the non-AP MLD
43
corresponding to the requested link(s) as described in 35.3.5.4 (Usage and rules of Basic Multi-Link element
44
in the context of multi-link (re)setup, authentication, and FT action frame exchange between two MLDs). A
45
46 non-AP MLD may request to (re)set up link(s) with a subset of APs affiliated with the AP MLD.
47
48 In the (Re)Association Response frame, the AP MLD shall indicate the requested link(s) that are accepted
49
and the requested link(s) that are rejected for (re)setup and the capabilities and operational parameters of the
50
requested link(s) as described in 35.3.5.4 (Usage and rules of Basic Multi-Link element in the context of
51
52 multi-link (re)setup, authentication, and FT action frame exchange between two MLDs). The AP MLD may
53 do one of the following:
54
— accept all the links that are requested for (re)setup, or
55
56 — accept a subset of the links that are requested for (re)setup, or
57
— reject all the links that are requested for (re)setup.
58
59
60 The (Re)Association Response frame shall be transmitted by the AP MLD through the affiliated AP that
61 receives the (Re)Association Request frame.
62
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 505
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 506 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 A link that is requested by the non-AP MLD for (re)setup in the (Re)Association Request frame and is
2 accepted by the AP MLD in the (Re)Association Response frame is a setup link between the AP MLD and
3
the associated non-AP MLD.
4
5 NOTE 2—The corresponding AP of the setup link might be removed after the (Re)Association Request/Response frame
6 exchange as defined in 35.3.6 (Multi-Link reconfiguration).
7
8
NOTE 3—The links that are requested for resetup are independent of the existing setup links with an associated AP
9
MLD.
10
11 NOTE 4—The capability and operation parameters of each requested link during multi-link resetup are independent of
12 the capability and operation parameters of each existing setup link with an associated AP MLD.
13
14
NOTE 5—The link requested by the non-AP MLD might not exist because the AP MLD has removed the corresponding
15
affiliated AP (see 35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affiliated APs)) in which case the AP MLD might reject the requested link or the
16
AP MLD might add the corresponding affiliated AP (see 35.3.6.2.1 (Adding affiliated APs)) and the AP MLD might
17
accept the requested link.
18
19
The AP MLD shall not accept a link that is requested for (re)setup if any of the following condition is true:
20
21 — The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD corresponding to the link does not support all of
22 the rates in the BSSBasicRateSet parameter and all of the membership selectors in the
23
BSSMembershipSelectorSet parameter of the AP affiliated with the AP MLD corresponding to the
24
link in the MLME-START.request primitive.
25
26 — The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD corresponding to the link does not support all of
27
the MCSs in the Basic HT-MCS Set field of the HT Operation parameter of the AP affiliated with the
28
AP MLD (if present) corresponding to the link in the MLME-START.request primitive.
29
30 — The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD corresponding to the link does not support all of
31 the <VHT-MCS, NSS> tuples indicated by the Basic VHT-MCS And NSS Set field of the VHT
32
Operation parameter of the AP affiliated with the AP MLD (if present) corresponding to the link in
33
the MLME-START.request primitive.
34
35 — The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD corresponding to the link does not support all of
36
the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples indicated by the Basic HE-MCS And NSS Set field of the HE Operation
37
parameter of the AP affiliated with the AP MLD corresponding to the link in the MLME-
38
39 START.request primitive.
40 — The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD corresponding to the link does not support all of
41
the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuples indicated by the Basic EHT-MCS And NSS Set field of the EHT
42
Operation parameter of the AP affiliated with the AP MLD corresponding to the link in the MLME-
43
44 START.request primitive.
45
46 An MLD that requests or accepts multi-link (re)setup for any two links ensures that each link is located on
47
different nonoverlapping operating channels.
48
49
50 If the link on which the (Re)Association Request frame was received cannot be accepted by the AP MLD,
51 the AP MLD shall treat the multi-link (re)setup as a failure and shall not accept any requested links. If the
52 link on which the (Re)Association Request frame was received is accepted by the AP MLD, the multi-link
53
(re)setup is successful.
54
55
NOTE 6—See 35.3.5.4 (Usage and rules of Basic Multi-Link element in the context of multi-link (re)setup,
56
authentication, and FT action frame exchange between two MLDs) for the setting of the Status Code field.
57
58
An AP MLD shall assign a single AID to a non-AP MLD upon successful multi-link setup. All the non-AP
59
STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall have the same AID as the one assigned to the non-AP MLD
60
61 during multi-link setup.
62
63 NOTE 7—In a multiple BSSID set, the first 2n bits of the partial virtual bitmap of TIM element are reserved for the
64 indication of group addressed frame for the BSSIDs in the set (see 11.1.3.8.5 (Traffic advertisement in a multiple BSSID
65 set)). As a result, an AP affiliated with an AP MLD does not assign, to a non-AP MLD, an AID value that is less than 2N
506 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 where N is the maximum of the value carried in the MaxBSSID Indicator (n) field of the Multiple BSSID element
2 corresponding to each link that is accepted as part of the multi-link (re)setup where the AP affiliated with the AP MLD
3 belongs to a multiple BSSID set.
4
5 After successful multi-link (re)setup between a non-AP MLD and an AP MLD, the non-AP MLD is
6
associated with the AP MLD following the (re)association procedure between MLDs as described in 11.3
7
(STA authenticationAuthentication and association) (i.e., in State 3 or State 4, see 11.3.2 (State variables)),
8
9 and the non-AP MLD and the AP MLD set up link(s) for multi-link operation (see 35.3 (Multi-link
10 operation).
11
12
For each setup link, the corresponding non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD is in the same
13
associated state as the non-AP MLD and is associated with the corresponding AP affiliated with the AP
14
15 MLD. For each setup link, there is no mapping between the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD
16 and the AP affiliated with the AP MLD provided to the DS.
17
18
NOTE 8—The non-AP MLD and the AP MLD have an MLD association (see 11.3 (STA authenticationAuthentication
19
and association)), and the DS is notified of this mapping between the non-AP MLD and the AP MLD (see 4.5.3.3
20
(Association)).
21
22
23 For each setup link, the functionalities between a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD and its
24 associated AP affiliated with the AP MLD are enabled unless the functionalities have been extended to the
25
MLD level and specified otherwise.
26

28 An example of multi-link setup is shown in Figure 35-13 (Example of multi-link setup).

44
In this example, the AP MLD has three affiliated APs: AP 1 operates in the 2.4 GHz band, AP 2 operates in
45
46 the 5 GHz band, and AP 3 operates in the 6 GHz band. Non-AP MLD initiates the multi-link setup
47 procedure and non-AP STA 1 affiliated with the non-AP MLD sends an Association Request frame to AP 1
48 affiliated with the AP MLD, i.e., the TA field of the Association Request frame is set to the MAC address of
49
the non-AP STA 1 and the RA field of the Association Request frame is set to the MAC address of the AP 1.
50
The Association Request frame includes a Basic Multi-Link element that indicates the MLD MAC address
51
52 of the non-AP MLD and complete profile of non-AP STA 1 (in the frame body of the Association Request
53 frame), non-AP STA 2 (in a Per-STA Profile subelement carried in the Basic Multi-Link element), and non-
54 AP STA 3 (in a Per-STA Profile subelement carried in the Basic Multi-Link element) to request three links
55
to be setup (one link between AP 1 and non-AP STA 1, one link between AP 2 and non-AP STA 2, and one
56
link between AP 3 and non-AP STA 3). AP MLD then responds to the requested multi-link setup, and AP 1
57
58 affiliated with the AP MLD sends an Association Response frame to non-AP STA 1 affiliated with the non-
59 AP MLD, i.e., the TA field of the Association Response frame is set to the MAC address of the AP 1 and the
60 RA field of the Association Response frame is set to the MAC address of the non-AP STA 1, to indicate
61
successful multi-link setup. The Association Response frame includes a Basic Multi-Link element that
62
indicates the MLD MAC address of the AP MLD and complete profile of AP 1 (in the frame body of the
63
64 Association Response frame), AP 2 (in a Per-STA Profile subelement carried in the Basic Multi-Link
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 507

## 第 508 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 element), and AP 3 (in a Per-STA Profile subelement carried in the Basic Multi-Link element). After
2 successful multi-link setup between the non-AP MLD and AP MLD, three links are setup (link 1 between
3
AP 1 and non-AP STA 1, link 2 between AP 2 and non-AP STA 2, and link 3 between AP 3 and non-AP
4
STA 3).
5
6
7 35.3.5.2 Multi-link security
8
9
After a successful multi-link (re)setup between a non-AP MLD and an AP MLD, a PMKSA and a PTKSA
10
are established between the non-AP MLD and the AP MLD. In addition, a GTKSA, an IGTKSA if
11
12 management frame protection is enabled, and a BIGTKSA if beacon protection is enabled, are established
13 between the non-AP MLD and the AP MLD for each setup link (see Clause 12 (Security)). The PTKSA is
14 used for cryptographic encapsulation and decapsulation of individually addressed MPDUs across all setup
15
links and the GTKSA of a link is used for cryptographic encapsulation and decapsulation of group addressed
16
MPDUs on the link as described in 12.5.2.3 (CCMP cryptographic encapsulation), 12.5.4.3 (GCMP
17
18 cryptographic encapsulation), 12.5.2.4 (CCMP decapsulation), and 12.5.4.4 (GCMP decapsulation). When
19 management frame protection is enabled, the IGTKSA of a link is used to provide integrity protection for
20 group addressed robust management frames across on the link as described in 12.6.19 (Protection of robust
21
Management frames). When beacon protection is enabled, the BIGTKSA of a link is used to provide
22
integrity protection for Beacon frames on the link as described in 12.6.23 (Protection of Beacon frames).
23
24
25 Different APs affiliated with an AP MLD use different GTK/IGTK/BIGTK. Each AP and the corresponding
26 non-AP STA affiliated with an associated non-AP MLD maintains a single PN/IPN/BIPN for each GTK/
27
IGTK/BIGTK. The GTK/IGTK/BIGTK of each setup links are delivered to the non-AP MLD using a single
28
4-way handshake as defined in 12.7.6 (4-way handshake). When a GTK/IGTK/BIGTK update is triggered
29
30 for an AP affiliated with the AP MLD, the updated GTK/IGTK/BIGTK may be delivered to the non-AP
31 MLD using the Group key handshake over any enabled link as defined in 12.7.7 (Group key handshake).
32
33 NOTE—When a non-AP MLD changes the link used for group addressed frame reception, the non-AP MLD supplicant
34 is able to request a group addressed handshake by sending an EAPOL-Key frame to the AP MLD authenticator with the
35 Key Type set to Group (0) and the Request bit set to 1 (see 12.7.7.1 (General)) to refresh Key RSC/BPN/IPN.
36
37 35.3.5.3 Multi-link tear down procedure
38
39
An MLD tears down all the setup links with an associated MLD by sending a Disassociation frame through
40
one of the STAs affiliated with the MLD, on a setup link, to the STA affiliated with the associated MLD
41
42 subject to additional constraints (see 35.3.7 (Link management)). The MLD and the associated MLD shall
43 follow the MLD disassociation procedure as described in 11.3 (STA authenticationAuthentication and
44 association).
45
46
After multi-link teardown, all the non-AP STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD and the non-AP MLD are
47
48 in the unassociated state (see 11.3.2 (State variables)).
49
50 35.3.5.4 Usage and rules of Basic Multi-Link element in the context of multi-link (re)setup,
51
authentication, and FT action frame exchange between two MLDs
52
53
54 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that initiates a multi-link (re)setup with an AP MLD shall
55 include a Basic Multi-Link element in an (Re)Association Request frame it transmits.
56
57
NOTE 1—When a (Re)Association Request frame is sent from a non-AP EHT STA with dot11MultiLinkActivated set
58
to false, the Basic Multi-Link element is not carried in the (Re)Association Request frame (see Table 9-62 (Association
59
Request frame body), Table 9-64 (Reassociation Request frame body), and 35.3.1 (General)).
60
61
62 The Basic Multi-Link element carried in the (Re)Association Request frame shall include the Common Info
63 field and may include the Link Info field.
64
65
