1 NOTE 2—The conditions for the presence of subfields in the Common Info field are defined in 9.4.2.312.2.3 (Common
2 Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element).
3
4 If there is other requested link(s) in addition to the link on which the (Re)Association Request frame is
5
transmitted, the Basic Multi-Link element carried in the (Re)Association Request frame shall include the
6
Link Info field, and for each requested link, the Link Info field shall contain the corresponding Per-STA
7
8 Profile subelement(s). For each Per-STA Profile subelement included in the Link Info field, the Complete
9 Profile subfield of the STA Control field shall be set to 1 (see 35.3.3.3 (Advertisement of complete or partial
10 per-link information)).
11
12
If there is no other requested link in addition to the link on which the (Re)Association Request frame is
13
14 transmitted, the Basic Multi-Link element carried in the (Re)Association Request frame shall not include the
15 Link Info field.
16
17
The AP that is affiliated with the AP MLD and that responds to an (Re)Association Request frame that
18
carries a Basic Multi-Link element shall include a Basic Multi-Link element in the (Re)Association
19
20 Response frame that it transmits.
21
22 NOTE 3—When a (Re)Association Response frame is sent to a non-AP EHT STA with dot11MultiLinkActivated set to
23 false, the Basic Multi-Link element is not carried in the (Re)Association Response frame (see Table 9-63 (Association
24
Response frame body), Table 9-65 (Reassociation Response frame body), and 35.3.1 (General)).
25
26
The Basic Multi-Link element carried in the (Re)Association Response frame shall include the Common
27
28 Info field and may include the Link Info field.
29
NOTE 4—The conditions for the presence of subfields in the Common Info field are defined in 9.4.2.312.2.3 (Common
30
Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element).
31
32
33 If there is other requested link(s) in addition to the link on which the (Re)Association Request frame was
34 transmitted, the Basic Multi-Link element carried in the (Re)Association Response frame shall contain the
35 Link Info field, and for each requested link, the Link Info field shall contain the corresponding Per-STA
36
Profile subelement(s).
37
38
39 For each Per-STA Profile subelement included in the Link Info field, the Complete Profile subfield of the
40 STA Control field shall be set to 1 (see 35.3.3.3 (Advertisement of complete or partial per-link information))
41 and the Status Code field included in the STA Profile subfield of the Per-STA Profile subelement shall
42
indicate SUCCESS if the link is accepted or the failure cause if the link is not accepted. The Status Code
43
field in the (Re)Association Response frame body shall indicate, as defined in 9.4.1.9 (Status Code field),
44
45 whether the link on which the (Re)Association Request frame is received is accepted or not. The Status Code
46 field included in the STA Profile subfield of the Per-STA Profile subelement shall indicate
47 DENIED_LINK_ON_WHICH_THE_(Re)ASSOCIATION_FRAME_IS_
48
TRANSMITTED_NOT_ACCEPTED if the Status Code is not set to
49
REFUSED_REASON_UNSPECIFIED and the link corresponding to the Per-STA Profile subelement is not
50
51 accepted only because the link on which the (Re)Association Request frame is transmitted is not accepted.
52
53 If there is no other requested link in addition to the link on which the (Re)Association Response frame is
54
transmitted, the Basic Multi-Link element carried in the (Re)Association Response frame shall not include
55
the Link Info field.
56
57
58 A STA affiliated with an MLD shall include a Basic Multi-Link element in an Authentication frame or FT
59 action frame that it transmits with the following rules:
60
— the STA shall include the MLD MAC address of the MLD with which the STA is affiliated in the
61
62 Common Info field of the element
63
— the STA shall set all subfields in the Presence Bitmap subfield of the Multi-Link Control field of the
64
element to 0

## 第 510 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — the STA shall not include the Link Info field of the element.
2
3
35.3.6 Multi-Link reconfiguration
4
5
6 35.3.6.1 General
7
8 Multi-link (ML) reconfiguration refers to a set of procedures through which an AP MLD can add one or
9
more affiliated APs to the AP MLD, or remove one or more affiliated APs from the AP MLD.
10
11
12 35.3.6.2 Adding or removing affiliated APs
13
14 35.3.6.2.1 Adding affiliated APs
15
16
An AP MLD may add one or more affiliated APs to the AP MLD (see 6.3.11.2 (MLME-START.request)).
17
18 Each added affiliated AP shall be announced through the Basic Multi-Link element (by changing the
19 Maximum Number Of Simultaneous Links subfield of the MLD Capabilities and Operations subfield), and
20 through the Reduced Neighbor Report element (by including a TBTT Information field with MLD
21
Parameter field for the added AP), in the Beacon and Probe Response frames transmitted by other APs
22
affiliated with the same AP MLD.
23
24
25 If an existing AP of the AP MLD where the affiliated AP is being added corresponds to a nontransmitted
26 BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, then the AP that corresponds to the transmitted BSSID in the same multiple
27
BSSID set shall follow the procedures in 35.3.4.4 (Multi-Link element usage rules in the context of
28
discovery) and 35.3.4.1 (AP behavior) to announce the added affiliated AP through the Basic Multi-Link
29
30 element and the Reduced Neighbor Report element.
31
32 For the added affiliated AP, rules defined in 35.3.4.4 (Multi-Link element usage rules in the context of
33
discovery) and 35.3.4.1 (AP behavior) shall be followed for including Basic Multi-Link element and
34
Reduced Neighbor Report element in the Beacon and Probe Response frames transmitted by the added AP
35
36 when it is not a nontransmitted BSSID of a multiple BSSID set and for including those elements by the
37 transmitted BSSID of a multiple BSSID set when the added AP is a nontransmitted BSSID of that multiple
38 BSSID set.
39
40 NOTE—The MAC address of any added affiliated AP that is a co-hosted AP is assumed to be within the address space
41 defined by the value of the Max Co-Hosted BSSID Indicator field (see 9.4.2.249 (HE Operation element) and
42 26.17.7 (Co-hosted BSSID set)). Similarly, the MAC address of any added affiliated AP that is nontransmitted BSSID is
43 assumed to be within the address space defined by the value of the MaxBSSID Indicator (see 9.4.2.45 (Multiple BSSID
44 element) and 11.1.3.8 (Multiple BSSID procedure)).
45
46 35.3.6.2.2 Removing affiliated APs
47
48
An AP MLD may remove one or more of its affiliated APs (see 6.3.136.2 (MLME-BSS-AP-
49
50 REMOVAL.request)). An AP MLD that is an NSTR mobile AP MLD shall not remove the affiliated AP
51 operating on the primary link (see 35.3.19 (NSTR mobile AP MLD operation)). The AP MLD shall
52 announce the removal of any affiliated AP through a Reconfiguration Multi-Link element (see 9.4.2.312.4
53
(Reconfiguration Multi-Link element)) in all Beacon frames transmitted by its affiliated APs, as well as all
54
Probe Response frames it transmits, until the affiliated AP has been removed.
55
56
57 When an AP MLD with which an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set is
58 affiliated with removes one or more affiliated APs, the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element carrying
59
information of the removed AP(s) shall be included within the nontransmitted BSSID profile of the Multiple
60
BSSID element contained in the Beacon frame and Probe Response frame transmitted by the transmitted
61
62 BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set.
63
64
65
510 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 511 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 In the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element the MLD MAC Address Present subfield shall be set to 0. The
2 AP MLD for which AP removal information is provided in the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element shall be
3
identified by the MLD MAC Address subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element for the same AP MLD
4
carried in the same management frame.
5
6
NOTE 1—In a Multi-Link Probe Response frame sent in response to a multi-link probe request directed to a
7
nontransmitted BSSID of a multiple BSSID set, for a Reconfiguration Multi-Link element included in the
8
nontransmitted BSSID profile of the Multiple BSSID element, the corresponding the AP MLD for which AP removal
9
information is provided is identified by the MLD MAC Address subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element, included in
10
the same management frame outside of the Multiple BSSID element, which has the AP MLD ID subfield set to same
11
value as the BSSID Index subfield of the Multiple-BSSID Index element carried in the same nontransmitted BSSID
12
profile.
13
14
For each affiliated AP that the AP MLD intends to remove, the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element shall
15
16 include a Per-STA Profile subelement with the subfields of the STA Control field set as following: The Link
17 ID subfield shall identify the AP being removed, the Complete Profile subfield shall be set to 0, the STA
18 MAC Address Present subfield shall be set to 0, the AP Removal Timer Present subfield shall be set to 1,
19
and the AP Removal Timer subfield shall be set to the number of TBTTs of that affiliated AP before it is
20
removed or for NSTR mobile AP MLD the AP Removal Timer subfield shall be set to the number of the
21
22 TBTTs of the AP operating on the primary link. The initial value of the AP Removal Timer subfield should
23 point to a TBTT value that provides sufficiently large enough time to announce the removal of affiliated AP
24 such that all associated non-AP MLDs including the ones in power save mode have the opportunity to
25
receive Reconfiguration Multi-Link element at least once before the AP is removed. The Per-STA Profile
26
subelement shall not include a STA Profile field.
27
28
29 To remove an affiliated AP, the SME of the AP MLD shall remove the AP from the AP MLD and terminate
30 the BSS corresponding to the affiliated AP.
31
32
The affiliated AP may transmit BSS Transition Management Request frame(s) to notify of BSS termination
33
34 to associated non-AP STAs that support BTM and are not affiliated with a non-AP MLD or to notify of BSS
35 termination to non-AP MLDs associated with the AP MLD of the affiliated AP. The affiliated AP shall
36 transmit BSS Transition Management Request frame(s) if there are associated non-AP STAs that are not
37
affiliated with a non-AP MLD and that support BTM to notify such non-AP STAs of BSS termination.
38
39
40 If the affiliated AP being removed transmits BSS Transition Management Request frame(s) to notify
41 termination of the BSS corresponding to the affiliated AP, the SME of that affiliated AP shall perform the
42 following procedure to terminate the BSS:
43
1) it shall follow the procedure in 11.21.7.3 (BSS transition management request) and 35.3.23 (BSS
44
45 transition management for MLDs) to transmit BSS Transition Management Request frame(s). It
46 shall set the fields in the BSS Transition Management Request frame(s) as follows:
47
— The Disassociation Imminent, BSS Termination Included, and Link Removal Imminent sub-
48
49 fields of the Request Mode field are set to 1; the Preferred Candidate List Included field is set
50 per 9.6.13.9 (BSS Transition Management Request frame format) if the BSS Transition Candi-
51 date List Entries field is included; other subfields of the Request Mode field are reserved.
52
— The Disassociation Timer field is set to the number of TBTTs of the affiliated AP before it
53
transmits a Disassociation frame to the STA(s) receiving the BSS Transition Management
54
55 Request frame. The Disassociation Timer field value shall point to a TBTT at or later than the
56 TBTT pointed to by the value of the AP Removal Timer field of the Reconfiguration Multi-
57 Link element in transmitted beacons.
58
— The BSS Termination Duration field shall be present and contain a BSS Termination Duration
59
subelement (see 9.4.2.36 (Neighbor Report element)), with the BSS Termination TSF field of
60
61 the subelement set to the value of the TSF timer when the BSS the affiliated AP belongs to will
62 be terminated. The BSS Termination TSF field value shall indicate a time that is later than the
63 TBTT the Disassociation Timer field value points to.
64
65

## 第 512 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — The BSS Transition Candidate List Entries field may be included specifying one or more
2 Neighbor Report elements to provide BSS transition candidate list.
3
— No other optional fields shall be present in the BSS Transition Management Request frame.
4
5 2) It shall start a disassociation timer with the initial value set to the value of the Disassociation Timer
6 field, and shall decrement the timer by one after transmitting each Beacon frame, until the timer has
7 the value of 0. The Disassociation Timer field in all subsequent transmitted BSS Transition Manage-
8
ment Request frames shall be set to the value of this timer.
9
10 3) Once the disassociation timer reaches a value of 0, and before the TSF indicated by the BSS Termi-
11 nation TSF field, it shall follow the procedure in 11.3.6.8 (AP, AP MLD, or PCP disassociation initi-
12
ation procedure) to transmit Disassociation frame(s) to all non-AP STAs associated with the AP
13
being removed that are not affiliated with a non-AP MLD. The affiliated AP shall not transmit Dis-
14
15 association frames until the disassociation timer has a value of 0.
16 4) It shall terminate the BSS corresponding to the affiliated AP at the time indicated by the BSS Termi-
17
nation TSF field.
18
19
NOTE 2—Since the non-AP STAs cannot discover or associate with the AP affiliated with NSTR mobile AP MLD
20
operating on the nonprimary link, the NSTR mobile AP MLD does not use the BTM procedure to disassociate non-AP
21
STAs when it is removing the AP operating on the nonprimary link.
22
23
A BSS Transition Management Request frame transmitted by the affiliated AP that is being removed may
24
provide preference for other AP MLDs to associate with as per 35.3.23 (BSS transition management for
25
26 MLDs) for the non-AP MLDs that have a single setup link with the AP being removed.
27
28 When a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD receives a BSS Transition Management Request frame
29
from an AP with BSS Termination Included subfield and Link Removal Imminent subfield equal to 1, the
30
non-AP MLD shall interpret the BTM to indicate that the BSS corresponding to the AP is being terminated.
31
32
33 When the affiliated AP being removed is not transmitting BSS Transition Management Request frame(s) to
34 notify the termination of the corresponding BSS, the SME of the affiliated AP shall terminate the
35
corresponding BSS at the TBTT indicated by the value of the AP Removal Timer subfield.
36
37
38 At the TBTT indicated by the value of the AP Removal Timer subfield in transmitted Reconfiguration
39 Multi-Link elements, the AP MLD shall remove the affiliated AP indicated by the Link ID subfield in the
40 STA Control field of the Per-STA Profile subelement that includes the AP Removal Timer subfield.
41
42
At the TBTT indicated by the value of the AP Removal Timer subfield in transmitted Reconfiguration
43
44 Multi-Link elements, the AP MLD shall disassociate a non-AP MLD if the link corresponding to the
45 removed AP is the only setup link between the AP MLD and the non-AP MLD.
46
47
At the TBTT indicated by the value of the AP Removal Timer subfield in transmitted Reconfiguration
48
Multi-Link elements, an associated non-AP MLD shall consider the link corresponding to the removed AP
49
50 nonexistent, and the SME of the affiliated non-AP STA associated with the removed affiliated AP shall
51 delete any information maintained for that link. After a non-AP MLD deletes any information maintained
52 for the link corresponding to the removed AP, if there are no other setup links with the AP MLD, then the
53
non-AP MLD shall consider that it has been disassociated from the AP MLD and shall delete MLD
54
association information.
55
56
NOTE 3—Once an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is removed, the other APs affiliated with the same AP MLD do not
57
include the TBTT Information field for the removed AP in the Reduced Neighbor Report element. Further, the removed
58
AP is not included by the transmitted BSSIDs of the multiple BSSID sets corresponding to the APs affiliated with the
59
same AP MLD.
60
61
62 NOTE 4—An AP MLD with two affiliated APs might remove one of its affiliated APs, and in such case, the AP MLD
63 has only one affiliated AP after the AP removal. Further, in such case, the non-AP MLD that is associated with the AP
64 MLD with two setup links has only one non-AP STA with a setup link after the AP is removed.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 If a non-AP MLD removes a setup link locally from its multi-link setup as a result of the removal of an AP
2 affiliated with its associated AP MLD, and that results in a TID not being mapped to any of the remaining
3
setup links (if exists) in either direction for that non-AP MLD, then the non-AP MLD and the AP MLD shall
4
operate with that TID mapped to all remaining enabled links for that direction after the removal of the setup
5
6 link, until a new TID-to-link mapping is established for that TID. Otherwise both the AP MLD and the non-
7 AP MLD shall continue to operate based on the currently established TID-to-link mapping for that TID on
8 the remaining setup links after the removal of the setup link.
9
10
If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is removed, any STR or NSTR requirements and capabilities (see
11
12 35.3.16.2 (Multi-link device capability and operation signaling), 35.3.16.3 (Simultaneous transmit and
13 receive (STR) operation), and 35.3.16.4 (Nonsimultaneous transmit and receive (NSTR) operation)) that
14 correspond to a link pair that includes the link corresponding to the removed AP shall no longer apply.
15
16
If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is removed and if the link associated with the removed AP is one of the
17
18 EMLSR links or the EMLMR links for one or more non-AP MLDs, the AP MLD shall remove the
19 corresponding link from the EMLSR links and/or EMLMR links of those non-AP MLDs.
20
21
35.3.7 Link management
22
23
24 35.3.7.1 TID-to-link mapping
25
26 35.3.7.1.1 General
27
28
The TID-to-link mapping mechanism allows an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD that performed or are
29
30 performing multi-link setup to determine how Data frames belonging to TIDs 0–7 and management frames
31 will be assigned for transmission, on the setup links between the two MLDs in DL and UL.
32
33
An AP MLD may support TID-to-link mapping negotiation. A non-AP MLD that performs multi-link
34
(re)setup on at least two links with an AP MLD that sets the TID-To-Link Mapping Negotiation Support
35
36 subfield of the MLD Capabilities field of the Basic Multi-Link element to a nonzero value shall support
37 TID-to-link mapping negotiation with the TID-To-Link Mapping Negotiation Support subfield of the MLD
38 Capabilities field of the Basic Multi-Link element it transmits to at least 1. An MLD with
39
dot11EHTBaseLineFeaturesImplementedOnly equal to true shall not set the TID-To-Link Mapping
40
Negotiation Support subfield of MLD Capabilities field of the Basic Multi-Link element to 3.
41
42
43 Editor’s Note: The MIB variable dot11EHTBaseLineFeaturesImplementedOnly is removed because of
44 CID 10213. Task Group discussion is needed to modify the above sentence.
45
46
By default, all TIDs shall be mapped to all setup links for both DL and UL (see 35.3.7.1.2 (Default mapping
47
48 mode)). When a negotiated TID-to-link mapping is in effect according to the procedure defined in 35.3.7.1.3
49 (Negotiation of TID-to-link mapping), 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe
50 Response frames), and 35.3.7.1.8 (Association procedures for TID-to-link mapping), then a TID can be
51
mapped to a link set, which is a subset of setup links, spanning from only one setup link to all the setup links,
52
with restrictions defined in 35.3.7.1.3 (Negotiation of TID-to-link mapping).
53
54
55 A setup link is defined as enabled for a non-AP MLD if at least one TID is mapped to that link either in DL
56 or in UL and is defined as disabled if no TIDs are mapped to that link both in DL and UL. At any point in
57
time, a TID shall always be mapped to at least one setup link both in DL and UL, which means that a TID-
58
to-link mapping change is only valid and successful if it will not result in having any TID for which the link
59
60 set for DL or UL is made of zero setup links. By default, all setup links are enabled (see 35.3.7.1.2 (Default
61 mapping mode)).
62
63
If a link is enabled for a non-AP MLD, then:
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 513
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 514 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — it may be used for individually addressed frame exchange, subject to the power state of the non-AP
2 STA operating on that link and only MSDUs or A-MSDUs with TIDs mapped to that link may be
3
transmitted on that link between the corresponding non-AP STA and AP affiliated with the non-AP
4
MLD and AP MLD, respectively, in the direction (DL/UL) corresponding to the TID-to-link
5
6 mapping.
7 — Individually addressed Management frames, QoS Null frames, and Control frames may be sent on
8
any enabled links between the corresponding non-AP MLD and AP MLD both in DL and UL.
9
10
11 If a link is disabled for a non-AP MLD, it shall not be used for individually addressed frame exchange
12 between the corresponding non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD and AP affiliated with the
13 associated AP MLD, including Management and Control frames, except that if the link is disabled for a non-
14
AP MLD but is not advertised as disabled by the AP MLD (see 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping
15
in Beacon and Probe Response frames)), then the link may be used for class 1 and 2 Management frames,
16
17 class 1 Control frames and TID-to-link Mapping Request, TID-to-link Mapping Response and TID-to-link
18 Mapping Teardown frames, if initiated by the non-AP MLD.
19
20
A STA affiliated with an MLD that operates on a link disabled by an advertised TID-to-link mapping (see
21
35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames)) shall suspend all
22
23 wireless functionalities on that link until the link is enabled.
24
25 NOTE 1— Suspension of wireless functionalities refers to functionalities such as frame generation, schedules,
26
scoreboard maintenances, etc., while still preserving previously negotiated parameters with the peer EHT STA(s).
27
28 NOTE 2—Group addressed frames delivery procedure is defined in 35.3.15 (Multi-link operation group addressed
29 frames).
30
31 NOTE 3—The AP affiliated with AP MLD that operates on link which is disabled for an associated non-AP MLD
32 (following the procedure described in 35.3.7.1 (TID-to-link mapping)) uses this link for any frame exchange with other
33 non-AP STAs affiliated with other associated non-AP MLD(s) for which this link is enabled (if any such non-AP STAs
34 exist).
35
36
If a link that is setup on a DFS owner’s operating channel is advertised as disabled by AP MLD (see
37
35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames)), then before resuming
38
39 operations on it (i.e., enabling the link), the AP MLD shall comply with the applicable regulatory
40 requirements.
41
42
If a TID is mapped in UL to a set of enabled links for a non-AP MLD, then the non-AP MLD may use any
43
link within this set of enabled links to transmit individually addressed MSDUs or A-MSDUs that are
44
45 destined to the AP MLD and that correspond to that TID.
46
47 If a TID is mapped in DL to a set of enabled links for a non-AP MLD, then:
48
— The non-AP MLD may retrieve individually addressed buffered BUs available at the AP MLD that
49
50 are MSDUs or A-MSDUs corresponding to that TID on any link within this set of enabled links.
51
— The AP MLD may use any link within this set of enabled links to transmit individually addressed
52
MSDUs or A-MSDUs that are destined to the non-AP MLD and that correspond to that TID, subject
53
54 to the power state of the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD on each of these links.
55
NOTE 4—The non-AP MLD can retrieve BUs buffered by the AP MLD on any mapped link. In addition, the AP MLD
56
can recommend link(s) as defined in 35.3.12.4 (Traffic indication).
57
58
59 A non-AP MLD may retrieve buffered BUs that are individually addressed MMPDUs available at the AP
60 MLD on any enabled link. An AP MLD may use any enabled links to transmit individually addressed
61 management frames (see Table 11-3 (Bufferable/nonbufferable classification of MMPDUs)) subject to the
62
rules defined in 35.3.14 (Multi-link device individually addressed Management frame delivery) and subject
63
to the power state of the non-AP STA on each of the links (see 35.3.12 (Multi-link power management)).
64

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 If a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD is in active mode on a link with a set of TIDs mapped for
2 DL transmission, its associated AP affiliated with the AP MLD shall transmit to the non-AP STA:
3
— MSDUs/A-MSDUs, if any, corresponding to that set of negotiated TIDs for the non-AP MLD, and
4
5 — MMPDUs, if any, for the non-AP MLD or its affiliated non-AP STAs, subject to the rules defined in
6
35.3.14 (Multi-link device individually addressed Management frame delivery),
7
8
9 unless the MSDUs/A-MSDUs and/or MMPDUs stated above are transmitted to another non-AP STA that is
10 affiliated with the same non-AP MLD and in active mode or in PS mode and in the awake state.
11
12 NOTE 5—Operation with non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD in power save mode are defined in 35.3.12.4
13 (Traffic indication).
14
15 35.3.7.1.2 Default mapping mode
16
17
Under this mode, all TIDs are mapped to all setup links for DL and UL, and all setup links are enabled. A
18
non-AP MLD associated with an AP MLD shall operate under this mode if the following two conditions are
19
20 met:
21 — a TID-to-link mapping is not advertised by the AP MLD (see 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link
22
mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames)),
23
24 — a TID-to-link mapping negotiation for a different mapping did not occur, was unsuccessful while
25 having no successfully negotiated TID-to-link mapping before or was torn down.
26
27
35.3.7.1.3 Negotiation of TID-to-link mapping
28
29
30 An MLD that supports TID-to-link mapping negotiation has dot11TIDtoLinkMappingActivated equal to
31 true and shall set to a nonzero value the TID-to-link Mapping Negotiation Support subfield in the MLD
32
Capabilities and Operations field of the Basic Multi-Link element that it transmits. An MLD that does not
33
support TID-to-link mapping negotiation has dot11TIDtoLinkMappingActivated equal to false and shall set
34
35 the TID-to-link Mapping Negotiation Support subfield to 0. If the TID-to-link Mapping Negotiation Support
36 subfield value received from a peer MLD is equal to 1, the MLD that initiates a TID-to-link mapping
37 negotiation with the peer MLD shall send only the TID-to-link Mapping element where all TIDs are mapped
38
to the same link set. If the TID-to-link Mapping Negotiation Support subfield value received from a peer
39
MLD is equal to 3, the MLD that initiates a TID-to-link mapping negotiation with the peer MLD shall send
40
41 the TID-to-link Mapping element where each TID is mapped to the same or different link set.
42
43 During a multi-link (re)setup procedure, a non-AP MLD may initiate a TID-to-link mapping negotiation by
44
including the TID-to-link Mapping element in the (Re)Association Request frame if an AP MLD has
45
indicated a support of TID-to-link mapping negotiation. Otherwise, the non-AP MLD shall not include the
46
47 TID-to-link Mapping element in the (Re)Association Request frame.
48
49 After the multi-link (re)setup is successful and 4-way handshake is complete (if RSNA is required), to
50
negotiate a new TID-to-link mapping, an initiating non-AP MLD with dot11TIDtoLinkMappingActivated
51
equal to true shall send an individually addressed TID-to-link Mapping Request frame to a responding MLD
52
53 that has indicated support of TID-to-link mapping negotiation.
54
55 An AP MLD with dot11TIDtoLinkMappingActivated equal to true that initiates a TID-to-link mapping
56
negotiation may perform one of the following:
57
58 — Send an individually addressed TID-to-link Mapping Request frame to a non-AP MLD
59
— Advertise a TID-to-link mapping by including a TID-To-Link Mapping element in Beacon and
60
Probe Response frames as defined in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and
61
62 Probe Response frames).
63
64
65

## 第 516 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 Upon receiving the individually addressed TID-to-link Mapping Request frame, the responding MLD shall
2 send an individually addressed TID-to-link Mapping Response frame to the initiating MLD according to the
3
following rules:
4
5 — If the responding MLD accepts the requested TID-to-link mapping in the TID-to-link Mapping
6 element in the received TID-to-link Mapping Request frame, it shall set to 0 (SUCCESS) the Status
7 Code in the TID-to-link Mapping Response frame.
8
9 — Otherwise, the responding MLD shall indicate rejection of the proposed TID-to-link mapping by
10 setting to either 133 (DENIED_TID_TO_LINK_MAPPING) or
11 134 (PREFERRED_TID_TO_LINK_MAPPING_SUGGESTED) the Status Code in the TID-to-link
12
Mapping Response frame. When the Status Code in the TID-to-link Mapping Response frame is
13
134 (PREFERRED_TID_TO_LINK_MAPPING_SUGGESTED), the responding MLD is
14
15 suggesting a preferred mapping as indicated in the TID-to-link Mapping element included in the
16 frame.
17
18
An MLD may suggest a preferred TID-to-link mapping to a peer MLD by sending an unsolicited TID-to-
19
link Mapping Response frame with the Dialog Token field set to 0 that includes the TID-to-link Mapping
20
21 element and sets the Status Code to 134 (PREFERRED_TID_TO_LINK_MAPPING_SUGGESTED). An
22 MLD shall not send an unsolicited TID-to-link Mapping Response frame that includes the TID-to-link
23 Mapping element and sets the Status Code to either 0 (SUCCESS) or 133
24
(DENIED_TID_TO_LINK_MAPPING).
25
26
27 When initiating a new TID-to-link mapping negotiation with a peer MLD, an MLD should take into account
28 the preferred TID-to-link mapping of the peer MLD if it has indicated one. In addition, an AP MLD should
29 take into account the traffic flow(s) from the non-AP MLD and the capabilities and constraints (e.g., single
30
radio operation), if any, of the non-AP MLD when providing a preferred TID-to-link mapping or initiating a
31
new TID-to-link mapping negotiation with the non-AP MLD.
32
33
34 A multi-radio non-AP MLD should accept a TID-to-link mapping initiated by its associated AP MLD. A
35 TID-to-link mapping negotiation is successful if a MLD successfully transmits or receives a TID-to-link
36
Mapping Response frame with the value of the Status Code field equal to 0 (SUCCESS).
37
38
39 When two MLDs have negotiated a TID-to-link mapping, either MLD may tear down the negotiated TID-
40 to-link mapping by sending an individually addressed TID-To-Link Mapping Teardown frame, except a
41 non-AP MLD shall not tear down a negotiated TID-to-link mapping if the current TID-to-link mapping was
42
established by an advertisement of TID-to-link mapping. After teardown, if a mapping scheme is advertised
43
by the AP MLD as described in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response
44
45 frames), the MLDs shall operate in the established mode as described in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link
46 mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames), otherwise they shall operate in the default mapping mode
47 (see 35.3.7.1.2 (Default mapping mode)).
48
49
Once an MLD has successfully negotiated the TID-to-link mapping with a peer MLD, both the MLD and the
50
51 peer MLD shall update uplink and/or downlink TID-to-link mapping information according to the
52 negotiated TID-to-link mapping. In case TID-to-link mapping of a specific TID is missing in the
53 negotiation, the most recent TID-to-link mapping of this TID shall remain unchanged and valid. If an MLD
54
has failed to negotiate the TID-to-link mapping with a peer MLD, the most recent TID-to-link mapping of
55
all TID shall remain unchanged and valid.
56
57
NOTE—If there is no successfully negotiated TID-to-link mapping for a TID, then the TID is mapped to all setup links
58
for DL and UL.
59
60
When an MLD has successfully negotiated with a peer MLD an uplink and/or downlink TID-to-link
61
62 mapping in which the bit position i of the Link Mapping Of TID n field in the TID-to-link Mapping element
63 in the (Re)Association Request frame, TID-To-Link Mapping Request frame, Beacon frame, or Probe
64
65

1 Response frame is set to 0, TID n shall not be mapped to the link associated with the link ID i in the uplink
2 and/or downlink based on the Direction subfield in the TID-To-Link Mapping element.
3
4
When an MLD has successfully negotiated with a peer MLD an uplink and/or downlink TID-to-link
5

9

12 35.3.7.1.4 Power state after enablement
13
14 When a link becomes enabled for a non-AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD after successful
15
association with an AP MLD with (Re)Association Request/Response frames transmitted on that link or
16
after successful TID-to-link mapping negotiation with TID-To-Link Mapping Request/Response frames
17
18 transmitted on that link, the power management mode of the non-AP STA, immediately after the
19 acknowledgement of the (Re)Association Response frame or of the TID-To-Link Mapping Response frame,
20 is active mode.
21
22
When a link becomes enabled for a non-AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD after successful
23
24 association with an AP MLD with (Re)Association Request/Response frames transmitted on another link or
25 after successful TID-to-link mapping negotiation with TID-To-Link Mapping Request/Response frames
26 transmitted on another link, the power management mode of the non-AP STA, immediately after the
27
acknowledgement of the (Re)Association Response frame or of the TID-To-Link Mapping Response frame,
28
is power save mode, and its power state is doze.
29
30
31 When a link becomes enabled for a non-AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD at the end of an
32 advertised TID-to-link mapping (see 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe
33
Response frames)), the power management mode of the non-AP STA, immediately after the end of the
34
advertised TID-to-link mapping, is power save mode, and its power state is doze.
35
36
37 35.3.7.1.5 Power state and TWT schedules after disablement
38
39
When a link becomes disabled for a non-AP MLD:
40
41 — The APSD scheduled SPs of the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD and operating on the
42 link shall be deleted.
43
— If the link has been disabled using an individual TID-to-link mapping negotiation, then the TWT
44
45 agreements and TWT memberships of the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD and
46 operating on the link shall be torn down.
47
— If the link has been disabled due to AP advertisement of TID-to-link mapping (see 35.3.7.1.7
48
(Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames)), if the non-AP STA
49
50 affiliated with the non-AP MLD corresponding to the disabled link has set the TWT Information
51 Frame Disabled field to 0 in the TWT element sent during a TWT setup, then the non-AP STA shall
52 consider all TWT Individual and Broadcast agreements suspended until the link is enabled, unless
53
the TWT agreements are already suspended with a resumption time after the Expected Duration
54
expires. Otherwise, if the non-AP STA has not set the TWT Information Frame Disabled field to 0,
55
56 then all TWT Individual agreements and broadcast TWT memberships shall be torn down.
57
— The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD and operating on the link may cease maintaining
58
a power state and power management mode.
59
60 — The AP to which the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD is associated and operating on
61 the link may cease maintaining a power management status that indicates in which power
62
management mode the STA is currently operating.
63
64
65

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that has transmitted a frame to the AP affiliated with its
2 associated AP MLD on a disabled link, if allowed by the rules defined in 35.3.7.1.1 (General) and from
3
which it expects a response, shall remain in the awake state until such a response is received or until the
4
procedure has timed out.
5
6
7 35.3.7.1.6 Use of More Data subfield by an MLD
8
9
An AP MLD uses the More Data subfield as defined in 9.2.4.1.8 (More Data subfield) in frames transmitted
10
by one of its affiliated AP to a non-AP STA in PS mode affiliated with the non-AP MLD to indicate to the
11
12 non-AP MLD that more individually addressed BUs are buffered for that non-AP MLD. The indicated
13 buffered BUs are buffered at the AP MLD for the non-AP MLD and correspond only to Data frames for the
14 non-AP MLD and with TIDs that are mapped to this link by the most recent DL TID-to-link mapping
15
(negotiated TID-to-link mapping or default mode mapping, see 35.3.7.1 (TID-to-link mapping)) or
16
Management frames for the non-AP MLD or for a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD (see
17
18 Table 11-3 (Bufferable/nonbufferable classification of MMPDUs) and 35.3.12.4 (Traffic indication)).
19
20 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall follow the procedure defined in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation) for setting
21
the More Data subfield and the EOSP subfield, except that in individually addressed frames the More Data
22
subfield is used to indicate the presence of more BUs at the AP MLD for a non-AP MLD, as defined above.
23
24
25 The indicated frames correspond only to Data frames for the non-AP MLD and with TIDs that are mapped
26 to this link by the most recent DL TID-to-link mapping (negotiated TID-to-link mapping or default mode
27
mapping, see 35.3.7.1 (TID-to-link mapping)) or Management frames (see Table 11-3 (Bufferable/
28
nonbufferable classification of MMPDUs) and 35.3.12.4 (Traffic indication)) that are not buffered because
29
30 another non-AP STA affiliated with the same non-AP MLD is in active mode (see conditions in 35.3.12.4
31 (Traffic indication)).
32
33
When a non-AP STA that is in PS mode and that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating with default
34
mapping (see 35.3.7.1.2 (Default mapping mode)) receives an individually addressed MPDU from its
35
36 associated AP affiliated with the associated AP MLD with the More Data subfield set to 1, then at least one
37 of any non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall follow or continue following the procedure
38 defined in 11.2.3.7 (Receive operation for STAs in PS mode) and 11.2.3.8 (Receive operation using APSD)
39
and may send PS-Poll frames or UAPSD trigger frames, if needed, to retrieve buffered BUs buffered at the
40
AP MLD.
41
42
43 When a non-AP STA that is in PS mode and that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating with a
44 negotiated non-default TID-to-link mapping (see 35.3.7.1.3 (Negotiation of TID-to-link mapping)) receives
45
an individually addressed MPDU from its associated AP with the More Data subfield set to 1 on a link
46
(receiving link), then at least one of any non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD that is operating on
47
48 the link (receiving link) or another link to which any of the TIDs that is mapped to the link (receiving link) is
49 also mapped shall follow or continue following the procedures defined in 11.2.3.7 (Receive operation for
50 STAs in PS mode) and 11.2.3.8 (Receive operation using APSD) and may send PS-Poll frames or UAPSD
51
trigger frames, if needed, with any TID that is mapped to this operating link to retrieve the buffered BUs
52
buffered at the AP MLD.
53
54
55 35.3.7.1.7 Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames
56
57
An AP MLD may advertise a mandatory TID-to-link mapping by including a TID-To-Link Mapping
58
element in the Beacon and Probe Response frames that the APs affiliated with the AP MLD transmit.
59
60
61 An AP that advertises a TID-to-link mapping shall include the Mapping Switch Time field and sets it to the
62 time, in units of TUs, of a DTIM Beacon of one of the APs affiliated with the AP MLD. Beginning at the
63
indicated time, the indicated TID-to-link mapping is established and the Mapping Switch Time field is no
64
longer included. Figure 35-14 (An illustration of an advertised TID-to-link mapping taking effect on all

31 Figure 35-14—An illustration of an advertised TID-to-link mapping taking effect on all links
32
33
34
35 An AP MLD shall not advertise a TID-to-link mapping that does not map all TIDs to the same link set, both
36
for DL and UL. The Direction field of an advertised TID-To-Link Mapping element shall be set to 2.
37
38 NOTE 1—An advertised TID-to-link mapping will include a mapping for all TIDs.
39
40
NOTE 2—If the Link ID of each AP in a multiple BSSID set and affiliated with different MLDs is different, then
41
inheritance will not apply to an advertised TID-to-link mapping for the APs that are part of a multiple BSSID set, and
42
therefore the TID-To-Link Mapping element needs to be carried in each nontransmitted BSSID profile to which an
43
advertised mapping applies.
44
45
An AP MLD shall include two TID-To-Link Mapping elements in the Beacon and Probe Response frames
46
47 that the APs affiliated with the AP MLD transmit, if there is already an established advertised TID-to-link
48 mapping and a new nondefault advertised TID-to-link mapping will replace it. In this case, the AP MLD
49 shall not include the Mapping Switch Time field in the currently established advertised TID-To-Link
50
Mapping element, and shall include the Mapping Switch Time field in the new TID-To-Link Mapping
51
element, in order to indicate an advertised TID-to-link mapping that will be established in the future. The
52
53 value of the Expected Duration field of the existing TID-To-Link Mapping element shall indicate a
54 remaining duration that ends at the same time as indicated by the Mapping Switch Time field of the new
55 TID-To-Link Mapping element.
56
57 NOTE 3—If the newly advertised TID-to-link mapping is the default mapping, the AP MLD sets the Expected Duration
58 field of the currently advertised TID-to-link mapping to the remaining time until the default mapping is established as
59 described in 9.4.2.314 (TID-To-Link Mapping element) and does not include the TID-To-Link Mapping element for the
60 newly advertised TID-to-link mapping in the Beacon and Probe Response frames. After the establishment of the default
61 mapping, no TID-To-Link Mapping elements are included in the Beacon or Probe Response frames transmitted by the
62 APs affiliated with the AP MLD.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 All APs affiliated with an AP MLD that advertises a TID-to-link mapping shall include the same mapping in
2 all Beacon and Probe Response frames from the time at which the TID-to-link mapping is first advertised
3
until the time at which the TID-to-link mapping is no longer advertised, and shall include the Expected
4
Duration field in all TID-to-link mapping elements in Beacons. From when a new TID-to-link mapping is
5
6 advertised in a Beacon frame until the advertised TID-to-link mapping is established, the Mapping Switch
7 Time field shall be included in the TID-To-Link Mapping element and set to the time, in units of TUs, at
8 which the TID-to-link mapping will be established, then not included thereafter. The time indicated by the
9
Mapping Switch Time field shall be the TBTT of the DTIM Beacon of one of the APs affiliated with the AP
10
MLD. The Mapping Switch Time field should initially be set to a sufficiently large value. After an
11
12 advertised TID-to-link mapping is established, the duration indicated by Expected Duration field shall
13 indicate the time when the advertised TID-to-link mapping is expected to end. During the advertisement of
14 the TID-to-link mapping the time indicated may be updated to indicate an earlier time than initially
15
indicated, but shall not be updated to indicate a later time than initially indicated. The duration indicated by
16
Expected Duration field shall be exact when the duration is smaller than two DTIM periods of the AP
17
18 transmitting the frame carrying the field.
19
20 At the time indicated by the Mapping Switch Time field of a TID-To-Link Mapping element in a Beacon or
21
a Probe Response frame received by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD from an AP affiliated
22
with its associated AP MLD, or at the time indicated by the Expected Duration field of an existing
23
24 advertised TID-to-link mapping which will be replaced by an advertised default mapping, the non-AP MLD
25 shall update its TID-to-link mapping according to the rules that establish a TID-to-link mapping in this
26 subclause and with the consequences of the updated mapping defined in 35.3.7.1.1 (General).
27
28
The TID-to-link mapping that is established in a non-AP MLD following an advertised TID-to-link mapping
29
30 from its associated AP MLD is derived as follows:
31 — The set of mapped links for each TID and direction for a non-AP MLD are the set of links that are
32
included in the non-AP MLD multi-link setup with the associated AP MLD and have been mapped
33
to that TID for that direction in the advertised TID-to-link mapping.
34
35
NOTE 4—An individually negotiated TID-to-link mapping whose negotiation was completed prior to the establishment
36
of an advertised TID-to-link mapping is discarded at the time of the establishment of the advertised TID-to-link
37
mapping.
38
39
40 NOTE 5—A non-AP MLD ignores links that are included in the link mappings of an advertised TID-to-link mapping
41 that are not part of the non-AP MLD multi-link setup procedure. For example, if the AP MLD operates on links 1, 2, and
42 3, and it advertises that link 3 is disabled and all TIDs are mapped to links 1 and 2, then for a non-AP MLD that is
43 associated with the AP MLD using links 1 and 2 the default mapping will apply. In this case, for a non-AP MLD that is
44 associated with the AP MLD using links 1 and 3, link 3 will be disabled.
45
46 NOTE 6—In absence of an advertised mapping by the AP a default TID-to-link mapping is assumed unless an
47 individual TID-to-link mapping is successfully negotiated.
48
49 NOTE 7—No TID-To-Link Mapping Request nor TID-To-Link Mapping Response frames are transmitted by non-AP
50 STAs affiliated with the associated non-AP MLDs in response to an advertised TID-to-link mapping.
51
52 A non-AP MLD that is associated with an AP MLD that advertises a TID-to-link mapping may initiate a
53
negotiation for a TID-to-link mapping that is different from the TID-to-link mapping established from the
54
advertisement as described in this subclause. Any MLD shall not initiate a negotiation for a TID-to-link
55
56 mapping that maps a TID to a link if the requested TID is not already mapped to the link in the advertised
57 TID-to-link mapping.
58
59
Figure 35-15 (Example TID-to-link mapping frame exchange) shows an example sequence of TID-to-link
60
mapping frame exchanges. The non-AP MLD operates in default mapping mode in the beginning of the
61
62 sequence. The non-AP MLD then initiates a negotiation of a TID-to-link mapping A. The AP MLD accepts
63 the request, after which TID-to-link mapping A is active for the non-AP MLD. Next the AP MLD starts to
64 advertise a TID-to-link mapping B. At the time indicated by the Mapping Switch field of the advertised

3

5

8 B ends (by expected duration reaching 0). At this point the non-AP MLD also reverts to a default mapping.
9
Note that the ending of the former advertised TID-to-link mapping is treated as an advertisement of a new
10

32 35.3.7.1.8 Association procedures for TID-to-link mapping
33
34 During a multi-link (re)setup procedure, a non-AP MLD may initiate a TID-to-link mapping negotiation by
35
including the TID-to-link Mapping element in the (Re)Association Request frame if an AP MLD has
36
indicated a support of TID-to-link mapping negotiation.
37
38
39 An MLD that includes two TID-To-Link Mapping elements in a (Re)Association Request frame or a
40 (Re)Association Response frame shall set the Direction subfield in one of the TID-To-Link Mapping
41
elements to 0 and the Direction subfield in the other TID-To- Link Mapping element to 1.
42
43
44 After receiving the (Re)Association Request frame, the AP MLD shall reply to the (Re)Association Request
45 frame according to 11.3.5.3 (Authentication—destination STA or MLD), 11.3.5.5 (Deauthentication—
46 destination STA or MLD), and 35.3.5 (Multi-link (re)setup), and perform the following TID-to-link
47
mapping negotiation procedure:
48
49 — Where the AP MLD advertises a TID-to-link mapping that is already established according to
50 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames), if the non-AP
51
MLD does not include at least one TID-to-link Mapping Request element or requests a mapping that
52
maps TIDs to a link in a direction that is not enabled in the advertised mapping, the AP shall include
53
54 in the (Re)Association Response frame a TID-To-Link Mapping element with the Mapping Switch
55 Time Present subfield equal to 0 and indicating the TID-to-link mapping that is advertised in
56 Beacons for each of the links accepted in the association procedure. After the transmission of the
57
(Re)Association Response frame the TID-to-link mapping included in that frame is established and
58
shall be used during the association unless and until a new TID-to-link mapping is advertised or
59
60 negotiated.
61 — Otherwise, if the AP MLD does not accept an individually requested TID-to-link mapping in an
62
Association Request frame, the AP MLD shall indicate rejection of the proposed TID-to-link
63
mapping by including in the (Re)Association Response frame the TID-to-link Mapping element that
64
65

1 suggests a preferred TID-to-link mapping, and the default TID-to-link mapping remains established

19 35.3.7.2 Dynamic link transitions
20
21
A non-AP MLD may use the power states of its affiliated non-AP STAs (see 35.3.12 (Multi-link power
22
23 management)) to dynamically change the link(s) on which it operates. Figure 35-16 (Example of link
24 transition operation by a single radio non-AP MLD using power states) provides an illustration of operation
25 of a single radio non-AP MLD with default mapping (all TIDs mapped to all setup links), where the non-AP
26
MLD transitions from operating on link 1 with non-AP STA 1 to operating on link 2 with non-AP STA 2,
27
where both non-AP STA 1 and non-AP STA 2 are affiliated with the non-AP MLD.
28
29
30 AP(cid:3)MLD PPDU(cid:3)transmission(cid:3)carrying(cid:3)BUs(cid:3)from(cid:3) Non(cid:882)AP(cid:3)MLD

45
46 In this example, while operating on link 1:
47
48 — non-AP STA 1 affiliated with the non-AP MLD might use active mode or power save mode with the
49 awake state to retrieve BUs from the AP MLD or to send frames to the AP MLD and might use doze
50 state when there is no buffered BUs or no frames to send to the AP MLD.
51
— non-AP STA 2 and non-AP STA 3 stay in doze state.
52
53
54 In this example, while operating on link 2:
55
— non-AP STA 2 affiliated with the non-AP MLD might use active mode or power save mode with the
56
57 awake state to retrieve BUs from the AP MLD or to send frames to the AP MLD and might transition
58 to doze state when there is no buffered BUs or no frames to send to the AP MLD.
59
— non-AP STA 1 and non-AP STA 3 stay in doze state.
60
61
62 The link transition from link 1 to link 2 is achieved in this example by having non-AP STA 1 transition to
63 doze state and non-AP STA 2 transition to active mode or awake state.

## 第 523 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.7.3 Affiliated AP link disablement and enablement
2
3
35.3.7.3.1 General
4
5
6 An AP MLD shall use the procedures described in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon
7 and Probe Response frames) in order to disable or enable a link for all associated non-AP MLDs. Further
8 rules pertaining to the disablement and enablement are described in the subclauses below: 35.3.7.3.2
9
(Affiliated AP link disablement) and 35.3.7.3.3 (Affiliated AP link enablement).
10
11
12 35.3.7.3.2 Affiliated AP link disablement
13
14 Upon receiving an MLME-BSS-LINK-DISABLE.request primitive, the affiliated AP shall advertise a TID-
15
to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response frames that does not map any TIDs to the disabled link on
16
which the AP is operating. The Mapping Switch Time field for the advertised TID-to-link mapping shall
17
18 point to the same time as indicated in the DisableTimer parameter of the MLME-BSS-LINK-
19 DISABLE.request primitive. The Expected Duration field of the advertised TID-to-link mapping shall
20 indicate the same duration as the ExpectedDuration parameter of the MLME-BSS-LINK-DISABLE.request
21
primitive.
22
23
24 Additionally, in order to advertise the link disablement, the SME of that affiliated AP shall perform the
25 following:
26
1) It shall follow the procedure in 11.21.7.3 (BSS transition management request) to notify all associ-
27
ated STAs that support BTM, with the BSS Transition Management Request frame fields set as fol-
28
29 lows:
30
• The Disassociation Imminent and Link Removal Imminent subfields of the Request Mode field
31
are set to 1, the BSS Termination Included subfield is set to 0, and other subfields of the Request
32
33 Mode field are reserved.
34 • The Disassociation Timer field is set to the number of TBTTs of the affiliated AP before it trans-
35 mits a Disassociation frame to the STA(s) receiving the BSS Transition Management Request
36
frame. The Disassociation Timer field value shall point to a TBTT at or later than the time
37
pointed to by the value of the Mapping Switch Time field for the advertised TID-to-link map-
38
39 ping.
40 • No other optional fields shall be present in the BSS Transition Management Request frame.
41
2) It shall start a disassociation timer with the initial value set to the value of the Disassociation Timer
42
field, and shall decrement the timer by one after transmitting each Beacon frame, until the timer has
43
44 the value of 0. The Disassociation Timer field in all subsequent transmitted BSS Transition Manage-
45 ment Request frames shall be set to the value of this timer.
46
3) Once the disassociation timer reaches a value of 0, it should follow the procedure in 11.3.6.8 (AP,
47
48 AP MLD, or PCP disassociation initiation procedure) to transmit Disassociation frames to all associ-
49 ated STAs that are not affiliated with a non-AP MLD. The affiliated AP shall not transmit Disassoci-
50 ation frames until the disassociation timer has a value of 0.
51
52
When an AP MLD advertises that a link is disabled for all associated non-AP MLDs, after the time indicated
53
54 by the Mapping Switch Time field is reached:
55 — the Disabled Link Indication subfield shall be set to 1 in the MLD Parameters subfield corresponding
56
to the AP affiliated with the AP MLD and operating on the link thati s included in the Neighbor AP
57
Information field in the Reduced Neighbor Report element carried in the Beacon or Probe Response
58
59 frames transmitted by any of the APs affiliated with the AP MLD and any APs that set the Co-
60 Located AP bit of the BSS Parameters subfield of the TBTT Information field of the Neighbor AP
61 Information field to 1 for the disabled AP. If the Disabled AP Link Indication subfield corresponding
62
to a reported AP is set to 1, then the Neighbor AP TBTT Offset subfield included in the same TBTT
63
Information field of the Reduced Neighbor Report element shall be set to 255.

22

31 35.3.7.3.3 Affiliated AP link enablement
32
33
Upon receiving the MLME-BSS-LINK-ENABLE.request primitive and after the time indicated by the
34
35 EnableTimer parameter in the primitive, or after the expiry of the expected duration advertised in an existing
36 TID-to-link mapping, an affiliated AP shall advertise a TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe Response
37 frames that maps at least one TID to the link on which the AP is operating or stop advertising the TID-to-
38
link mapping that indicated no TIDs mapped to the enabled link. If a new nondefault TID-to-link mapping is
39
advertised to replace the existing one, in the frames advertising the new TID-to-link-mapping, the Mapping
40
41 Switch Time field shall indicate the same time as the Expected Duration field of the currently advertised
42 TID-to-link mapping.
43
44
When an AP MLD stops advertising that a link is disabled for all associated non-AP MLDs:
45
46 — the Disabled AP Link Indication subfield shall be set to 0 in the MLD Parameters subfield
47 corresponding to the AP affiliated with the AP MLD and operating on the link that is included in the
48
Neighbor AP Information field in the Reduced Neighbor Report element carried in the Beacon or
49
Probe Response frames transmitted by any of the APs affiliated with the AP MLD
50
51 — the AP operating on this link shall retain unchanged GTK/IGTK/BIGTK keys as before this link was
52 advertised as disabled for all associated non-AP MLDs
53
54 — the AP operating on this link shall transmit a TSF that is equivalent to the expected TSF if this link
55 was never advertised as disabled
56
— frame exchange operation on this link between the affiliated AP and non-AP STAs affiliated with the
57
associated non-AP MLDs that include the enabled link in their multi-link setup can be initiated by
58
59 any member of the BSS that is affiliated with an MLD following CCA performance until a frame is
60 detected by which it can set its NAV, or a period equal to NAVSyncDelay has transpired, whichever
61 is earlier. The frame exchange is initiated using all the link parameters that were defined before the
62
link has been defined as an unavailable link subject to power state (see 35.3.12 (Multi-link power
63

35.3.8 Block ack procedures in Multi-link operation
13
14
15 An MLD shall follow the mechanisms defined in 11.5 (Block ack operation) and 35.4 (EHT
16 acknowledgment procedure) with additional rules as defined in this subclause for performing block ack
17 operation.
18
19
For each TID, there shall not be more than one block ack agreement established between two MLDs and the
20
21 agreement shall apply to all the links to which the TID is mapped to (i.e., there are no independent block ack
22 agreements for each TID on a per-link basis).
23
24
In this subclause, the MLD with data to send using the block ack mechanism is referred to as the originator
25
MLD, and the MLD that is the intended recipient of that data is referred to as the recipient MLD.
26
27
28 To setup a block ack agreement between two MLDs, an originator MLD shall send an ADDBA Request
29 frame through an affiliated STA to the recipient MLD, on any enabled link, indicating the TID for which the
30
block ack agreement is being set up. Upon receiving an ADDBA Request frame, the recipient MLD shall
31
respond through an affiliated STA, on any enabled link, with an ADDBA Response frame subject to the
32
33 power states of the STAs operating on the link. The recipient MLD has the option of accepting or rejecting
34 the request. If the recipient MLD accepts the request, then a block ack agreement is established between the
35 originator MLD and the recipient MLD for the TID specified in the ADDBA frames as defined in
36
10.25.2 (Setup and modification of the block ack parameters).
37
38 NOTE 1—An originator MLD can attempt a retransmission of an ADDBA Request frame on any enabled link. A
39 recipient MLD can attempt a retransmission of an ADDBA Response frame on any enabled link.
40
41
If an MLD has established a block ack agreement with another MLD, then QoS Data frames for the TID
42
associated with the block ack agreement may be exchanged between the two MLDs on any link to which the
43
44 TID is mapped by following the procedure described in 35.3.7.1 (TID-to-link mapping) and 35.3.12 (Multi-
45 link power management).
46
47
An originator MLD shall maintain a single transmit buffer control that uses WinStart and WinSize for
48 O O
49 each block ack agreement negotiated with the recipient MLD to submit MPDUs for transmission across
50 links subject to TID-to-Link mapping restriction (see 35.3.7 (Link management)). An originator MLD shall
51 release the transmit buffer associated with a successfully received MPDU upon receiving BlockAck frame
52
containing the reception status for that MPDU.
53
54
55 In a block ack agreement between two MLDs, the buffer size is indicated based on the Buffer Size subfield
56 (of the Block Ack Parameter Set field) together with the Extended Buffer Size field (when ADDBA
57 Extension element is included).
58
59 NOTE 2—The ADDBA Extension element is optionally present in an ADDBA Request or ADDBA Response frame
60 (see 9.6.4 (Block Ack Action frame details)). When block ack agreement is negotiated between two MLDs, if ADDBA
61 Extension element is present, then the total buffer size is computed as described in 9.4.2.139 (ADDBA Extension
62 element).
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 525
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 526 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 During the block ack agreement establishment, the Block Ack Timeout field and buffer size indicated in the
2 ADDBA Request frame are advisory.
3
4
When a block ack agreement is established between two MLDs, the originator may change the size of its
5
6 transmission window (WinSizeO) so that the transmit window meets the following conditions:
7 — Is not greater than the buffer size indicated in the ADDBA Response frame.
8
9 — Is not greater than 1024 if the sender and receiver of the ADDBA Response frame are MLDs.
10
11 If the buffer size indicated in the ADDBA Response frame is smaller than the buffer size indicated in the
12
ADDBA Request frame, the originator shall change the size of its transmission window (WinSizeO) such
13
that:
14
15 — The transmission window is not greater than the buffer size indicated in the ADDBA Response
16 frame.
17
18 — The transmission window is not greater than 1024 if the sender and the receiver of the ADDBA
19 Response frame are MLDs.
20
21
A STA affiliated with a recipient MLD shall provide, to the STA affiliated with the originator MLD that is
22
operating on the same link, the reception status for any MPDU, with ACK policy other than No Ack, that is
23
24 received on the link on which the STA affiliated with the recipient MLD is operating on. When a TID is
25 mapped to more than one link, a STA affiliated with a recipient MLD may provide (if available), to the STA
26 affiliated with the originator MLD that is operating on the same link, reception status indicating successful
27
reception of any MPDU, which belongs to that TID and has an ACK policy other than No Ack, that is
28
received by a STA affiliated with the recipient MLD that is operating on a different link.
29
30
31 An originator MLD shall update the reception status of an MPDU in its transmit buffer corresponding to a
32 block ack agreement if the received status indicates successful reception.
33
34
An originator MLD shall not update the reception status of an MPDU in its transmit buffer corresponding to
35
36 a block ack agreement that has already been acknowledged as successful.
37
38 A recipient MLD shall maintain a single common receive reordering buffer for each <peer MLD, TID>
39
tuple under a block ack agreement, independent of the number of links that are setup. The receive reordering
40
buffer shall be responsible for reordering MSDUs or A-MSDUs so that MSDUs or A-MSDUs are eventually
41
42 passed up to the next MAC process in the order of received sequence number. It shall also be responsible for
43 identifying and discarding duplicate frames (i.e., frames that have the same sequence number as a currently
44 buffered frame) that are part of this block ack agreement. It shall maintain its own state independent of the
45
scoreboard context control to perform this reordering as specified in 10.25.6.6 (Receive reordering buffer
46
control operation). Each received MPDU shall be analyzed by the scoreboard context control as well as by
47
48 the receive reordering buffer control. The bitmap corresponding to each scoreboard context control shall
49 have the same size WinSizeR, which is set to the smaller of bitmap length and buffer size indicated in the
50 ADDBA Response. For each <peer MLD, TID> tuple under a block ack agreement, a recipient MLD shall
51
have one of the following modes of operation:
52
53 — maintain an independent scoreboard context control and partial state operation at each STA affiliated
54 with the MLD,
55
— have a common (single) scoreboard context control maintained by the MLD with partial state
56
57 operation on each setup link,
58 — have a common (single) scoreboard context control maintained by the MLD with full state operation
59
on each setup link.
60
61
NOTE 3—A peer MLD is identified based on its MLD MAC address.
62
63
If a recipient MLD has independent scoreboard context control at an affiliated STA (i.e., the STA is not able
64
to be in sync with the information at the common reordering buffer or the information at another STA
65
526 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.

Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 affiliated of the same MLD to update its local scoreboard context), then the affiliated STA shall implement
2 partial state operation and should discard the temporary record after the end of the current TXOP.
3
4 NOTE 4—If a recipient MLD has independent scoreboard context control at an affiliated STA (STA 1), then STA 1’s
5 WinStart might not be within 211 of the WinStart at another affiliated STA (STA 2) of the same MLD. As a result,
R R
6 STA 1 can fail to accurately update the scoreboard context and hence, might provide an incorrect reception status for an
7 MPDU received in subsequent TXOP. Therefore, it is recommended that STA 1 discards its temporary record in a timely
8 manner. If the affiliated STA can be in sync with the latest information at another STA affiliated with the same MLD,
9 then it does not have to discard the temporary record at the end of the current TXOP.
10
11 If two MLDs have successfully negotiated a protected block ack agreement, they shall follow the procedure
12
described in 10.25.7 (Protected block ack agreement). In a protected block ack agreement between two
13
MLDs, the originator MLD shall transmit a robust ADDBA Request frame, via its affiliated STA that is
14
15 operating on an enabled link to which the TID belonging to the block ack agreement is mapped, to advance
16 the WinStart and WinStart at the recipient MLD.

35.3.9 Fragmentation in multi-link operation
19
20
21 A STA affiliated with an MLD shall not use the nondynamic fragmentation procedure described in
22 10.4 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and MMPDU fragmentation).

35.3.10 BSS parameter critical update procedure
25
26
27 If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is not in a multiple BSSID set or corresponds to a transmitted BSSID in
28 a multiple BSSID set, the AP shall
29
— include in Beacon and Probe Response frames it transmits a BSS Parameters Change Count subfield
30
31 for each of all APs affiliated with the same AP MLD as the AP; include in a (Re)Association
32 Response frame it transmits a BSS Parameters Change Count subfield for each of all APs that are
33 requested for (re)setup in the received (Re)Association Request frame.
34
35 • The BSS Parameters Change Count subfield value for each AP is initialized to 0, and shall be
36 incremented (modulo 256 excluding the value 255) by 1 when a critical update occurs to the BSS
37 parameters of that AP as defined in 11.2.3.15 (TIM Broadcast).
38
• In Beacon and Probe Response frames, the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield for each of
39
the other AP(s) affiliated with the AP MLD shall be carried in the MLD Parameters subfield in
40
41 the TBTT Information field of the Reduced Neighbor Report element corresponding to that AP
42 where each of the other AP(s) is identified by the Link ID subfield of the MLD Parameters sub-
43 field.
44
• In the (Re)Association Response frame, the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield for each of
45
the other AP(s) affiliated with the AP MLD shall be carried in the STA Info subfield in the Per-
46
47 STA Profile subelement of Basic Multi-Link element corresponding to that AP where each of the
48 other AP(s) is identified by the Link ID subfield of the STA Control field of the Per-STA Profile
49 subelement.
50
• The BSS Parameters Change Count subfield for the AP shall be carried in the Common Info field
51
of the Basic Multi-Link element where the AP is identified by the Link ID subfield of the Com-
52
53 mon Info field.
54 — set the Critical Update Flag subfield of the Capability Information field to 1 in Beacon and Probe
55
Response frames until and including the next DTIM Beacon frame on the link on which the AP is
56
operating if there is a change to a value carried in the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield of the
57
58 MLD Parameters field in the Reduced Neighbor Report element for any AP affiliated with the same
59 AP MLD as the AP or a value carried in the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield in the Common
60 Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element. Otherwise set the Critical Update Flag subfield of the
61
Capability Information field to 0.
62
63 — For each reported AP affiliated with the same AP MLD as the AP, set the All Updates Included
64 subfield to 1 in the MLD Parameters subfield in the TBTT Information field of the Reduced
65

element in a multi-link probe response.

41 When a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD receives a BSS Parameter Change Count subfield for a

45

48

51
52

53

1 operating on nonprimary link shall only be advertised on the primary link in the MLD Parameters subfield in
2 the TBTT Information field of the Reduced Neighbor Report element corresponding to that AP.

4
Figure 35-17 (An example of critical update operation) illustrates two APs affiliated with the same AP
5
6 MLD. AP1 and AP2 affiliated with the AP MLD operate on Link 1 and Link 2, respectively. The figure
7 shows the values carried in the Critical Update Flag subfield, the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield,
8 and the All Updates Included subfield corresponding to AP1 in the Beacon frames transmitted by AP2 when
9
critical updates occur in AP1’s BSS. In the illustration, the value of the BSS Parameters Change Count
10
subfield for AP1 is equal to 5 in Beacon 21 and the Critical Update Flag and All Updates Included
11
12 (corresponding to AP1) subfields are set to 0. First, a critical update that does not correspond to an element
13 listed in 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel
14 quieting) is announced by AP1 in Beacon 12, which causes the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield (for
15
AP1) to increment by one (to 6) in Beacon 22. Also, in Beacon 22 AP2 sets the Critical Update Flag subfield
16
to 1. The All Updates Included subfield (corresponding to AP1) is set to 0 since the element corresponding
17
18 to the latest critical update is not included in Beacon 22. Next, in Beacon 13, AP1 includes a Quiet element
19 to advertise a quiet interval, which results in the BSS Parameters Change Count subfield (for AP1) to be
20 incremented by one (to 7). Since this critical update corresponds to an element listed in 35.3.11 (Multi-link
21
procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting), AP2 includes the
22
Quiet element in the per-STA profile corresponding to AP1 in Beacon 23 and sets the Critical Update Flag
23
24 and All Updates Included (corresponding to AP1) subfields to 1 and 1, respectively. The Critical Update
25 Flag subfield is set to 1 until the next DTIM Beacon of AP2 (i.e., until Beacon 25). The All Updates
26 Included subfield corresponding to AP1 is set to 1 in Beacons 24, 25, and 26 since these Beacon frames
27
include the element corresponding to the last critical update. Finally, a critical update, not corresponding to
28
elements listed in 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and
29
30 channel quieting) is announced by AP1 in Beacon_17, which causes AP1’s BSS Parameters Change Count
31 subfield to increment by one (to 8). Although the Quiet element is still included in the per-STA profile
32 corresponding to AP1 in Beacons 27 and 28, the element corresponding to the latest critical update is not

35.3.11 Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and
42
channel quieting
43
44
45 In this subclause, the term affected AP is used to identify an AP that is subject to channel switching,
46 extended channel switching, and channel quieting among all the APs that are affiliated with an AP MLD.
47
48
If an AP (affected AP) affiliated with an AP MLD includes any of the following applicable elements outside
49
50 the Basic Multi-Link element in the Beacon frame, Probe Response frame or (Extended) Channel Switch
51 Announcement frame it transmits:
52
— Channel Switch Announcement element
53
54 — Extended Channel Switch Announcement element
55
— Max Channel Switch Time element
56
57 — Quiet element corresponding to quiet intervals other than quiet intervals scheduled to protect R-TWT
58 SPs (see 35.8.5.2 (Quieting STAs during R-TWT SPs))
59
— Quiet Channel element
60
61
62 then one of the following shall apply if other APs are affiliated with the same AP MLD as the affected AP:
63
— Another AP (reporting AP) affiliated with the same AP MLD and not corresponding to a
64
65 nontransmitted BSSID shall carry the corresponding element(s) in the STA Profile field of the Per-

4

8
nontransmitted BSSID profile corresponding to the reporting AP in the Multiple BSSID element

— The timing fields in the Channel Switch Announcement element, the Extended Channel Switch
14
15 Announcement element, the Quiet element, and the Quiet Channel element shall be applied in
16 reference to the most recent TBTT and Beacon Interval indicated in the corresponding element(s) of
17 the affected AP and not to the TBTT and Beacon Interval of the reporting AP.
18
19 NOTE 1—The affected AP can correspond to a transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set or an AP with
20 dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to false. The case where the affected AP corresponds to nontransmitted BSSID in
21 a multiple BSSID set is covered in the next paragraph.
22
23 NOTE 2—The Switch Time field in the Max Channel Switch Time element carried in the per-STA profile of the
24 reported AP is not tied to a TBTT on the affected link. Instead, it provides an estimated time when the first Beacon frame
25 will be transmitted on the new channel of the affected link after the channel switch has occurred.
26
27
NOTE 3—For the Beacon and Probe Response frames all five elements are applicable. For the (Extended) Channel
28
Switch Announcement frame, the applicable elements include the Channel Switch Announcement, Extended Channel
29
Switch Announcement, and Max Channel Switch Time elements.
30
31

32
33 elements in the Beacon frame or Probe Response frame it transmits so that any of these elements is inherited
34 for the affected AP in these frames:
35
— Channel Switch Announcement element
36
37 — Extended Channel Switch Announcement element
38
— Max Channel Switch Time element
39
40 — Quiet element corresponding to quiet intervals other than quiet intervals scheduled to protect R-TWT
41 SPs (see 35.8.5.2 (Quieting STAs during R-TWT SPs))
42

47

49 nontransmitted BSSID shall carry the corresponding element(s) in the STA Profile field of the Per-
50 STA Profile subelement corresponding to the affected AP contained in the Basic Multi-Link element
51
included in a Beacon frame and Probe Response frame that it transmits.
52

56

57

58
