1 reference to the most recent TBTT and Beacon Interval included in the corresponding element(s) of
2 the affected AP and not with respect to the TBTT and Beacon Interval of the reporting AP.
3
4 NOTE 4—The Switch Time field in the Max Channel Switch Time element is not tied to a TBTT on the affected link.
5 Instead, it provides an estimated time when the first Beacon frame will be transmitted on the affected link after the
6 channel switch has occurred.
7
8 If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is switching channel, the Channel Switch Announcement element, or
9
the Extended Channel Switch Announcement element with the Channel Switch Count field of the
10
(Extended) Channel Switch Announcement element set to a nonzero value, and the Max Channel Switch
11
12 Time element shall be included in every Beacon and Probe Response frames on all links of the AP MLD
13 from right after the time the AP includes the elements in the Beacon frame it transmits until the estimated
14 channel switch time. After the estimated channel switch time, the Channel Switch Announcement element
15
and the Extended Channel Switch Announcement element shall not be included in the per-STA profile of the
16
affected AP in the Beacon and Probe Response frames and the Max Channel Switch Time element shall be
17
18 included in the per-STA profile of the affected AP in every Beacon and Probe Response frames on all links
19 of the AP MLD until the affected AP resumes BSS operation on the new channel. The value carried in the
20 Switch Time field indicates the adjusted estimated time of the first Beacon frame in the new channel.
21
22 NOTE 5—The reporting AP might increase the value carried in the Switch Time field of the Max Channel Switch Time
23 element if the affected AP performs a second channel switch between the target time of a first channel switch and the
24 time at which the affected AP will start beaconing on the new channel corresponding to the first channel switch.
25
26 When an AP (affected AP) affiliated with an MLD is switching from an initial operating class/channel to a
27
target operating class/channel at a target switch time using channel switch announcement procedure or
28
extended channel switch announcement procedure, and if another AP is affiliated with the same AP MLD as
29
30 the affected AP, then:
31 — the other AP (reporting AP) affiliated with the AP MLD shall set the Operating Class and Channel
32
Number fields corresponding to the affected AP that is reported in the Reduced Neighbor Report
33
element in Beacon and Probe Response frames it transmits (or that the transmitted BSSID in the
34
35 same multiple BSSID set as the reporting AP transmits if the reporting AP corresponds to a
36 nontransmitted BSSID) to the initial operating class/channel before the target switch time,
37
— the other AP (reporting AP) affiliated with the AP MLD shall set the Operating Class and Channel
38
39 Number fields corresponding to the affected AP that is reported in the Reduced Neighbor Report
40 element in Beacon and Probe Response frames it transmits (or that the transmitted BSSID in the
41 same multiple BSSID set as the reporting AP transmits if the reporting AP corresponds to a
42
nontransmitted BSSID) to the target operating class/channel at and after the target switch time.
43
44 — Between the target switch time and the time at which the AP will start beaconing in the target
45 operating class/channel, the Neighbor AP TBTT Offset subfield for the corresponding AP in the
46 Reduced Neighbor Report element shall be set to 255.
47
48
If an AP (affected/reported AP) of an AP MLD is switching from an initial operating class/channel to a
49
50 target operating class/channel at a target switch time using channel switch announcement or extended
51 channel switch announcement procedure and includes a Max Channel Switch Time element in the Beacon
52 and Probe Response frames it sends, and another AP (reporting AP) affiliated with the AP MLD, if any,
53
receives a (Re)Association Request frame to perform multi-link setup with the AP MLD with the AP
54
(affected/reported AP) as a requested link, then the other AP (reporting AP) shall include the complete
55
56 profile for the AP indicating the target operating class/channel and a Max Channel Switch Time element in
57 the per-STA profile corresponding to the AP (affected/reported AP) in the Basic Multi-Link element
58 included in the (Re)Association Response frame it sends in response to indicate the time at which the AP
59
(affected/reported AP) will start beaconing, if the (Re)Association Response frame is sent between the last
60
beacon on the initial operating class/channel and the first beacon on the target operating class/channel.
61
62 Otherwise, the other AP (reporting AP), if any, should not include a Max Channel Switch Time element or
63 (Extended) Channel Switch Announcement element in (Re)Association Response frames.

1 When an AP (affected/reported AP) of an AP MLD has announced quiet intervals other than quiet intervals
2 scheduled to protect R-TWT SPs (see 35.8.5.2 (Quieting STAs during R-TWT SPs)) using Quiet element
3
and optionally Quiet Channel element, and another AP (reporting AP) of the same AP MLD, if any, receives
4
a (Re)Association Request frame to perform multi-link setup with the AP MLD with the AP (affected/
5
6 reported AP) as a requested link, then the other AP (reporting AP), if any, shall include the corresponding
7 Quiet element and Quiet Channel element (if present) in the per-STA profile corresponding to the AP
8 (affected/reported AP) in the Basic Multi-Link element included in the (Re)Association Response frame it
9
sends in response. Otherwise, the other AP (reporting AP) should not include a Quiet element and Quiet
10
Channel element in (Re)Association Response frames.
11
12
13 For the example shown in Figure 35-18 (Example of an AP carrying a Quiet element to signal channel
14 quieting on another link), AP 1 and AP 2 are two APs affiliated with an AP MLD that operate on Link 1 and
15
Link 2, respectively. The Beacon frame transmitted by AP 1 (the affected AP) includes a Quiet element to
16
indicate a scheduled quiet interval on Link 1. From this point onward and until the quiet interval begins on
17
18 Link 1, AP 2 (the reporting AP) includes a Quiet element in the Per-STA Profile subelement corresponding
19 to AP 1 in the Basic Multi-Link element carried in its Beacon frames. Although not shown in the figure,
20 Quiet element will also be included in the Per-STA Profile subelement of the Basic Multi-Link element
21
corresponding to AP 1 carried in the Probe Response frames transmitted by AP 2. The values of the Quiet
22
Count field, Quiet Offset field, and the Quiet Duration field of the Quiet element carried on Link 2 are set by
23
24 AP 2 with reference to Link 1. As the value of the Beacon Interval for AP 2 is greater than the value of
25 beacon interval for AP 1, the Quiet Count field of the Quiet element carried in the Per-STA Profile
26 subelement corresponding to AP 1 is decremented at a faster rate (i.e., 2 in this example) in every
27
subsequent beacon transmitted by AP 2. A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD, which is capable of
28
operating on Link 2, transmits a (Re-)Association Request frame to AP 2 (not shown in the figure), in order
29
30 to perform multi-link setup. The multi-link setup includes Link 1 as one of the links. Since the
31 (Re)Association Response frame is transmitted by AP 2 after the quiet interval has started on Link 1, AP 2
32 includes the Quiet element in the per-STA profile corresponding to AP 1 in the (Re)Association Response
33
frame it transmits. The Quiet Count field of the Quiet element carried in the (Re)Association Response
34
frame is set to 128 to indicate that the quiet interval on Link 1 started in the beacon interval that occurred
35
36 one TBTT in the past on Link 1 (see 9.4.2.22 (Quiet element)).

26 Channel Switch Time element provides an estimate of time until the first TBTT on the new channel on
27
Link 1. The non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD operating on Link 1 does not transmit a frame
28
until it hears the first Beacon frame from AP 1 on Link 1.

55

29 links
30
31 NOTE 6—Advertising the pertinent element(s) for a duration that includes the DTIM Beacon frame on a link makes it
32 possible for a non-AP MLD that is monitoring only another link, if any, and is in doze state to wakeup only to receive the
33 DTIM beacon on that link to get the notification (by receiving the element(s) in the per-STA profile, corresponding to
34 the affected AP, of the Basic Multi-Link element).
35
36 NOTE 7—When the other AP affiliated with the same AP MLD corresponds to a nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple
37 BSSID set and the transmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set operates as an EMA AP, then the profile for a BSS
38 corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID is expected to appear in the DTIM beacon for that BSSID (as described in
39 11.1.3.8.3 (Discovery of a nontransmitted BSSID profile)). With this mechanism, a non-AP STA, that is associated with
40 an AP corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID, can receive the profile (and any updates carried within the profile) in
41 a DTIM Beacon frame without having to wake up for additional beacons thus conserving power in the process.
42
43
35.3.12 Multi-link power management
44
45
46 35.3.12.1 General
47
48 Each non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that is operating on an enabled link shall maintain its own
49
power management mode and power states as defined in 11.2 (Power management) and 10.47 (Target wake
50
time (TWT)). Frame exchanges on an enabled link are possible when the non-AP STA affiliated with the
51
52 non-AP MLD operating on that link is in the awake state (see 11.2.3 (Power management in a non-DMG
53 infrastructure network)).
54
55
Figure 35-21 (Each non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD maintains its own power state) illustrates
56
the power save operation for each non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD during multi-link operation.
57
58 The example assumes all TIDs are mapped to all or a subset of links. As depicted in the figure, during the
59 initial portion of the illustration, both non-AP STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD are in active mode and
60 are involved in frame exchange with the respective APs on the links. Each non-AP STA affiliated with the
61
non-AP MLD indicates that it is in active mode by setting to 0 the Power Management subfield (namely PM
62
bit in the figure) in the Frame Control field of a transmitted frame. At some point in time, non-AP STA 2
63
64 affiliated with the non-AP MLD operating on Link 2 indicates to AP 2 that it is entering power save mode
65
536 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 537 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 (i.e., sets PM bit to 1) and transitions to doze state after the successful frame exchange. Non-AP STA 2
2 remains in doze state for the rest of the illustration. After a period of time, non-AP STA 1 enters power save
3
mode (i.e., sets PM bit to 1) after the successful frame exchange. While operating in power save mode, non-
4
AP STA 1 wakes up to receive the Beacon frame transmitted by AP 1 and determines that AP MLD has BUs
5
6 for the non-AP MLD. Based on this determination, non-AP STA 1 indicates to AP 1 that it has transitioned
7 to awake state by transmitting a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame on Link 1. Non-AP STA 1 participates in
8 frame exchange with AP 1 while in awake state.
9

34
35
36 35.3.12.2 Basic BSS operation
37
38
A non-AP MLD shall be able to perform basic operations (such as receiving a traffic indication, time
39
40 synchronization, receiving BSS parameter updates) about the AP MLD and all the APs affiliated with the
41 AP MLD on one or more link(s). This is accomplished in addition to other power save mechanisms (such as
42 individual TWT agreement or U-APSD), if setup, between the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD
43
and the corresponding AP affiliated with the AP MLD with which the non-AP MLD has performed
44
association. With these mechanisms, a non-AP MLD can receive basic information about the AP MLD and
45
46 all the APs affiliated with the AP MLD on a single link while the other non-AP STA(s) affiliated with the
47 non-AP MLD are in doze state.
48
49 NOTE—Each AP affiliated with an AP MLD provides a critical updates indication when there is an update to the BSS
50 parameters for another AP affiliated with the AP MLD (see 35.3.10 (BSS parameter critical update procedure)).
51
52 All non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall set each of the ACs U-APSD Flag subfields in the
53 QoS Info field to the same value across all setup links (see 35.3.5 (Multi-link (re)setup)).
54
55
35.3.12.3 MLD max idle period management
56
57
58 During multi-link setup, if the AP affiliated with an AP MLD includes a BSS Max Idle Period element in the
59 (Re)Association Response frame sent to a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD, then the value
60
carried in the Max Idle Period field is applied at the MLD level. When the max idle period value in the BSS
61
Max Idle Period element is applied at the MLD level, it is referred to as the MLD max idle period. The AP
62
63 MLD shall use this timeout value for making disassociation decisions for the non-AP MLD to which the
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 537
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 538 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 (Re)Association Response frame is sent. An AP MLD may provide different BSS Max Idle Period values
2 for different non-AP MLDs.
3
4
The non-AP MLD shall send, to its associated AP MLD, at least one keepalive frame (such as Data frame,
5
6 PS-Poll frame, or Management frame), via one of its affiliated non-AP STAs, per BSS Max Idle Period if the
7 non-AP MLD intends to avoid getting disassociated from the AP MLD due to nonreceipt of frames. A
8 keepalive frame shall be protected or unprotected as indicated in the Idle Options subfield.
9
10
A non-AP MLD is considered inactive by its associated AP MLD if the AP MLD has not received a Data
11
12 frame, a PS-Poll frame, or a Management frame (protected or unprotected as specified in this paragraph) or
13 a frame exchange sequence initiated by any of the STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD on any enabled
14 link for a time period greater than or equal to the time specified by the Max Idle Period subfield of the BSS
15
Max Idle Period element. An AP MLD may disassociate a non-AP MLD if:
16
17 — the Idle Options subfield of the BSS Max Idle Period element requires protected keepalive frames
18 and no protected frames are received from any non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD for a
19 duration of the BSS Max Idle Period.
20
21 — the Idle Options subfield allows unprotected or protected keepalive frames and no protected or
22 unprotected frames are received from any non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD for a
23 duration of the BSS Max Idle Period.
24
25 NOTE—The AP MLD can disassociate or deauthenticate the non-AP MLD at any time for other reasons even if the
26 non-AP MLD satisfies the keepalive frame transmission requirements.
27
28 35.3.12.4 Traffic indication
29
30
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD where the AP is not a member of a multiple BSSID set shall indicate
31
32 pending buffered traffic for a non-AP MLD associated with that AP MLD using the partial virtual bitmap of the
33 TIM element as described in 9.4.2.5 (TIM element) and by following the rules described in this subclause.
34
35
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD where the AP corresponds to a transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set
36
shall indicate pending buffered traffic for a non-AP MLD associated with any AP MLD that has an affiliated
37
38 AP in the same multiple BSSID set as the AP using the partial virtual bitmap of the TIM element as described
39 in 9.4.2.5 (TIM element), 11.1.3.8.5 (Traffic advertisement in a multiple BSSID set), and by following the rules
40 described in this subclause.
41
42
An AP MLD may use Multi-Link Traffic Indication element and TIM element carried in a Beacon frame to
43
44 recommend a non-AP MLD to use one or more enabled links to retrieve individually addressed buffered
45 BU(s). An AP MLD may also use Multi-Link Traffic Indication element and AID Bitmap element in a Link
46 Recommendation frame to recommend a non-AP MLD to use one or more enabled links for all exchanges
47
both for DL and UL. The AP’s indication may be carried in a broadcast or a unicast frame.
48
49
50 An AP MLD shall buffer a BU with a TID at the AP MLD if the TID is not mapped to any link on which the
51 corresponding non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD is in active mode, and it shall set the bit in the
52 partial virtual bitmap of the TIM element that corresponds to the AID of the non-AP MLD to 1. The traffic
53
indication for a non-AP MLD that is indicated by the bit in the partial virtual bitmap of the TIM element in a
54
Beacon frame that matches the AID of the non-AP MLD shall be set to a value that reflects the buffer status
55
56 of the BUs at the AP MLD for that non-AP MLD when each AP affiliated with the AP MLD that is
57 operating on a corresponding link that is part of the multi-link setup schedules the Beacon frame.
58
59
An AP MLD shall buffer an MMPDU (see Table 11-3 (Bufferable/nonbufferable classification of
60
MMPDUs)) and intended for receipt by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD in the AP MLD when
61
62 all non-AP STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD are in power save mode. In this case, the bit in the partial
63 virtual bitmap of the TIM element that corresponds to the AID of the non-AP MLD shall be set to 1. An AP
64 MLD shall not buffer a TPC Request frame or a Link Measurement Request frame.
65

1 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall include the Multi-Link Traffic Indication element (see 9.4.2.315
2 (Multi-Link Traffic Indication element)) in a Beacon frame it transmits if at least one of the associated non-
3
AP MLD has successfully negotiated a TID-to-link mapping (see 35.3.7.1.3 (Negotiation of TID-to-link
4
mapping)) with the AP MLD for DL or bidirectional traffic and not all TIDs are mapped to all enabled links
5
6 and the AP MLD has buffered BU(s) with TID(s) that are not mapped to all the enabled links for the non-AP
7 MLD(s). The Multi-Link Traffic Indication element includes Per-Link Traffic Indication Bitmap subfield(s)
8 in the Per-Link Traffic Indication Bitmap List field. The Per-Link Traffic Indication Bitmap subfield(s)
9
corresponds to the AID(s) of the non-AP MLD(s) or STA(s), starting from the bit number k of the traffic
10
indication virtual bitmap. The AID Offset subfield of the Multi-Link Traffic Indication Control field of the
11
12 Multi-Link Traffic Indication element contains the value k. The order of the Per-Link Traffic Indication
13 Bitmap subfield(s) follows the order of the bits that are set to 1 in the Partial Virtual Bitmap subfield of the
14 TIM element that corresponds to the AID(s) of the non-AP MLD(s) or STA(s). If a non-AP MLD has
15
successfully negotiated a TID-to-link mapping with an AP MLD with a nondefault mapping, the bit position
16
i of the Per-Link Traffic Indication Bitmap subfield that corresponds to the link with the link ID that is equal
17
18 to i on which a non-AP STA of the non-AP MLD is operating shall be set to 1 if the AP MLD has buffered
19 BU(s) with TID(s) that are mapped to that link or MMPDU(s) for that non-AP MLD, otherwise the bit shall
20 be set to 0. The Bitmap Size subfield of the Multi-Link Traffic Indication Control field should be set to m,
21
where m is equal to the largest link ID value minus the smallest link ID value amongst the bits that are set to
22
1 in the Per-Link Traffic Indication Bitmap subfield(s).
23
24
25 If a non-AP MLD is in the default mapping mode (see 35.3.7.1.2 (Default mapping mode)) or all TIDs are
26 mapped to all enabled links, the bit position i of the Per-Link Traffic Indication Bitmap subfield that
27
corresponds to the link with the link ID equals to i on which a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD
28
is operating may be set to 1 to indicate to the non-AP MLD a link on which buffered BU(s) should be
29
30 retrieved. An example of the construction of the Multi-Link Traffic Indication element is shown in
31 Figure 35-22 (Example of Multi-Link Traffic Indication element construction). A non-AP MLD that
32 successfully negotiated a TID-to-link mapping with an AP MLD and not all TIDs are mapped to all enabled
33
links shall determine which AP has buffered BU(s) with TID(s) by interpreting a Multi-Link Traffic
34
Indication element.

26 When an AP affiliated with an AP MLD receives a PS-Poll frame or a U-APSD trigger frame from a non-AP
27

28
to the STA, if the buffered BU(s) are available and not discarded for implementation dependent reasons,
29
30 otherwise it may transmit a QoS Null frame.
31
32 If a buffered BU is an MMPDU that is intended for one non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD (see
33

34

35
36 operating on, following the procedure above, the MMPDU shall carry he MLO Link Information element to
37 determine the intended destination non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD (see 35.3.14.2
38 (Identification of the Intended STA)).
39
40 NOTE 1—If a buffered MMPDU is intended for one non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD (see Table 11-3
41 (Bufferable/nonbufferable classification of MMPDUs)), the MMPDU does not carry information in the frame body to
42 determine the intended destination non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD or does not have correct content to be
43 transmitted to another non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD, and the MMPDU needs to be transmitted due to
44 reception of a PS-Poll frame or a U-APSD trigger frame from another non-AP STA affiliated with an associated non-AP
45 MLD that is in power save mode, then the MMPDU needs to be discarded.

1 indicate to the non-AP MLD that it should exchange frames on this link both in DL and UL, while

39 35.3.12.5 WNM sleep mode in multi-link operation
40
41 An MLD that implements WNM sleep mode shall indicate its capability by setting the WNM Sleep Mode
42
field to 1 in the Extended Capabilities element that is transmitted by its affiliated STAs. All STAs affiliated
43
with an MLD shall advertise the same WNM Sleep Mode capability.
44
45
46 A non-AP MLD may transmit, via one of its affiliated non-AP STAs, a WNM Sleep Mode Request frame
47 (see 9.6.13.19 (WNM Sleep Mode Request frame format)) to an AP affiliated with an AP MLD that has
48
indicated support for WNM sleep mode capability.
49
50
51 An AP MLD shall send, via one of its affiliated APs, a WNM Sleep Mode Response frame in response to a
52 WNM Sleep Mode Request frame received from a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD. An AP
53 MLD may send, via one of its affiliated APs, the WNM Sleep Mode Response frame without solicitation
54
upon the AP MLD’s deletion of all traffic filter sets established according to the traffic filtering agreement
55
between the AP MLD and the non-AP MLD (see 9.6.13.20 (WNM Sleep Mode Response frame format)).
56
57
58 The WNM sleep state is maintained by the MLD and WNM sleep mode procedures defined in 11.2.3 (Power
59 management in a non-DMG infrastructure network) and 11.2.3.16 (WNM sleep mode) are performed at the
60
MLD level and apply to all the STAs affiliated with the MLD.
61
62
63
64
65

## 第 542 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.12.6 Operation for MLD listen interval
2
3
During multi-link (re)setup, the value carried in Listen Interval field in the (Re)Association Request frame
4
sent by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD to an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is requested at the
5
6 MLD level. The value of the Listen Interval field shall be in units of the maximum value of beacon intervals
7 corresponding to the links that the non-AP MLD intends to setup in the (Re)Association Request frame (see
8 9.4.1.6 (Listen Interval field)). The AP affiliated with the AP MLD may reject the multi-link (re)setup
9
because the listen interval requested by the non-AP MLD is too large. After successful multi-link (re)setup,
10
the AP MLD shall use the listen interval in determining the lifetime of frames that it buffers for the non-AP
11
12 MLD.
13
NOTE—The value of the Listen Interval field is not changed after successful multi-link (re)setup.
14
15
16 The AP MLD may delete buffered BUs for the implementation dependent reasons (subject to 11.2.3.10 (AP
17 and AP MLD aging function)), including the use of an aging function and availability of buffers where the
18 aging function is based on the listen interval indicated by the non-AP MLD in its (Re)Association Request
19
frame or the WNM sleep interval specified by the non-AP MLD in the WNM Sleep Mode Request frame.
20
21
22 If all non-AP STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD and operating on enabled links are in power save mode,
23 at least one of these non-AP STAs shall wake up to receive at least one Beacon frame scheduled for
24 transmission within the interval of duration equal to the listen interval indicated by the non-AP MLD in its
25
(Re)Association Request frame, starting from the last TBTT for which another non-AP STA or the same
26
non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD was in awake state.
27
28

30 listen interval).

1 affiliated with the AP MLD. The non-AP STA 1 requests three links to be setup (link 1 between AP 1 and

15

16

17

1 by the non-AP MLD is still 250 ms and it is not changed along with the accepted links in the multi-link setup

13 35.3.13 Multi-link device individually addressed data delivery without block ack negotiation
14
15
An MLD may deliver individually addressed QoS Data frames belonging to a TID without block ack
16
negotiation to an associated MLD on the setup links subject to additional constraints in 35.3.7 (Link
17
18 management) and 35.3.9 (Fragmentation in multi-link operation).
19
20 An MLD shall follow the rules described in 10.3.2.14.2 (Transmitter requirements) to determine the
21
sequence number of an individually addressed QoS Data frame belonging to a TID that is delivered to the
22
associated MLD.
23
24
25 An MLD shall follow the rules as described in 10.3.2.14.3 (Receiver requirements) to discard duplicate
26 individually addressed QoS Data frames belonging to a TID without block ack negotiation that are delivered
27
from the associated MLD.
28
29
30 An MLD shall maintain a transmit MSDU timer for each MSDU passed to the MAC through the MAC SAP.
31 The transmit MSDU timer shall be started when the MSDU is passed to the MAC through the MAC SAP.
32 Non-AP STAs affiliated with an non-AP MLD shall have the same dot11EDCATableMSDULifetime, and
33
APs affiliated with an AP MLD shall have the same dot11QAPEDCATableMSDULifetime.
34
35
36 When A-MSDU aggregation is used, the MLD maintains a single timer for the whole A-MSDU. The timer
37 is restarted each time an MSDU is added to the A-MSDU. The result of this procedure is that no MSDU in
38 the A-MSDU is discarded before a period of dot11EDCATableMSDULifetime or
39
dot11QAPEDCATableMSDULifetime has elapsed.
40
41
42 For an MLD, the frame retry count and retry limit for each MSDU or A-MSDU that belongs to a TID that
43 requires acknowledgment is implementation specific.
44
45
An MLD shall continue to deliver the failed individually addressed QoS Data frame belonging to a TID
46
without block ack negotiation to an associated MLD on the setup links subject to additional constraints (see
47
48 35.3.7 (Link management)) until any of the following conditions occurs:
49 — The retry limit is met.
50
51 — The transmit MSDU timer for the MSDU or the A-MSDU (if the A-MSDU is used) exceeds
52 dot11EDCATableMSDULifetime or dot11QAPEDCATableMSDULifetime.
53
— The individually addressed QoS Data frame is successfully delivered.
54
55
56 A MLD shall not transmit other individually addressed QoS Data frames through a STA affiliated with the
57 MLD belonging to the TID without block ack negotiation to any STA affiliated with the associated MLD
58 while the current individually addressed QoS Data frame belonging to the TID without block ack negotiation
59
has not yet completed to the point of success, failed due to retry limit, or other MAC discard (e.g., lifetime
60
expiration).
61
62
63
64
65

1 35.3.14 Multi-link device individually addressed Management frame delivery
2
3
35.3.14.1 General
4
5
6 This subclause describes rules for individually addressed management frame delivery by a MLD with the
7 exception of the following frames specified below:
8
— CSI frame
9
10 — Noncompressed Beamforming frame
11
— Compressed Beamforming frame
12
13 — VHT Compressed Beamforming frame
14
— HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame
15
16 — EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame
17
— Probe Response frame
18
19 — LMR frame
20
— FTM frame
21
22
23 An MLD with dot11QMFActivated equal to false shall follow the rules described in 10.3.2.14.2 (Transmitter
24 requirements) to determine the sequence number of an individually addressed Management frame (except
25 the frames that are excluded above) that is delivered to the associated MLD.
26
27
An MLD with dot11QMFActivated equal to false shall follow the rules as described in 10.3.2.14.3 (Receiver
28
29 requirements) to discard duplicate individually addressed Management frames (except the frames that are
30 excluded above) that are delivered from the associated MLD.
31
32
An MLD with dot11QMFActivated equal to false shall maintain a transmit MMPDU timer for each
33
MMPDU (except the frames that are excluded above). The transmit MMPDU timer shall be started when the
34
35 MMPDU is passed to the MAC.
36
37 For an MLD with dot11QMFActivated equal to false, the frame retry counter and retry limit for each
38
MMPDU that belongs to a TC that requires acknowledgment is implementation specific.
39
40
41 An MLD with dot11QMFActivated equal to false shall continue to deliver the failed individually addressed
42 Management frame (except the frames that are excluded above) to an associated MLD on the setup links
43 subject to additional constraints (see 35.3.7 (Link management))) until any of the following conditions
44
occurs:
45
46 — The retry limit is met.
47
— The transmit MMPDU timer for the MMPDU exceeds dot11EDCATableMSDULifetime.
48
49 — The individually addressed Management frame is successfully delivered.
50
51
Between a MLD and an associated peer MLD, a STA affiliated with the MLD with dot11QMFActivated
52
equal to false shall not transmit other individually addressed Management frames (except the frames that are
53
54 excluded above) over a setup link while the current individually addressed Management frame (except the
55 frames that are excluded above) being transmitted by any STA affiliated with the same MLD over a setup
56 link has not yet completed to the point of success, failed due to retry limit, or other MAC discard (e.g., lifetime
57
expiration).
58
59
60 Between an AP MLD and an associated non-AP MLD subject to additional constraints (see 35.3.7 (Link
61 management)), an MLD may transmit an individually addressed MMPDU, which is intended for one or more
62 STA(s) affiliated with the associated MLD operating on an enabled link, to another STA (other than the
63
intended STA(s)) affiliated with the associated MLD operating on a setup link through a STA affiliated with the
64
MLD operating on the setup link if the MMPDU satisfies all the following conditions:
65

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — The MMPDU is a Class 3 frame
2
— The MMPDU is not a TPC Request frame, a TPC Report frame, a Link Measurement Request frame
3
or a Link Measurement response frame
4
5 — The MMPDU is classified as a bufferable MMPDU
6
— The MMPDU is not one of the frames listed at the beginning of 35.3.14.1 (General).
7
8
NOTE—MMPDU only includes the Frame Body field of the management frame and does not include a MAC header
9
and a frame check sequence (FCS) of the management frame (see 3.2 (Definitions specific to IEEE 802.11)).
10
11
Otherwise, an MLD shall not transmit an individually addressed MMPDU, which is intended for one or
12
13 more STA(s) affiliated with the associated MLD operating on an enabled link, to another STA (other than the
14 intended STA(s)) affiliated with the associated MLD operating on a setup link through an STA affiliated
15 with the MLD operating on the setup link subject to additional constraints (see 35.3.7 (Link management)).
16
17
An individually addressed MMPDU transmitted by an MLD through an affiliated STA is intended for a STA
18
19 affiliated with the peer MLD unless specified otherwise to be intended for an MLD or to be capable of
20 intended for more than one STA affiliated with the peer MLD.
21
22
Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD, the following individually addressed MMPDUs shall be intended
23
for an MLD:
24
25 — Authentication frame that includes a Basic Multi-Link element
26
— (Re)Association Request/Response frame that includes a Basic Multi-Link element
27
28 — Deauthentication frame
29
— Disassociation frame
30
31 — Block Ack Action frame
32
— SA Query Action frame
33
34 — Multi-link probe request/response
35
— WNM Sleep Mode Request/Response frame
36
37 — TID-To-Link Mapping Request/Response/Teardown frame
38
— EPCS Priority Access Enable Request/Enable Response/Teardown frame
39
40 — EML Operating Mode Notification frame
41
— SCS Request/Response frame
42
43 — MSCS Request/Response frame
44
— BSS Transition Management Request/Response frame
45
46
47 A non-AP MLD may transmit an individually addressed MMPDU that is an Authentication frame that includes
48 a Basic Multi-Link element or a (Re)Association Request frame that includes a Basic Multi-Link element or a
49
multi-link probe request or a Deauthentication frame or a Disassociation frame to any AP affiliated with the
50
AP MLD subject to additional constraints (see 35.3.7 (Link management)).
51
52
53 An AP MLD may transmit an individually addressed MMPDU that is a Deauthentication frame or a
54 Disassociation frame to any non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD subject to additional constraints (see
55
35.3.7 (Link management)).
56
57
58 An MLD may transmit an individually addressed MMPDU that is a Class 3 frame that is intended for an
59 associated MLD to any STA affiliated with the associated MLD operating on a setup link through an STA
60 affiliated with the MLD operating on the setup link subject to additional constraints (see 35.3.7 (Link
61
management)).

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.14.2 Identification of the Intended STA
2
3
Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, an individually addressed MMPDU
4
that is not a TWT Setup frame that includes a Link ID Bitmap subfield in its TWT element and that is intended
5
6 for one or more STA(s) affiliated with the associated MLD operating on an enabled link shall follow the below
7 procedure:
8
— If the individually addressed MMPDU is transmitted to another STA (other than the intended
9
STA(s)) affiliated with the associated MLD operating on a setup link through a STA affiliated with
10
11 the MLD operating on the setup link, then the individually addressed MMPDU shall include MLO
12 Link Information element that identifies the intended link(s) of the MMPDU as the last element but
13 before the Vendor Specific element(s) (if present).
14
15 — Otherwise, the individually addressed MMPDU may include MLO Link Information element that
16 identifies the intended link(s) of the MMPDU as the last element but before the Vendor Specific
17 element(s) (if present).
18
19 NOTE—If the MLO Link Information element is not present in the individually addressed MMPDU, the individually
20 addressed MMPDU cannot be retransmitted to different STA as described in the first bullet above.
21
22 Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, a TWT Setup frame that includes a
23
Link ID Bitmap subfield in its TWT element shall not include a MLO Link Information element.
24
25
26 Only one bit in the Link ID Bitmap subfield of the MLO Link Information element shall be set to 1.
27
28 Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, an individually addressed MMPDU
29
that is intended for an associated MLD shall not include MLO Link Information element.
30
31
32 Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, if an individually addressed MMPDU
33 that carries MLO Link Information element is received by a STA affiliated with the MLD, then the MLD shall
34 discard the MMPDU if the MLO Link Information element indicates any link that is not an enabled link.
35
36
35.3.15 Multi-link operation group addressed frames
37
38
39 35.3.15.1 AP MLD operation for group addressed frames
40
41
Each AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall schedule for transmission, all buffered group addressed frames
42
that arrive via the DS, immediately following the next DTIM beacon with the following exceptions:
43
44 — an AP that operates on a link that has been advertised as disabled for all associated non-AP MLDs
45 according to procedures defined in 35.3.7.1.7 (Advertised TID-to-link mapping in Beacon and Probe
46
Response frames) is exempt from this rule
47
48 — a TWT scheduling AP that schedules buffered group addressed frames during specific broadcast
49 TWT SPs as defined in 26.8.3.2 (Rules for TWT scheduling AP).
50
51
An AP MLD that schedules for transmission a group addressed Data frame received from an associated non-
52
53 AP MLD shall set the SA field of the group addressed Data frame equal to the MLD MAC address of the
54 non-AP MLD.
55
56
Each AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall schedule:
57
58 — the transmission of the buffered group addressed Management frames independently from the
59 transmission of buffered group addressed Management frames of other AP(s) affiliated with the
60 same AP MLD.
61
62 — the transmission of the buffered group addressed Data frames that are expected to be received by a
63 non-AP MLD in all the enabled links setup with the non-AP MLD.
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 547
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 548 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 NOTE 1—Additional and exceptional rules of group addressed frame delivery for an NSTR mobile AP MLD are
2 defined in 35.3.19 (NSTR mobile AP MLD operation).
3
4
If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD, is not part of a multiple BSSID set, then the AP shall indicate if each of
5
the other AP(s) in the same AP MLD has buffered group addressed frames by using a bit in the Partial
6
7 Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element after the bit corresponding to AID 0.
8
— The indication is in the DTIM beacon sent by the AP and is based on the latest information about the
9
other APs that the AP has when the AP schedules the DTIM beacon.
10
11 — These bits in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element for the other AP(s) in the same AP
12 MLD shall be contiguous.
13
14 • The bits 1 to N of the bitmap in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field are for the AP MLD where N is
15 equal to 2^(Group Addressed BU Indication Exponent + 1) – 1, and the Group Addressed BU
16 Indication Exponent is carried in the Group Addressed BU Indication Exponent subfield of the
17
EHT Operation Parameters field. The AIDs from 1 to N shall not be allocated to a non-AP STA,
18
and or a non-AP MLD that has a multi-link setup with the AP MLD and has a setup link on
19
20 which the AP operates.
21 • The first n bits of N bits are used to indicate that one or more group addressed frames are buff-
22 ered for each AP of the other AP(s) in the same AP MLD in an increasing order of their link IDs
23
The remaining (N – n) bits are set to 0.
24
25
26 If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD corresponds to a transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, then the
27 AP shall indicate if each of the other AP(s) affiliated with the same AP MLD has buffered group addressed
28 frames by using a bit in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element after the last bit corresponding to
29
a nontransmitted BSSID (maximum possible number of BSSIDs – 1) which is in the same multiple BSSID
30
as the AP.
31
32 — The indication is in the DTIM beacon sent by the AP and is based on the latest information about the
33 other APs that the AP has when the AP schedules the DTIM Beacon.
34
35 — These bits in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element for the other AP(s) affiliated with
36 the same AP MLD shall be contiguous.
37
• The bits X to X + N – 1 of the bitmap in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field are for the AP MLD
38
39
where X – 1 is the last bit corresponding to the nontransmitted BSSID (if any) that is in the same
40 multiple BSSID set as the AP and N is equal to 2^(Group Addressed BU Indication
41 Exponent + 1) – 1, and the Group Addressed BU Indication Exponent is carried in the Group
42 Addressed BU Indication Exponent subfield of the EHT Operation Parameters field. The AIDs
43
from X to X + N – 1 shall not be allocated to a non-AP STA, and to a non-AP MLD that has a
44
multi-link setup with the AP MLD and has a setup link on which the AP operates.
45
46 • The first n bits of N bits are used to indicate that one or more group addressed frames are buff-
47 ered for each AP of the other AP(s) in the same AP MLD in an increasing order of their link IDs.
48 The remaining (N – n) bits are set to 0.
49
50 NOTE 2—The AP indicates the presence of its buffered group addressed frames following 11.2.3.6 (AP operation).
51
52 If an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is a nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, then the AP that
53
corresponds to the transmitted BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set shall indicate if each of the other
54
AP(s) affiliated with the same AP MLD as the nontransmitted BSSID has buffered group addressed frames
55
56 by using a bit in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element after the last bit corresponding to the
57 nontransmitted BSSID (maximum possible number of BSSIDs – 1) which is in the same multiple BSSID as
58 the AP.
59
60 — The indication is in the DTIM beacon corresponding to that nontransmitted BSSID sent by the
61 transmitted BSSID of the same multiple BSSID set as the nontransmitted BSSID and is based on the
62 latest information about the other APs affiliated with the AP MLD that the transmitted BSSID has
63
when it schedules the DTIM Beacon.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — These bits in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element for the other AP(s) affiliated with
2 the same AP MLD shall be contiguous. The AP shall set the Group Addressed BU Indication Limit
3
subfield of the EHT Operation element to 1 if the total number of bits needed to indicate the presence
4
of buffered group addressed frames of all other APs affiliated with the same AP MLDs as all
5
6 nontransmitted BSSIDs in the TIM element is greater than 48 bits, otherwise the AP shall set the
7 Group Addressed BU Indication Limit subfield to 0. For the kth nontransmitted BSSID affiliated
8 with an MLD, where k is numbered in an increasing order of AP MLD ID of this MLD and starts
9
from 1:
10
11 • The bits Y + (k – 1) (cid:117) N to Y + k (cid:117) N – 1 of the bitmap in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field, if less
12 than Y + 48, are for the AP MLD with which the kth nontransmitted BSSID is affiliated where
13 Y – 1 is the last bit for the AP MLD with which the AP that corresponds to the transmitted
14
BSSID is affiliated with and N is equal to 2^(Group Addressed BU Indication Exponent + 1) – 1,
15
and the Group Addressed BU Indication Exponent is carried in the Group Ad-dressed BU Indi-
16
17 cation Exponent subfield of the EHT Operation Parameters field. The AIDs from Y + (k – 1) (cid:117) N
18 to Y + k (cid:117) N – 1 shall not be allocated to a non-AP STA, and to a non-AP MLD that has a multi-
19 link setup with the AP MLD and has a setup link in which the AP operates.
20
• The first n bits of N bits are used to indicate that one or more group addressed frames are buff-
21
ered for each AP of the other AP(s) in the same AP MLD as the kth nontransmitted BSSID in an
22
23 increasing order of their link IDs. The remaining (N – n) bits are set to 0.
24
25 Figure 35-25 (Example of APs affiliated with an AP MLD and each affiliated AP belongs to a multiple
26
BSSID set) shows an example of group addressed BU indication in Partial Virtual Bitmap field sent by an
27
28
AP (AP-11) affiliated with an AP MLD (AP MLD 1). In this example, the AP MLD 1 has three affiliated
29 APs: AP-11, AP-12, and AP-13. AP-11 operates on link 1, and corresponds to transmitted BSSID of a
30 multiple BSSID that also includes AP-21 affiliated AP MLD 2 and AP-31 affiliated with AP MLD 3, and the
31
maximum possible number of BSSIDs (2n) in this multiple BSSID set is equal to 4. AP-12 operates on
32
33 link 2, and is in a multiple BSSID that also includes AP-32 affiliated AP MLD 3. AP-13 operates on link 3,
34 and in a multiple BSSID that also includes AP-23 affiliated AP MLD 2. The group addressed BU indication
35 exponent is carried in the Group Addressed BU Indication Exponent subfield of the EHT Operation
36
Parameters field sent by AP-11 and it is equal to 1, then N = 2^(Group Addressed BU Indication
37
Exponent + 1) – 1 = 3. As shown in Figure 35-26 (Example of group addressed BU indication in Partial
38
39 Virtual Bitmap field sent by an AP affiliated with AP MLD), the bits 1 to 2 of the bitmap are used to indicate
40 that one or more group addressed frames are buffered for AP-21 and AP-31 corresponding to a
41 nontransmitted BSSID, respectively. Bits 4 and 5 of the bitmap are used to indicate that one or more group
42
addressed frames are buffered for AP-12 and AP-13 affiliated with AP MLD 1, respectively. Bit 7 of the
43
bitmap is used to indicate that one or more group addressed frames are buffered for AP-23 affiliated with AP
44
45 MLD 2. Bit 10 of the bitmap is used to indicate that one or more group addressed frames are buffered for
46

35.3.15.2 Non-AP MLD receive operation for group addressed frames
48
49
50 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall follow the item (e) defined in 11.2.3.7 (Receive
51 operation for STAs in PS mode) to receive the group addressed BUs sent by its associated AP affiliated with
52
the associated AP MLD.
53
54
55 If an indication of buffered group addressed frames in the TIM element about an AP affiliated with an AP
56 MLD is received by any non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD, the non-AP STA affiliated with the
57 non-AP MLD that is associated with the AP and that stays awake to receive group addressed BUs shall elect
58
to receive all group addressed frames that are scheduled for delivery on the link that the non-AP STA is
59
operating on.
60
61
62 A non-AP MLD shall filter out the group addressed MPDU with the SA field set to the MLD MAC address
63 of the non-AP MLD.

35.3.16 Multi-link channel access
8
9
10 35.3.16.1 General
11
12 A STA, which is affiliated with an MLD, shall contend for the WM on its link independently from the other
13
STA(s) affiliated with the same MLD, unless explicitly stated otherwise in the subclauses below.
14
15
16 35.3.16.2 Multi-link device capability and operation signaling
17
18 35.3.16.2.1 General
19
20
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall set the MLD Capabilities and Operations Present subfield in the
21
22 Multi-Link Control field of the Basic Multi-Link element to 1 when carried in Beacon, Probe Response, and
23 (Re)Association Response frames it transmits. Otherwise, it shall set the MLD Capabilities and Operations
24 Present subfield to 0.
25
26
A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall set the MLD Capabilities and Operations Present
27
28 subfield in the Multi-Link Control field of the Basic Multi-Link element to 1 when carried in a
29 (Re)Association Request frame it transmits. Otherwise, it shall set the MLD Capabilities and Operations
30 Present subfield to 0.
31
32
An AP MLD shall set the Maximum Number Of Simultaneous Links subfield in the Common Info field of
33
34 the Basic Multi-Link element to the number of affiliated APs minus 1.
35
36 A single radio non-AP MLD shall set the Maximum Number Of Simultaneous Links subfield in the
37
Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element carried in transmitted Management frames to 0.
38
39
40 A single radio non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true shall set the Maximum
41 Number Of Simultaneous Links subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element to 0.
42
43
If a multi-radio non-AP MLD requests more than one link during multi-link setup, the multi-radio non-AP
44
MLD shall set the Maximum Number Of Simultaneous Links subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic
45
46 Multi-Link element carried in transmitted Management frames to a value equal to or larger than 1.
47
48 A multi-radio non-AP MLD shall announce each pair of links formed by links that requested a multi-link
49
setup as STR or NSTR in a transmitted (Re)Association Request frame, by setting the corresponding bit in
50
the NSTR Indication Bitmap subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element to 0 or 1, respectively (see
51
52 9.4.2.312.2 (Basic Multi-Link element)).
53
54 An MLD shall be capable of simultaneously transmitting or receiving frames on affiliated STAs up to a
55
value indicated in the Maximum Number Of Simultaneous Links subfield in the Basic Multi-Link element
56
plus 1, under the rules defined in subclauses below.
57
58
59 An MLD shall set the NSTR Link Pair Present subfield value to 1 in a STA Control field that corresponds to
60
link ID i (where 0(cid:100)i(cid:31)15) only if it is a multi-radio MLD and contains at least one NSTR link pair formed
61
by the link with link ID i; otherwise it shall set the subfield value to 0. An NSTR mobile AP MLD shall set
62
63 the NSTR Link Pair Present subfield value to 1 in the STA Control field that corresponds to link ID i unless
64 the NSTR mobile AP MLD has removed the nonprimary link, in which case NSTR mobile AP MLD shall
65

1 set the subfield to 0. An AP MLD that is not an NSTR mobile AP MLD shall set the NSTR Link Pair Present
2 subfield value in each STA Control field to 0.
3
4
An MLD shall set to 0 every bit in the NSTR Indication Bitmap subfield, if present, of the Basic Multi-Link
5
6 element that corresponds to a link pair where one of the STAs in the link pair operates in the 2.4 GHz band
7 and the other STA operates in the 5 GHz or 6 GHz band.
8
9
A non-AP MLD may set the Frequency Separation For STR subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic
10
Multi-Link element to a nonzero value if it intends to indicate the minimum frequency separation that is
11
12 recommended between two links for the non-AP MLD for STR operation; otherwise the non-AP MLD shall
13 set the Frequency Separation For STR subfield to 0.
14
15
An AP MLD might take into account the information provided by associated non-AP MLDs in the
16
Frequency Separation For STR subfield in their transmitted Multi-Link elements when the AP MLD intends
17
18 to set up BSSs in the future referring to the information provided by those non-AP MLDs or switch the BSS

20 information provided by the Frequency Separation For STR subfield is out of scope of the standard.
21
22 NOTE 1—The non-AP MLD ensures that the minimum frequency separation indicated in the Frequency Separation For
23 STR subfield starts from the frequency edge of the maximum supported bandwidth indicated by the Supported Channel
24 Width Set subfield in the HE Capabilities element and the Support For 320 MHz in 6 GHz subfield in the EHT
25 Capabilities element of each link.
26
27 The ability of a non-AP MLD to perform STR operation on a pair of setup links may change after multi-link
28
setup. The non-AP MLD may use a Management frame on any enabled link to inform the AP MLD about
29
the ability change to perform STR operation.
30
31
32 NOTE 2—The ability might change due to an AP switching BSS operating channels of one or more of the setup links
33 with the non-AP MLD.

35 35.3.16.2.2 Non-AP MLD operation parameter update
36
37
An MLD shall set the Operation Parameter Update Support subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic
38
39 Multi-Link element it transmits in a Beacon, Probe Response, (Re)Association Request, and
40 (Re)Association Response frames to 1 if its dot11OperationParameterUpdateImplemented is true; otherwise
41 the MLD shall set it to 0. A STA affiliated with an MLD in which
42
dot11OperationParameterUpdateImplemented is true is referred to as operation parameter update capable.
43
44
45 An operation parameter update capable non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD may notify an operation
46 parameter update capable AP affiliated with the associated AP MLD of change in its operation parameters
47 by transmitting a Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame including a Reconfiguration Multi-Link
48
element with Operation Update Type subfield set to 0 after one of the following conditions occurs:
49
50 — at least one link is enabled or disabled for the non-AP MLD;
51
— the associated AP MLD removes at least one of its affiliated AP;
52
53 — the associated AP MLD adds at least one affiliated AP to the AP MLD.
54
55
Otherwise, the non-AP STA shall not transmit a Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame with operation
56
update type set to 0.
57
58
59 In the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element of a Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame with Operation
60 Update Type subfield set to 0 transmitted by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD:
61
— all subfields in the Presence Bitmap subfield of the Multi-Link Control field in the Reconfiguration
62
63 Multi-Link element shall be set to 0;
64
65

1 — all subfields of the STA Control field in the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element except the Link ID

9 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not transmit a Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame.

An operation parameter update capable AP affiliated with an AP MLD that received a Multi-Link Operation
12

13

16 <ANA> (DENIED_ OPERATION_PARAMETER _UPDATE).

28 the corresponding Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame.
29
30
After receiving the Multi-Link Operation Update Response frame in which the Status Code is equal to the

32 value 0 (SUCCESS), the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall apply the operation parameters
33 indicated in the Operation Parameter Info subfield in the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element of the
34 corresponding Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame.
35
36
After receiving the Multi-Link Operation Update Response frame in which a Status Code is equal to the
37
38 value <ANA> (DENIED_OPERATION_PARAMETER _UPDATE), the non-AP STA affiliated with the
39 non-AP MLD shall not apply the operation parameters indicated in the Operation Parameter Info subfield in
40 the Reconfiguration Multi-Link element of the corresponding Multi-Link Operation Update Request frame.

transmitted by the non-AP STA.

52 value to update the Maximum A-MSDU Length subfield received in HT Capabilities element transmitted by
53
the non-AP STA.

56 35.3.16.3 Simultaneous transmit and receive (STR) operation
57
58 When a pair of links on which an MLD operates is an STR link pair, a STA that is affiliated with the MLD
59
and that is operating on a link in that STR link pair shall access the WM on that link by following the rules
60
defined in 10.3 (DCF) and 10.23.2 (HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)) regardless of any
61
62 activity occurring on the other link within that STR link pair, unless explicitly stated otherwise.
63

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 All pairs of links for an AP MLD that is not an NSTR mobile AP MLD and that operates on more than one
2 link shall be STR link pairs.
3
4 NOTE—If an AP MLD that is not an NSTR mobile AP MLD operates on only one link, any STR requirements and
5 capabilities that correspond to a link pair no longer apply.
6
7 Figure 35-27 (Channel access of two MLDs over an STR link pair) shows an example of an AP MLD and a
8
non-AP MLD that are operating over an STR link pair and that are contending for access to the WM and
9
subsequent frame exchanges between two MLDs on those links. After the AP MLD has performed a multi-
10
11 link setup with the non-AP MLD to set up link 1 and link 2 successfully and the links are enabled, then AP 2
12 may receive data frames from non-AP STA 2 on link 2, while AP 1 contends for the WM and then transmits
13 data frames to non-AP STA 1 on link 1 after it obtains a TXOP.

30 35.3.16.4 Nonsimultaneous transmit and receive (NSTR) operation
31
32
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD that has gained the right to initiate transmission of a frame of an AC on a
33
link through the rules for EDCA backoff in 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP) may choose not to
34
35 transmit any frame corresponding to that AC due to expected interference caused by the transmission at the
36 STA operating on the other link of an NSTR link pair that the link belongs to within the intended recipient
37 non-AP MLD and due to lack of availability of an alternative frame in the queue that would not introduce
38
the opportunity for such interference.
39
40
41 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating on a link of an NSTR link pair that has gained the
42 right to initiate transmission of a frame of an AC on a link through the rules for EDCA backoff in
43 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP) or that is enabled by an AP that is the TXOP holder to use a portion
44
of the obtained TXOP through the rules for Triggered TXOP sharing in 35.2.1.2 (Triggered TXOP sharing
45
procedure)may choose not to transmit any frame corresponding to that AC due to expected interference
46
47 caused by the transmission at the non-AP STA operating on the other link of the NSTR link pair within the
48 non-AP MLD and due to lack of availability of an alternative frame in the queue that would not introduce
49 the opportunity for such interference.
50
51
An AP or non-AP STA affiliated with an MLD that has gained the right to initiate transmission of a frame as
52
53 described in 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP) for an AC but does not transmit any frame
54 corresponding to that AC for the reasons stated above may:
55
— invoke a backoff for the EDCAF associated with that AC as allowed per item h) of 10.23.2.2 (EDCA
56
backoff procedure)
57
58 — consider the transmit queue for that AC as empty until any frame exists in the queue which if
59
transmitted, the transmitter determines, will not cause an unacceptable level of interference caused
60
by transmission at the STA operating on the other link of a NSTR link pair that the AP or non-AP
61
62 STA belongs to, at which time the queue is considered to have become nonempty and backoff is
63 invoked per the procedure described in item a) of 10.23.2.2 (EDCA backoff procedure) regardless of
64 whether the medium is busy or not.
65

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD should not transmit a frame that solicits an immediate response to a non-
2 AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD on a link that is a member of one or more NSTR link pairs for
3
that non-AP MLD, if the immediate response is expected to overlap in time with group addressed MPDUs
4
scheduled on a link that is a member of any of those NSTR link pairs and any of the other non-AP STA(s)
5
6 affiliated with the non-AP MLD is expected to be receiving those group addressed MPDUs.
7
8 If a non-AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD successfully obtains a TXOP on one link of one of its
9
NSTR link pairs before the TBTT of the other link of the NSTR link pair, then it should end its TXOP before
10
the TBTT of the other link if the other non-AP STA affiliated with the same non-AP MLD intends to receive
11
12 the Beacon frame scheduled at that TBTT on that link.
13
NOTE—The non-AP STA might not do so if it is not aware of the TSF of the other link.
14
15
16 35.3.16.5 PPDU end time alignment on a NSTR link pair
17
18 35.3.16.5.1 General
19
20
In this subclause “simultaneously transmit” means more than one PPDU is transmitted on more than one
21
22 link, where each PPDU is transmitted over one link, and those transmissions overlap in time. Likewise,
23 “simultaneously trigger” means more than one TB PPDU is triggered on more than one link, where each
24 PPDU is triggered over one link, and those transmissions overlap in time. If a non-AP MLD that is receiving
25
a PPDU on a link that is part of an NSTR link pair for that MLD concurrently transmits another PPDU on
26
another link that is part of the same NSTR link pair for that MLD, then the non-AP MLD might fail to
27
28 receive the PPDU on the link because of the interference caused by its transmission on the other link. This
29 subclause specifies a mechanism to align the end time of PPDUs that are simultaneously transmitted to the
30 non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating on a pair of NSTR links for that MLD, which helps
31
to reduce the chances of the occurrence of such self-interference among non-AP STAs affiliated with the
32
same MLD.
33
34
35 When more than one AP affiliated with an AP MLD simultaneously transmits to the non-AP STAs affiliated
36 with a non-AP MLD operating on a pair of NSTR links for that MLD and at least one of the PPDUs carries
37
a frame that is soliciting an immediate response, then
38
39 — The AP shall align the end time of the PPDUs soliciting an immediate response per the rules defined
40 in this subclause.
41
42 NOTE 1— In this way the response PPDU to any of the PPDUs transmitted by the AP will not overlap with any of these
43 PPDUs soliciting immediate response.
44
45 When an AP MLD is required to align the end time of simultaneously transmitted PPDUs, it shall satisfy the
46 following conditions:
47
48 — The AP MLD shall ensure that the difference between the end times of simultaneously transmitted
49 PPDUs is less than or equal to 8 (cid:541)s (see NOTE 2), where the end time of the PPDU is the time of the
50 end of the last OFDM symbol or the time of the end of the packet extension if present, whichever is
51
later.
52
53 — The AP MLD shall ensure that the end time of one or more PPDUs that carry a frame soliciting an
54 immediate response frame is at most 4 (cid:541)s (see NOTE 3) earlier than the end time of any of the
55 PPDUs containing a Trigger frame with the CS Required subfield set to 1.
56
57 NOTE 2—The difference between the end times of transmitting PPDUs needs to be less than SIFS minus a timing
58 margin, so that the response PPDU to any of the PPDUs transmitted by the AP will not overlap with any of these PPDUs.
59 To balance the implementation complexity at a transmitter side and a receiver side, the timing margin is set to half of
60 SIFS.
61
62
NOTE 3—The value of 4 (cid:541)s is derived from aRxTxTurnaroundTime being equal to 4 (cid:541)s for the purpose of this
63
requirement.
64
65

This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 556 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An AP MLD may use any type of padding to align the end time of transmitted PPDUs, such as using the
2 Padding field in a Trigger frame, post-EOF A-MPDU padding, padding in HE-SIG-B or EHT-SIG field for
3
MU transmission, aggregating other MPDUs in the A-MPDU, or a packet extension.
4
5
6 When an AP MLD simultaneously solicits one or more TB PPDU transmissions from a pair of non-AP
7 STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD and operating on a pair of NSTR links for that MLD, each AP affiliated
8 with the AP MLD shall independently solicit a PPDU following the mechanisms defined in 26.5.2 (UL MU
9
operation) with the following exceptions:
10
11 — An AP affiliated with the AP MLD shall not transmit a Trigger frame with the CS Required subfield
12 set to 1 to a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD and operating on a link that is part of NSTR
13 link pair for that MLD, when at least one PPDU from other STAs operating on the other link that is
14
part of the same NSTR link pair is scheduled for transmission before a timer with a value of 12 (cid:541)s
15
(see NOTE 4) has expired after the PPDU containing the Trigger frame.
16
17 — If the AP MLD allows the frames in the TB PPDUs to solicit control response frames from the AP
18
MLD, then the UL Length subfield values in the soliciting Basic Trigger frames shall be set to the
19
same value.
20
21
NOTE 4—12 (cid:541)s is derived from aSIFSTime + aSignalExtension – aRxTxTurnaroundTime, where
22
aRxTxTurnaroundTime is equal to 4 (cid:541)s for the purpose of this calculation.
23
24
An example showing the relationship between the end times of DL PPDUs sent over the NSTR link pair
25
26 (link 1 and link 2, link 1 and link 3, link 2 and link 3) between an AP MLD (whose affiliated APs are AP 1,
27 AP 2, and AP 3) and a non-AP MLD (whose affiliated STAs are STA 1, STA 2, and STA 3) is shown in
28 Figure 35-28 (PPDU end time alignment timing relationships). An AP affiliated with the AP MLD operating
29
on link 1 solicits a TB PPDU requiring the carrier sense from a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP
30
MLD. In this case the difference between the end time of the soliciting DL PPDU sent on link 1 and the
31
32 starting time of the first solicited PPDU (in the figure, Ack frame on link 2) that is sent from any STA
33 affiliated with the same non-AP MLD immediately after the soliciting DL PPDU is greater than or equal to
34 12 (cid:541)s. Accordingly, the end time of the soliciting PPDU sent on link 2 cannot be more than 4 (cid:541)s earlier than
35
the end time of the soliciting PPDU sent on link 1. To avoid overlapping in time between any of the DL
36
PPDUs and the response PPDU to any of the DL PPDUs, the difference between the end times of the DL
37
38 PPDUs on link 2 and link 3 cannot be greater than 8 (cid:541)s.

## 第 557 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating on a pair of NSTR links for that MLD shall not
2 transmit a PPDU carrying an MPDU with SRS Control subfield to an AP unless a non-AP STA affiliated
3
with the non-AP MLD has received from the AP MLD a Basic Multi-Link element with the SRS Support
4
subfield equal to 1. A STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall not transmit a TB PPDU carrying an MPDU
5
6 with SRS Control subfield.
7
8 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not transmit a PPDU carrying an MPDU with SRS Control subfield
9
to a STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD.
10
11 NOTE 1—If the received SRS Support subfield from an AP is equal to 0, a non-AP STA might not be able to perform
12 multiple frame transmission in a TXOP over NSTR link pair(s) with the AP, unless the expected duration of solicited
13 PPDU transmitted on NSTR link pair(s) are the same.
14
15
If non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating on a pair of NSTR links simultaneously transmit
16
PPDUs to the respective APs affiliated with an AP MLD that has dot11SRSOptionImplemented equal to
17
18 true, the transmitted PPDUs solicit control response frames and the non-AP MLD intends to align the end
19 times of the PPDUs sent in response by the peer APs, then at least one of the PPDUs soliciting a control
20 response frame shall carry an MPDU with SRS Control subfield. The non-AP STA shall set the PPDU
21
Response Duration subfield of the SRS Control subfield to a value that is equal to or longer than the
22
maximum of the expected duration of the response PPDUs on all links, where the expected duration of the
23
24 response PPDU is calculated based on the following parameters:
25 — PPDU format that is HE SU PPDU, or an EHT MU PPDU with EHT-SIG MCS equals 0 and
26
addressed to a single STA,
27
28 — Bandwidth that is equal to the bandwidth of the soliciting PPDU, with BCC coding if the bandwidth
29 is 20 MHz and LDPC coding if the bandwidth is greater than 20 MHz,
30
— NSS and number of LTFs that are equal to one,
31
32 — GI that is equal to the longest mandatory GI value (3.2 μs),
33
— MCS that is selected following the rate selection rules defined in 10.6.6.5 (Rate selection for control
34
35 response frames), 26.17.1 (Basic HE BSS operation), 26.15.3 (MCS, NSS, BW and DCM selection),
36 35.15 (EHT BSS operation), and 35.14 (PPDU format, BW, MCS, NSS, and DCM selection rules),
37
— A PSDU length that is equal to or greater than the length of a PSDU that contains the larger of a
38
39 Multi-STA BlockAck frame and a Compressed BlockAck frame expected in response to the
40 soliciting PPDU.
41
42

43
is shown in Figure 35-29 (An example of SRS control based timing alignment of the PPDUs carrying
44

## 第 558 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An EHT AP affiliated with an AP MLD that transmits a PPDU in response to a frame containing an SRS
2 Control subfield shall:
3
— Have the duration of the PPDU to be equal to the duration that is specified in the PPDU Response
4
5 Duration subfield of the soliciting SRS Control subfield.
6
— Use a non-HT (duplicated) PPDU, or an HE SU PPDU, or an EHT MU PPDU format addressed to a
7
single STA. If the PSDU carried in the response PPDU contains an A-MPDU then the contents of the
8
9 A-MPDU shall be as defined in Table 9-533 (A-MPDU contents in the control response context). If
10 the PPDU is an HE SU PPDU then it shall not use DCM encoding
11
12 NOTE 2—If the PPDU carrying the response is an HE SU PPDU or an EHT MU PPDU addressed to one non-AP STA,
13 then the AP might use any type of padding to ensure that the duration of the PPDU is equal to the duration that is
14 specified in the PPDU Response Duration subfield of the soliciting SRS Control subfield.
15
16 35.3.16.6 Start time sync PPDUs medium access
17
18
Each STA affiliated with an MLD operating on a pair of NSTR links for that MLD that aligns the start times
19
of the PPDUs scheduled for transmission on more than one link shall ensure that the EDCA rules on each
20
21 link permit access to the medium on all the links at the time of issuance of the PHY-TXSTART.request for
22 each link.
23
24
A STA affiliated with an MLD operating on a link that is part of an NSTR link pair for that MLD shall
25
follow the channel access procedure described below:
26
27 1) The STA may initiate transmission on a link when the medium is idle as indicated by the
28 physical and virtual CS mechanism and one of the following conditions is met:
29
30 a) The STA obtained an EDCA TXOP following the procedure in 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an
31 EDCA TXOP).
32
b) The backoff counter of the STA is already zero, and the STA operating on the other link of
33
NSTR link pair of the affiliated MLD obtains an EDCA TXOP following the procedure in
34
35 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP).
36
2) When the backoff counter of the STA reaches zero, it may choose to not transmit and keep its
37
backoff counter at zero. A STA with backoff counter that has already reached zero may initiate
38
39 transmission only following condition 1b).
40 3) A STA with backoff counter that has already reached zero and that chose not to transmit
41
following condition 1b) may perform a new backoff procedure following deferral as described
42
in 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP) and 10.3.4.3 (Backoff procedure for DCF) before
43
44 being allowed to initiate transmission on a link following condition 1a). In such a case,
45 CW[AC] and QSRC[AC] are left unchanged.
46
47 NOTE 1—The backoff counters for each link count down as specified in 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP).
48
49 NOTE 2—The decision to choose to not transmit when the backoff counter of the STA reaches zero as in 2) or to
50 perform a new backoff procedure to be allowed to initiate transmission following condition 1a) as in 3) is
51 implementation specific.
52
53 A STA that chooses not to transmit after the backoff counter reached zero on a link of NSTR link pair may
54
have one or more EDCAF backoff counters with value zero on that link. The STA that initiates transmission
55
on that link following condition a) or b), and has one or more EDCAF backoff counters that already reached
56
57 zero shall choose only one EDCAF to gain an EDCA TXOP. The basis for selection is implementation
58 specific.
59
60
A STA with backoff counter that has already reached zero on a link and has a frame available for
61
transmission shall follow channel access procedures described in 10.23.2.4 (Obtaining an EDCA TXOP)
62
63 after it detects medium transition from busy to idle.
64
65
558 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.

## 第 559 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 The STA with backoff counter that has already reached zero and is initiating transmission following
2 condition b) is not mandated to initiate transmission on a slot boundary of the link on which the STA
3
operates. The STA that is initiating transmission following condition b) shall commence the transmission no
4
later than 4 μs following slot boundary of the link on which the other STA whose backoff counter reaches
5
6 zero operates.
7
NOTE 3—The value of 4 μs is derived from aRxTxTurnaroundTime being equal to 4 μs for the purpose of this
8
requirement.
9
10
11 35.3.16.7 Error recovery on a NSTR link pair within PIFS
12
13 After two PPDUs with end time alignment (and the PPDUs carrying the expected response frames also have
14
end time alignment) are transmitted by each STA affiliated with an MLD on two links that belong to a NSTR
15
link pair of the MLD, if the two STAs intend to transmit more PPDUs on both links in their respective
16
17 TXOPs, when a failure happens on at least one of the two links, the MLD conducts the procedures described
18 in this subclause.
19
20
If the MLD ensures that the difference between the end times of the two PPDUs carrying the expected
21
response frames is less than or equal to 4 μs, the MLD may use either SIFS or PIFS between the end time of
22
23 the PPDU carrying the response frame and the next PPDU sent in the same TXOP on the link where the
24 response frame is received correctly, regardless of the PPDU receive status of the other link of the NSTR
25 link pair.
26
27 NOTE 1—The value of 4 μs is derived from aRxTxTurnaroundTime used in 35.3.16.5 (PPDU end time alignment on a
28 NSTR link pair).
29
30 NOTE 2—It is stricter to maintain the difference between the end times of the two PPDUs carrying the expected
31 response frame be less than or equal to 4 μs, when compared with the requirement of PPDU end time alignment in
32 35.3.16.5 (PPDU end time alignment on a NSTR link pair).
33
34
NOTE 3—If SIFS is used between the end time of the PPDU carrying the response frame and the next PPDU sent in the
35
same TXOP on the first link of the NSTR link pair where the response frame is received correctly, the PIFS recovery on
36
the second link of the NSTR link pair might fail due to the interference caused by the transmission of the STA operating
37
on the first link.
38
39
If the MLD ensures that the difference between the end times of the two PPDUs carrying the expected
40
41 response frames is less than or equal to 8 μs (see 35.3.16.5 (PPDU end time alignment on a NSTR link
42 pair)), after two PPDUs with end time alignment (and the PPDUs carrying the expected response frames
43 also have end time alignment) are transmitted by STAs affiliated with the MLD on two links that belongs to
44
a NSTR link pair of the MLD, if PHY-RXSTART.indications are received on both links, but the response
45
frames contained in the corresponding PPDUs are not successfully received in at least one of the links of the
46
47 NSTR link pair, then:
48 — On the link on which the response frame ends last, if the response frame is successfully received, the
49
time from the end of the PPDU carrying the response frame to the next PPDU sent in the same TXOP
50
should be larger than or equal to SIFS and smaller than or equal to PIFS;
51
52 — On the link on which the response frame ends last, if the response frame is not successfully received
53
(i.e., FCS fails), the time from the end of the PPDU carrying the response frame to the next PPDU
54
sent in the same TXOP should be larger than or equal to PIFS - 4 μs and smaller than or equal to
55
56 PIFS;
57 — On the link on which the response frame ends first, the time from the end of the PPDU carrying the
58
response frame to the next PPDU sent in the same TXOP should be PIFS.
59
60
61 If the time from the end of the received PPDU carrying the response frame to the next PPDU sent in the
62 same TXOP is larger than SIFS and less than PIFS, then the STA affiliated with the MLD shall ensure that
63 the medium is idle through an ED-based CCA before the transmission of the next PPDU.
64
