## 第 560 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.16.8 Medium access recovery procedure
2
3
35.3.16.8.1 General
4
5
6 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD or an NSTR mobile AP MLD that operates on an NSTR link
7 pair is considered to have lost medium synchronization when the other STA, which is affiliated with the
8 same MLD and operates on that link pair, transmits a PPDU, except when both STAs ended a transmission at
9
the same time.
10
11
12 A STA that has lost medium synchronization as described above shall start a MediumSyncDelay timer and
13 begin counting down from the end of that transmission if that transmission is longer than
14 aMediumSyncThreshold unless its previous MediumSyncDelay timer has not expired. The STA may choose
15
not to (re)start the MediumSyncDelay timer if the transmission event is shorter than or equal to
16
aMediumSyncThreshold. The aMediumSyncThreshold is set to 72 μs.
17
18
NOTE 1—The value of 72 μs is chosen to cover at least the PPDU lengths of RTS/CTS/ACK frames using non-HT or
19
non-HT duplicate PPDU format with 6 Mb/s data rate, as well as the PPDU lengths of most typical BlockAck frames.
20
21
When a non-AP MLD is operating in the EMLSR mode, a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that
22
23 is operating on one of the EMLSR links is considered to have lost medium synchronization if it is not able to
24 perform CCA during frame exchanges that includes the link switch delays between an AP affiliated with an
25 AP MLD and one of the other non-AP STAs operating on the other EMLSR links, which are affiliated with
26
the same non-AP MLD. The non-AP STA that has lost medium synchronization shall start a
27
MediumSyncDelay timer and begin counting down immediately after returning to the listening operation if
28
29 the duration of the loss of medium synchronization is longer than aMediumSyncThreshold; otherwise, the
30 non-AP STA may not start the MediumSyncDelay timer.
31
32 NOTE 2—The link switch delays include the delay switching from the listening operation to the frame exchanges and
33 the delay switching from the frame exchanges to the listening operation (see 35.3.17 (Enhanced multi-link single radio
34 operation)).
35
36 A STA shall not start a MediumSyncDelay timer unless the STA is one of the following:
37
— a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating on an NSTR link pair or
38
39 — a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD operating on an EMLSR link or
40
— an AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD operating on the nonprimary link of an NSTR link
41
pair.
42
43
44 The MediumSyncDelay timer is a single timer, shared by all EDCAFs within a STA, whose value is set to
45 dot11MSDTimerDuration. The STA initializes dot11MSDTimerDuration to aPPDUMaxTime defined in
46
Table 36-70 (EHT PHY characteristics). A non-AP STA shall update dot11MSDTimerDuration with the
47
value contained in the Medium Synchronization Delay Information field, if present, of the Basic Multi-Link
48
49 element in the most recent frame received from its associated AP. In addition, the timer resets to zero when
50 any of the following events occur:
51
— The STA receives a PPDU with a valid MPDU.
52
53 — The STA receives a PPDU whose corresponding RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is not
54 UNSPECIFIED.
55
56
If a STA that operates on a NSTR link pair has lost medium synchronization, due to transmission by another
57
58 STA that is affiliated with the same MLD and operates on that link pair, and its previous MediumSyncDelay
59 timer has not expired, then at the end of that transmission it shall continue the previous MediumSyncDelay
60 timer except that the STA shall update the timer value as described above if that transmission is longer than
61
aMediumSyncThreshold.
62
63
64
65

This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 561 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.16.8.2 MediumSyncDelay OFDM ED based recovery procedure
2
3
A STA that is capable of obtaining a TXOP while the MediumSyncDelay timer has a nonzero value shall use
4
dot11MSDOFDMEDthreshold instead of dot11OFDMEDThreshold as specified in 36.3.21.6.3 (CCA
5
6 sensitivity for the primary 20 MHz channel) in order to detect a channel busy condition in the primary
7 20 MHz channel if the MediumSyncDelay timer has a nonzero value.
8
9
If a STA is capable of obtaining a TXOP while the MediumSyncDelay timer has a nonzero value, it shall
10
perform the following when the timer has a nonzero value:
11
12 — If it is a non-AP STA, it shall transmit an RTS frame to its associated AP as the initial frame in an
13 obtained TXOP.
14
15 — If it is an AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD, then the AP shall transmit an RTS frame to
16 an associated non-AP STA as the initial frame in an obtained TXOP and follow the rules defined in
17 35.3.19 (NSTR mobile AP MLD operation).
18
— Shall not attempt to initiate more than dot11MSDTXOPMAX since the start of the timer.
19
20
21 Otherwise, it shall perform CCA until the MediumSyncDelay timer has expired before it initiates a
22 transmission.
23
24
A STA that has a nonzero MediumSyncDelay timer shall not transmit any PPDU using OBSS PD-based
25
26 spatial reuse operation.
27
28 An AP affiliated with an AP MLD may include the Medium Synchronization Delay Information subfield in
29
the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element carried in transmitted (Re)Association Response or
30
Multi-Link Probe Response frames to provide medium synchronization information used by the AP MLD.
31
32
33 The default value of dot11MSDOFDMEDthreshold is –72 dBm and the default value of
34 dot11MSDTXOPMAX is 1, respectively. Each non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall set
35
dot11MSDTXOPMAX and dot11MSDOFDMEDthreshold to the most recent values carried in the Medium
36
Synchronization Maximum Number Of TXOPs subfields and Medium Synchronization OFDM ED
37
38 Threshold subfield, respectively, if they are present in the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link
39 element received by any non-AP STA affiliated with the same non-AP MLD from its associated AP
40 affiliated with the AP MLD with which the non-AP MLD has performed multi-link setup.
41
42 NOTE—If either the intra-BSS NAV or the Basic NAV is nonzero in the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD
43 when it starts the MediumSyncDelay timer, the non-AP STA does not initiate any TXOP and follows the same rules as
44 an HE STA to respond to any RTS or MU-RTS frame until both NAVs expire.
45
46 During the aCCAtime (see 36.3.21.6.3 (CCA sensitivity for the primary 20 MHz channel)) immediately
47
following the end of the transmission event that caused loss of medium synchronization and subsequent
48
initiation of the MediumSyncDelay timer at the non-AP STA, if the received signal strength exceeds the –
49
50 62 dBm threshold for the primary 20 MHz channel and no start of a PPDU is detected, the STA should defer
51 for EIFS beginning when the received signal strength falls below the threshold.
52
53
35.3.16.8.3 AP assisted medium synchronization recovery procedure
54
55
56 AP assisted medium synchronization recovery procedure is a service provided by an AP MLD to help a non-
57 AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that has lost medium synchronization to transmit a frame without
58 causing the collision with the existing transmission.
59
60
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD with dot11AAROptionImplemented that is equal to true shall set the
61
62 AAR Support subfield in the MLD Capabilities and Operations field in a Basic Multi-Link element it
63 transmits to 1; otherwise the AP shall set the AAR Support subfield to 0.
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 561
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 562 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD with dot11AAROptionImplemented that is equal to true and
2 that belongs to an NSTR link pair shall transmit the AAR Control subfield in a frame that solicits an
3
immediate response to its associated AP affiliated with an AP MLD if it has received a Basic Multi-Link
4
element from the AP with the AAR Support subfield equal to 1 and an assisted STA that belongs to the
5
6 NSTR link pair needs assistance in transmitting frames to its associated AP in the other link.
7
8 The AAR Control subfield transmitted by the STA shall indicate the link identifier(s) of the other assisting
9
AP(s) affiliated with the same AP MLD operating on the enabled link(s) by setting the corresponding bits to
10
1.
11
12
13 Each of the other assisting AP(s) affiliated with the AP MLD should schedule for a transmission a Trigger
14 frame to the assisted non-AP STA that is associated with it and affiliated with the non-AP MLD to solicit an
15
UL frame(s) after the AP affiliated with the same AP MLD successfully received the AAR Control subfield
16
in a frame if it does not have frame exchanges already scheduled with another non-AP STA.
17
18
NOTE—If the CS Required subfield in a Trigger frame is 1, then the non-AP STA uses CCA-ED threshold as defined in
19
36.3.21.6 (CCA sensitivity) during the SIFS between the Trigger frame and the PPDU sent in response to the Trigger
20
frame.
21
22
A non-AP STA with dot11AAROptionImplemented that is equal to false shall not transmit a frame
23
24 containing an AAR Control subfield to its associated AP.
25
26 A non-AP STA shall not transmit a frame containing an AAR Control subfield with a value of 1 in the bit
27
identifying the link identifier of the associated AP.
28
29
30 An AP shall not transmit the AAR Control subfield in a frame to its associated non-AP STAs.
31
32 Figure 35-30 (Example of an AP assisted medium synchronization recovery procedure) provides an
33
illustration of the AP assisted medium synchronization recovery procedure, where AP 2 and AP 3 are
34
requested to help STA 2 and STA 3 that have lost medium synchronization to transmit a frame, respectively.
35
36 In this example, for the non-AP MLD, link 1 and link 2 are an NSTR link pair, link 1 and link 3 are an
37 NSTR link pair, and link 2 and link 3 are a STR link pair. At the beginning, STA 1 transmits Data frames to
38 AP 1, while transmitting the AAR Control subfield carried in the Data frames to AP 1, requesting AP 2 and
39
AP 3 to provide the medium synchronization recovery service to help STA 2 and STA 3 transmit uplink
40
frames, respectively. In this case, the bits corresponding to link 2 and link 3 in the AAR Control subfield are
41
42 set to 1. Because of the interference caused by the transmission from STA 1, STA 2 and STA 3 lose medium
43 synchronization. Then STA 2 and STA 3 start the MediumSyncDelay timer at the end of the transmission of
44 STA 1. After receiving Data frames at AP 1, AP 2 and AP 3 transmit Trigger frames to STA 2 and STA 3,
45
46

23 35.3.16.9 Multi-link retransmit procedures
24
25
If an MLD has an established block ack agreement with another MLD for a TID, and the transmission of a
26
QoS Data frame of the TID on a link is unsuccessful, the MLD may attempt retransmissions of the frame on
27
28 any link to which the TID is mapped, subject to the applicable lifetime limit for that frame and subject to any
29 other restrictions that apply to the link where the retransmission is scheduled.
30
31
If an MLD does not have a block ack agreement with another MLD for a TID, then the QoS Data frames for
32
that TID with failed transmission attempts are delivered following the rules defined in 35.3.13 (Multi-link
33
34 device individually addressed data delivery without block ack negotiation).
35
NOTE—A retransmitted QoS Data frame is not encapsulated with a new PN when retransmitted on another link.
36
37
38 Individually addressed management frames with failed transmission attempts are delivered following the
39 rules defined in 35.3.14 (Multi-link device individually addressed Management frame delivery).
40
41 35.3.17 Enhanced multi-link single radio operation
42
43
44 The enhanced multi-link single radio (EMLSR) operation defined in this subclause allows a non-AP MLD
45 with multiple receive chains to listen on the EMLSR links when the corresponding non-AP STAs affiliated
46 with the non-AP MLD are in awake state as defined below for an initial Control frame sent by an AP
47
affiliated with an AP MLD in a non-HT (duplicate) PPDU, followed by frame exchanges on the link on
48
which the initial Control frame was received.
49
50
51 In EMLSR mode, a non-AP MLD shall follow the rules defined in this subclause.
52
53
A non-AP MLD may operate in the EMLSR mode on a specified set of the enabled links between the non-
54
AP MLD and its associated AP MLD. The specified set of the enabled links on which the EMLSR mode is
55
56 applied is called EMLSR links. The EMLSR links shall be indicated in the EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield of
57 the EML Control field of the EML Operating Mode Notification frame by setting the bit positions of the
58 EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield to 1. For the EMLSR mode enabled in a single radio non-AP MLD, the
59
STA(s) affiliated with the non-AP MLD that operates on the enabled link(s) that corresponds to the bit
60
position(s) of the EMLSR Link Bitmap subfield set to 0 shall be in doze state if a non-AP STA affiliated
61
62 with the non-AP MLD that operates on one of the EMLSR links is in awake state.
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 563
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

1 When a non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true (re)associates with an AP
2 MLD, the EMLSR mode is disabled by default.
3
4
An MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true shall set the EML Capabilities Present
5
6 subfield to 1 and shall set the EMLSR Support subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link
7 element (9.4.2.312.2 (Basic Multi-Link element)) to 1 in all Management frames that include the Basic
8 Multi-Link element except Authentication frames. An MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal
9
to false and dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true (see 35.3.18 (Enhanced multi-link multi-radio
10
operation)) shall set the EML Capabilities Present subfield to 1 and shall set the EMLSR Support subfield of
11
12 the EML Capabilities subfield to 0. An MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to false and
13 dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to false shall set the EML Capabilities Present subfield to 0.
14
15
When a non-AP MLD is operating in EMLSR mode on the EMLSR links, the non-AP STAs operating on
16
the EMLSR links and affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall not operate in dynamic SM power save mode
17
18 (11.2.6 (SM power save)) on the EMLSR links.
19
20 When a non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true intends to enable the EMLSR
21
mode on the EMLSR links, a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall transmit an EML
22
Operating Mode Notification frame with the EMLSR Mode subfield of the EML Control field of the frame
23
24 set to 1 to an AP affiliated with an AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true. An AP
25 affiliated with the AP MLD that received the EML Operating Mode Notification frame from the non-AP
26 STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD should transmit an EML Operating Mode Notification frame with the
27
EML Control field set to the same value as the EML Control field in the received EML Operation Mode
28
Notification frame, after the AP MLD is ready to serve the non-AP MLD in the EMLSR mode operation, to
29
30 one of the non-AP STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD within the timeout interval indicated in the
31 Transition Timeout subfield in the EML Capabilities subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element starting at the
32 end of the PPDU that is transmitted by the AP affiliated with the AP MLD carrying the immediate
33
acknowledgement to the EML Operating Mode Notification frame transmitted by the STA affiliated with the
34
non-AP MLD. After the successful transmission of the EML Operating Mode Notification frame by the non-
35
36 AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD, the non-AP MLD shall operate in the EMLSR mode and the other
37 non-AP STAs operating on the corresponding EMLSR links shall transition to active mode after the
38 transition delay indicated in the Transition Timeout subfield in the EML Capabilities subfield of the Basic
39
Multi-Link element or immediately after receiving an EML Operating Mode Notification frame from one of
40
the APs operating on the EMLSR links and affiliated with the AP MLD. Any of the other non-AP STAs
41
42 operating on the corresponding EMLSR link shall not transmit a frame with the Power Management subfield
43 set to 1 before receiving the EML Operating Mode Notification frame from one of the APs operating on the
44 EMLSR links and affiliated with the AP MLD or before the end of the timeout interval.
45
46
When a non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true intends to disable the EMLSR
47
48 mode, a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall transmit an EML Operating Mode Notification
49 frame with the EMLSR Mode subfield of the EML Control field of the frame set to 0 to an AP affiliated with
50 an AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLSROptionActivated equal to true. An AP affiliated with the AP MLD that
51
received the EML Operating Mode Notification frame from the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP
52
MLD should transmit an EML Operating Mode Notification frame with the EML Control field set to the
53
54 same value as the EML Control field in the received EML Operation Mode Notification frame, after the AP
55 MLD is no longer serving the non-AP MLD in the EMLSR mode operation, to one of the non-AP STAs
56 affiliated with the non-AP MLD within the timeout interval indicated in the Transition Timeout subfield in
57
the EML Capabilities subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element starting at the end of the PPDU that is
58
transmitted by the AP affiliated with the AP MLD carrying the immediate acknowledgement to the EML
59
60 Operating Mode Notification frame transmitted by the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD. After
61 the successful transmission of the EML Operating Mode Notification frame by the non-AP STA affiliated
62 with the non-AP MLD, the non-AP MLD shall disable the EMLSR mode and the other non-AP STAs
63
operating on the corresponding EMLSR links shall transition to power save mode after the transition delay
64
indicated in the Transition Timeout subfield in the EML Capabilities subfield of the Basic Multi-Link
65

NOTE 1—Each of the STAs on the other links of the EMLSR links can transmit a frame with the Power Management
8
subfield set to 1 and transition to power save mode immediately after successful transmission of the frame. (see
9

11
12 When a non-AP MLD is operating in the EMLSR mode with an AP MLD supporting the EMLSR mode, the
13 following applies:
14
— The non-AP MLD shall be able to listen on the EMLSR link(s), by having its affiliated non-AP
15
STA(s) corresponding to those links in awake state. The listening operation includes CCA and
16
17 receiving the initial Control frame of frame exchanges that is initiated by the AP MLD.
18
NOTE 2—A non-AP STA operating on one of the EMLSR links can change its power management mode and
19
follows the procedure in 11.2 (Power management). A non-AP STA can listen on one of the EMLSR links in
20
active mode or in PS mode when it is in awake state.
21
22 — An AP affiliated with the AP MLD that initiates frame exchanges that are not group addressed Data
23 or Management frames with the non-AP MLD on one of the EMLSR links shall begin the frame
24
exchanges by transmitting the initial Control frame to the non-AP MLD with the limitations
25
specified below.
26
27 • The initial Control frame of frame exchanges shall be sent in the non-HT PPDU or non-HT
28 duplicate PPDU format using a rate of 6 Mb/s, 12 Mb/s, or 24 Mb/s.
29
• The non-AP MLD shall indicate the minimum MAC padding duration of the Padding field of the
30
initial Control frame in the EMLSR Padding Delay subfield of the EML Capabilities subfield in
31
32 the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element carried in a (Re)Association Request
33 frame that it transmits. The non-AP MLD may include an updated EMLSR Padding Delay dura-
34 tion in the EMLSR Parameter Update field in the EML Operating Mode Notification frame. The
35
AP affiliated with the AP MLD shall set the MAC padding duration of the Padding field of the
36
initial Control frame to be greater than or equal to the MAC padding duration in the EMLSR
37
38 Padding Delay subfield.
39 • The initial Control frame shall be an MU-RTS Trigger frame or a BSRP Trigger frame. A non-
40 AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD that is in the listening operation and that receives an MU-
41
RTS Trigger Frame or BSRP Trigger frame addressed to it shall respond as defined in 35.5.2.3
42
(Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation) except when the frame exchanges initiated by the
43
44 initial Control frame on one of the EMLSR links overlaps with group addressed frame transmis-
45 sions on the other EMLSR link where the non-AP STA intends to receive the group addressed
46 frames. The number of spatial streams for the response to the BSRP Trigger frame shall be lim-
47
ited to one.
48
49
NOTE 3—Whether to use the MU-RTS Trigger frame or the BSRP Trigger frame as the initial Control frame to
50
initiate the frame exchanges is implementation specific and out of scope of this standard.
51
52
— After receiving the initial Control frame of frame exchanges and transmitting an immediate response
53
frame as a response to the initial Control frame, a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD that
54
55 was listening on the corresponding link shall be able to transmit or receive frames on the link on
56 which the initial Control frame was received and shall not transmit or receive on the other EMLSR
57 link(s) until the end of the frame exchanges, and subject to its spatial stream capabilities, operation
58
mode, and the minimum MAC padding duration of the Padding field of the initial Control frame, the
59
60
non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall be capable of receiving a PPDU that is sent
61 using more than one spatial stream on the link on which the initial Control frame was received a
62 SIFS after the end of its response frame transmission solicited by the initial Control frame. During
63 the frame exchanges, the other AP(s) affiliated with the AP MLD shall not transmit frames to the
64
other non-AP STA(s) affiliated with the non-AP MLD on the other EMLSR link(s).

35.3.18 Enhanced multi-link multi-radio operation
60
61
62 The enhanced multi-link multi-radio (EMLMR) operation defined in this subclause allows a non-AP MLD
63 with multiple radios in multiple links to listen on the EMLMR links when the corresponding STAs affiliated
64
with the non-AP MLD are in awake state as defined below for an initial frame sent by an AP affiliated with
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 569
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 570 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 an AP MLD in a PPDU whose Nss satisfy the receiving STA’s receiving capabilities, followed by frame
2 exchanges that satisfy the MCS, Nss capabilities in EMLMR mode on the link on which the initial frame
3
was received.
4
5
6 A non-AP MLD may operate in the EMLMR mode on a specified set of the enabled links as defined in
7 9.4.1.74 (EML Control field) between the non-AP MLD and its associated AP MLD. The specified set of the
8 enabled links in which the EMLMR mode is applied is called EMLMR links. A non-AP STA affiliated with
9
the non-AP MLD that is on an EMLMR link is an EMLMR STA. The EMLMR links shall be indicated in
10
the EMLMR Link Bitmap subfield of the EML Control field of the EML Operating Mode Notification
11
12 frame (see 9.6.35.8 (EML Operating Mode Notification frame details)) by setting the bit positions of the
13 EMLMR Link Bitmap subfield to 1.
14
15
An MLD with dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true shall set the EML Capabilities Present
16
subfield to 1 and shall set the EMLMR Support subfield of the Common Info field of transmitted Basic
17
18 Multi-Link elements to 1; otherwise, the MLD shall set the EMLMR Support subfield to 0 if present.
19
20 A non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true shall indicate the number of spatial
21
streams N that it supports for reception and transmission on any EMLMR link during EMLMR operation
22 SS
23 in the EMLMR Supported MCS And NSS Set subfield of the EML Control field of the EML Operating
24 Mode Notification frame.
25
26
A non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD operating on any of EMLMR links shall not be a 20 MHz-
27
only non-AP EHT STA.
28
29
30 The supported rates, HT-MCS, VHT-MCS, and HE-MCS for a bandwidth and N shall be the same as the
SS
31
supported EHT-MCS for the corresponding bandwidth and N unless the corresponding MCS is not
32 SS
33 defined. If the MCS is not defined in the corresponding PHY amendment, the highest MCS support is
34 implied.
35
36
When a non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true (re)associates with an AP
37
MLD, the EMLMR mode is disabled by default. If a non-AP MLD with
38
39 dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true intends to enable or disable EMLMR mode after
40 association with an AP MLD that sets its EMLMR Support subfield to 1, then a non-AP STA affiliated with
41 the non-AP MLD shall transmit an EML Operating Mode Notification frame with EMLMR Mode subfield
42
equal to 1 or 0, respectively.
43
44
45 After successful transmission of the EML Operating Mode Notification frame from the non-AP STA
46 affiliated with the non-AP MLD to an AP affiliated with an AP MLD, the non-AP STA initializes the
47 transition timeout timer with the value in the Transition Timeout subfield value of the Basic Multi-Link
48
element received from the AP MLD. After transmitting Ack solicited by the EML Operating Mode
49
Notification frame from a non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD, the AP MLD initializes the
50
51 transition timeout timer with the value in the Transition Timeout subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element
52 announced by the AP MLD. The transition timeout timer begins counting down from the end of the PPDU
53 containing the Ack solicited by the EML Operating Mode Notification frame. The AP should send an EML
54
Operating Mode Notification frame for confirming the mode switch at the AP MLD side to the non-AP STA
55
with EML Control field set to the same value as EML Control field in the received EML Operating Mode
56
57 Notification frame from the non-AP STA before the transition timeout expires.
58
59 The non-AP MLD shall transit to the indicated mode immediately after successfully receiving the EML
60
Operating Mode Notification frame from the AP or immediately after the transition timeout timer expires,
61
whichever comes first.
62
63
64

## 第 571 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 A non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEMLMROptionActivated equal to true shall indicate the minimum padding
2 duration required for the non-AP MLD for EMLMR link switch in the EMLMR Delay subfield in the
3
Common Info field of transmitted Basic Multi-Link elements.
4
5 NOTE 1—The EMLMR link switching, which is the action of switching transmit chains and receive chains from one
6 link to another link, can happen during the transmission time of the initial response frame. However, the duration of
7 initial response frame can be different depending on the initial frame. The non-AP MLD might determine the minimum
8 padding duration such that it can be satisfied even when the shortest initial response frame is used on EMLMR links
9 (e.g., a CTS frame in non-HT PPDU with the highest rate in the BSSBasicRateSet parameters).
10
11
When an AP affiliated with an AP MLD transmits a PPDU that initiates a frame exchange with a non-AP
12
MLD operating in EMLMR mode, the AP shall ensure that the padding duration of the PPDU is longer than
13
14 or equal to the minimum padding duration value indicated by the EMLMR Delay field of the Basic Multi-
15 Link element received from the non-AP MLD.
16
17 NOTE 2—The initial frame exchange can be any frame exchange with the requirement the soliciting frame needs to
18 satisfy the padding requirement, e.g., through Trigger frame padding if the soliciting frame is Trigger frame, through
19 MPDU Delimiter padding if the soliciting frame is carried in A-MPDU.
20
21 Within a TXOP initiated by an AP affiliated with AP MLD with an EMLMR STA affiliated with a non-AP
22 MLD as the TXOP responder, the non-AP MLD shall switch to its per-link spatial stream capabilities
23
defined by EHT Capabilities element or the latest OM (if exists) after the time indicated in the EMLMR
24
Transition Delay subfield of the EML Capabilities subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-
25
26 Link element if any of the following conditions is met and this is defined as the end of the frame exchange
27 sequence:
28
— The MAC of the STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD that received the initial frame does not
29
receive a PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive during a timeout interval of aSIFSTime + aSlotTime
30
31 + aRxPHYStartDelay starting at the end of the PPDU transmitted by the STA affiliated with the non-
32 AP MLD as a response to the most recently received frame from the AP affiliated with the AP MLD
33 or starting at the end of the reception of the PPDU containing a frame for the STA from the AP
34
affiliated with the AP MLD that does not require immediate acknowledgement.
35
36 — The MAC of the STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD that received the initial frame receives a
37 PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive during a timeout interval of aSIFSTime + aSlotTime +
38
aRxPHYStartDelay starting at the end of the PPDU transmitted by the STA affiliated with the non-
39
AP MLD as a response to the most recently received frame from the AP affiliated with the AP MLD
40
41 or starting at the end of the reception of the PPDU containing a frame for the STA from the AP
42 affiliated with the AP MLD that does not require immediate acknowledgement and the STA
43 affiliated with the non-AP MLD does not detect, within the PPDU corresponding to the PHY-
44
RXSTART.indication any of the following frames:
45
46 • an individually addressed frame with the RA equal to the MAC address of the STA affiliated
47 with the non-AP MLD
48 • a Trigger frame that has one of the User Info fields addressed to the STA affiliated with the non-
49
AP MLD
50
• a CTS-to-self frame with the RA equal to the MAC address of the AP affiliated with the AP
51
52 MLD
53 • a Multi-STA BlockAck frame that has one of the Per AID TID Info fields addressed to the STA
54 affiliated with the non-AP MLD
55
• a NDP Announcement frame that has one of the STA Info fields addressed to the STA affiliated
56
with the non-AP MLD and a sounding NDP
57

59
recently received frame from the AP affiliated with the AP MLD that requires immediate response
60
after a SIFS.
61

64 addressed to the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD if the AP intends to continue the frame

1 exchanges with the STA and did not receive the response frame from this STA for the most recently

4
When a non-AP MLD operates in the EMLMR mode, after the initial frame exchange subject to its per-link
5
6 spatial stream capabilities and operating mode defined by the exchanged Operating Mode Notification

After the end of the frame exchange sequence, each non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD in the
19

20
21 and operating mode defined by EHT Capabilities element, the exchanged Operating Mode Notification

24
Each EMLMR STA independently maintains its own power management mode and awake/doze state in
25
power save mode.

30
MLD. The non-AP MLD shall switch to its per-link spatial stream capabilities defined by EHT Capabilities
31
element or the latest OM (if exists) on the EMLMR links after the time duration indicated in the EMLMR
32
33 Delay subfield after the end of the TXOP.

36

37
affiliated with the AP MLD shall not initiate the frame exchanges with another EMLMR STA of the non-AP
38
39 MLD, and another EMLMR STA of the non-AP MLD shall not do the frame exchange with its associated
40 AP.

## 第 573 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.19 NSTR mobile AP MLD operation
2
3
35.3.19.1 General
4
5
6 An AP MLD that is an NSTR mobile AP MLD shall set dot11EHTNSTRMobileAPMLDImplemented to
7 true, otherwise it shall set dot11EHTNSTRMobileAPMLDImplemented to false. An NSTR mobile AP
8 MLD shall have at most two links and shall follow the restrictions below:
9
— If NSTR mobile AP MLD has two links, the links shall be part of an NSTR link pair
10
11 — The NSTR mobile AP MLD shall operate with one or two affiliated APs including the AP operating
12
on the primary link
13
14 — Each AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD may optionally support the following features in
15 addition to the optional features supported by an AP affiliated with an AP MLD which is not an
16 NSTR mobile AP MLD:
17
18 • Support of DL and UL OFDMA operation
19 • Support of two or more spatial streams
20 • Support for 160 MHz operating channel width in the 6 GHz band
21
• Support for MRU for DL/UL OFDMA if DL/UL OFDMA operation is supported
22
23 — The NSTR mobile AP MLD is in a mobile device that is typically battery powered
24
25 NOTE 1—An NSTR mobile AP MLD follows the same rules defined in 35.3.2 (Multi-link device addressing).
26
27 An NSTR mobile AP MLD shall designate one link of an NSTR link pair as the primary link. The other link
28 of the NSTR link pair is the nonprimary link. When the NSTR mobile AP MLD intends to change the
29 channel/operating class for the primary link, it shall perform channel switch procedure. The NSTR mobile
30
AP MLD shall schedule for transmissions of Beacon and Probe Response frames and group addressed Data
31
frames only on the primary link.
32
33
NOTE 2—An NSTR mobile AP MLD that intends to switch its primary and nonprimary links performs a simultaneous
34
channel switch on the primary link and nonprimary link following procedures defined in 11.8.8 (Selecting and
35
advertising a new channel), 11.8.9 (Channel Switch Announcement element operation), 11.9 (Extended channel
36
switching (ECS)), and 35.3.19.3 (NSTR mobile AP MLD multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel
37
switching, and channel quieting).
38
39
TSF timers of all APs affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD shall be the same.
40
41
NOTE 3—Since TSF timers of all APs affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD are the same, a non-AP MLD that is
42
associated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD only needs to maintain one TSF timer for all the links.
43
44
A non-AP MLD shall perform frame exchanges during the authentication, (re)association, and 4-way
45
46 handshake procedures only on the primary link of the NSTR mobile AP MLD.
47
48 NOTE 4—No frame exchange is allowed to be initiated through EDCA channel access on the nonprimary link alone
49
50 Non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD that is associated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD and APs
51 affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD shall follow the procedure defined in 35.3.16.6 (Start time sync
52 PPDUs medium access) when intending to transmit in the nonprimary link with the following additional
53
constraints:
54
55 — A non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD may initiate a PPDU transmission to its associated
56 AP affiliated with the NSTR mobile AP MLD in the nonprimary link only if the other non-AP STA
57
affiliated with the same non-AP MLD in the primary link is also initiating the PPDU as a TXOP
58
holder to its associated AP with the same start time.
59
60 — An AP affiliated with the NSTR mobile AP MLD may initiate a PPDU transmission to its associated
61 non-AP STA in the nonprimary link only if the other AP affiliated with the same NSTR mobile AP
62
MLD in the primary link is also initiating the PPDU as a TXOP holder with the same start time.
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 573
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 574 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 APs affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD that are simultaneously transmitting PPDUs to the associated
2 non-AP STAs shall align the end time of PPDUs following the same rules that are defined for an AP MLD in
3
35.3.16.5 (PPDU end time alignment on a NSTR link pair).
4
5
6 Non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD that are simultaneously transmitting PPDUs to the associated
7 APs affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD shall align the end time of PPDUs following the same rules
8 that are defined for an AP MLD in 35.3.16.5 (PPDU end time alignment on a NSTR link pair).
9
10 NOTE 5—The end time alignment of PPDUs carrying the response frames follow the same rules as those for the
11 soliciting PPDUs.
12
13 An NSTR mobile AP MLD shall set the SRS Support subfield in the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-
14
Link element it transmits to 1 to indicate support for the reception of a frame that carries an SRS Control
15
subfield if its dot11SRSOptionImplemented is true; otherwise, the MLD shall set it to 0.
16
17
18 If non-AP STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD or its associated NSTR mobile AP MLD simultaneously
19 transmit PPDUs to a STA affiliated with an MLD that has dot11SRSOptionImplemented equal to true, and
20
the transmitted PPDUs solicit control response frames and the MLD intends to align the end times of the
21
PPDUs sent in response by the peer STAs, then at least one of the PPDUs soliciting a control response frame
22
23 shall carry an MPDU with SRS Control subfield following the procedure defined in 35.3.16.5.2 (End time
24 alignment of response PPDUs using SRS Control field).
25
26
An AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD that is operating on the primary link shall not be disabled.
27
28
29 An NSTR mobile AP MLD shall not set the TID-To-Link Mapping Negotiation Support subfield of MLD
30 Capabilities field of the Basic Multi-Link element to 3. The TID-to-link mapping between the non-AP MLD
31 and NSTR mobile AP MLD shall be default mapping mode or all TIDs mapped to the primary link.
32
33
35.3.19.2 Discovery of an NSTR mobile AP MLD
34
35
36 The discovery procedure for an NSTR mobile AP MLD is the same as the procedure described in 35.3.4
37 (Discovery of an AP MLD) with the following exceptions:
38
— An AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD and that is operating on the primary link of an
39
40 NSTR link pair shall indicate that it is an NSTR mobile AP MLD by setting AP MLD Type
41 Indication subfield to 1 in MLD Capabilities and Operations field of Common Info field in the Basic
42 Multi-Link element.
43
44 — An AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD and that is operating on the primary link of an
45 NSTR link pair shall include a Reduced Neighbor Report element with the MLD Parameters subfield
46 present in a TBTT Information field corresponding to a reported AP affiliated with the NSTR mobile
47
AP MLD and that is operating on the nonprimary link of the NSTR link pair in a Beacon and Probe
48
Response frames that it transmits. The Neighbor AP TBTT Offset subfield, the BSSID subfield, the
49
50 Short-BSSID subfield, the BSS Parameters subfield and the 20 MHz PSD subfield shall not be
51 present in the TBTT Information Field for that reported AP. The TBTT Information Field Type
52 subfield shall set to 1 to identify, together with the TBTT Information Length subfield, the format of
53
the TBTT Information field for the reported AP operating on the nonprimary link.
54
55 — A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall not transmit a Probe Request frame to the AP
56 affiliated with the NSTR mobile AP MLD and that is operating on the nonprimary link of the NSTR
57 link pair. To request a complete profile of the AP operating on the nonprimary link, a non-AP STA
58
affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall send a multi-link probe request to an AP affiliated with the
59
NSTR mobile AP MLD and that is operating on the primary link (see 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link
60
61 probe request and response)).
62
— The NSTR mobile AP MLD shall not respond to any received Probe Request frames on the
63
nonprimary link.
64
65

## 第 575 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.19.3 NSTR mobile AP MLD multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended
2 channel switching, and channel quieting
3
4
Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting for an AP
5
6 affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD on the nonprimary link follow the same rules defined in 35.3.11
7 (Multi-link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting) with the
8 following exceptions:
9
— An AP affiliated with an NSTR mobile AP MLD on the primary link may schedule channel
10
11 switching and quiet intervals for the AP affiliated with the same NSTR mobile AP MLD on the
12 nonprimary link by including the corresponding elements in the STA Profile field of the Per-STA
13 Profile subelement corresponding to the AP on the nonprimary link carried in Beacon frames and
14
Probe Response frames that it transmits on the primary link.
15
16 — The timing fields in the Channel Switch Announcement element, the Extended Channel Switch
17 Announcement element, the Quiet element, and the Quiet Channel element shall be applied in
18
reference to the most recent TBTT and BI indicated in the corresponding element(s) of the AP
19
operating on the primary link.
20
21
NOTE 1—The TBTT and the BI are not defined for a BSS of an AP operating on the nonprimary link. This is because
22
the AP does not send the Beacon frame for its BSS.
23
24
— The Switch Time field in the Max Channel Switch Time element included in the Per-STA Profile
25
26 subelement corresponding to the AP operating on the nonprimary link indicates an adjusted
27 estimated time until the corresponding AP resumes BSS operation on the new channel/class.
28
29 NOTE 2—The non-AP MLD can determine that the AP operating on the nonprimary link is in process for switching its
30 operating channel/class to the new channel/class when the most recently received per-STA profile that is corresponding
31 to the nonprimary link includes the Max Channel Switch Time element and does not include the (Extended) Channel
32 Switch Announcement element. While AP operating on the nonprimary link is in the process of channel switching, the
33 non-AP MLD does not transmit a frame to that AP.
34
35 35.3.20 Multi-link operation in a multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set
36
37
Each AP in a multiple BSSID set is a member of a different ESS while all APs affiliated with the same AP
38
MLD belong to the same ESS (see 35.3.1 (General) and AA.3 (Example illustrating the relationship between
39
40 multi-link operation and multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set)). Therefore, an AP MLD shall not
41 have more than one affiliated AP amongst APs that are members of the same multiple BSSID set.
42
43
Each AP in a co-hosted BSSID set is a member of a different ESS while all APs affiliated with the same AP
44
MLD belong to the same ESS (see 35.3.1 (General) and AA.3 (Example illustrating the relationship between
45
46 multi-link operation and multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set)). Therefore, an AP MLD shall not
47 have more than one affiliated AP amongst APs that are members of the same co-hosted BSSID set.
48
49
Each AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall be independently configured to operate as a transmitted or as a
50
nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, or as an AP belonging to a co-hosted BSSID set, or as an AP
51
52 that is neither a member of a multiple BSSID set nor a member of a co-hosted BSSID set. Annex AA
53 provides example configurations. Each AP MLD, that is affiliated with APs belonging to a multiple BSSID
54 set or a co-hosted BSSID set, shall independently assign a Link ID to each of its affiliated APs.
55
56
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD that is a member of a multiple BSSID set shall follow the procedures
57
58 described in 11.1.3.8 (Multiple BSSID Procedure). A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall
59 follow the procedure described in 11.1.3.8 (Multiple BSSID Procedure) during discovery and after
60 association when the peer AP is a member of a multiple BSSID set. An AP affiliated with an AP MLD that
61
is a member of a co-hosted BSSID set shall follow the rules described in 26.17.7 (Co-hosted BSSID set). A
62
non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall follow the procedure described in 26.17.7 (Co-hosted
63
64 BSSID set) when the peer AP is a member of a co-hosted BSSID set.
65

1 An AP corresponding to the transmitted BSSID shall not include a Basic Multi-Link element in the
2 Nontransmitted BSSID Profile subelement of a Multiple BSSID element unless the AP corresponding
3
nontransmitted BSSID is affiliated with an AP MLD. When carried in a Nontransmitted BSSID Profile
4
subelement of a Multiple BSSID element included in a Beacon or Probe Response frame that is not a multi-
5
6 link probe response, the Basic Multi-Link element shall not include the Per-STA Profile subelement
7 corresponding to reported AP affiliated with the same AP MLD unless conditions defined in 35.3.11 (Multi-
8 link procedures for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting) are satisfied for
9
the reported AP.
10
11
12 When an AP corresponding to a transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set transmits a multi-link probe
13 response in response to a multi-link probe request directed to an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted
14 BSSID in the same multiple BSSID set (see 35.3.4.2 (Use of multi-link probe request and response)), the
15
Probe Response frame shall also include Basic Multi-Link element corresponding to the AP MLD, with
16
which the transmitted BSSID is affiliated with, outside the Multiple BSSID element and the Basic Multi-
17
18 Link element shall not carry complete profile for any of the reported APs and shall not include the AP MLD
19 ID subfield in the Common Info field.
20
21 NOTE—When an AP corresponding to a transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set transmits a multi-link probe
22 response in response to a multi-link probe request directed to an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID in the
23 same multiple BSSID set, the Probe Response frame:
24
25 — carries Basic Multi-Link element, outside the Multiple BSS element, containing a profile of the requested AP(s)
26 affiliated with the AP MLD with which the AP corresponding to that nontransmitted BSSID is affiliated with
27 and the AP MLD ID subfield of the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element is set to the BSSID
28
Index of the nontransmitted BSSID.
29
— can include Basic Multi-Link element corresponding to the AP MLD(s) of other APs corresponding to the
30
31 nontransmitted BSSIDs in the multiple BSSID set. Such Basic Multi-Link element(s) do not carry complete
32 profile for any reported AP(s) and are carried in the corresponding nontransmitted BSSID profile(s) contained in
33 the multiple BSSID element.
34 — carries Reduced Neighbor Report element containing information of the other AP(s) affiliated with the
35
transmitting AP’s (transmitted BSSID’s) AP MLD and the information of other AP(s) affiliated with the AP
36
MLD(s) of all the nontransmitted BSSIDs in the same multiple BSSID set by following the rules in 35.3.4.1 (AP
37
behavior).
38
39
40 35.3.21 TDLS procedure in multi-link operation
41
42 35.3.21.1 General
43
44
When the frames that are exchanged during TDLS discovery or setup do not include a TDLS Multi-Link
45
46 element or include a TDLS Multi-Link element containing only the Common Info field carrying only the AP
47 MLD MAC Address, then the TDLS direct link discovery or setup respectively, is for a single link. A TDLS
48 non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall only negotiate TDLS over a single link.
49
50 NOTE 1—The single link TDLS direct link can be established between a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD
51 and another non-AP STA that might not be affiliated with a non-AP MLD.
52
53 A non-AP MLD that intends to establish a single link TDLS direct link with a peer STA on one of its links
54
follows the procedures defined in 11.20 (Tunneled direct-link setup), with additional rules as defined in
55
35.3.21.2 (TDLS direct link over a single link).
56
57
58 TDLS discovery and setup (typically discovery frame exchange followed by setup frame exchange) between
59 a non-AP MLD and a peer STA involves frames that are sent and received via an intermediate AP (MLD) or
60
sent and received through direct communication (see Table 11-13a (Frame type and their pathway in a
61
TDLS setup)).
62
63
64
65
576 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 577 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 NOTE 2—As an alternative to transmitting a TDLS Discovery Request frame, a non-AP MLD can discover a TDLS
2 peer by sending an unsolicited TDLS Discovery Response frame or a TDLS Setup Request frame without exchanging
3 TDLS discovery frames (see 11.20.3 (TDLS discovery)).
4
5 35.3.21.2 TDLS direct link over a single link
6
7
When a non-AP MLD that has performed multi-link setup with an AP MLD establishes a single link TDLS
8
9 direct link on one of its links, it shall set the context (i.e., security, SN/PN, BA) for the TDLS direct link with
10 respect to the MLD MAC address of the non-AP MLD. For ease of description in the rest of this subclause,
11 the single link TDLS context is described with respect to a TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP
12
MLD. The TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD shall be able to receive frames sent over the
13
direct link with RA field set to the MLD MAC address of the non-AP MLD. When a TDLS non-AP STA
14
15 affiliated with the non-AP MLD initiates TDLS discovery or TDLS setup, it shall set the TA field of frames
16 sent over the TDLS direct link to the MLD MAC address of the non-AP MLD.
17
18
Frames that traverse the intermediate AP (MLD) are sent or received by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-
19
AP MLD. Frames sent over the direct link are sent or received by a TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with the
20
21 non-AP MLD. The TDLS direct link, when successfully established, is between the TDLS non-AP STA
22 affiliated with the non-AP MLD and a TDLS peer STA at the other end of the direct link.
23
24
If the TDLS initiator is a non-AP MLD, then the TDLS initiator STA Address field contained in the Link
25
Identifier element of the TDLS frames shall be set to the MLD MAC address of the non-AP MLD.
26
27
28 When a non-AP MLD initiates a TDLS discovery operation, it may need to transmit more than one TDLS
29 Discovery Request frame with the BSSID field of the Link Identifier element set to a different BSSID in
30
each attempt. In each instance, the attempted BSSID corresponds to a different AP affiliated with the AP
31
MLD. Since the TDLS Discovery Response frame is received over the direct link, the initiating non-AP
32
33 MLD shall be able to determine the link(s) on which the peer STA or non-AP MLD is operating on.
34
NOTE 1—Due to the nature of multi-link operation, when a Data frame traverses an AP MLD, it can be relayed on any
35
available link. Furthermore, when a frame that was transmitted by a STA of a non-AP MLD traverses an AP MLD, the
36
AP MLD sets the SA field to the transmitting STA’s non-AP MLD MAC address. Therefore, when a non-AP STA
37
affiliated with a non-AP MLD receives a frame from its corresponding associated AP that is affiliated with an AP MLD,
38
it cannot determine the link where the frame originated from and it cannot determine if the initiating STA is affiliated
39
with a non-AP MLD or not. Consequently, the non-AP MLD initiating a TDLS discovery does not know the BSSID of
40
the link where the intended peer STA is operating on.
41
42
43 When attempting to establish a TDLS direct link over a single link, a TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with a
44 non-AP MLD shall include a TDLS Multi-Link element containing only the Common Info field carrying
45 only the AP MLD MAC Address field (see 9.4.2.312.5 (TDLS Multi-Link element)) in the TDLS Discovery
46
Request frame and TDLS Discovery Response frame that it transmits. A TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with
47
a non-AP MLD shall not respond to a TDLS Discovery Request frame if the frame carries TDLS Multi-Link
48
49 element and the MLD MAC address carried in the AP MLD MAC Address field of the TDLS Multi-Link
50 element does not match the MLD MAC address of the AP MLD with which the non-AP MLD has
51 performed multi-link setup.
52
53
After TDLS peer is successfully discovered, the non-AP MLD shall set the BSSID field contained in the
54
55 Link Identifier element of the subsequent TDLS frames to the BSSID of the corresponding AP affiliated
56 with the AP MLD that is operating on the link on which the TDLS direct link is established or being
57 established.
58
59
When attempting to establish a TDLS direct link over a single link, a TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with a
60
61 non-AP MLD shall include the TDLS Multi-Link element containing only the Common Info field carrying
62 only the AP MLD MAC Address field (see 9.4.2.312.5 (TDLS Multi-Link element)) in the TDLS Setup
63 Request frame. A TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall not respond to a TDLS Setup
64
Request frame if the frame carries TDLS Multi-Link element and the MLD MAC address carried in the AP
65

1 MLD MAC Address field of the TDLS Multi-Link element does not match the MLD MAC address of the
2 AP MLD with which the non-AP MLD has performed multi-link setup. A TDLS non-AP STA affiliated
3
with a non-AP MLD shall include the TDLS Multi-Link element in the TDLS Setup Response frame if the
4
soliciting TDLS Setup Request frame included TDLS Multi-Link element. A TDLS non-AP STA affiliated
5
6 with a non-AP MLD shall not respond to a TDLS Setup Response frame if the frame carries TDLS Multi-
7 Link element and the MLD MAC address carried in the AP MLD MAC Address field of the TDLS Multi-
8 Link element does not match the MLD MAC address of the AP MLD with which the non-AP MLD has
9
performed multi-link setup. A TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall include the TDLS
10
Multi-Link element in the TDLS Setup Confirm frame if the preceding TDLS Setup Response frame
11
12 included TDLS Multi-Link element.
13
14 When both STAs that are involved in a single link TDLS setup include a TDLS Multi-Link element carrying
15
the AP MLD MAC Address field in the frames exchanged during the TDLS setup phase, the TDLS TPK
16
generation shall include the AP MLD MAC address in addition to the MAC address of the affiliated AP
17
18 where the TDLS direct link is being established, as defined in Equation (12-2). When at least one of the
19 STAs that are involved in a single link TDLS setup, does not include TDLS Multi-Link element, in the
20 frames exchanged during TDLS setup phase, the STAs shall derive the TPK as defined in Equation (12-1).
21
22
After a TDLS direct link is successfully established between the TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with a non-
23
24 AP MLD and a TDLS peer STA at the other end of the TDLS direct link, STAs affiliated with the non-AP
25 MLD shall cease transmitting MSDUs to the TDLS peer, at the other end, through their associated AP that is
26 affiliated with the AP MLD to which the non-AP MLD has performed multi-link setup.
27
28 NOTE 2—The non-AP STAs affiliated with the non-AP MLD can transmit/receive frames to/from other STAs or the DS
29 via the AP MLD.
30
31 Figure 35-37 (Example A of TDLS discovery initiated by a non-AP MLD) and Figure 35-38 (Example B of
32
TDLS discovery initiated by a non-AP MLD) illustrate the scenario where the TDLS discovery is initiated
33
by a non-AP MLD (MLD_S). MLD_S has performed multi-link setup with an AP MLD (MLD_A). MLD_S
34
35 has two affiliated STAs, STA1 and STA2. STA3 is not capable of performing multi-link operation and is not
36 affiliated with a non-AP MLD. MLD_A has two affiliated APs, AP1 and AP2, where AP1 operates on link 1
37 and AP2 operates on link 2. STA1 and STA3 operate on link 1 and are associated with AP1. STA2 operates
38
on link 2 and is associated with AP2. In the example, MLD_S initiates TDLS discovery by transmitting two
39
TDLS Discovery Request frames (which are Data frames) as it does not know which link STA3 is operating
40
41 on and whether STA3 is an MLD or a STA not affiliated with an MLD. The first TDLS Discovery Request
42 frame (shown in Figure 35-37 (Example A of TDLS discovery initiated by a non-AP MLD)) has the BSSID
43 field in the Link Identifier element set to the BSSID of AP1 and the second TDLS Discovery Request frame
44
has this field set to the BSSID of AP2 (shown in Figure 35-38 (Example B of TDLS discovery initiated by a
45
non-AP MLD)). Both the frames have their A3 (DA) set to the STA3 MAC address and the To DS subfield
46
47 of the Frame Control field set to 1. The TDLS Discovery Request frame can be transmitted over either link 1
48 (through STA1 as represented by solid line) or link 2 (through STA2 as represented by dotted line). When
49 the TDLS Discovery Request frame is received at the AP MLD (i.e., through AP1 or AP2), it routes the
50
frame to STA3, through AP1 by setting the From DS subfield of the Frame Control field to 1 and A3 (SA) to
51
the non-AP MLD Address (i.e., MLD_S). STA3 discards the TDLS Discovery Request frame that had the
52
53 BSSID field of Link Identifier element set to BSSID of AP2 as it does not recognize the BSSID. STA3
54 recognizes the BSSID set to AP1 and responds with a TDLS Discovery Response frame, which is a
55 Management frame, with the RA set to the MLD_S and both To DS and From DS subfields set to 0. STA3
56
ignores the TDLS Multi-Link element as it does not recognize this element. The TDLS STA affiliated with
57
MLD_S receives the TDLS Discovery Response frame, which is sent on the TDLS direct link (see Table 11-
58
59 13a (Frame type and their pathway in a TDLS setup)). The TDLS initiator STA Address field and the TDLS
60 responder STA Address field contained in the Link Identifier element (denoted as LI in the figure) are
61

30

31

37
38 Request Discovery frame and identifies the intended TDLS direct link using the BSSID field of the Link
39 Identifier element. In this case, the BSSID is set to AP1 (i.e., link 1), so the non-AP MLD enables the TDLS
40 non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD on link 1. The TDLS non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP
41
MLD responds by transmitting a TDLS Discovery Response frame on the direct link to STA3 with the To
42
DS and From DS subfields of the Frame Control field set to 0, and A1 set to STA3 (i.e., RA = STA3,
43
44 TA = MLD_S, A3 = AP1). In both the TDLS Discovery Request and TDLS Discovery Response frames, the
45 BSSID, the TDLS initiator STA Address, and the TDLS responder STA Address fields in the Link Identifier
46 element (represented as LI in the figure) are set to AP1, STA3, and MLD_S, respectively.
47
48
Figure 35-40 (Transmission of TDLS Setup Request frame between two STAs each affiliated with a
49
50 different non-AP MLD) and Figure 35-41 (Transmission of TDLS Setup Response frame between two STAs
51 each affiliated with a different non-AP MLD) illustrate the case where a single link TDLS direct link is set
52 up between two non-AP MLDs that have performed multi-link setup with the same AP MLD. The example
53
assumes that the two non-AP MLDs have performed TDLS discovery and that the initiating non-AP MLD
54
(in this example, MLD_S) has decided to perform single link TDLS setup for link 1. As shown in the
55
56 figures, the TDLS Setup Request frame is transmitted by the non-AP MLD, MLD_S, through affiliated

47 35.3.22 Proxy ARP service in AP MLDs
48
49 Implementation of the proxy ARP service is optional for an AP MLD. When supported, an AP MLD
50
implements the proxy ARP service, as defined in 11.21.14 (Proxy ARP service), at the MLD level.
51
52
53 All APs affiliated with an AP MLD shall have the same setting of the Proxy ARP field in the Extended
54 Capabilities element. If an AP MLD supports Proxy ARP service, then all affiliated APs of the AP MLD
55 shall set the Proxy ARP field to 1 in their Extended Capabilities elements.
56
57 NOTE—For an associated STA that is not affiliated with an MLD, the Proxy ARP service is provided by the AP
58 affiliated with the AP MLD, with which the STA is associated, as defined in 11.21.14 (Proxy ARP service).
59

23 MLD1(cid:882)M
24 MLD1(cid:882)IPv4,(cid:3)MLD1(cid:882)IPv6
25 Figure 35-44—Example of proxy ARP service by an AP MLD
26
27
28
29 In this example, the AP MLD has two affiliated APs: AP1 operates on channel 1 in the 5 GHz band an AP2
30
operates on channel 2 in the 6 GHz band. The AP MLD, AP1 and AP2, are connected to the DS, which is
31
connected to the LAN via a portal (e.g., via Ethernet interface(s)). Two non-AP MLDs, Non-AP MLD1 and
32
33 Non-AP MLD2, each with two affiliated STAs operating on channel 1 and channel 2, respectively, are
34 associated with the AP MLD. The MLD MAC address of Non-AP MLD1 is MLD1-M, while IPv4 address
35 MLD1-IPv4 and IPv6 address MLD1-IPv6 are assigned to Non-AP MLD1. STA5, which is a STA that is
36
not affiliated with a non-AP MLD, is associated with AP1. The MAC address of STA5 is STA5-M, while
37
IPv4 address STA5-IPv4 is assigned to STA5. STA6 is a device connected to the AP-MLD via the DS.
38
39 When the AP MLD receives from STA6, via the DS, an ARP request with the target IP address set as the
40 Non-AP MLD1’s IPv4 address, MLD1-IPv4, the proxy ARP service in AP MLD responds to STA6 with an
41 ARP response packet with the Sender’s MAC Address set as MLD1-M. When the AP MLD receives from
42
STA5, on channel 1, an ARP request with the target IP address set as MLD1-IPv4, the proxy ARP service in
43
AP MLD responds to STA5 with an ARP response packet with the Sender’s MAC Address set as MLD1-M.
44
45 When the AP MLD receives from Non-AP MLD2, on channel 2, a Neighbor Solicitation message with the
46 target IP address set as the Non-AP MLD1’s IPv6 address, MLD1-IPv6, the proxy ARP service in AP MLD
47 responds to Non-AP MLD2 with a Neighbor Advertisement message with the target link layer address set as
48
MLD1-M. It is not shown in the figure, but when an ARP request is received by AP1 from non-AP STA3
49
affiliated with Non-AP MLD2, on channel 1, with the target IP address set as STA5’s IPv4 address, STA5-
50
51 IPv4, the proxy ARP service in AP1 responds to non-AP STA3 with an ARP response packet with the
52 Sender’s MAC Address set as STA5-M. However, if the ARP request with the target IP address set as
53 STA5’s IPv4 address is received by AP2 from non-AP STA4 affiliated with Non-AP MLD2, on channel 2,
54
the ARP request is forwarded to AP1 (e.g., via the DS) and the proxy ARP service in AP1 responds to non-
55
AP STA4 (e.g., via the DS and AP2) with an ARP response packet with the Sender’s MAC Address set as
56
57 STA5-M.
58
59 35.3.23 BSS transition management for MLDs
60
61
A STA affiliated with an MLD has dot11BSSTransitionActivated equal to true (see 11.21.7.1 (BSS
62
63 transition capability)) and shall follow the procedure define in 11.21.7 (BSS transition management for
64 network load balancing), except that:
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 583
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 584 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — The procedure is applied between the SMEs of an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD and not between the
2 SMEs of an AP affiliated with an AP MLD and a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD.
3
— If the Neighbor Report element that is carried in the BSS Transition Candidate List Entries field of a
4
5 BSS Transition Management Query/Request or Response frame includes a Basic Multi-Link
6 element, it describes the preference for a target AP MLD candidate and not for a target BSS
7 candidate, otherwise it describes the preference for a target BSS candidate.
8
9 — The Preference field value of a Neighbor Report element that includes a Basic Multi-Link element
10 describing an AP MLD provides the indication of preference for the given AP MLD, within the
11 given list at the given time.
12
— If an AP MLD intends to provide preference for a reported AP MLD without recommendation on
13
14 specific affiliated APs, it shall:
15
• include a Neighbor Report element for one of the APs affiliated with the AP MLD, and include a
16
Basic Multi-Link element in the Neighbor Report.
17
18 • set to 0 all subfields of the Presence Bitmap subfield.
19 • not include any Per-STA Profile subelement in the Basic Multi-Link element.
20
— If an AP MLD intends to provide preference for a reported AP MLD with only a subset of
21
recommended affiliated APs, it shall:
22
23 • include a Neighbor Report element for one of the recommended APs affiliated with the AP
24
MLD, and include a Basic Multi-Link element in the Neighbor Report element of this reported
25
AP.
26
27 • include a Link ID Info field in the Common Info field of the Basic Multi-Link element with the
28 field value set to that corresponding to the AP reported in the Neighbor Report element.
29 • set to 0 all subfields of the Presence Bitmap subfield of the Basic Multi-Link element included in
30
the Neighbor Report element of the reported AP except the Link ID Info Present subfield.
31
• include in the Basic Multi-Link element included in the Neighbor Report element of the reported
32
33 AP a Per-STA Profile subfield only for each of the other recommended affiliated APs (if any),
34 and with all the fields set to 0 in the STA Control field, except the Link ID field.
35 • If multiple Neighbor Report elements are used to report the same AP MLD with the same recom-
36
mended subset of affiliated APs, the Preference field value in these elements shall be the same. If
37
multiple Neighbor Report elements are used to report the same AP MLD with different recom-
38
39 mended subset of affiliated APs, the Preference field value in these elements may be different.
40 — When an AP affiliated with an AP MLD transmits a BSS Transition Management Request frame
41
with the Link Removal Imminent subfield equal to 0 and the Disassociation Imminent field equal to
42
1 to a non-AP MLD, the Disassociation Timer field in the BSS Transition Management Request
43
44 frame shall be set to 0 or set to the number of TBTTs that will occur prior to the AP MLD
45 disassociating the non-AP MLD.
46
— When an AP affiliated with an AP MLD transmits a BSS Transition Management Request frame
47
48 with the Link Removal Imminent subfield equal to 0 and the BSS Termination Included field equal
49 to 1 to a non-AP MLD, the BSS termination means that the AP MLD is shutting down, and the non-
50 AP MLD will be disassociated from the AP MLD.
51
— A non-AP MLD that receives a BSS Transition Management Request frame with the Link Removal
52
53 Imminent subfield equal to 1 follows the procedure defined in 35.3.6.2.2 (Removing affiliated APs).
54
55 NOTE—An AP MLD can use this protocol to recommend a non-AP MLD to do (re)association with the same AP MLD
56 with a different set of links, or to initiate a TID-to-link mapping change if that would match the recommendation.

## 第 585 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.3.24 TWT operation
2
3
35.3.24.1 General
4
5
6 An EHT TWT STA shall follow the rules as described in 26.8 (TWT operation), except that within trigger-
7 enabled SPs, the Trigger frame may be an MU-RTS TXS Trigger frame with response rules defined in
8 35.2.1.2 (Triggered TXOP sharing procedure).
9
10
35.3.24.2 Individual TWT agreements
11
12
13 An MLD may negotiate individual TWT agreements with a peer MLD as defined in 10.47.1 (TWT
14 overview) and 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements) via an enabled link except the following:
15
— A TWT requesting STA affiliated with the MLD may indicate the link(s) that are requested for
16
17 setting up TWT agreement(s) in the Link ID Bitmap subfield, if present, of a TWT element in the
18 TWT request.
19
• If only one link is indicated in the Link ID Bitmap subfield of the TWT element, then a single
20
21 TWT agreement is requested for the STA affiliated with the same MLD, which is operating on
22 the indicated link. The Target Wake Time field of the TWT element shall be in reference to the
23 TSF time of the link indicated by the TWT element.
24
— A TWT responding STA affiliated with a peer MLD that receives a TWT request that contains a
25
26 Link ID Bitmap subfield in a TWT element shall respond with a TWT response that indicates the
27 link(s) in the Link ID Bitmap field of a TWT element. The link(s), if present, in the TWT element
28 carried in the TWT response, shall be the same as the link(s) indicated in the TWT element of the
29
soliciting TWT request.
30
31
NOTE—The individual TWT agreement is negotiated between the STAs affiliated with the MLDs that are operating on
32
an enabled link and is not negotiated between two MLDs.
33
34
During the negotiation of individual TWT agreements, a TWT requesting STA affiliated with an MLD and a
35
TWT responding STA affiliated with a peer MLD may include multiple TWT elements where each of the
36
37 Link ID Bitmap subfields in each TWT element indicates different link(s) in the same TWT Setup frame.
38 The TWT parameters provided by each TWT element shall be applied and be in reference to the respective
39 link that is indicated by the Link ID Bitmap subfield in that TWT element to setup TWT agreement on that
40
link.
41
42
43 An example of TWT agreements negotiated for multiple links is shown in Figure 35-45 (Example of TWT
44 agreements negotiation across multiple links).
45
46
47
AP 1 AP 2 AP 3 AP 1 AP 2 AP 3
48 AP MLD 2.4 GHz 5 GHz 6 GHz AP MLD 2.4 GHz 5 GHz 6 GHz

49 agreement setup

51

53
TWT agreements setup on link 1, link 2 and link 3

Figure 35-45—Example of TWT agreements negotiation across multiple links

60
61
62 In this example, an AP MLD has three affiliated APs: AP 1 operates on 2.4 GHz band, AP 2 operates on
63 5 GHz band, and AP 3 operates on 6 GHz band. Non-AP STA 1 affiliated with the non-AP MLD sends three
64
TWT elements in a TWT request to AP 1 affiliated with the AP MLD. These three TWT elements indicate
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 585
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 586 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 the links of AP 1, AP 2, and AP 3, respectively, requesting three TWT agreements to be setup on three links,
2 and they have different TWT parameters, such as target wake up time, and all are with a value of Demand
3
TWT in the TWT Setup Command field. AP 1 sends three TWT elements in a TWT response to non-AP
4
STA 1 and these three TWT elements indicate the links of AP 1, AP 2, and AP 3 respectively; and they are
5
6 all with a value of Accept TWT in the TWT Setup Command field. After successful TWT agreements setup
7 on the three links, three TWT SPs with different TWT parameters exist on these three links (Link 1 between
8 AP 1 and non-AP STA 1, Link 2 between AP 2 and non-AP STA 2, and Link 3 between AP 3 and non-AP
9
STA 3), respectively. For these three TWT agreements, the Target Wake Time field of the TWT element that
10
indicates Link 1 is in reference to the TSF time of Link 1, the Target Wake Time field of the TWT element
11
12 that indicates Link 2 is in reference to the TSF time of Link 2 and the Target Wake Time field of the TWT
13 element that indicates Link 3 is in reference to the TSF time of Link 3.
14
15
Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, if an individually addressed TWT
16
information frame for individual TWT, which is intended for one STA affiliated with the associated MLD
17
18 with a setup link, is transmitted to another STA affiliated with the associated MLD with a setup link and an
19 acknowledgement in response to the TWT information frame is received, then the STA of the intended link
20 shall consider the corresponding TWT agreement of the intended link suspended starting as soon as practical
21
after the TWT information frame exchange rather than immediately as described in 26.8.4.2 (TWT
22
Information frame exchange for individual TWT).
23
24
25 35.3.24.3 Broadcast TWT operation
26
27
Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, if an individually addressed TWT
28
information frame for broadcast TWT with All TWT subfield set to 1, which is intended for one STA
29
30 affiliated with the associated MLD with a setup link, is transmitted to another STA affiliated with the
31 associated MLD with a setup link and an acknowledgement in response to the TWT information frame is
32 received, then the STA of the intended link shall consider all the broadcast TWT schedules as suspended
33
starting as soon as practical after the TWT information frame exchange rather than immediately as described
34
in 26.8.4.3 (TWT Information frame exchange for broadcast TWT).
35
36
37 35.3.24.4 Flexible wake time operation
38
39
Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, if an individually addressed TWT
40
information frame for flexible wake time, which is intended for one STA affiliated with the MLD with a
41
42 setup link, is received by another STA affiliated with the MLD with a setup link, then the corresponding PM
43 mode change and power state change for the STA of the intended link shall start as soon as practical after the
44 individually addressed TWT information frame exchange rather than immediately as described in
45
26.8.4.4 (TWT Information frame exchange for flexible wake time).
46
47
48 Between an AP MLD and a non-AP MLD associated with the AP MLD, if an individually addressed TWT
49 information frame for flexible wake time, which is intended for one STA affiliated with the associated MLD
50 with a setup link, is transmitted to another STA affiliated with the associated MLD with a setup link and an
51
acknowledgement in response to the TWT information frame is received by the transmitting STA affiliated
52
with the MLD, then the corresponding PM mode change and power state change for the STA of the intended
53
54 link shall start as soon as practical after the individually addressed TWT information frame exchange rather
55 than immediately as described in 26.8.4.4 (TWT Information frame exchange for flexible wake time).
56
57
58
59
60
61
