## 第 587 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.4 EHT acknowledgment procedure
2
3
35.4.1 Overview
4
5
6 The EHT acknowledgment procedure builds on the features defined for HT-immediate block ack (see
7 10.25.6 (HT-immediate block ack extensions)) and HE acknowledgement (see 26.4 (HE acknowledgment
8 procedure)), with the following extensions:
9
10 — Support for BlockAck Bitmap field lengths of 512 and 1024
11
12 An EHT AP shall not transmit a Multi-STA BlockAck frame that contains a BlockAck Bitmap field with
13
length equal to 512 or 1024 bits as a response to an HE TB PPDU generated by at least one non-EHT HE
14
STA, except that an EHT AP may transmit a Multi-STA BlockAck frame that contains a BlockAck Bitmap
15
16 field with length equal to 512 or 1024 in an individually addressed RU.
17
18 An EHT AP that sends a Multi-STA BlockAck frame where the Per AID TID Info fields are all addressed to
19
a single recipient STA and that is not sent in response to an EHT TB PPDU shall set the RA field of the
20
Multi-STA BlockAck frame to the address of the recipient STA.
21
22
23 An EHT STA that receives a Multi-STA BlockAck frame that is a response to frames requiring
24 acknowledgment shall examine Per AID TID Info field received in the Multi-STA BlockAck frame, and
25
shall process each Per AID TID Info field using the procedure defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowledgment context
26
in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame).
27
28
29 An EHT STA that transmits a Multi-TID BlockAckReq frame in an EHT TB PPDU may set each of the TID
30 Value subfields in the Per TID Info subfields of the BAR Information field of the Multi-TID BlockAckReq
31
frame to a TID that corresponds to any AC.
32
33
34 An EHT STA that transmits a BlockAckReq frame in an EHT TB PPDU may set the TID subfield in the
35 AID TID Info field in the BAR Information field of the BlockAckReq frame to a TID that corresponds to
36 any AC.
37
38
An EHT STA that responds to an EHT MU PPDU with an EHT TB PPDU follows the same rules as an HE
39
40 STA that responds to an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU with an SU PPDU as defined in
41 26.4.4.4 (Responding to an HE MU PPDU, HE SU PPDU, or HE ER SU PPDU with an HE TB PPDU) with
42 the following changes:
43
— Replacing HE MU PPDU, HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU by EHT MU PPDU
44
45 — Replacing HE TB PPDU by EHT TB PPDU
46
— Replacing HE STA by EHT STA.
47
48
49 An EHT STA that responds to an EHT MU PPDU with a PPDU carrying a frame addressed to a single STA
50 follows the same rules as an HE STA that responds to an HE MU PPDU with an TB PPDU as defined in
51
26.4.4.3 (Responding to an HE MU PPDU with an SU PPDU) with the following changes:
52
53 — Replacing HE MU PPDU by EHT MU PPDU
54
— Replacing SU PPDU by a PPDU carrying a frame addressed to a single STA
55
56 — Replacing HE STA by EHT STA.
57
58
An EHT AP that responds to an EHT TB PPDU with a PPDU other than EHT MU PPDU addressed to
59
multiple STAs follows the same rules as an HE AP that responds to an HE TB PPDU with an SU PPDU as
60
61 defined in 26.4.4.5 (Responding to an HE TB PPDU with an SU PPDU) with the following changes:
62 — Replacing HE TB PPDU by EHT TB PPDU
63
64 — Replacing SU PPDU by a PPDU carrying a frame addressed to a single STA
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 587
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 588 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — Replacing HE STA by EHT STA.
2
3
An EHT AP that responds to an EHT TB PPDU with an EHT MU PPDU follows the same rules as an HE
4
AP that responds to an HE TB PPDU with an HE MU PPDU as defined in 26.4.4.6 (Responding to an HE
5
6 TB PPDU with an HE MU PPDU) with the following changes:
7 — Replacing HE TB PPDU by EHT TB PPDU
8
9 — Replacing HE MU PPDU by EHT MU PPDU
10 — Replacing HE STA by EHT STA.
11
12
35.4.2 Block ack procedures
13
14
15 The BA agreement setup between two EHT STAs where at least one of them is not affiliated with the MLD
16 follows the procedure defined in 10.25 (Block acknowledgment (block ack)) and the following procedure:
17
18 — When a block ack agreement is established between two EHT STAs where at least one of them is not
19 affiliated with the MLD, the originator may change the size of its transmission window (WinSize )
O
20 so that the transmit window meets the following conditions:
21
• Is not greater than the buffer size indicated in the ADDBA Response frame.
22
23 • Is not greater than 1024 if the sender and receiver of the ADDBA Response frame are the EHT
24 STAs.
25
— If the buffer size indicated in the ADDBA Response frame is smaller than the buffer size indicated in
26
27 the ADDBA Request frame, the originator shall change the size of its transmission window
28 (WinSize ) such that:
O
29
• The transmission window is not greater than the buffer size indicated in the ADDBA Response
30
frame.
31
32 • The transmission window is not greater than 1024 if the sender and the receiver of the ADDBA
33 Response frame are EHT STAs.
34
35
Both the Compressed BlockAck frame and Multi-STA BlockAck frame allow different Block Ack Bitmap
36
subfield lengths. The length of the Block Ack Bitmap subfield is indicated in the Fragment Number subfield
37
38 of the Block Ack Starting Sequence Control field as defined in 9.3.1.8 (BlockAck frame format). The
39 allowed Block Ack Bitmap lengths for each of the negotiated buffer sizes are defined in Table 35-1
40 (Negotiated buffer size and Block Ack Bitmap subfield length).
41
42
43
Table 35-1—Negotiated buffer size and Block Ack Bitmap subfield length
44
45
46
Block Ack Bitmap subfield Block Ack Bitmap subfield
47
Negotiated buffer size length (bits) in a Compressed length (bits) in a Multi-STA
48
BlockAck frame BlockAck frame
49
50
1–64 64 32 or 64
51
52 65–128 64 or 256 32, 64, or 128
53
54 129–256 64 or 256 32, 64, 128, or 256
55
257–512 64, 256, or 512 32, 64, 128, 256, or 512
56
57 513–1024 64, 256, 512, or 1024 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, or 1024
58
59 NOTE—A 32-bit Block Ack Bitmap subfield length is not allowed unless the originator has set the
60 32-bit BA Bitmap Support field in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities
61 element to 1.

## 第 589 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.5 MU operation
2
3
35.5.1 EHT DL MU operation
4
5
6 35.5.1.1 General
7
8 When transmitting or receiving an EHT MU PPDU, the rules defined in 26.5.1.1 (General), 26.5.1.2 (RU
9
addressing in an HE MU PPDU), and 26.5.1.3a (Minimum RU allocation in an HE MU PPDU) that apply to
10
an HE MU PPDU shall also apply to an EHT MU PPDU. In cases where a rule in 26.5.1.1 (General),
11
12 26.5.1.2 (RU addressing in an HE MU PPDU) or 26.5.1.3a (Minimum RU allocation in an HE MU PPDU)
13 refers to RUs in an HE MU PPDU, the rule also applies to RUs and MRUs in an EHT MU PPDU.
14
15
An EHT AP shall not transmit an EHT MU PPDU with an RU that is narrower than the PPDU bandwidth
16
and that is allocated to more than one STA (DL MU-MIMO) unless the AP has received from each STA an
17
18 EHT Capabilities element with the Partial Bandwidth DL MU-MIMO subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities
19 Information field equal to 1.
20
21
35.5.1.2 RU allocation in an EHT MU PPDU
22
23
24 An EHT STA shall not transmit a 320 MHz EHT MU PPDU in the 6 GHz band with a 2(cid:117)996+484-tone,
25 3(cid:117)996-tone, 3(cid:117)996+484-tone or 4(cid:117)996-tone RU or MRU allocated to the other EHT STA, unless the EHT
26 STA has received an EHT Capabilities element with the Support For 320 MHz In 6 GHz subfield in the
27
EHT PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 1 from the other EHT STA and the other EHT STA is in
28
320 MHz operating bandwidth.
29
30
31 A non-AP EHT STA with dot11EHTSupportFor242ToneRUInBWWiderThan20Implemented equal to false
32 shall set the Support For 242-tone RU In BW Wider Than 20 MHz subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities
33
Information field in the EHT Capabilities element to 0.
34
35
36 An AP shall not transmit a 40 MHz, 80 MHz, 160 MHz or 320 MHz EHT MU PPDU with a 242-tone RU
37 allocated to a 20 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA, unless the AP has received from the 20 MHz operating
38 non-AP EHT STA an EHT Capabilities element with the Support For 242-tone RU in BW Wider Than
39
20 MHz subfield in the EHT Capabilities Information field equal to 1.
40
41
42 In a 40 MHz, 80 MHz, 160 MHz or 320 MHz EHT MU PPDU, an AP shall not allocate to a 20 MHz
43 operating non-AP STA an RU or MRU that is not supported by the STA as indicated in 36.3.2.6 (RU and
44 MRU restrictions for 20 MHz operation). An AP shall follow the rules in 36.3.2.5 (20 MHz operating non-
45
AP EHT STAs participating in wider bandwidth OFDMA), 36.3.2.7 (80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STAs
46
participating in wider bandwidth OFDMA), and 36.3.2.8 (160 MHz operating non-AP EHT STAs
47
48 participating in wider bandwidth OFDMA) if allocating RU(s) or MRU(s) to an non-AP EHT STA whose
49 operating bandwidth is smaller than the BSS operating channel width.
50
51
An EHT AP shall not allocate an RU or MRU in the secondary 160 MHz channel of a 320 MHz EHT MU
52
PPDU or EHT TB PPDU to an 80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA. An EHT AP shall not allocate an RU
53
54 or MRU in the secondary 80 MHz channel of a 160 MHz or 320 MHz EHT MU or EHT TB PPDU to an
55 80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA, if the 80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA has not set up SST
56 operation on the secondary 80 MHz channel with the EHT AP or there is an inactive 20 MHz subchannel
57
within the secondary 80 MHz channel.
58
59
60 An EHT AP shall not allocate an RU or MRU on the secondary 160 MHz in a 320 MHz EHT MU PPDU or
61 EHT TB PPDU to a 160 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA.
62
63
64
65

## 第 590 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.5.2 EHT UL MU operation
2
3
35.5.2.1 General
4
5
6 EHT UL MU operation allows an AP to solicit simultaneous immediate response frames from one or more
7 non-AP EHT STAs. EHT UL MU operation expands the UL MU functionalities inherited from HE with the
8 additional capability of responding with EHT TB PPDUs, with bandwidths up to 320 MHz.
9
10
An EHT STA that is a mesh STA shall not transmit or receive EHT TB PPDUs.
11
12
13 An EHT STA with dot11EHTPartialBWULMUMIMOImplemented equal to true shall set the Partial
14 Bandwidth UL MU-MIMO subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities
15
element to 1. An EHT STA with dot11EHTPartialBWULMUMIMOImplemented equal to false shall set the
16
Partial Bandwidth UL MU-MIMO subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field in the EHT
17
18 Capabilities element to 0.
19
20 An EHT AP shall not transmit a triggering frame in the 6 GHz band which allocates an RU or MRU that
21
occupies the secondary 160 MHz channel to a non-AP EHT STA, unless the AP has received from the non-
22
AP EHT STA an EHT Capabilities element with the Support For 320 MHz In 6 GHz subfield in the EHT
23
24 PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 1 and the non-AP EHT STA is in 320 MHz operating
25 bandwidth.
26
27
A non-AP EHT STA with dot11HEDeviceClass equal to ClassA shall meet the Class A requirements
28
specified in 36.3.16 (Transmit requirements for PPDUs sent in response to a triggering frame) when
29
30 transmitting an EHT TB, non-HT or non-HT Duplicate PPDU in response to a triggering frame. A non-AP
31 EHT STA with dot11HEDeviceClass equal to ClassB shall meet the Class B requirements specified in
32 36.3.16 (Transmit requirements for PPDUs sent in response to a triggering frame) when transmitting an EHT
33
TB, non-HT or non-HT Duplicate PPDU in response to a triggering frame.
34
35 NOTE—A non-AP EHT STA uses the Device Class subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field in the HE
36 Capabilities element to indicate its device class based on dot11HEDeviceClass. See 26.5.2.1 (General).
37
38
An EHT AP shall not set the UL EHT-MCS subfield of an EHT variant User Info field to 15 in a transmitted
39
Trigger frame if the RU assigned by that User Info field is used for UL MU MIMO transmission.
40
41
42 An EHT AP shall not set the UL EHT-MCS subfield of an EHT variant User Info field to 14 in a transmitted
43 Trigger frame.
44
45
A non-AP EHT STA shall set the EHT TRS Support subfield in the EHT MAC Capabilities Information
46
field in the EHT Capabilities element to 1 if its dot11EHTTRSOptionImplemented is true; otherwise the
47
STA shall set it to 0.
48
49
50 An EHT AP shall not trigger a non-AP EHT STA to send an HE TB PPDU that covers the secondary
51 160 MHz.
52
53
35.5.2.2 Rules for soliciting UL MU frames
54
55
56 35.5.2.2.1 General
57
58 An EHT STA shall follow the rules defined in 26.5.2.2.1 (General), where
59
60 — Rules related to HE STAs also apply to EHT STAs.
61 — Rules related to triggering frames also apply to triggering frames soliciting EHT TB PPDUs.
62
63 — Rules related to HE MU and HE TB PPDUs also apply to EHT MU and EHT TB PPDUs,
64 respectively.
65
590 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An EHT AP shall not transmit an HE PPDU that carries a Trigger frame soliciting an EHT TB PPDU.
2
3
An EHT AP shall not transmit an EHT PPDU that carries a Trigger frame soliciting an HE TB PPDU.
4
5
6 An EHT AP shall not transmit a Trigger frame soliciting an OFDMA transmission using EHT TB PPDU
7 that uses UL MU-MIMO within an RU or MRU to a non-AP EHT STA from which the AP has not received
8 an EHT Capabilities element with the Partial Bandwidth UL MU-MIMO subfield of the EHT PHY
9
Capabilities Information field equal to 1.
10
11
12 In a 40 MHz, 80 MHz, 160 MHz, or 320 MHz EHT TB PPDU, an AP shall not allocate to a 20 MHz
13 operating non-AP STA an RU or MRU that is not supported by the STA as indicated in 36.3.2.6 (RU and
14 MRU restrictions for 20 MHz operation). An AP shall follow the rules defined in 36.3.2.5 (20 MHz
15
operating non-AP EHT STAs participating in wider bandwidth OFDMA), 36.3.2.7 (80 MHz operating non-
16
AP EHT STAs participating in wider bandwidth OFDMA), and 36.3.2.8 (160 MHz operating non-AP EHT
17
18 STAs participating in wider bandwidth OFDMA) when assigning an RU or MRU to a non-AP EHT STA
19 whose operating bandwidth is smaller than the BSS operating channel width.
20
21
35.5.2.2.2 Requirements for allocating resources
22
23
24 An EHT AP shall follow the requirements for allocating resources specified in 26.5.2.2.2 (Requirements for
25 allocating resources) where rules related to HE STAs also apply to EHT STAs, and rules related to HE TB
26 PPDUs also apply to EHT TB PPDUs, except that the negotiation of block ack bitmap lengths is additionally
27
defined in 35.4.2 (Block ack procedures).
28
29
30 35.5.2.2.3 Padding for a triggering frame
31
32 A Trigger frame may include the Padding field to extend the frame length to give the recipient STAs enough
33
time to prepare a response for transmission a SIFS after the frame is received. The Padding field, if present,
34
shall be at least two octets in length and shall be set to all 1s. If the Padding field is present in a Trigger
35
36 frame, its length shall be computed as described below.
37
38 An EHT AP shall ensure that there is sufficient padding in a triggering frame as specified in 26.5.2.2.3
39
(Padding for a triggering frame) if the triggering frame is neither an initial Control frame of a frame
40
exchange sequence with a non-AP MLD operating in the EMLSR mode, nor an initial frame of a frame
41
42 exchange sequence with a non-AP MLD operating in the EMLMR mode.
43
44 When an EHT AP of an AP MLD transmits an initial Control frame to initiate a frame exchange with a non-
45
AP MLD operating in the EMLSR mode, the AP shall ensure that the number of bits in the PSDU following
46
47 the last bit of the User Info field addressed to the non-AP MLD is at least L PAD(cid:15)MAC defined in Equation (35-
48 1) together with the padding requirement defined in 26.5.2.2.3 (Padding for a triggering frame).
49
50
51 L PAD(cid:15)MAC = N DBPS m PAD (35-1)
52
53 where
54
55 (cid:173)0(cid:15) i f EMLSR_PADDING_DELAY is 0
56 m PAD = (cid:174) EMLSR_PADDING_DELAY+2
57 (cid:175)2 (cid:15) Otherwise
58
EMLSR_PADDING_DELAYis the value of the EMLSR Padding Delay subfield in the EML
59
60 Capabilities subfield in the Multi-Link element if the EMLSR Padding Delay is not updated in
61 an EML Operating Mode Notification frame, or an updated EMLSR Padding Delay included
62 in the EMLSR Parameter Update field of an EML Operating Mode Notification frame.
63
N is defined in Table 17-4 (Modulation-dependent parameters).
64 DBPS
65

24 NOTE—The initial frame of a frame exchange sequence to initiate a frame exchange with a non-AP MLD operating in

26 EHT PPDU. However, for HT PPDU, VHT PPDU, HE PPDU, or EHT PPDU, there are other methods to do the padding
27 for the initial frame, so the above padding method only applies to the case where the initial frame is sent using non-HT
28 or non-HT duplicate PPDU.

35.5.2.2.4 Allowed settings of the Trigger frame fields and TRS Control subfield
31
32
33 An EHT AP may transmit a Trigger frame that solicits an EHT TB PPDU from an EHT STA subject to the
34 rules defined in 26.5.2.2 (Rules for soliciting UL MU frames) and the additional rules defined below.
35
36
An EHT AP that includes the Special User Info field in a Trigger frame shall set all bits of the Disregard In
37
U-SIG-1 subfield and the four LSBs of the Disregard In U-SIG-2 subfield to 1. The MSB of the Disregard In
38
39 U-SIG-2 subfield is implementation specific and should be set to 0.
40
41 An EHT AP shall not transmit a Trigger frame that solicits both an HE TB PPDU and an EHT TB PPDU.
42
The EHT AP shall not transmit a Trigger frame that contains a User Info field whose AID12 subfield is
43
equal to 0 or 2045 unless both B54 and B55 in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame are equal to 1.
44
45
46 The AID12 subfield of the Special User Info field shall be set to 2007. An EHT AP that includes the Special
47 User Info field in a Trigger frame shall set Special User Info Field Flag subfield to 0 and the Special User
48
Info field shall be placed immediately after the Common Info field. An EHT AP shall set the value of B54 in
49
the Common Info field of a Trigger frame to 1 if there exists any HE variant User Info field in the Trigger
50
51 frame. Otherwise, the EHT AP shall set the value of B54 in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame to 0.
52
NOTE 1—An EHT AP does not assign an AID value of 2007 to any STA or non-AP MLD (see 35.15 (EHT BSS
53
operation)).
54
55
56 An EHT AP shall set the UL Length subfield of a transmitted Trigger frame that solicits an EHT TB PPDU
57 to the value given by Equation (27-11) with m = 2, except that TXTIME is defined by Equation (36-110).
58
59 NOTE 2—This is the same rule as that of an AP that transmits a Trigger frame that solicits an HE TB PPDU (see
60 26.5.2.2.4 (Allowed settings of the Trigger frame fields and TRS Control field)).
61
62 An AP shall not send a frame with a TRS Control subfield that solicits an EHT TB PPDU to a non-AP STA
63
from which the AP has not received an EHT MAC Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities
64
element with the EHT TRS Support subfield equal to 1.
65

## 第 593 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An AP shall not send an EHT MU PPDU with a 4(cid:117)996-tone RU if the 4(cid:117)996-tone RU carries a TRS Control
2 subfield.
3
4
The AP affiliated with an AP MLD and operating on a link shall not set an ACI value in the Preferred AC
5
6 subfield in the Trigger Dependent User Info field of the User Info field of a Basic Trigger frame for a non-
7 AP STA that is affiliated with a non-AP MLD if no TID that corresponds to this ACI is mapped to the link
8 for the non-AP MLD by the TID-to-link mapping (see 35.3.7 (Link management)).
9
10 NOTE 3—If one of the two TIDs matching the indicated Preferred AC is not mapped to the link where the Trigger frame
11 is sent, then data frames belonging to the unmapped TID cannot be transmitted on that link, following 35.3.7 (Link
12 management).
13
14 35.5.2.2.5 AP access procedures for UL MU operation
15
16
An EHT AP shall follow the AP access procedures for UL MU operation as specified in 26.5.2.2.5 (AP
17
18 access procedures for UL MU operation).
19
20 35.5.2.3 Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation
21
22
35.5.2.3.1 General
23
24
25 A non-AP EHT STA that transmits a TB PPDU shall satisfy the conditions defined in 26.5.2.3.1 (General),
26 26.5.2.3.2 (Conditions for not responding with an HE TB PPDU), 26.5.2.3.5 (RA field for frames carried in
27
an HE TB PPDU), 26.5.2.4 (A-MPDU contents in an HE TB PPDU), and 35.5.2.3.4 (Conditions for not
28
responding with a TB PPDU)where rules related to HE TB PPDUs also apply to EHT TB PPDUs. A User
29
30 Info field that is addressed to a non-AP STA is either an HE variant or EHT variant. The User Info field is an
31 HE variant addressed to a non-AP STA if the B39 of the User Info field is set to 0 and the B54 of Common
32 Info field is set to 1 in the Trigger frame; otherwise, it is an EHT variant.
33
34
If a non-AP EHT STA receives an EHT variant User Info field in a Trigger frame that is not MU-RTS
35
36 Trigger frame in which the AID12 subfield matches its AID, then the STA shall respond with an EHT TB
37 PPDU. If a non-AP EHT STA receives an HE variant User Info field in a Trigger frame that is not MU-RTS
38 Trigger frame in which the AID12 subfield matches its AID, then the STA shall respond with an HE TB
39
PPDU.
40
41
42 An EHT STA shall not transmit an EHT TB PPDU if the B55 of the Common Info field is set to 1.
43
NOTE—A non-AP EHT STA is an HE STA, so the non-AP EHT STA might contend for an RA-RU and transmit an HE
44
TB PPDU, if the STA receives an HE variant User Info field that allocates RA-RU(s) in a Trigger frame (see 26.5.4 (UL
45
OFDMA-based random access (UORA))).
46
47
48 A non-AP EHT STA shall not send an EHT TB PPDU unless it is explicitly triggered by an AP in the
49 operation modes described in 35.5.2.3.2 (TXVECTOR parameters for EHT TB PPDU response to Trigger
50 frame).
51
52
A non-AP EHT STA shall not send an HE TB PPDU on the secondary 160 MHz.
53

6 35.5.2.4 UL MU CS mechanism for EHT STAs
7
8 An EHT STA shall follow the rules defined in 26.5.2.5 (UL MU CS mechanism), except that the EHT STA
9
shall use the rules defined in 36.3.21.6.4 (Per 20 MHz CCA sensitivity) instead of those defined in
10
27.3.20.6.5 (Per 20 MHz CCA sensitivity) when CCA is performed on any nonpunctured 20 MHz
11
12 subchannel in an EHT BSS.
13
14 Specifically, if the CS Required subfield in a Trigger frame is 1, then the non-AP STA shall consider the
15
status of the CCA (using energy detect defined in 36.3.21.6.4 (Per 20 MHz CCA sensitivity) and the virtual
16
carrier sense (NAV)) during the SIFS between the PPDU that contains the Trigger frame and the PPDU sent
17
18 in response to the Trigger frame. In this case, the non-AP STA shall sense the medium using energy detect
19 after receiving the PPDU that contains the Trigger frame (i.e., during the SIFS), and it shall perform the
20 energy detect at least in the subchannel that contains the non-AP STA’s UL allocation, where the sensed
21
subchannel consists of one or more occupied 20 MHz channels. The non-AP STA may transmit the solicited
22
PPDU if all the occupied 20 MHz channels containing the RUs allocated in the Trigger frame are considered
23
24 idle. If the non-AP STA detects that any of the occupied 20 MHz channels containing the allocated RUs is
25 not idle, then the non-AP STA shall not transmit.
26
27
35.5.3 Operation of the two BQR Control subfields
28
29
30 An EHT STA may set dot11TwoBQRsOptionImplemented to true if it is associated with an EHT AP that
31 supports 320 MHz.
32
33
An EHT STA with dot11TwoBQRsOptionImplemented equal to true shall set the Two BQRs Support
34
subfield in the EHT MAC Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits to 1;
35
36 otherwise the EHT STA shall set the Two BQRs Support subfield to 0.
37
38 An EHT STA with dot11TwoBQRsOptionImplemented equal to true shall set the BQR Support subfield in
39
the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1.
40
41
42 The EHT STA may report the channel availability information as specified in 36.3.21.6.4 (Per 20 MHz CCA
43 sensitivity) to its associated AP in two BQR Control subfields of frames it transmits if the AP has indicated
44 its support in the Two BQRs Support subfield of its EHT Capabilities element; otherwise the STA shall not
45
report the channel availability information in the two BQR Control subfields.
46
47 NOTE—An EHT STA is an HE STA and as such inherits all the functionalities defined in 26.5.6 (Bandwidth query
48 report operation).

62 MPDU length capability indicated in the EHT Capabilities element, HE Capabilities element, VHT

## 第 598 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.7 EHT sounding operation
2
3
35.7.1 General
4
5
6 Transmit beamforming and DL MU-MIMO require knowledge of the channel state to compute a steering
7 matrix that is applied to the transmit signal to optimize reception at one or more receivers. EHT STAs use
8 the EHT sounding protocol to determine the channel state information. The EHT sounding protocol provides
9
explicit feedback mechanisms, defined as EHT non-trigger-based (non-TB) sounding (see 35.7.3 (Rules for
10
EHT sounding protocol sequences)) and EHT trigger-based (TB) sounding, where the EHT beamformee
11
12 measures the channel using a training signal (i.e., an EHT sounding NDP) transmitted by the EHT
13 beamformer and sends back a transformed estimate of the channel state (see 35.7.3 (Rules for EHT sounding
14 protocol sequences)). The EHT beamformer uses this estimate to derive the steering matrix.
15
16
The EHT beamformee returns an estimate of the channel state in an EHT compressed beamforming/CQI
17
18 report carried in one or more EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frames. There are three types of EHT
19 compressed beamforming/CQI report:
20
— SU feedback: The EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report consists of an EHT Compressed
21
22 Beamforming Report field
23 — MU feedback: The EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report consists of an EHT Compressed
24
Beamforming Report field and EHT MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field
25
26 — CQI feedback: The EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report consists of an EHT CQI Report field
27
28 NOTE—Use of EHT TB sounding does not necessarily imply MU feedback. EHT TB sounding might also be used to
29 obtain SU feedback and CQI feedback.
30
31 The EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report is carried in a single EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI
32 frame if the resulting frame is less than or equal to 11454 octets in length (see 35.7.3 (Rules for EHT
33
sounding protocol sequences)). Otherwise, the EHT beamforming feedback is segmented and each segment
34
is carried in an EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame.
35
36
37 An EHT beamformer shall support a maximum MPDU length for the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI
38 report that is the minimum of 11454 octets and the maximum length of the EHT compressed
39
beamforming/CQI report that the EHT beamformer intends to solicit from its EHT beamformees.
40
41
42 35.7.2 EHT sounding protocol
43
44 An SU beamformer is an EHT STA that sets the SU Beamformer subfield to 1 in the EHT PHY Capabilities
45
Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits.
46
47
48 An SU beamformee is an EHT STA that sets the SU Beamformee subfield to 1 in the EHT PHY Capabilities
49 Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits. A non-AP EHT STA that is not a 20 MHz-
50 only non-AP EHT STA with 20 MHz-Only Limited Capabilities Support subfield equal to 1 shall set the SU
51
Beamformee subfield to 1. A 20 MHz-only non-AP EHT STA with 20 MHz-Only Limited Capabilities
52
Support equal to 1 may set the SU Beamformee subfield to 1. An EHT AP may set the SU Beamformee
53
54 subfield to 1.
55
56 An MU beamformer is an EHT AP that sets at least one of the following MU beamformer subfields: MU
57
Beamformer (BW (cid:148) 80 MHz), MU Beamformer (BW = 160 MHz), and MU Beamformer (BW = 320 MHz)
58
to 1 in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits. A non-AP
59
60 EHT STA shall set all three MU beamformer subfields, MU Beamformer (BW (cid:148) 80 MHz), MU
61 Beamformer (BW = 160 MHz), and MU Beamformer (BW = 320 MHz) subfields to 0. An MU beamformer
62 is also an SU beamformer and shall set the SU Beamformer subfield to 1 in the EHT PHY Capabilities
63
Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits.
64
65
598 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 599 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An EHT AP that sets the MU Beamformer (BW = 160 MHz) subfield to 1 shall set the MU Beamformer
2 (BW (cid:148) 80 MHz) subfield to 1. An EHT AP that sets the MU Beamformer (BW = 320 MHz) subfield to 1
3
shall set both the MU Beamformer (BW (cid:148) 80 MHz) and MU Beamformer (BW = 160 MHz) subfields to 1.
4
An EHT AP that indicates no support for 320 MHz channel width in the EHT Capabilities element shall set
5
6 the MU Beamformer (BW = 320 MHz) subfield to 0. An EHT AP that indicates no support for 160 MHz
7 channel width in the HE Capabilities element shall set both the MU Beamformer (BW = 160 MHz) and MU
8 Beamformer (BW = 320 MHz) subfields to 0.
9
10 NOTE 1—A non-AP STA might use the value of the MU Beamformer subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities
11 Information field of the AP to determine the AP with which it will associate.
12
13 A non-AP EHT STA that is not a 20 MHz-only non-AP EHT STA with 20 MHz-Only Limited Capabilities
14
Support subfield equal to 1 shall support operation as an MU beamformee. A 20 MHz non-AP EHT STA
15
with 20 MHz-Only Limited Capabilities Support subfield equal to 1 may support operation as a MU
16
17 beamformee. An EHT AP does not support operation as an MU beamformee.
18
19
The term EHT beamformer refers to both the SU beamformer and MU beamformer. The term EHT
20
21 beamformee refers to both the SU beamformee and the MU beamformee.
22
23 The type of feedback (SU, MU, or CQI) solicited by an EHT beamformer from an EHT beamformee is
24 indicated in the Feedback Type And Ng and Codebook Size subfields in the STA Info field identifying the
25
EHT beamformee in the EHT NDP Announcement frame as defined in Table 9-44 (Feedback Type And Ng
26
subfield and Codebook Size subfield encoding for HE and EHT TB sounding) and Table 9-45 (Feedback
27
28 Type And Ng subfield and Codebook Size subfield encoding for HE and EHT non-TB sounding).
29
30 The bandwidth (partial or full) of the feedback solicited by an EHT beamformer from an EHT beamformee
31
depends on the Partial BW Info subfield in the STA Info field identifying the EHT beamformee in the EHT
32
NDP Announcement frame, the bandwidth of the PPDU carrying the EHT NDP Announcement frame, and
33
34 the operating bandwidth of the EHT beamformee. The bandwidth of the PPDU carrying the EHT NDP
35 Announcement frame and the subsequent EHT sounding NDP shall be the same.
36
37
Full bandwidth SU, MU or CQI feedback refers to the feedback mode where the feedback RU or MRU size
38
indicated in the Partial BW Info subfield of the EHT NDP Announcement frame spans all the available
39
40 bandwidth within an EHT beamformee’s operating bandwidth. Partial bandwidth SU, MU or CQI feedback
41 refers to the feedback mode where the feedback RU or MRU size indicated in the Partial BW Info subfield
42 in the STA Info field identifying the EHT beamformee in the EHT NDP Announcement frame spans a part
43
of the available bandwidth within an EHT beamformee’s operating bandwidth.
44
45 — If the EHT beamformee’s operating bandwidth is larger than or equal to the bandwidth of the EHT
46 sounding NDP, the available bandwidth is the entire PPDU bandwidth of the EHT sounding NDP
47
when preamble puncturing is not applied on the PPDU and is the entire occupied PPDU bandwidth
48
of the EHT sounding NDP when preamble puncturing is applied on the PPDU.
49
50 — If the EHT beamformee’s operating bandwidth is smaller than the bandwidth of the EHT sounding
51 NDP, the available bandwidth is the beamformee’s entire operating bandwidth when preamble
52
puncturing is not applied to any 20 MHz subchannel within the beamformee’s operating bandwidth
53
and is the entire occupied bandwidth within the beamformee’s operating bandwidth when preamble
54
55 puncturing is applied to any 20 MHz subchannel within the beamformee’s operating bandwidth.
56
NOTE 2—For example, if a 160 MHz EHT sounding NDP has a 20 MHz puncturing
57
58
59 • The available bandwidth is 140 MHz when the beamformee’s operating bandwidth is 160 MHz or
60 320 MHz.
61 • The available bandwidth is 80 MHz when the beamformee’s operating bandwidth is 80 MHz and 20 MHz
62 puncturing is not within the beamformee’s operating bandwidth.
63 • The available bandwidth is 60 MHz when the beamformee’s operating bandwidth is 80 MHz and 20 MHz
64
puncturing is within the beamformee’s operating bandwidth.
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 599
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 600 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An EHT beamformer shall set the Partial BW Info subfield in an EHT NDP Announcement frame to a value
2 that is listed in Table 9-45b (Settings for BW, Partial Bandwidth Info subfield in the EHT NDP
3
Announcement frame).
4
5
6 An EHT NDP Announcement frame shall not request feedback on a 242-tone RU that is signaled as
7 punctured in the U-SIG field of the EHT sounding NDP that follows the EHT NDP Announcement frame.
8
9
An EHT NDP Announcement frame shall not request partial bandwidth feedback on a 242-tone RU outside
10
of the beamformee’s operating channel width.
11
12
13 An SU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth SU feedback from an SU beamformee in an EHT non-TB
14 sounding sequence. An SU beamformer shall not solicit partial bandwidth SU feedback from an SU
15
beamformee in an EHT non-TB sounding sequence. In an EHT non-TB sounding sequence case, the
16
occupied subchannel(s) indicated by the BW and Puncturing Channel Information fields in the U-SIG field
17
18 of the EHT sounding NDP shall be the same as the requested subchannel(s) indicated in the Partial BW Info
19 subfield of the immediately preceding EHT NDP Announcement frame. Furthermore, the punctured
20 subchannel(s) indicated by the BW and Puncturing Channel Information fields in the U-SIG field of the
21
EHT sounding NDP shall not include other punctured subchannel(s) in addition to those indicated in the
22
Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield of the most recent EHT Operation element for non-TB sounding.
23
24
NOTE 3—In the EHT TB sounding sequence, the punctured subchannel(s) indicated by the BW and Puncturing Channel
25
Information fields in the U-SIG field of the EHT sounding NDP might include other punctured subchannel(s) in addition
26
to those indicated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield of the most recent EHT Operation element, following the
27
rules defined in 35.15.2 (Preamble puncturing operation).
28
29
An SU beamformer may solicit partial bandwidth or full bandwidth SU feedback from an SU beamformee in
30
31 an EHT TB sounding sequence if the SU beamformee indicates support by setting the Triggered SU
32 Beamforming Feedback subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities
33 element it transmits to 1.
34
35
An MU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth MU feedback from an MU beamformee in an EHT TB
36
37 sounding sequence. An MU beamformer may solicit partial bandwidth MU feedback from an MU
38 beamformee in an EHT TB sounding sequence if the MU beamformee indicates support by setting the
39 Triggered MU Beamforming Partial BW Feedback subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field
40
in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits to 1. An MU beamformer shall not solicit MU feedback in an
41
EHT non-TB sounding sequence.
42
43
44 An MU beamformer may solicit partial bandwidth or full bandwidth CQI feedback from an MU
45 beamformee in an EHT TB sounding sequence if the MU beamformee indicates support by setting the
46
Triggered CQI Beamforming Feedback subfield to 1.
47
48
49 An MU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth CQI feedback from an MU beamformee in an EHT non-TB
50 sounding sequence if the MU beamformee indicates support by setting the Non-Triggered CQI
51 Beamforming Feedback subfield to 1.
52
53
An EHT beamformer shall not send an EHT NDP Announcement frame that initiates an EHT TB sounding
54
55 sequence with a STA Info field identifying an EHT beamformee if the STA Info field and the PHY
56 Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element most recently received from the EHT
57 beamformee meet any of the following conditions (see Table 9-44 (Feedback Type And Ng subfield and
58
Codebook Size subfield encoding for HE and EHT TB sounding)):
59
60 — The Feedback Type And Ng subfield and Codebook Size subfield in the STA Info field indicates SU
61 and Ng = 16, and the Ng = 16 SU Feedback subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information
62 field is 0.
63
64
65

An EHT beamformee indicates the maximum number of spatial streams it can receive in a 20 MHz,
23
24 40 MHz, or 80 MHz EHT sounding NDP in the Beamformee SS ((cid:148) 80 MHz) subfield in the EHT PHY
25 Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits.
26
27
An EHT beamformee shall set the Beamformee SS ((cid:148) 80 MHz) subfield to indicate a maximum number of
28
spatial streams of 4 or greater.
29
30
31 An EHT beamformee shall set the Beamformee SS (= 160 MHz) subfield to indicate a maximum number of
32 spatial streams of 4 or greater.
33
34
An EHT beamformee shall set the Beamformee SS (= 320 MHz) subfield to indicate a maximum number of
35
36 spatial streams of 4 or greater.
37
38 An EHT beamformee indicates the maximum number of spatial streams it can receive in a 160 MHz EHT
39
sounding NDP in the Beamformee SS (= 160 MHz) subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field
40
in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits.
41
42
43 An EHT beamformee indicates the maximum number of spatial streams it can receive in a 320 MHz EHT
44 sounding NDP in the Beamformee SS (= 320 MHz) subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field
45
in the EHT Capabilities element it transmits.
46
47
48 An EHT beamformer shall not transmit a 20 MHz, 40 MHz, or 80 MHz EHT sounding NDP with a
49 TXVECTOR parameter NUM_STS that is greater than the maximum number of spatial streams indicated in
50 the Beamformee SS ((cid:148) 80 MHz) subfield of any STA identified by a STA Info field in the preceding EHT
51
NDP Announcement frame.
52
53
54 An EHT beamformer shall not transmit a 160 MHz EHT sounding NDP with a TXVECTOR parameter
55 NUM_STS that is greater than the maximum number of spatial streams indicated in the Beamformee
56 SS( = 160 MHz) subfield of any STA identified by a STA Info field in the preceding EHT NDP
57
Announcement frame.
58
59
60 An EHT beamformer shall not transmit a 320 MHz EHT sounding NDP with a TXVECTOR parameter
61 NUM_STS that is greater than the maximum number of spatial streams indicated in the Beamformee
62 SS (= 320 MHz) subfield of any STA identified by a STA Info field in the preceding EHT NDP
63
Announcement frame.

3
Number Of Supported EHT-LTFs subfield of any STA identified by a STA Info field in the preceding EHT
4
NDP Announcement frame.

6

exceeds the value indicated in the Number Of Sounding Dimensions (= 320 MHz) subfield.

30 An EHT beamformer may solicit partial bandwidth feedback from one or more EHT beamformees with
31 operating channel width smaller than the bandwidth of the PPDU carrying the EHT NDP Announcement
32 frame and EHT sounding NDP.
33
34
An EHT beamformee indicates the maximum supported data rate used in the EHT TB PPDU carrying the
35
36 EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report in the TB Sounding Feedback Rate Limit subfield in the EHT
37 PHY Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element sent by the EHT beamformee.
38
39
An EHT beamformer shall not solicit an EHT TB PPDU with a BFRP Trigger frame that indicates a data
40
rate greater than the data rate indicated by the EHT beamformee in the TB Sounding Feedback Rate Limit
41
42 subfield. The data rate indicated in the BFRP Trigger frame is computed based on the RU Allocation
43 subfield, PS160 subfield, UL EHT-MCS subfield, and SS Allocation/RA-RU Information subfield in the
44 EHT variant User Info field of the BFRP Trigger frame. The data rate is computed based on 1.6 μs GI.
45
46
A 320 MHz EHT beamformer shall not send a 320 MHz EHT NDP Announcement frame to solicit partial
47
48 bandwidth feedback from an EHT beamformee with 20 MHz operating channel width.
49
50 An EHT NDP Announcement frame carried by a PPDU of bandwidth larger than 40 MHz shall not solicit
51
from an EHT beamformee with 40 MHz operating channel width.
52
53
54 In an EHT non-TB sounding sequence, a 20 MHz operating EHT beamformee shall support SU feedback for
55 242-tone RU solicited with an EHT NDP Announcement frame carried by a PPDU of bandwidth of
56 20 MHz.

58
In an EHT TB sounding sequence, a 20 MHz operating EHT beamformee may support SU feedback for

61 20 MHz, 40 MHz, 80 MHz, and 160 MHz.
62
63
A 20 MHz operating EHT beamformee shall support MU feedback for 242-tone RU solicited with an EHT
64
NDP Announcement frame carried by a PPDU of bandwidth of 20 MHz.

## 第 612 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.7.3 Rules for EHT sounding protocol sequences
2
3
An EHT non-TB sounding sequence is initiated by an EHT beamformer with an individually addressed EHT
4
NDP Announcement frame comprising exactly one STA Info field, followed after SIFS by an EHT sounding
5
6 NDP. The EHT beamformee responds after SIFS with an EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame.
7
8 An example of an EHT non-TB sounding sequence with a single EHT beamformee is shown in Figure 35-46
9
(An illustration of EHT non-TB sounding).
10
11
12
13
14 EHT(cid:3)beamformer EHT(cid:3)NDP EHT(cid:3)sounding(cid:3)
15 Announcement SIFS NDP SIFS
16
EHT(cid:3)Compressed(cid:3)
17 EHT(cid:3)beamformee Beamforming/CQI
18
19
20 Figure 35-46—An illustration of EHT non-TB sounding
21
22
23
24 An EHT beamformer that initiates the EHT non-TB sounding sequence shall transmit the EHT NDP
25 Announcement frame with a single STA Info field, the STA Info field having a value in the AID11 field
26 other than 2047 and with the AID11 field in that STA Info field set to the AID of the STA identified by the
27
RA field or to 0 if the STA identified by the RA field is an associated AP, mesh STA or IBSS STA. The EHT
28
NDP Announcement frame shall be followed after a SIFS by an EHT sounding NDP, which shall be
29
30 followed after a SIFS by a PPDU containing one or more EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frames.
31
32 An EHT beamformer may initiate an EHT non-TB sounding sequence with an EHT beamformee to solicit
33
SU or CQI feedback.
34
35
36 An EHT beamformer that initiates an EHT TB sounding sequence shall transmit an EHT NDP
37 Announcement frame with two or more STA Info fields and the RA field set to the broadcast address. The
38 EHT NDP Announcement frame shall be followed after a SIFS by an EHT sounding NDP, which shall be
39
followed after a SIFS by a BFRP Trigger frame. Each EHT beamformee that is addressed by a BFRP Trigger
40
frame shall respond after a SIFS with an EHT TB PPDU containing one or more EHT Compressed
41
42 Beamforming/CQI frames.
43
44 An example of an EHT TB sounding sequence with more than one EHT beamformee is shown in Figure 35-
45
47 (An illustration of EHT TB sounding).

## 第 613 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 An EHT beamformer may initiate an EHT TB sounding sequence to solicit SU, MU, or CQI feedback
2 variant.
3
4
An EHT beamformer may initiate an EHT TB sounding sequence to solicit a feedback variant only if the
5
6 feedback variant is computed based on parameters supported by the EHT beamformee; otherwise the EHT
7 beamformer shall not solicit a feedback variant computed based on parameters not supported by the EHT
8 beamformee (see 35.7.2 (EHT sounding protocol)).
9
10
An EHT AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to true shall not send an EHT NDP Announcement
11
12 frame with the TA field set to the transmitted BSSID to a non-AP STA that is associated with an AP
13 corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID in the multiple BSSID set unless the AP has received from the
14 non-AP STA an HE Capabilities element with the Rx Control Frame To MultiBSS subfield in the HE MAC
15
Capabilities Information field equal to 1.
16
17
18 An AP that transmits an EHT NDP Announcement frame identifying EHT STAs shall set the TA field of the
19 frame to the MAC address of the AP, unless dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented is true and the EHT NDP
20 Announcement frame identifies STAs from at least two different BSSs of the multiple BSSID set, in which
21
case, the AP shall set the TA field of the frame to the transmitted BSSID. If the EHT NDP Announcement
22
frame is transmitted in a non-HT duplicate PPDU then the TA field of the EHT NDP Announcement frame
23
24 is a bandwidth signaling TA (see 10.6.6.6 (Channel Width selection for Control frames)).
25
26 An EHT beamformer that transmits an EHT NDP Announcement frame to an EHT beamformee that is an
27
associated AP, mesh STA, or IBSS STA, shall include one STA Info field in the EHT NDP Announcement
28
frame and shall set the AID11 field in the STA Info field of the frame to 0.
29
30
31 When an EHT beamformer is an AP and EHT beamformees are non-AP STAs, the EHT beamformer that
32 transmits an EHT NDP Announcement frame to one or more EHT beamformees shall set the AID11 subfield
33
to 11 LSBs of the AID of each EHT beamformee.
34
35
36 An EHT NDP Announcement frame shall not include multiple STA Info fields that have the same value in
37 the AID11 subfield.
38
39
In an EHT TB sounding sequence, a STA Info field in the EHT NDP Announcement frame that solicits SU
40
41 or MU feedback indicates the subcarrier grouping, Ng, codebook size and the number of columns, Nc, to
42 be used by the EHT beamformee identified by the STA Info field for the generation of the SU or MU
43 feedback.
44
45
In an EHT non-TB sounding sequence where the STA Info field in the EHT NDP Announcement frame
46
47 solicits SU feedback, the subcarrier grouping, Ng, codebook size and the number of columns, Nc, used for
48
the generation of the SU feedback are determined by the EHT beamformee.
49
50
51 In an EHT TB sounding sequence, a STA Info field in the EHT NDP Announcement frame that solicits CQI
52 feedback indicates the Nc to be used by the EHT beamformee identified by the STA Info field for the
53
generation of the CQI feedback.
54
55
56 In an EHT non-TB sounding sequence where the STA Info field in the EHT NDP Announcement frame
57 solicits CQI feedback, the Nc used for the generation of the CQI feedback is determined by the EHT
58
beamformee.
59
60
61 An EHT beamformer that transmits an EHT NDP Announcement frame as part of an EHT TB sounding
62 sequence shall set the Nc Index subfield of the STA Info field to indicate a value less than or equal to the
63
minimum of:
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 613

## 第 614 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — The maximum number of supported receive spatial streams according to the corresponding EHT
2 beamformee’s EHT-MCS Map (20 MHz-Only Non-AP STA), EHT-MCS Map (BW (cid:148) 80 MHz,
3
Except 20 MHz-Only Non-AP STA), EHT-MCS Map (BW = 160 MHz), and EHT-MCS Map
4
(BW = 320 MHz) subfields in the Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field in the EHT Capabilities
5
6 element sent by the EHT beamformee.
7 — The maximum number of supported receive spatial streams according to the Rx NSS indicated in the
8
most recently received Operating Mode Notification frame, Operating Mode Notification element
9
with the Rx NSS Type subfield equal to 0, or OM Control subfield if EHT OM Control subfield is
10
11 not present in the same A-Control field, or EHT OM Control subfield together with the OM Control
12 subfield sent by the corresponding EHT beamformee (see 35.9 (Operating mode indication)).
13
— The maximum supported Nc indicated by the Max Nc subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities
14
15 Information field in the EHT Capabilities element sent by the EHT beamformee.
16
17 An EHT beamformer that transmits an EHT NDP Announcement frame shall set the Partial BW Info
18
subfield in a STA Info field to indicate the feedback subcarrier indices of the solicited EHT compressed
19
beamforming/CQI report (see 9.3.1.19 (VHT/HE NDP Announcement frame format)).
20
21
22 The EHT beamformer shall set the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH or
23 CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT, the Partial BW Info subfield of the EHT NDP Announcement frame,
24
depending on the operating channel width of the beamformee, the operating channel width of the
25
beamformer, and the feedback RU or MRU size, as defined in Table 9-45b (Settings for BW, Partial
26
27 Bandwidth Info subfield in the EHT NDP Announcement frame).
28
29
The EHT beamformer shall use the lowest scidx(cid:11)0(cid:12), which is the lower bound of the scidx(cid:11)0(cid:12) indicated
30
by the Partial BW Info subfield of a STA Info field that is equal to the maximum of:
31
32 — The minimum subcarrier index located within the channel width indicated in the VHT Operation
33
Information field of either the HE Operation element or the VHT Operation element, whichever is
34
present, or within the channel width indicated in the HT Operation element, if present, or 6 GHz
35
36 Operation Information field of the HE Operation element, if present (see 9.4.2.249 (HE Operation
37 element) and 9.4.2.311 (EHT Operation element)).
38
— The minimum subcarrier index located within the channel width indicated in the most recently
39
received Operating Mode Notification frame, Operating Mode Notification element with the Rx NSS
40
41 Type subfield equal to 0, or OM Control subfield if EHT OM Control subfield is not present in the
42 same A-Control field, or EHT OM Control subfield together with the OM Control subfield sent by
43 the corresponding EHT beamformee (see 35.9 (Operating mode indication)).
44
45
46 The EHT beamformer shall use the highest scidx(cid:11)Ns–1(cid:12), which is the upper bound of the scidx(cid:11)Ns–1(cid:12)
47 indicated by the Partial BW Info subfield of a STA Info field that is equal to the minimum of:
48
— The maximum subcarrier index located within the channel width indicated in the VHT Operation
49
50 Information field of either the HE Operation element or the VHT Operation element, whichever is
51 present, or within the channel width indicated in the HT Operation element, if present, or 6 GHz
52 Operation Information field of the HE Operation element, if present (see 9.4.2.249 (HE Operation
53
element) and 9.4.2.311 (EHT Operation element)).
54
55 — The maximum subcarrier index located within the channel width indicated in the most recently
56 received Operating Mode Notification frame, Operating Mode Notification element with the Rx NSS
57
Type subfield equal to 0, or OM Control subfield if EHT OM Control subfield is not present in the
58
same A-Control field, or EHT OM Control subfield together with the OM Control subfield sent by
59
60 the corresponding EHT beamformee (see 35.9 (Operating mode indication)).
61
62 In an EHT non-TB sounding sequence soliciting SU feedback, B26 (in the Feedback Type And Ng subfield),
63
the Codebook Size subfield, and the Nc Index subfield in the STA Info field of the EHT NDP
64
Announcement frame are reserved.

## 第 615 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 In an EHT non-TB sounding sequence soliciting CQI feedback, the Nc Index subfield in an EHT NDP
2 Announcement frame is reserved.
3
4
An EHT beamformer that has initiated an EHT TB sounding sequence shall transmit a BFRP Trigger frame
5
6 to solicit feedback and may send additional BFRP Trigger frame(s) in the same TXOP, with any STA being
7 triggered only once within the TXOP. Figure 35-47 (An illustration of EHT TB sounding) shows an example
8 with two BFRP Trigger frames. The EHT beamformer uses the additional BFRP Trigger frames to solicit
9
EHT compressed beamforming/CQI reports from EHT beamformees not addressed in a previous BFRP
10
Trigger frame. An EHT beamformer shall not transmit a BFRP Trigger frame that solicits a STA identified
11
12 in the EHT NDP Announcement frame of an EHT TB sounding sequence unless it is in the same TXOP as
13 the EHT TB sounding sequence. In the EHT TB sounding sequence, the STAs identified in the NDP
14 Announcement frame should be the same as the ones identified in the Trigger frame(s) in the same TXOP.
15
16
An EHT beamformer that sends a BFRP Trigger frame shall set the Feedback Segment Retransmission
17
18 Bitmap fields of the BFRP Trigger frame to all 1s.
19
NOTE 1—The BFRP Trigger frame contains one or more User Info fields, each of which identifies an EHT
20
beamformee.
21
22
23 An EHT beamformee that receives an EHT NDP Announcement frame soliciting SU feedback as part of an
24 EHT non-TB sounding sequence shall generate an EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report for SU
25
feedback with Nc in the range 1 to 8, Ng = 4 or Ng = 16, and codebook size (cid:11)(cid:73)(cid:15)(cid:92)(cid:12) = (cid:94)4(cid:15)2(cid:96) or
26
27 (cid:11)(cid:73)(cid:15)(cid:92)(cid:12) = (cid:94)6(cid:15)4(cid:96). The EHT beamformee shall transmit the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report a
28 SIFS after the EHT sounding NDP.
29
30
An EHT beamformee that receives an EHT NDP Announcement frame soliciting CQI feedback as part of an
31
32 EHT non-TB sounding sequence shall generate an EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report for CQI
33 feedback with Nc determined by the EHT beamformee.
34
35
An EHT beamformee that receives an EHT NDP Announcement frame soliciting CQI feedback as part of an
36
37 EHT TB sounding sequence shall generate an EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report for CQI feedback
38 with Nc determined by the EHT beamformer.
39
40
41 An EHT beamformee that receives an EHT NDP Announcement frame as part of an EHT non-TB sounding
42 sequence shall transmit its EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report a SIFS after the EHT sounding NDP.
43 The TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH for the PPDU containing the EHT compressed
44
beamforming/CQI report shall be set to indicate a bandwidth not wider than that indicated by the
45
RXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH of the EHT sounding NDP.
46
47
48 An EHT beamformee that receives an EHT NDP Announcement frame as part of an EHT TB sounding
49 sequence with a STA Info field identifying it soliciting SU or MU feedback shall generate an EHT
50
compressed beamforming/CQI report using the feedback type, Ng, codebook size, and Nc indicated in the
51
52 STA Info field.
53
54 If the EHT beamformee receives a BFRP Trigger frame with a matching User Info field, the EHT
55
beamformee transmits an EHT TB PPDU containing the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report
56
following the rules defined in 35.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation). If the EHT NDP
57
58 Announcement frame has the TA field set to the transmitted BSSID, and the EHT beamformee is a non-AP
59 STA associated with an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID that supports receiving Control
60 frames with TA field set to the transmitted BSSID, then the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report sent
61
in response shall have the RA field set to as defined in 26.5.2.3.5 (RA field for frame carried in an HE TB
62
PPDU).
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 615

## 第 616 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 NOTE 2—A non-AP EHT beamformee that transmits an OM Control subfield with the UL MU Disable field set to 1
2 does not respond to BFRP Trigger frames (see 35.9 (Operating mode indication)).
3
4 An EHT beamformee that transmits an EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI Report frame sets the Partial
5
BW Info subfield of the EHT MIMO Control field to indicate the range of subcarriers for which compressed
6
beamforming/CQI information is provided. The Partial BW Info subfield shall be set to the value of the
7
8 Partial BW Info subfield of the NDP Announcement frame for the EHT beamformee.
9
10 An EHT beamformee that transmits EHT compressed beamforming feedback shall include neither the EHT
11
compressed beamforming report information nor the EHT MU exclusive beamforming report information if
12
the transmission duration of the PPDU carrying the EHT compressed beamforming report information and
13
14 any EHT MU exclusive beamforming report information would exceed the maximum PPDU duration (see
15 Table 9-34 (Maximum data unit sizes (in octets) and durations (in microseconds))).
16
17
The Sounding Dialog Token Number field in the EHT MIMO Control field shall be set to the same value as
18
the Sounding Dialog Token Number field in the corresponding EHT NDP Announcement frame.
19
20
21 The SNR per subcarrier computation should be done on at least four subcarriers in a 26-tone RU.
22
23
35.7.4 Rules for generating segmented feedback
24
25
26 If the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report solicited by the EHT beamformer would result in an EHT
27 Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame that exceeds 11454 octets in length, then the EHT compressed
28 beamforming/CQI report shall be split into up to eight feedback segments. Each feedback segment shall be
29
included in a separate EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame and shall contain successive portions of
30
the EHT compressed beamforming/CQI report. Each feedback segment shall be of equal length except the
31
32 last feedback segment that may be smaller. Each EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame that includes a
33 feedback segment that is not the last feedback segment shall have a length of 11454 octets. Each feedback
34 segment is identified by the value of the Remaining Feedback Segments subfield and the First Feedback
35
Segment subfield in the EHT MIMO Control field as defined in 9.4.1.70 (EHT MIMO Control field). The
36
other nonreserved subfields of the EHT MIMO Control field shall be the same for all feedback segments.
37
38 All feedback segments shall be sent in a single A-MPDU contained in a PPDU and shall be included in the
39 A-MPDU in the descending order of the values of the Remaining Feedback Segments subfield.
40
41
An EHT beamformer, which sends a BFRP Trigger frame to retrieve an EHT compressed beamforming/CQI
42
report from an EHT beamformee, shall solicit all possible feedback segments by setting all of the bits in the
43
44 Feedback Segment Retransmission Bitmap subfield to 1 in the User Info field identifying the EHT
45 beamformee.
46
47
An EHT beamformer, which fails to receive some or all of the feedback segments of the EHT compressed
48
beamforming/CQI report from the EHT beamformee, shall not use a BFRP Trigger frame to request
49
50 retransmission of the feedback segments. In this case, the EHT beamformer may repeat the entire sounding
51 sequence.
52
53
35.7.5 EHT sounding NDP transmission
54
55
56 The TXVECTOR parameters for an EHT sounding NDP shall be set as follows:
57 — FORMAT is set to EHT_MU.
58
59 — APEP_LENGTH is set to 0.
60 — EHT_LTF_TYPE is set to either 2(cid:117) EHT-LTF or 4(cid:117) EHT-LTF (if supported).
61
62 — If EHT_LTF_TYPE is 2(cid:117) EHT-LTF, then GI_TYPE is set to either 0.8 μs GI or 1.6 μs GI.
63 — If EHT_LTF_TYPE is 4(cid:117) EHT-LTF, then GI_TYPE is set to 3.2 μs GI.
64
65

41 35.8 Restricted TWT (R-TWT)
42
43
35.8.1 General
44
45
46 Restricted TWT operation described in this subclause enables the STAs in the BSS to use enhanced medium
47 access protection and resource reservation mechanisms for delivery of latency sensitive traffic.
48
49
An EHT STA with dot11RestrictedTWTOptionImplemented equal to true shall set the Restricted TWT
50
51 Support subfield in its transmitted EHT Capabilities element to 1 and shall set the Broadcast TWT Support
52 subfield in its transmitted HE Capabilities element to 1; otherwise, the EHT STA shall set the Restricted
53 TWT Support subfield in its transmitted EHT Capabilities element to 0.
54
55
A non-AP EHT STA establishes membership for one or more R-TWT schedules with its associated EHT AP
56
57 by following the rules defined in 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation) with the additional rules defined in
58 35.8.2 (R-TWT membership setup). An EHT AP that has dot11RestrictedTWTOptionImplemented equal to
59 true may announce one or more R-TWT SPs as described in 35.8.4 (R-TWT SPs announcement). EHT STAs
60
that support R-TWT operation follow the rules as defined in 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation) and the
61
additional rules and restrictions that are defined in the subclauses below.
62
63
64
65

## 第 618 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.8.2 R-TWT membership setup
2
3
35.8.2.1 Latency sensitive traffic differentiation
4
5
6 This subclause defines a mechanism that differentiates latency sensitive traffic from other types of traffic.
7
8 35.8.2.2 The setup procedure
9
10
An R-TWT membership is established using the same procedure used to set up a broadcast TWT
11
12 membership as described in 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation) except that the broadcast TWT element(s)
13 carried in the TWT Setup frame (9.6.24.8 (TWT Setup frame format)) include one or more Restricted TWT
14 Parameter Set fields as described in 9.4.2.199 (TWT element).
15
16
An R-TWT scheduling AP is an EHT AP with dot11TWTOptionActivated equal to true that sets the
17
18 Restricted TWT Support subfield in the transmitted EHT Capabilities element to 1.
19
20 An R-TWT scheduled STA is a non-AP EHT STA that sets the Restricted TWT Support subfield in the
21
transmitted EHT Capabilities element to 1 and sends to or receives from an R-TWT scheduling AP a
22
broadcast TWT element carrying one or more Restricted TWT Parameter Set field(s).
23
24
25 An R-TWT scheduling AP should set the Trigger field to 1 in the Restricted TWT Parameter Set field(s) it
26 transmits.
27
28
When included in an individually addressed TWT Setup frame transmitted by an R-TWT scheduling AP or
29
30 R-TWT scheduled STA, the Restricted TWT Traffic Info Present subfield of the Broadcast TWT Info field
31 included in a Restricted TWT Parameter Set field shall be set to 1.
32
33
The R-TWT scheduling AP and the R-TWT scheduled STA should set the Restricted TWT Traffic Info field
34
(see 9.4.2.199 (TWT element)) to identify the TID(s) that carry latency sensitive traffic in DL and UL for the
35
36 R-TWT membership being set up. The TID(s) indicated as latency sensitive traffic in DL and UL in the
37 Restricted TWT Traffic Info field shall be within the set of TIDs that are mapped in DL and UL,
38 respectively, to the link on which the R-TWT membership is being setup (see 35.3.7.1 (TID-to-link
39
mapping)).
40
41
42 The TID(s) that are specified in the Restricted TWT Traffic Info field of the TWT element (as described in
43 9.4.2.199 (TWT element)) in a TWT Response frame that indicates Accept TWT are referred to as R-TWT
44 DL TID(s) or R-TWT UL TID(s), and collectively as R-TWT TID(s), in the subsequent text and the
45
following subclauses.
46
47
48 When a TID-to-link mapping update results in a mapping where none of the R-TWT TID(s) are mapped
49 onto the link on which the R-TWT membership is set up any more, the corresponding R-TWT membership
50 is considered as torn-down.
51
52
A non-AP EHT STA that is also an R-TWT scheduled STA may signal QoS Characteristics element for a
53
54 traffic flow intended to be delivered over R-TWT SPs of an R-TWT schedule in an SCS Request frame to
55 the R-TWT scheduling AP as per procedures defined in 35.17 (EHT SCS procedure).
56
57
If an R-TWT scheduling AP has received QoS Characteristics element(s) from an R-TWT scheduled STA
58
whose TID and Direction fields match an R-TWT TID and its specified direction for an R-TWT schedule
59
60 setup, the R-TWT scheduling AP may use those parameters in QoS Characteristics element(s) as guidance
61 for R-TWT schedule setup.
62
63
64
65

## 第 619 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.8.3 Broadcast TWT operation
2
3
The TWT scheduling AP affiliated with an AP MLD, while announcing a broadcast TWT schedule in its
4
BSS, may indicate whether the schedule is an aligned schedule. An aligned schedule is a broadcast TWT
5
6 schedule that is available across multiple links such that the target wake times of the schedules on the
7 multiple links are aligned. Other TWT parameters of the aligned schedules on those multiple links remain
8 the same as each other.
9
10
TWT scheduled STAs affiliated with a non-AP MLD that are interested in joining an existing aligned
11
12 schedule on multiple links may send request to join the schedule on those links separately.
13
14 35.8.4 R-TWT SPs announcement
15
16
If there is any R-TWT membership set up, the EHT AP shall announce the R-TWT schedule information by
17
18 including Restricted TWT Parameter Set field(s) in the broadcast TWT element as specified in 9.4.2.199
19 (TWT element) contained in transmitted Management frames, which are specified in 26.8.3 (Broadcast
20 TWT operation). The membership is setup either with its associated EHT AP, or with any nontransmitting
21
AP that belongs to the same multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set as the transmitting AP. In a multiple
22
BSSID set, the transmitted BSSID shall include all advertised R-TWT schedules for the transmitted BSSID
23
24 and all nontransmitted BSSIDs in the same multiple BSSID set as described in 11.1.3.8.4 (Inheritance of
25 element values).
26
27
An R-TWT scheduling AP that includes a Restricted TWT Parameter Set field in a broadcast TWT element
28
shall set the Restricted TWT Traffic Info Present subfield of the Restricted TWT Parameter Set field to 0 if
29
30 the Negotiation Type subfield of the TWT element is equal to 2.
31
32 When advertising an R-TWT schedule, the R-TWT scheduling AP shall set the value of the Restricted TWT
33
Schedule Info subfield as follows:
34
35 — If the schedule does not have any non-AP member STA, or the schedule is suspended for all member
36 STAs, the AP shall set the value to 0 (a.k.a. the schedule is idle); otherwise,
37
— If the R-TWT schedule is for the BSS operated by the transmitting AP, the AP shall set the value to 2
38
39 to indicate the schedule is not available for accepting new membership due to resource constraints
40 (a.k.a. the schedule is full), and otherwise shall set the value to 1;
41
— If the R-TWT schedule is for a BSS operated by a nontransmitting AP that corresponds to a
42
nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set or that belongs to a co-hosted BSSID set, and the
43
44 schedule is not idle, the AP shall set the value to 3.
45
46 A non-AP STA should not request to establish membership in an R-TWT schedule advertised by the R-TWT
47
scheduling AP with the Restricted TWT Schedule subfield set to 2.
48
49
NOTE—The R-TWT scheduling AP that receives a request from a non-AP STA to establish membership in an R-TWT
50
schedule advertised by the AP with Restricted TWT Schedule Info subfield set to 2 might reject the request.
51
52
An R-TWT scheduling AP when announcing an R-TWT schedule, shall set the Target Wake Time field in
53
the TWT element in transmitted Management frames to TSF [10:25], where TSF corresponds to the first R-
54
55 TWT SP start time of the corresponding R-TWT agreement.
56
57 The R-TWT scheduling AP shall determine the start time of R-TWT SPs that happen after the first R-TWT
58
SP (next R-TWT SP start time) in a periodic R-TWT schedule based on the start time of the first R-TWT SP
59
and the TWT wake interval of the corresponding R-TWT schedule.
60
61
62
63
64
65

## 第 620 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.8.5 Channel access rules for R-TWT SPs
2
3
35.8.5.1 TXOP and backoff procedures rules for R-TWT SPs
4
5
6 A non-AP EHT STA with dot11RestrictedTWTOptionImplemented set to true as a TXOP holder shall
7 ensure the TXOP ends before the start time of any active R-TWT SPs that are advertised by its associated
8 AP or the AP corresponding to the transmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set in which its associated AP
9
belongs to, as specified in 35.8.4 (R-TWT SPs announcement). In addition, before starting transmission of
10
any PPDU, the non-AP EHT STA with dot11RestrictedTWTOptionImplemented set to true shall check if
11
12 there is enough time for the frame exchange to complete prior to the start of the R-TWT SP and, if there is
13 not enough time, then the STA shall defer transmission by selecting a random backoff count using the
14 present CW (without advancing to the next value in the sequence). The QSRC[AC] for the MSDU or A-
15
MSDU is not affected.
16
17 NOTE—The R-TWT schedule(s) announced in a Beacon or Probe Response frame and that is not in the nontransmitted
18 BSSID profile include the schedule(s) for both transmitted BSSID and nontransmitted BSSID(s), if any, as specified in
19 35.8.4 (R-TWT SPs announcement).
20
21
An EHT AP with dot11RestrictedTWTOptionImplemented set to true as a TXOP holder shall ensure the
22
TXOP ends before the start time of any active R-TWT SP advertised by itself as specified in 35.8.4 (R-TWT
23
24 SPs announcement) unless the remaining portion of TXOP fallen within the R-TWT SP is used for the
25 delivery of DL frames of R-TWT DL TID(s) or to solicit the UL frames of R-TWT UL TID(s).
26
27
When an R-TWT SP starts, a member STA may suspend decrementing the backoff counter of any AC that
28
does not have any R-TWT TID(s) mapped to until it has delivered all its frames from R-TWT TID(s), and
29
30 resume the decrementing afterwards or when the SP is ended.
31
32 When a non-AP STA, which is affiliated with a non-AP MLD and operates on one of a pair of NSTR or
33
EMLSR or EMLMR links, is a member of a R-TWT SP on the first link; if the second non-AP STA
34
affiliated with the same MLD is not a member of any other R-TWT SPs on the second link that overlap with
35
36 the first SP, then the second non-AP STA and its associated AP (referred as the second AP), if their
37 respective dot11RestrictedTWTOptionImplemented equal to true, should follow the rules below:
38
— The second AP as a TXOP holder on the second link should ensure its TXOP ends no later than T
39
amount of time before the start time of the R-TWT SP on the first link,
40
41 — The second non-AP STA as a TXOP holder on the second link should ensure its TXOP ends no later
42
than T amount of time before the start time of the R-TWT SP on the first link,
43
44
45 where T equals to one of the following values:
46 — 0 if the two non-AP STAs operate on a pair of NSTR links,
47
48 — the EMLSR transition delay, indicated in the EMLSR Transition Delay subfield, as specified for the
49 pair of EMLSR links if the two non-AP STAs belong to a pair of EMLSR links,
50
— the EMLMR delay, indicated in the EMLMR Delay subfield, as specified for the pair of EMLMR
51
links if the two non-AP STAs belong to a pair of EMLMR links.
52
53
54 35.8.5.2 Quieting STAs during R-TWT SPs
55
56
An R-TWT scheduling AP may schedule at most one quiet interval that overlaps with an R-TWT SP. Such a
57
quiet interval, referred to as an overlapping quiet interval in this subclause, if scheduled, shall have a
58
59 duration of 1 TU, and shall start at the same time as the corresponding R-TWT SP.
60
61 To schedule overlapping quiet intervals for one or more R-TWT SPs that belong to one or more periodic or
62
aperiodic R-TWT schedules, the EHT AP may do so by transmitting one or more Quiet elements in Beacon
63
and Probe Response frames. An EHT AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not include in its transmitted
64
65

1 Beacon or Probe Response frames any Quiet elements that correspond to overlapping quiet intervals that are
2 scheduled and advertised by other APs affiliated with the same AP MLD (see 35.3.11 (Multi-link procedures
3
for channel switching, extended channel switching, and channel quieting)).
4
5 NOTE 1—Unless specified otherwise (e.g., through the rules in this subclause), the channel access and transmission
6 rules during quiet intervals are defined in 11.8.3 (Quieting channels for testing), 26.17.1 (Basic HE BSS operation), and
7 26.17.2 (HE BSS operation in the 6 GHz band). AP can still use quiet intervals for channel testing by managing or
8 avoiding the overlap between R-TWT SPs and quiet intervals that it schedules.

Non-AP EHT STAs may behave as if overlapping quiet intervals do not exist.
11

15
35.8.6 Traffic delivery
16
17
18 An R-TWT scheduling AP or a member R-TWT scheduled STA that has initiated or participated in a frame
19 exchange during an R-TWT SP shall ensure that QoS Data frames of the R-TWT TID(s) are delivered first
20 during the R-TWT SP.
21
22
In a trigger-enabled R-TWT SP, the following apply:
23
24 — When scheduling the transmission of Trigger frames, the R-TWT scheduling AP shall first trigger
25 member R-TWT scheduled STAs to facilitate them to first deliver their QoS Data frames of R-TWT
26
UL TID(s), if any.
27
28 — The triggered member STA follows the rules specified in 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-
29 enabled single-TID A-MPDU) to aggregate MPDUs except that the STA shall first include QoS Data
30
frames (if any) of TID(s) in the R-TWT UL TID(s).
31
32 NOTE—The R-TWT scheduling AP might still include the 12 LSB of the AID of a STA that is not a member of this R-
33 TWT SP in Trigger frame(s) transmitted in trigger-enabled SPs.
34
35
If an R-TWT scheduling AP has established SCS stream(s) described by QoS Characteristics element(s)
36
with an R-TWT scheduled STA whose TID and Direction fields match an R-TWT TID and its specified
37
38 direction for an R-TWT schedule established with the R-TWT scheduled STA, the AP should follow the
39 rules specified in 35.17 (EHT SCS procedure) for scheduling of downlink or uplink QoS Data frames when
40 scheduling QoS Data frames for that R-TWT TID in corresponding R-TWT SPs, in addition to the traffic
41
delivery rules specified in this clause.
42
43
44
35.9 Operating mode indication
45
46
47 An EHT AP that supports 320 MHz shall set dot11EHTOMIOptionImplemented to true.
48
49 An EHT STA with dot11EHTOMIOptionImplemented that is equal to true shall set the EHT OM Control
50
Support subfield in the EHT MAC Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element it
51
transmits to 1; otherwise the EHT STA shall set the EHT OM Control Support subfield to 0.
52
53
54 An EHT STA with dot11EHTOMIOptionImplemented that is equal to true shall set
55 dot11OMIOptionImplemented to true.
56
57
An EHT STA that transmits a frame with an A-Control subfield of HE variant HT Control field, which
58
59 includes an EHT OM Control subfield shall concatenate the OM Control subfield within the same A-Control
60 subfield after the EHT OM Control field. An EHT STA shall not include an EHT OM Control field in an A-
61 Control field unless the OM Control field is present in the same A-Control field.
62
63 NOTE 1—An EHT STA is an HE STA and as such inherits all the functionalities defined in 26.9 (Operating mode
64 indication).

35.10 EHT Spatial reuse operation
47
48
49 35.10.1 General
50
51
An EHT STA follows the rules defined in 26.10 (Spatial reuse operation) and the additional rules defined as
52
below.
53
54
55 An EHT STA follows the rules defined in 26.2.3 (SRG PPDU identification) and the following rule:
56
— A received EHT PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU is an SRG PPDU if the bit in the SRG BSS Color
57
58 Bitmap field indexed by the value of the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is 1 (see
59 9.4.2.252 (Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element)).
60
61
An EHT STA follows the rules defined in 35.2.3 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classification for EHT
62
STA).

1 35.10.2 OBSS PD-based spatial reuse operation
2
3
An EHT STA follows the rules defined in 26.10.2.2 (General operation with non-SRG OBSS PD level) and
4
26.10.2.3 (General operation with SRG OBSS PD level) and the following rules:
5
6 — The PHY-CCARESET.request primitive shall be issued at the end of the PPDU if the PPDU is an
7 EHT MU PPDU addressed to a single STA and the RXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE
8
indicates SR_DELAYED.
9
10 — If the PHY-CCARESET.request primitive is issued before the end of the received PPDU, and a
11 TXOP is initiated within the duration of the received PPDU, then the TXOP and the duration of the
12
transmitted PPDU within that TXOP shall be limited to the duration of the received PPDU if the
13
received PPDU is an EHT MU PPDU addressed to multiple STAs and the RXVECTOR parameter
14
15 SPATIAL_REUSE indicates SR_RESTRICTED.
16 — The received signal strength level used to determine if it is below the non-SRG OBSS PD level or
17
SRG OBSS PD level is measured in dBm/20 MHz from the L-STF or L-LTF fields in at least one of
18
the nonpunctured 20 MHz subchannels of the PPDU or the PHY SYNC field, shortSYNC field or
19
20 Long PHY SYNC field, whichever exists and which is used to determine PHY-CCA.indication. It is
21 implementation specific on which of these 20 MHz subchannel(s) the received signal strength level
22 is measured.
23
24
An EHT STA follows the rules defined in 26.10.2.4 (Adjustment of OBSS PD and transmit power), except
25
26 that the following applies:
27
— If using OBSS PD-based spatial reuse, an EHT STA shall maintain an OBSS PD level and may
28
adjust this OBSS PD level in conjunction with its transmit power. The adjustment shall be made in
29
30 accordance with Equation (35-4).

42
43
An EHT STA identifies a PSR opportunity if the following two conditions are met:
44

46 PPDU that is identified as an inter-BSS PPDU (see 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classifi-
47
cation)).
48
49 2) An PSRT PPDU is queued for transmission and the intended transmit power of the PSRT PPDU in
50 dBm shall meet the following condition in Equation (35-5):
51
52

PSRR(cid:15)20MHz

60

61
62 PPDU are present. The measurement method is implementation specific.
