1 35.11.1.2 POWER_BOOST_FACTOR
2
3
For an OFDMA EHT MU PPDU, the POWER_BOOST_FACTOR parameter in the TXVECTOR for an
4
5 occupied RU or MRU shall be in the range of (cid:62)1(cid:101) 2(cid:15) 2(cid:64) if the Power Boost Factor Support subfield of the
6 EHT PHY Capabilities Information field in the EHT Capabilities element from any recipient STA of the
7
PPDU equals 0; otherwise the POWER_BOOST_FACTOR shall be in the range of [0.5, 2].
8
9
10 For a non-OFDMA EHT MU PPDU (including the EHT SU transmission), the POWER_BOOST_FACTOR
11 shall be set to 1.
12
13
For an EHT sounding NDP PPDU, the POWER_BOOST_FACTOR shall be set to 1.
14
15 NOTE—For an EHT TB PPDU, the POWER_BOOST_FACTOR is not present in the TXVECTOR.
16
17
35.11.1.3 UPLINK_FLAG
18
19
20 An EHT STA shall set the parameter UPLINK_FLAG in the TXVECTOR following the rules defined in
21 26.11.2 (UPLINK_FLAG) and with the following additions:
22
— The rules that apply to an HE MU PPDU shall also apply to an EHT MU PPDU
23
24
25 35.11.1.4 BSS_COLOR
26
27
An EHT STA shall set the parameter BSS_COLOR in the TXVECTOR following the rules defined in
28
26.11.4 (BSS_COLOR) and with the following additions:
29
30 — The rules that apply to an HE MU PPDU shall also apply to an EHT MU PPDU
31
32
An EHT non-AP STA that transmits an EHT MU PPDU addressed to a STA that is not a member of the
33
transmitting STA’s EHT BSS, shall set the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR to 0.
34
35
36 35.11.1.5 TXOP_DURATION
37
38

39
26.11.5 (TXOP_DURATION) and with the following additions:
40
41 — The rules that apply to an HE MU PPDU shall also apply to an EHT MU PPDU
42
43
An EHT STA that is a TXOP responder that transmits an EHT MU PPDU addressed to a single STA shall
44
45 set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to UNSPECIFIED, if the RXVECTOR parameter
46 TXOP_DURATION of the EHT PPDU that solicits a response from the STA is UNSPECIFIED.

51 An EHT STA shall set the parameter TRIGGER_RESPONDING in the TXVECTOR following the rules
52 defined in 26.11.8 (TRIGGER_RESPONDING).

54
35.11.2 SPATIAL_REUSE
55
56
57 The contents of the Spatial Reuse fields are carried in the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE for an
58 EHT PPDU indicating spatial reuse information. The behavior of STAs upon reception of an EHT PPDU
59 with different SPATIAL_REUSE values is described in 26.10.2 (OBSS PD-based spatial reuse operation)
60
and 35.10 (EHT Spatial reuse operation). The different values that may be indicated in the SPATIAL_
61
REUSE parameter of the TXVECTOR are listed in Table 27-22 (Spatial Reuse field encoding for an HE SU
62
63 PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, and HE MU PPDU) that is applied to EHT MU PPDU and Table 27-23 (Spatial
64 Reuse field encoding for an HE TB PPDU) that is applied to EHT TB PPDU. The value PSR_DISALLOW
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 625
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 626 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 is used to prohibit PSR-based spatial reuse during the transmission of the corresponding PPDU. The value
2 PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED is used to prohibit both PSR-based spatial reuse and
3
non-SRG OBSS PD-based spatial reuse during the transmission of the corresponding PPDU. The
4
interpretation of other values are described in this subclause and in 35.10 (EHT Spatial reuse operation) and
5
6 26.10 (Spatial reuse operation). The conditions for a STA to set the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter to its
7 different values are described in this subclause.
8
9
For a PPDU with a value of EHT_TB for the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT, the SPATIAL_REUSE
10
parameter contains an array of one to two values, depending on the TXVECTOR parameter
11
12 CH_BANDWIDTH. If the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW20, the value is the
13 SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to the 20 MHz subband. If the TXVECTOR parameter
14 CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW40, the first value in the array is the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies
15
to the first lowest 20 MHz subband, and the second value is the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to
16
the second lowest 20 MHz subband. If the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW80, the first
17
18 value in the array is the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to each 20 MHz subchannel of the first
19 lowest 40 MHz subband, and the second value applies to each 20 MHz subchannel of the second lowest
20 40 MHz subband. If the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW160, the first value in the array
21
is the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to each 20 MHz subchannel of the first lowest 80 MHz
22
subband, and the second value applies to each 20 MHz subchannel of the second lowest 80 MHz subband. If
23
24 the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW320-1 or CBW320-2, the first value in the array is
25 the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to each 20 MHz subchannel of the first lowest 160 MHz
26 subband, and the second value applies to each 20 MHz subchannel of the second lowest 160 MHz subband.
27
28
An EHT STA that transmits an EHT TB PPDU sets the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE as
29
30 defined in 35.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation).
31
32 A non-AP STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits an EHT MU PPDU may set
33
the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE, when permitted by the rules defined in
34
26.11.6 (SPATIAL_REUSE), to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED if the
35
36 HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_value15_allowed subfield of the SR Control field of the most recently received
37 Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP is equal to 1. Otherwise, the non-AP STA shall
38 set it to PSR_DISALLOW.
39
40
An EHT STA that transmits an EHT TB PPDU determines the value of the TXVECTOR parameter
41
42 SPATIAL_REUSE according to 35.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation).
43
44 An EHT AP with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits an EHT MU PPDU may set the
45
TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to PSR_DISALLOW to disallow OBSS STAs from performing
46
EHT PSR-based SR transmission during the duration of the corresponding PPDU.
47
48
49 An EHT STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to false may set the TXVECTOR parameter
50 SPATIAL_REUSE to PSR_DISALLOW for any PPDU that is not an EHT TB PPDU, an EHT sounding
51
NDP, a PPDU containing an EHT NDP Announcement frame, or a PPDU containing a response to an HE
52
NDP Announcement frame.
53
54
55 A STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of an EHT PPDU to PSR_DISALLOW or,
56 if permitted by the other rules in this subclause, to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED, if
57
the STA is a non-AP EHT STA and the PSR Disallowed subfield of the SR Control field of the most recently
58
received Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP is equal to 1.
59
60
61 An EHT STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to
62 PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_ PROHIBITED for an EHT sounding NDP.
63

11
12 STA has not received a Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP, the STA shall not set the

21

22
recently transmitted Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element is equal to 0, or if the AP has not transmitted a
23
24 Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element, the AP shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of

28
TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_DELAYED.
29
30
31 An EHT STA that transmits an EHT MU PPDU configured for more than one user shall not set the
32 TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_DELAYED.
33
34
An EHT STA that transmits an EHT SU transmission shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter
35
36 SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_RESTRICTED.
37
38 An EHT AP that transmits an EHT MU PPDU that contains a Trigger frame and is configured for more than
39
one user should set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_RESTRICTED.
40
41
42 An EHT STA that transmits a PPDU that does not contain a Trigger frame shall not set the TXVECTOR
43 parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_DELAYED or SR_RESTRICTED.

The EHT-MCS Map (BW (cid:148) 80 MHz, Except 20 MHz-Only Non-AP STA) field in the Supported EHT-MCS
19
And NSS Set field in the EHT Capabilities element, if present, shall be set to the first three octets of
20
21 dot11EHTSupportedEhtMcsAndNssSetImplemented. The EHT-MCS Map (BW = 160 MHz) field in the
22 Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field in the EHT Capabilities element, if present, shall be set to the
23 second three octets of dot11EHTSupportedEhtMcsAndNssSetImplemented. The EHT-MCS Map (BW =
24
320 MHz) field in the Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field in the EHT Capabilities element, if present,
25
shall be set to the third three octets of dot11EHTSupportedEhtMcsAndNssSetImplemented.
26
27
28 35.11.4 CENTER_FREQUENCY_SEGMENT
29
30
A 20 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA shall issue a PHY-CONFIG.request primitive with the
31
CENTER_FREQUENCY_SEGMENT parameter in the PHYCONFIG_VECTOR set to the center
32
33 frequency of the primary 20 MHz channel except when the 20 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA sets
34 dot11HESubchannelSelectiveTransmissionImplemented equal to true in which case the 20 MHz operating
35 non-AP EHT STA may issue a PHY-CONFIG.request primitive with the
36
CENTER_FREQUENCY_SEGMENT parameter in the PHYCONFIG_VECTOR set to the center
37
frequency of any 20 MHz channel within the BSS bandwidth of 40 MHz, 80 MHz or 160 MHz by following
38
39 the procedure in 26.8.7 (HE subchannel selective transmission). The 20 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA
40 may also issue a PHY-CONFIG.request primitive with the CENTER_FREQUENCY_SEGMENT parameter
41 in the PHYCONFIG_VECTOR set to the center frequency of any 20 MHz channel within the primary
42
160 MHz when the BSS bandwidth is 320 MHz by following the procedure in 26.8.7 (HE subchannel
43
selective transmission).
44
45
46 An 80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA shall issue a PHY-CONFIG.request primitive with the
47 CENTER_FREQUENCY_SEGMENT parameter in the PHYCONFIG_VECTOR set to the center
48
frequency of the primary 80 MHz channel except when the 80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA sets
49
dot11HESubchannelSelectiveTransmissionImplemented equal to true and parks on an 80 MHz channel
50
51 without preamble puncturing. In this exceptional case, the 80 MHz operating non-AP EHT STA may issue a
52 PHY-CONFIG.request primitive with the CENTER_FREQUENCY_SEGMENT parameter in the
53 PHYCONFIG_VECTOR set to the center frequency of any 80 MHz channel within the primary 160 MHz of
54
the BSS bandwidth by following the procedure in 26.8.7 (HE subchannel selective transmission).
55
56
57 35.11.5 INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS
58
59 An EHT STA shall not transmit on any 20 MHz subchannel that is punctured as indicated in the
60
TXVECTOR parameter INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS (see Table 36-1 (TXVECTOR and RXVECTOR
61
parameters)).

9 35.12 Intra-PPDU power save for non-AP EHT STAs
10
11 A non-AP EHT STA that operates in intra-PPDU power save mode shall follow the rules defined in
12
26.14.1 (Intra-PPDU power save for non-AP HE STAs) and with the following additions:
13
14 — The conditions that apply to an HE MU PPDU shall also apply to an EHT MU PPDU, and
15
— The conditions that apply to an HE TB PPDU shall also apply to an EHT TB PPDU.

35.13 Nominal packet padding values selection rules
19
20
21 35.13.1 General
22
23
An EHT STA with dot11EHTPPEThresholdsRequired set to false may set the PPE Thresholds Present
24
subfield in the EHT Capabilities element that it transmits to 0.
25
26
27 An EHT STA with dot11EHTPPEThresholdsRequired set to true shall set the PPE Thresholds Present
28 subfield in the EHT Capabilities element that it transmits to 1.
29
30
35.13.2 PPET not present in both HE and EHT
31
32
33 An EHT STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 in both the EHT and HE Capabilities
34 elements, and the Common Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 0 in the EHT Capabilities element that it
35
transmits shall have a nominal packet padding of 0 μs for all constellations, N and large size RU
36 SS
37 allocations that it supports.
38
39 An EHT STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 in both the EHT and HE Capabilities
40
elements, and the Common Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 1 in the EHT Capabilities element that it
41
transmits shall have a nominal packet padding of 8 μs for all constellations, N and large size RU
42 SS
43 allocations that it supports (see 36.3.2.2 (Subcarriers and resource allocation for multiple RUs)).
44
45
An EHT STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 in both the EHT and HE Capabilities
46
elements, and the Common Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 2 in the EHT Capabilities element that it
47
48 transmits shall have a nominal packet padding of 16 μs for all constellations, N SS and large size RU
49 allocations that it supports.
50
51
An EHT STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 in both the EHT and HE Capabilities
52
53 elements, and the Common Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 3 in the EHT Capabilities element that it
54 transmits shall have a nominal packet padding of 16 μs for all modes with constellation order up to 1024-
55
QAM, N less than or equal to 8, and large size RU or MRU size less than or equal to 2(cid:117)996, and a nominal
56 SS
57 packet padding of 20 μs for all the other modes with a large size RU or MRU that the STA supports.
58
59 An EHT STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 in both the EHT and HE Capabilities
60
elements shall have a nominal packet padding of 0 μs for a small size RU or MRU (see 36.3.2.2 (Subcarriers
61
and resource allocation for multiple RUs)), if 4096-QAM is not used for the RU or MRU; or if the RU size
62
63 is 106 or the MRU size is 106+26 and EHT-MCS 15 is not applied to them. An EHT STA that sets the PPE
64 Thresholds Present subfield to 0 in both the EHT and HE Capabilities elements shall have a nominal packet

59

1 EHT-MCS 14 or EHT-MCS 15 is applied in a given RU, the nominal packet padding value shall be based
2 on the next larger RU allocation index (RU allocation index + 1). Examples of the selection of the RU
3
allocation index considering DCM include:
4
5 — If EHT-MCS 15 is applied to a 242-tone RU then the nominal packet padding value for a 484-tone
6 RU shall be used.
7

8
9 padding value for a 242-tone RU shall be used.
10 — If EHT-MCS 15 is applied to an RU or MRU indicated by the RU allocation index equal to 3 or 4,
11
then the nominal packet padding value for the same RU or MRU shall be used.
12
13 — If EHT-MCS 14 is applied, the RU allocation indices (b + DCM) for the 80 MHz, 160 MHz, and
14 320 MHz PPDUs shall be equal to 3, 3, and 4, respectively.

16
The PPETmax and PPET8 subfields for RU allocation index k shall be present in the PPE Thresholds Info
17
18 field only if bit k of the RU Index Bitmask subfield (bit 4 + k of the EHT PPE Thresholds field) is 1. When
19 there exists one or more 0s before the first 1 in the bitmask sequence in the RU Index Bitmask subfield, the
20 PPETmax and PPET8 subfields for each RU allocation index corresponding to these 0s shall not be present,
21
and the nominal packet padding value shall be 0 μs for these RU(s) or MRU(s). For example, if the bitmask
22
sequence of RU Index Bitmask subfield is [0 0 1 1 1], the nominal packet padding value shall be 0 μs for the
23
24 242-tone RU and 484-tone RU.
25
26 When there exists one or more 0s after the first 1 in the bitmask sequence in the RU Index Bitmask subfield,
27
the PPETmax and PPET8 subfields for each RU allocation index corresponding to these 0s shall not be
28
present, but the PPETmax and PPET8 values shall be present, and the values shall be the same as the
29
30 PPETmax and PPET8 values for the closest smaller RU allocation index with the bitmask value equal to 1 in
31 the RU Index Bitmask subfield. For example, if the bitmask sequence of RU Index Bitmask subfield is [1 0
32 0 1 1], the PPETmax and PPET8 values for 484-tone RU, 484+242-tone MRU, and 996-tone RU shall be the
33
same as for the 242-tone RU.
34
35
36 The PPETmax and PPET8 subfields for an NSS value n shall be present only if n is less than or equal to
37 (NSS_PE + 1), where NSS_PE is the value in the NSS_PE subfield in the EHT PPE Thresholds field of the
38 EHT Capabilities element. When the number of spatial streams of the EHT PPDU transmission is greater
39
than (NSS_PE + 1) and less than or equal to 8, the nominal packet padding value shall be 16 μs for all
40
supported RU or MRU sizes and constellations.
41
42
43 An EHT STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 1 in the EHT Capabilities element shall have
44 a nominal packet padding of 0 μs for a small size RU or MRU, if 4096-QAM is not used for the RU or
45
MRU; or if the RU size is 106 or the MRU size is 106+26 and EHT-MCS 15 is not applied to them. An EHT
46
STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 1 in the EHT Capabilities element shall have a nominal
47
48 packet padding value the same as the value for the 242-tone RU, if 4096-QAM is used for the RU or MRU;
49 or if the RU size is 106 or the MRU size is 106+26 and EHT-MCS 15 is applied to them.
50
51
35.13.5 STA behavior related to nominal packet padding
52
53
54 A STA transmitting an EHT MU PPDU shall provide the nominal packet padding in the TXVECTOR
55 parameter NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING for the minimal PE calculation (see 36.3.14 (Packet
56 extension)).
57
58
The nominal packet padding value for a broadcast RU or MRU contained in an EHT PPDU that a STA
59
60 transmits shall be set to 20 μs if the RU or MRU is modulated with 4096-QAM, or the RU or MRU is
61 greater than 2(cid:117)996, and shall be set to 16 μs for all other modes. A STA transmitting an EHT PPDU that
62 carries a broadcast frame shall not set the value of the TXVECTOR parameter
63
NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING to a value that is less than that required for any of the recipients and the
64
broadcast RU or MRU. A STA transmitting an EHT PPDU that carries a group addressed, but not broadcast,
65
634 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 635 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 frame shall not set the value of the TXVECTOR parameter NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING to a value
2 that is less than that required for any of the recipients in the group.
3
4
If a STA A is transmitting an EHT MU PPDU to a STA B, where the STA A has not received a frame
5
6 including the EHT Capabilities element from the STA B, then the STA A shall set the value of the
7 TXVECTOR parameter NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING to:
8
— 20 μs if the RU or MRU is modulated with 4096-QAM, or the RU or MRU size is greater than
9
2(cid:117)996-tone.
10
11 — 16 μs otherwise.
12
13 NOTE—One such situation is an AP transmitting to a nonassociated STA. Another such situation is a nonassociated
14 STA transmitting to an AP without having received a management frame including an EHT Capabilities element from
15 the AP, such as a Beacon or Probe Response frame.
16
17 A STA transmitting an EHT MU PPDU to a receiving STA shall include post-FEC padding determined by
18
the pre-FEC padding factor (see 36.3.13 (Data field)) and after including the post-FEC padding, the
19
transmitting STA shall include a packet extension with a duration computed based on the TXVECTOR
20
21 parameter NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING (see 36.3.14 (Packet extension)).
22
23
24 35.14 PPDU format, BW, MCS, NSS, and DCM selection rules
25
26 35.14.1 General
27
28
An EHT STA can transmit different PPDU formats, with different transmit parameters, such as channel
29
30 width, MCS, NSS, and DCM. This subclause defines the rules followed by an EHT STA for selecting these
31 parameters depending on the capabilities of the intended receiver(s) and other considerations.
32
33
35.14.2 PPDU format selection
34
35
36 An EHT STA that transmits non-HT, HT, VHT, or HE PPDUs shall follow the rules in 26.15.2 (PPDU
37 format selection).
38
39
An EHT AP may transmit an EHT MU PPDU as defined in 35.5.1 (EHT DL MU operation). A non-AP
40
EHT STA transmits EHT TB PPDUs as defined in 35.5.2 (EHT UL MU operation). An EHT STA may
41
42 transmit an EHT MU PPDU to a peer EHT STA subject to the restrictions defined below.
43
44 An EHT STA in 6 GHz band shall not transmit an EHT PPDU in EHT duplicate mode to a peer EHT STA if
45
the EHT Capabilities element received from that peer EHT STA has the Support Of EHT DUP (EHT-
46
MCS 14) In 6 GHz subfield equal to 0.
47
48
49 An EHT STA shall not transmit an EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 15 and MRUs listed in Table 9-401m
50 (Subfield of the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field) to a peer EHT STA if the EHT Capabilities
51
element received from that peer EHT STA has the Support Of EHT-MCS 15 In MRU subfield equal to 0.
52
53
54 An EHT STA shall send Control frames following the rules defined in 10.6.6 (Rate selection for Control
55 frames) and 26.15.2 (PPDU format selection) with the following additional exceptions:
56
— A Control frame sent by an EHT AP as a response to an EHT TB PPDU may be carried in any PPDU
57
58 format that is supported by the intended receivers.
59 — A Trigger frame that is not an MU-RTS Trigger frame may be carried in any PPDU format that is
60
supported by the intended receivers subject to the restrictions in 35.5.2 (EHT UL MU operation).
61
62 — An MU-RTS Trigger frame may be carried in an EHT MU PPDU whose TXVECTOR parameter
63 EHT_PPDU_TYPE is set to 1 (see 35.2.2.1 (MU-RTS Trigger frame transmission)).
64
65

## 第 636 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — A Control frame is carried in an EHT TB PPDU if it is sent as a response to a PPDU that contains a
2 Trigger frame that is not an MU-RTS Trigger frame (see 35.5.2 (EHT UL MU operation)).
3
— A Control frame sent by an EHT STA as a response to an EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 15 or 14 that
4
5 does not contain a Trigger frame should be carried in an EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 15 or 14,
6 respectively unless
7
• the most recently transmitted EHT PPDU by the STA that is correctly received by the transmitter
8
9 of the EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 15 or 14 was not an EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 15 or 14 in
10 which case the Control frame should be carried in non-HT (duplicate) PPDU.
11
— A Control frame sent by an EHT STA as a response to an EHT PPDU with MCS other than EHT-
12
MCS 14 and 15 or to a non-HT (duplicate) PPDU that does not contain a triggering frame should be
13
14 carried in a non-HT (duplicate) PPDU unless
15
• the most recent PPDU sent by the EHT STA to the recipient of the Control frame and received
16
correctly by the peer STA was an EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 14 or 15 in which case the Control
17
18 frame should be carried in EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 14 or 15, respectively.
19 — A Control frame that is not solicited by another frame and is not a Trigger frame may be carried in
20
EHT PPDU with EHT-MCS 14 or 15 subject to the restriction defined in this subclause.
21
22 — A Control frame sent by an EHT AP as a response solicited by SRS Control field is carried in the
23 PPDU that satisfies the requirements defined in 35.3.16.5.2 (End time alignment of response PPDUs
24
using SRS Control field).
25
26 — An EHT STA may transmit a BlockAck frame in an HE SU PPDU or in an EHT MU PPDU directed
27 to a single EHT STA if the PPDU duration of the HE SU PPDU or EHT MU PPDU (respectively) is
28 less than the PPDU duration of a non-HT PPDU containing the Control frame sent at the primary rate
29
(see 10.6.6.5.2 (Selection of a rate or MCS)).
30
31
32 35.14.3 MCS, NSS, BW selection
33
34 An EHT STA shall follow the rules defined in 10.6 (Multirate support) and 26.15.4 (Rate selection
35
constraints for HE STAs) for selecting the rate, MCS, NSS, and the rules defined in 10.3.2.7 (VHT and S1G
36
RTS procedure), 10.3.2.9 (CTS and DMG CTS procedure), 10.6.6.6 (Channel Width selection for Control
37
38 frames), and 10.6.12 (Channel Width in non-HT and non-HT duplicate PPDUs) for selecting the channel
39 width (bandwidth) of transmitted PPDUs with the following exceptions:
40
— EHT-MCS, NSS, and BW selection for an EHT TB PPDU are defined in 35.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA
41
42 behavior for UL MU operation).
43 — Rate and BW selection for a CTS sent in response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame are defined in
44
35.2.2.2 (CTS frame response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame).
45
46
47 An EHT STA that transmits an EHT PPDU to a receiving STA shall use an <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple that is
48 supported by the receiving STA as indicated by the Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field in the EHT
49 Capabilities element that the receiving STA transmits. If the Supported EHT-MCS and NSS set of the
50
receiving STA or STAs is not known, the transmitting STA shall transmit using a <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple
51
in the basic EHT-MCS and NSS set if the basic EHT-MCS and NSS set is not empty, otherwise the
52
53 transmitting STA shall transmit using a <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple in the mandatory EHT-MCS and NSS set.
54 An EHT STA is subject to all of the rules for HT STAs and VHT STAs that apply to its operating band (see
55 10.27 (Protection mechanisms)).
56
57
An EHT STA may transmit an EHT PPDU with 1024-QAM or 4096-QAM on a 26-, 52-, and 106-tone RU
58
59 to a recipient STA if it has received from the recipient STA an EHT Capabilities element with the Rx 1024-
60 QAM And 4096-QAM < 242-tone RU Support subfield in the EHT PHY Capabilities Information field
61 equal to 1; otherwise the EHT STA shall not transmit an EHT PPDU with 1024-QAM or 4096-QAM on a
62
26-, 52-, and 106-tone RU.
63

25 35.14.4 Rate selection constraints for EHT STAs
26
27
35.14.4.1 Receive EHT-MCS and NSS Set
28
29
30 The receive EHT-MCS and NSS set is the set of <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuples for PPDU bandwidths equal to
31 20 MHz, less than or equal to 80 MHz, less than or equal to 160 MHz PPDUs, less than or equal to 320 MHz
32
PPDUs that a STA is capable of receiving. The receive EHT-MCS and NSS set for a first STA is determined
33
by a second EHT STA for each <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple, NSS = 1, 2, …, 8, and PPDU bandwidth (less than
34
35 or equal to 20 MHz only, 80 MHz, and 160 MHz or 320 MHz) from the Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set
36 field in the EHT Capabilities element received from the first STA as follows:
37
— If support for the EHT-MCS for NSS spatial streams at that PPDU bandwidth is mandatory (see
38
39 36.1.1 (Introduction to the EHT PHY)), then the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at that bandwidth is
40 supported by the first STA on receive.
41
— Otherwise, if the Rx Max Nss that supports EHT-MCS n1–n2 (n1 and n2 indicate the MCS set being
42
applied) in EHT-MCS Map b subfield for b(cid:143){20 MHz only for 20 MHz only STA, (cid:148) 80 MHz for
43
44 (cid:116) 80 MHz STA, 160 MHz for (cid:116) 160 MHz STA, 320 MHz} indicates support and neither the
45 Operating Mode field nor the OM Control subfield and the optional EHT OM Control subfield is
46 received from the first EHT STA, then the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at PPDU bandwidth b for a
47
given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on receive as defined in 9.4.2.313.4
48
(Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field).
49
50 — Otherwise
51
• If the Operating Mode field is received from the first EHT STA, the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at
52
53 that PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on
54 receive as defined in as defined in 9.4.2.313.4 (Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field).
55 • If the OM Control subfield is received from the first EHT STA, the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at
56
that PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on
57
receive as defined in as defined in 9.4.2.313.4 (Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field).
58
59 • If the OM Control subfield and EHT OM Control subfield are received from the first EHT STA,
60 the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is sup-
61 ported by the first STA on receive as defined in 9.4.2.313.4 (Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set
62
field).

12 35.14.4.2 Transmit EHT-MCS and NSS Set
13
14 The transmit EHT-MCS and NSS set is the set of <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuples for PPDU bandwidth less than
15
or equal to 20 MHz only, 80 MHz, 160 MHz PPDUs or 320 MHz PPDUs that a STA is capable of
16
transmitting. The transmit EHT-MCS and NSS set of a first STA is determined by a second STA for each
17
18 <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple, NSS = 1, 2, …, 8, and PPDU bandwidth (less than or equal to 20 MHz only for
19 20 MHz only STA, 80 MHz for (cid:116) 80 MHz STA, 160 MHz or 320 MHz) from the Supported EHT-MCS
20
And NSS Set field received from the first STA as follows:
21
22 — If support for the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at that bandwidth is mandatory (see 36.1.1 (Introduction
23 to the EHT PHY)), then the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth is supported by the
24
first STA on transmit.
25
26 — Otherwise, if the Tx Max Nss that supports EHT-MCS n1–n2 (n1 and n2 indicate the MCS set being
27 applied) in the EHT-MCS Map b subfield where b is the PPDU bandwidth indicates support, then the
28 <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at PPDU bandwidth b for a given operating channel width is supported by
29
the first STA on receive as defined in 9.4.2.313.4 (Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field).
30
31 — Otherwise,
32
• If the OM Control subfield is received from the first EHT STA, the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at
33
that PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on trans-
34
35 mit as defined in as defined in 9.4.2.313.4 (Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set field).
36 • If the OM Control subfield and EHT OM Control subfield are received from the first EHT STA,
37 the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is sup-
38
ported by the first STA on transmit as defined in 9.4.2.313.4 (Supported EHT-MCS And NSS Set
39
field).
40
41 — Otherwise, the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth is not supported by the first STA
42
on transmit.
43
44
45 35.14.4.3 Additional rate selection constraints for EHT PPDUs
46
47 If a combination of HE MCS, NSS under a bandwidth is not allowed in the BSS per HE-MCSs that are
48
marked as unsupported as defined in 26.15.4.3 (Additional rate selection constraints for HE PPDUs), the
49
combination of EHT-MCS, NSS where EHT-MCS is equal to HE-MCS shall be disallowed under the
50
51 bandwidth. If a combination of EHT-MCS 0, NSS = 1 at a bandwidth is not allowed in a BSS, the EHT-
52 MCS 15 at the bandwidth shall be disallowed.
53
54

55
56
57 An AP that transmits group addressed frames except the Beacon frames in an EHT MU PPDU whose

59 NSS> tuple where the EHT-MCS is a mandatory EHT-MCS and NSS = 1.
60
61
A group addressed frame transmitted in an EHT MU PPDU whose TXVECTOR has EHT_PPDU_TYPE
62
63 equal to 1 shall be sent as an S-MPDU (see Table 9-633 (A-MPDU contents in the S-MPDU context)),
64

27 35.14.6 Additional rules for group addressed frames in an EHT MU PPDU with RUs except
28 Beacon frame
29
30
An EHT AP may include group addressed frames except the Beacon frames in an EHT MU PPDU with RUs
31
subject to the rules defined in this subclause.
32
33
34 An EHT AP that includes a group addressed frame in an EHT MU PPDU with RUs shall ensure that the
35 frame is included in a broadcast RU in the EHT MU PPDU. The EHT AP shall additionally ensure that the
36
following conditions are satisfied for the broadcast RU:
37
38 — The RU allocation shall comply with the rules in 36.3.2 (Subcarrier and resource allocation)
39
— The <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple shall have a mandatory EHT-MCS and NSS = 1
40
41 — The broadcast RU shall be located within:
42
• The primary 20 MHz channel if the group addressed frame is a FILS Discovery or a Probe
43
Response frame, except when the primary 20 MHz channel does not coincide with a PSC and the
44
45 AP is a 6 GHz-only AP, in which case the broadcast RU may be in a PSC that is within the BSS
46 operating channel width (see 26.17.2.3 (Scanning in the 6 GHz band)). The broadcast RU size
47 shall not exceed 106 subcarriers if the MU PPDU has a bandwidth that is greater than 20 MHz.
48
• The primary 20 MHz channel if the group addressed frame is addressed to at least one associated
49
non-AP STA that has not declared to be in the awake state. The broadcast RU size shall not
50
51 exceed 106 subcarriers if the MU PPDU has a bandwidth that is greater than 20 MHz.
52 • A bandwidth that is indicated as supported in reception by one or more associated non-AP STAs,
53 if the group addressed frame is addressed only to those non-AP STAs and the STAs have
54
declared to be in the awake state. The broadcast RU size shall not exceed the minimum common
55
bandwidth that is supported in reception by all STAs in the EHT Capabilities element they trans-
56
57 mit or in the most recently sent OM Control, EHT OM Control or OMN frames.
58 • The SST subchannel if the group addressed frame is addressed to one or more EHT SST STAs,
59 the primary 20 MHz channel does not coincide with the subchannel assigned to the EHT SST
60
STAs and the frame is not addressed to any STAs other than the EHT SST STAs in that subchan-
61
nel (see 26.8.7.2 (SST operation)). The broadcast RU size shall not exceed 106 subcarriers if the
62
63 SST subchannel is 20 MHz.
64 — The TXVECTOR parameters listed below shall be set as follows:

17 35.15 EHT BSS operation
18
19
35.15.1 Basic EHT BSS operation
20
21
22 The basic EHT-MCS and NSS set is the set of <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuples that are supported by all EHT STAs
23 that are members of an EHT BSS. It is established by the STA that starts the EHT BSS, indicated by the
24
Basic EHT-MCS And NSS Set field of the EHT Operation parameter in the MLME-START.request
25
primitive. Other EHT STAs determine the basic EHT-MCS and NSS set from the Basic EHT-MCS And NSS
26
27 Set field of the EHT Operation element in the BSS Description derived through the scan mechanism (see
28 11.1.4.1 (General)).
29
30
An EHT STA shall not attempt to join (MLME-JOIN.request primitive) a BSS unless it supports (i.e., is able
31
to both transmit and receive using) all of the <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuples in the basic EHT-MCS and NSS set.
32
33
34 If an EHT STA supports transmitting or receiving a PPDU, where the PPDU bandwidth is less than
35 320 MHz, at an <EHT-MCS, NSS> tuple, where the EHT-MCS is equal to the HE-MCS and less than 12,
36
then it shall also support the corresponding transmitting or receiving <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple, respectively.
37
For a lower EHT-MCS, the EHT STA shall support an equal or greater NSS than a higher EHT-MCS.
38
39
40 If the peer AP is operating as an EMA AP, an EHT non-AP STA should follow the procedure described in
41 11.1.3.8.3 (Discovery of a nontransmitted BSSID profile) for efficient discovery during scanning and to
42
save power after association.
43
44
NOTE 1—A non-AP EHT STA is also a non-AP HE STA (see 4.3.16a (Extremely high throughput (EHT) STA)).
45
Support for multiple BSSID capability is mandatory for a non-AP HE STA (see 11.1.3.8.1 (General)) and therefore,
46
support for multiple BSSID capability is also mandatory for a non-AP EHT STA.
47
48
An EHT AP shall not assign an AID value of 2007 to any STA or non-AP MLD.
49
50
51 An EHT AP operating in 5 or 6 GHz band shall set the EHT Operation Information Present subfield in the
52 EHT Operation element to 1 to announce to EHT non-AP STAs an BSS operating channel width through the
53 EHT Operation Information field that is different from the BSS operating channel width(s) that it announces
54
to non-EHT non-AP STAs in the same Management frame if the BSS operating channel width announced to
55
EHT non-AP STAs includes at least one punctured 20 MHz subchannel and/or is 320 MHz. Otherwise, the
56
57 EHT AP shall set the EHT Operation Information Present subfield to 0.
58
59 If a BSS operating channel width is announced in the EHT Operation element, then the announced BSS
60
operating channel width is the EHT BSS operating channel width. If a BSS operating channel width is not
61
announced in the EHT Operation element,
62
63 — In 6 GHz band, the HE BSS operating channel width announced in the HE Operation element is the
64
EHT BSS operating channel width
65

1 — In 5 GHz band, the HE BSS operating channel width announced by the combination of the HT and

3
with VHT Operation Information field is the EHT BSS operating channel width
4

9
10
11 If a BSS operating channel width is announced in the EHT Operation element, then an EHT AP shall
12 announce the BSS operating channel width(s) to non-EHT non-AP STAs with the following restrictions:
13
— The announced BSS operating channel width(s) to non-EHT non-AP STAs are less than the BSS
14
15 operating channel width in the EHT Operation element and the corresponding BSS shall not operate
16 as an 80+80 MHz BSS.
17
— If the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element is present, the announced
18

19
20 primary channel without covering any punctured 20 MHz subchannel indicated in the Disabled

22 puncturing operation).
23
24
In the 2.4 GHz band, an EHT STA shall not transmit an EHT PPDU to a recipient EHT STA that carries a
25
26 frame that is not an EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame (see 35.7.3 (Rules for EHT sounding
27 protocol sequences)) and that exceeds the maximum MPDU length capability indicated in the EHT
28 Capabilities element or in Reconfiguration Multi-Link element with operation update type equal to 0 last
29
received from the recipient EHT STA.
30
31
32 In the 5 GHz band, an EHT STA shall not transmit an EHT PPDU to a recipient EHT STA that carries a
33 frame that is not an EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame (see 35.7.3 (Rules for EHT sounding
34 protocol sequences)) and that exceeds the maximum MPDU length capability indicated in the VHT
35
Capabilities element or in Reconfiguration Multi-Link element with operation update type equal to 0 last
36
received from the recipient STA.
37
38
39 In the 6 GHz band, an EHT STA shall not transmit an EHT PPDU to a recipient EHT STA that carries a
40 frame that is not an EHT Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame (see 35.7.3 (Rules for EHT sounding
41
protocol sequences)) and that exceeds the maximum MPDU length capability indicated in the HE 6 GHz
42
Band Capabilities element or in Reconfiguration Multi-Link element with operation update type equal to 0
43
44 last received from the recipient EHT STA.
45
46 In the 2.4 GHz band, an EHT STA shall not transmit an HE PPDU to a recipient EHT STA that carries a
47
frame that is not an HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame (see 26.7.3 (Rules for HE sounding protocol
48
sequences)) and that exceeds the maximum MPDU length capability indicated in the EHT Capabilities
49
50 element or in Reconfiguration Multi-Link element with operation update type equal to 0 last received from
51 the recipient EHT STA.
52
53
In the 2.4 GHz band, an EHT STA that sets the value of the Maximum MPDU Length subfield in the EHT
54
MAC Capabilities Information field of the EHT Capabilities element to indicate 3895 octets shall set the
55
56 maximum A-MSDU length in the HT Capabilities element to indicate 3839 octets. In the 2.4 GHz band, an
57 EHT STA that sets the maximum MPDU length in the EHT Capabilities element to indicate 7991 octets or
58 11 454 octets shall set the maximum A-MSDU length in the HT Capabilities element to indicate 7935 octets.
59
An EHT STA that sets the Maximum MPDU Length subfield in the Operation Parameter Info subfield of the
60
Reconfiguration Multi-Link element corresponding to a STA operating in 2.4 GHz band to indicate 3895
61

58 35.15.2 Preamble puncturing operation
59
60

61
transmitted Management frames. The AP shall set the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap Present subfield to 1 and
62
63 include the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element if the AP punctures any
64 subchannel for the BSS. Otherwise, the AP shall set the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap Present subfield to 0
65

## 第 644 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 and not include the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element. The puncturing
2 pattern indicated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield of the EHT Operation element shall be one of
3
the non-OFDMA puncturing patterns defined in Table 36-30 (Definition of the Punctured Channel
4
Information field in the U-SIG for an EHT MU PPDU using non-OFDMA transmissions) whose
5
6 corresponding PPDU bandwidth value in the Table is equal to the operating channel width of the BSS. The
7 AP may set each bit in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield to a value subject to the following
8 constraints:
9
— The resulting puncturing pattern is one of the puncturing patterns selected above.
10
11 — A bit in the bitmap that corresponds to a 20 MHz subchannel outside the BSS bandwidth shall be set
12
to 1.
13
14 — The bit in the bitmap that corresponds to the primary 20 MHz subchannel shall be set to 0.
15
16 In an EHT BSS set up by an EHT AP that has included the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT
17
Operation element, an EHT STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS of an
18
HE, EHT, or non-HT duplicate PPDU based on the value indicated in the most recently exchanged Disabled
19
20 Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element for that BSS. If a 20 MHz subchannel is
21 indicated as a punctured subchannel in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation
22 element, the corresponding bit in the TXVECTOR parameter INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS shall be set to
23
1 and the punctured 20 MHz subchannel shall not be used by any PPDU that is transmitted within the
24
operating channel of the EHT AP to a member of the EHT BSS.
25
26
27 An EHT SU transmission that contains an MPDU soliciting an immediate response shall not puncture
28 20 MHz subchannels that are not indicated to be punctured in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in
29
the EHT Operation element, unless the EHT SU transmission carries a triggering frame that solicits a TB
30
PPDU from a responding EHT STA.
31
32
33 An EHT MU PPDU that is not an EHT SU transmission and solicits an immediate response from a STA
34 without including a triggering frame shall not puncture 20 MHz subchannels that are not indicated to be
35
punctured in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element.
36
37
NOTE 1—For example, an EHT MU PPDU using DL OFDMA that sets the ACK policy to implicit BAR to one of the
38
users without including a triggering frame cannot puncture 20 MHz subchannels that are not indicated to be punctured in
39
the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element.
40
41
Otherwise, an EHT STA may puncture other subchannels in addition to those indicated in the Disabled
42
Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation element in an EHT MU PPDU or a non-HT duplicate
43
44 PPDU. If the EHT STA punctures other subchannels in an EHT MU PPDU or a non-HT duplicate PPDU in
45 addition to those indicated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield and solicits a response to the PPDU,
46 the EHT STA shall use a triggering frame to solicit the response in a TB PPDU and assign an RU or MRU
47
within the nonpunctured subchannel set to a responding EHT STA.
48
49
NOTE 2—No other subchannels can be punctured in addition to those indicated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap
50
subfield (if present) in the EHT Operation element in the following cases:
51
52
— A PPDU carrying an MU-RTS Trigger frame or the solicited CTS frame (see 35.2.2.1 (MU-RTS Trigger frame
53
transmission)).
54
55 — An EHT sounding NDP for non-TB sounding (see 35.7.2 (EHT sounding protocol)).
56 — A PPDU that carries a CF-End frame from a non-AP EHT STA, as it might be followed by another CF-End
57
frame after SIFS (see 10.23.2.10 (Truncation of TXOP)).
58
59
Regardless if the EHT AP has included the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the EHT Operation
60
61 element, an EHT STA may use EHT MU PPDU preamble puncturing modes as defined in 36.3.12.11 (EHT
62 preamble of preamble punctured EHT MU PPDU) or EHT TB PPDU in which not all the 20 MHz
63 subchannels are assigned.

1 35.15.3 Channel switching methods for an EHT BSS
2
3
An EHT STA follows the rules defined in 11.38.4 (Channel switching methods for a VHT BSS) and the
4
additional rules defined in this subclause.
5
6
7 If a Channel Switch Announcement frame or an Extended Channel Switch Announcement frame is used to
8 announce a switch to an EHT BSS operating channel width wider than 160 MHz or to an EHT BSS
9
operating channel width including at least one punctured 20 MHz subchannel, then the Bandwidth
10
Indication element shall be present in the same frame. If an EHT STA determines the EHT BSS operating
11
12 channel bandwidth based on the Bandwidth Indication element in the frame, then the STA shall ignore the
13 Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch element in the same frame for determining the EHT BSS operating
14 channel bandwidth. When the Bandwidth Indication element is present along with the Wide Bandwidth
15
Channel Switch element in the frame,
16
17 — the announced BSS bandwidth in the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch element is the maximum
18 bandwidth including the primary channel without covering any punctured 20 MHz subchannel
19 indicated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the Bandwidth Indication element as
20
defined in 35.15.2 (Preamble puncturing operation), and
21
22 — the announced BSS bandwidth in the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch element is less than the BSS
23 bandwidth in the Bandwidth Indication element and the corresponding BSS shall not operate as an
24
80+80 MHz BSS.
25
26
27 If a Channel Switch Announcement element or an Extended Channel Switch Announcement element is used
28 to announce a switch to an EHT BSS operating channel width wider than 160 MHz or to an EHT BSS
29 operating channel width including at least one punctured 20 MHz subchannel, then Bandwidth Indication
30
subelement in the Channel Switch Wrapper element shall be present in the same frame. If an EHT STA
31
determines the EHT BSS operating channel bandwidth based on the Bandwidth Indication subelement in the
32
33 frame, then the STA shall ignore the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch subelement in the same frame for
34 determining the EHT BSS operating channel bandwidth. When the Bandwidth Indication subelement is
35 present along with the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch subelement in the frame,
36
— the announced BSS bandwidth in the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch subelement is the maximum
37
38 bandwidth including the primary channel without covering any punctured 20 MHz subchannel
39 indicated in the Disabled Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the Bandwidth Indication subelement as
40 defined in 35.15.2 (Preamble puncturing operation), and
41
42 — the announced BSS bandwidth in the Wide Bandwidth Channel Switch subelement is less than the
43 BSS bandwidth in the Bandwidth Indication subelement and the corresponding BSS shall not operate
44 as an 80+80 MHz BSS.
45
46 NOTE—If the EHT AP after switching to the new channel has an EHT BSS operating channel width wider than
47 160 MHz or EHT BSS operating channel width including at least one punctured 20 MHz subchannel, its associated EHT
48 STAs that have performed channel switching or extended channel switching can also determine that information in the
49 EHT Operation element in the Beacon or Probe Response frames received from the EHT AP on the new channel.
50
51
52 35.16 EPCS priority access
53
54 35.16.1 General
55
56
EPCS priority access is a mechanism that provides prioritized access to the wireless medium for authorized
57
58 users to increase their probability of successful communication during periods of network congestion.
59
60 An EPCS AP MLD or an EPCS non-AP MLD is an MLD that has a value of true for
61
dot11EHTEPCSPriorityAccessActivated. A STA affiliated with an EPCS MLD shall set to 1 the EPCS
62
Priority Access Support subfield of the EHT Capabilities element that it transmits. A STA affiliated with an
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 645
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 646 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 MLD that is not an EPCS AP MLD or an EPCS non-AP MLD shall set to 0 the EPCS Priority Access
2 Support subfield of the EHT Capabilities element that it transmits.
3
4
During the (re)association process, the AP MLD obtains information required to verify the authority of the
5
6 non-AP MLD to use EPCS priority access. An AP MLD that has dot11SSPNInterfaceActivated equal to true
7 may use the interworking procedures described in 11.22.5 (Interworking procedures: interactions with
8 SSPN) to retrieve permission for a non-AP MLD to use the EPCS priority access from an EPCS service
9
provider via the SSPN interface during association by the non-AP MLD. To support this exchange, an EPCS
10
non-AP MLD shall provide the home realm information of the EPCS provider and necessary authentication
11
12 parameters as described in 11.22.5 (Interworking procedures: interactions with SSPN). While other methods
13 of obtaining this authorization information are possible, they are outside the scope of this standard.
14
15
An AP MLD that successfully obtains permission for a non-AP MLD to use EPCS priority access shall
16
update the dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized for the non-AP MLD in the dot11InterworkingEntry. The
17
18 authorization information included in the dot11InterworkingEntry is passed from the prior AP MLD to the
19 new AP MLD in the same ESS during reassociation as described in 11.22.5.3 (Reporting and session control
20 with SSPN).
21
22
35.16.2 EPCS priority access operation
23
24
25 35.16.2.1 General
26
27
EPCS priority access is established at the MAC by the initiation of the SME. The EPCS priority access
28
between an AP MLD and its associated non-AP MLD can be in one of the following two states: enabled
29
30 state or torn down state. The protocols to enable and tear down EPCS priority access are described in this
31 subclause.
32
33
A non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD shall not send an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame
34
to an AP affiliated with the associated AP MLD unless RSNA with management frame protection (see
35
36 12.2.7 (Requirements for management frame protection) and 12.6 (RSNA security association
37 management)) has been successfully negotiated and are capable of invoking EPCS priority access.
38
39
An AP affiliated with an AP MLD shall not send an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame to a non-
40
AP STA affiliated with the associated non-AP MLD unless RSNA with management frame protection (see
41
42 12.2.7 (Requirements for management frame protection) and 12.6 (RSNA security association
43 management)) has been successfully negotiated and are capable of invoking EPCS priority access.
44
45
35.16.2.2 Setup procedures for EPCS priority access
46
47
48 35.16.2.2.1 General
49
50 EPCS priority access shall be in a torn down state upon the completion of successful multi-link setup
51
procedure (i.e., when non-AP MLD associates with an AP MLD).
52

32 As illustrated in Figure 35-48 (Enabling EPCS priority access), an MLD supporting EPCS priority access
33

34
After successful invocation of EPCS priority access, both the originator and the responder shall transition
35
36 EPCS priority access to the enabled state and apply the priority access treatment to EPCS traffic. The AP
37 MLD or non-AP MLD may send an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame through an affiliated AP or
38 affiliated non-AP STA, respectively, on any enabled link between them and, if authorized, EPCS priority
39
access treatment will be applied on all enabled links between the MLDs.
40
41 NOTE—When a non-AP STA, which is affiliated with a non-AP MLD, is associated with an AP affiliated with an AP
42 MLD with which the non-AP MLD has performed multi-link setup and the AP belongs to a multiple BSSID set, the
43 EPCS frame exchanges are performed between the intended AP (that can correspond to a transmitted BSSID or a
44 nontransmitted BSSID in the set) and the non-AP STA.

35.16.2.2.2 Procedures at the initiating EPCS non-AP MLD
47
48
49 When instructed to do so by a higher layer function and upon receipt of an
50 MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.request primitive, an EPCS non-AP MLD with EPCS priority access
51 in the torn down state shall follow the procedure below to request a change for the EPCS priority access state
52
to enabled.
53
54 a) The initiating EPCS non-AP MLD shall transmit an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame
55 (9.6.35.5 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame format)) via an affiliated STA to the
56 corresponding AP affiliated with the associated EPCS AP MLD.
57
58 b) If the initiating EPCS non-AP MLD receives an EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame
59 (9.6.35.6 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame format)) via an affiliated STA with a
60 matching dialog token and a value of SUCCESS in the Status Code field, then the initiating EPCS
61
non-AP MLD shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.confirm primitive with a value of
62
SUCCESS in the Status Code field indicating that EPCS priority access is in an enabled state. The
63
64 initiating EPCS non-AP MLD shall enable EPCS priority access so that subsequently transmitted
65

1 traffic receives EPCS priority access treatment using the procedure defined in 35.16.3 (EPCS
2 priority access procedure).
3
c) If the initiating EPCS non-AP MLD receives an EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame
4
5 (9.6.35.6 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame format)) via an affiliated STA with a
6 matching dialog token and a value not equal to SUCCESS in the Status Code field, then the
7 initiating EPCS non-AP MLD shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.confirm
8

9

10
11 EPCS priority access procedure. The higher layer function that triggers the EPCS priority access is
12 responsible for managing reattempts after receiving responses with a value other than SUCCESS.

14
When instructed to do so by a higher layer function and upon receipt of an
15

16

18 down state.
19
20 NOTE—An EPCS non-AP MLD can initiate the teardown procedure regardless of whether the EPCS AP MLD or the
21 EPCS non-AP MLD initiated the process to enable EPCS priority access.
22
23 a) The tearing down EPCS non-AP MLD shall transmit an EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame
24 (9.6.35.7 (EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame details)) via an affiliated STA to an AP affiliated
25
with the associated EPCS AP MLD. The tearing down EPCS non-AP MLD shall change the EPCS
26
priority access to the torn down state for all setup links so that subsequently transmitted traffic does
27
28 not receive EPCS priority access treatment.
29
b) During the process of disassociating an EPCS non-AP MLD, the EPCS AP MLD shall transition
30
EPCS priority access to the torn down state for that EPCS non-AP MLD.
31

33 35.16.2.2.3 Procedures at the initiating EPCS AP MLD
34
35
When instructed to do so by a higher layer function triggered via an external interface, and upon receipt of
36
an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.request primitive, an EPCS AP MLD shall follow the procedure
37
38 below to request the change of the EPCS priority access for an associated EPCS non-AP MLD to the
39 enabled state.
40
41 NOTE 1—The definition of the external interface is out of the scope of this standard.
42
43 a) An EPCS AP MLD with dot11SSPNInterfaceActivated equal to true shall verify if the
44 dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized for the EPCS non-AP MLD in the dot11InterworkingEntry is
45
set to true.
46
47 NOTE 2—Successful verification is defined when the dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized for the EPCS non-
48 AP MLD in the dot11InterworkingEntry is set to true. The verification of EPCS priority access authorization by
49 an EPCS AP MLD with dot11SSPNInterfaceActivated equal to false is out of scope of this standard.
50
51
b) If the verification is successful (see NOTE 2 above), the initiating EPCS AP MLD shall transmit an
52
EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame (9.6.35.5 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame
53
54 format)) via an affiliated STA to the corresponding non-AP STA affiliated with an associated EPCS
55 non-AP MLD, with EPCS priority access in the torn down state for that non-AP MLD.
56
i) The initiating EPCS AP MLD may include the Priority Access Multi-Link element in the
57
EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame to provide EDCA parameter set(s) and/or
58
59 MU EDCA parameter set(s) that the destination EPCS non-AP MLD will employ on the
60 corresponding setup links.
61
c) If the initiating EPCS AP MLD receives an EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame (9.6.35.6
62
63 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame format)) via an affiliated STA with a matching
64 dialog token and a value of SUCCESS in the Status Code field, then the initiating EPCS AP MLD
65

1 shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.confirm primitive with a value of SUCCESS in

3

21
22
23 NOTE 3—An EPCS AP MLD can initiate the teardown procedure regardless of whether the EPCS AP MLD or the
24 EPCS non-AP MLD initiated the process to enable EPCS priority access.
25
26 NOTE 4—The definition of the external interface is out of scope of this standard.
27
28
The tearing down EPCS AP MLD shall transmit an EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame (9.6.35.7 (EPCS
29
Priority Access Teardown frame details)) via an affiliated STA to a non-AP STA affiliated with an associated
30
31 EPCS non-AP MLD. The tearing down EPCS AP MLD shall change the EPCS priority access state to torn
32 down for all setup links. During the process of disassociating an EPCS non-AP MLD, the EPCS AP MLD
33 shall transition EPCS priority access to the torn down state for that EPCS non-AP MLD.
34
35
35.16.2.2.4 Procedure at the receiving AP MLD
36
37
38 Upon receipt of an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame (9.6.35.5 (EPCS Priority Access Enable
39 Request frame format)), an EPCS AP MLD shall use the following procedure to enable EPCS priority
40
access for the requesting non-AP MLD.
41
42 a) The receiving AP MLD shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.indication primitive.
43
b) Upon receipt of the MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.response primitive, the receiving AP
44
MLD shall reply to the initiating non-AP MLD with an EPCS Priority Access Enable Response
45
46 frame (9.6.35.6 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame format)) using the following
47 procedure:
48
i) For an AP MLD with dot11SSPNInterfaceActivated equal to true, if the
49
dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized for the requesting non-AP MLD in the
50
51 dot11InterworkingEntry is set to true indicating the requesting non-AP MLD is verified
52 for EPCS priority access, the AP MLD shall set the Status Code field to a value of
53 SUCCESS.
54
55 ii) For an AP MLD with dot11SSPNInterfaceActivated equal to true, if the
56 dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized for the requesting non-AP MLD in the
57 dot11InterworkingEntry is set to false, the AP MLD shall set the Status Code field to a
58
value of EPCS_DENIED_UNAUTHORIZED.
59
60 iii) If the receiving AP MLD cannot support EPCS priority access for the initiating non-AP
61 MLD for any other reason, the receiving AP MLD shall set the Status Code field with a
62 value of EPCS_DENIED_OTHER_REASON as defined in 9.4.1.9 (Status Code field).
63
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 649
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 650 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 NOTE 1—The verification for AP MLD with dot11SSPNInterfaceActivated equal to false is out of
2 scope of this standard.
3
4 iv) If the receiving AP MLD is unable to verify that the non-AP MLD is authorized for any
5
reason, such as a communication failure or overload condition, the receiving AP MLD
6
shall set the Status Code field with a value of
7
8 EPCS_DENIED_VERIFICATION_FAILURE as defined in 9.4.1.9 (Status Code field).
9
NOTE 2—Given temporary nature of this condition, higher layer function might attempt to invoke
10
the enable operation again after a suitable delay.
11
12
13 c) If the Status Code in the MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.response primitive is equal to
14 SUCCESS, the receiving AP MLD STA shall set the state of the EPCS priority access to enabled for
15 the requesting non-AP MLD.
16
i) The receiving AP MLD may include the Priority Access Multi-Link element in the EPCS
17
18 Priority Access Enable Response frame to provide the EDCA parameter set(s) and/or the
19 MU EDCA parameter set(s) that the initiating EPCS non-AP MLD will employ on the
20 corresponding links.
21
22 d) If the Status Code in the MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.response primitive is equal to a value
23 other than SUCCESS, the receiving AP MLD shall keep EPCS priority access in the torn down state
24 for the requesting non-AP MLD.
25
26
Upon receipt of an EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame (9.6.35.7 (EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame
27
28 details)), an EPCS AP MLD with EPCS priority access enabled state shall use the following procedure to
29 tear down EPCS priority access.
30
a) The receiving AP MLD shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSTEARDOWN.indication
31
primitive.
32
33 b) The receiving AP MLD shall change the EPCS priority access state to torn down for the requesting
34
non-AP MLD.
35
36
37 35.16.2.2.5 Procedures at the receiving non-AP MLD
38
39 Upon receipt of an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request frame (9.6.35.5 (EPCS Priority Access Enable
40
Request frame format)), a EPCS non-AP MLD with EPCS priority access in the torn down state shall use the
41
following procedure to enable EPCS priority access.
42
43 a) The receiving non-AP MLD shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.indication
44 primitive.
45
46 b) Upon receipt of the MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.response primitive, a non-AP STA
47 affiliated with the receiving non-AP MLD shall reply to the initiating AP MLD with an EPCS
48 Priority Access Enable Response frame (9.6.35.6 (EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame
49
format)). The receiving non-AP MLD should set the Status Code field to a value of SUCCESS
50
unless it is unable to support EPCS priority access for any reason. In that case, the non-AP MLD
51
52 shall set the Status Code field to a value of EPCS_DENIED_OTHER_REASON as defined in
53 9.4.1.9 (Status Code field).
54
c) If the Status Code in the MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.response primitive is equal to
55
SUCCESS, the receiving non-AP MLD shall change the state of the EPCS priority access to enabled
56
57 so that subsequently transmitted traffic receives EPCS priority access treatment using the procedure
58 defined in 35.16.3 (EPCS priority access procedure).
59
d) If the Status Code in the MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSENABLE.response primitive is equal to a value
60
61 other than SUCCESS, the receiving non-AP MLD shall keep the torn down state of the EPCS
62 priority access so it does not only apply to subsequently transmitted traffic.
63
64

1 Upon receipt of an EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame (9.6.35.7 (EPCS Priority Access Teardown frame
2 details)), a EPCS non-AP MLD with EPCS priority access enabled shall use the following procedure to tear
3
down EPCS priority access.
4
5 a) The receiving non-AP MLD shall issue an MLME-EPCSPRIACCESSTEARDOWN.indication
6 primitive.
7
b) The receiving non-AP MLD shall change the EPCS priority access state to torn down for all links so
8
9 that subsequently transmitted traffic does not receive EPCS priority access treatment.
10
11 35.16.3 EPCS priority access procedure
12
13
35.16.3.1 General
14
15
16 An EPCS AP MLD is an AP MLD with dot11EHTEPCSPriorityAccessActivated set to true.
17
18
An EPCS non-AP MLD is a non-AP MLD with dot11EHTEPCSPriorityAccessActivated set to true.
19
20
21 EPCS priority access procedure allows EPCS non-AP MLDs with priority access in the enabled state to gain
22 priority access to medium. If the negotiation to enable EPCS priority access between an EPCS AP MLD and
23 an EPCS non-AP MLD is successful, then the non-AP STA affiliated with the non-AP MLD applies EPCS
24
priority access to its EPCS traffic on all enabled links using the procedure described below.
25
26
27 An EPCS non-AP MLD shall apply EPCS priority access procedures only when its EPCS priority access
28 state is set to enabled. An EPCS AP MLD may apply EPCS priority access to EPCS traffic using the
29 procedure described below prior to completion of the negotiation to enable EPCS priority access.
30
31
35.16.3.2 EDCA operation using EPCS EDCA parameters
32
33
34 As part of the EPCS priority access procedure, a STA affiliated with an EPCS non-AP MLD shall manage its
35 EDCA parameter sets as follows:
36
— During the process of enabling EPCS priority access, the STA affiliated with the EPCS non-AP
37
38 MLD shall
39
• update its CWmin[AC], CWmax[AC], AIFSN[AC], and TXOP Limit [AC] state variables of
40
each access category to
41
42 • the values carried in the EDCA Parameters Set element included in the per-STA profile,
43 with the Link ID corresponding to the AP with which the STA is associated, carried in
44 the Priority Access Multi-Link element contained in an EPCS Priority Access Enable
45
Request or an EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame sent by an AP affiliated
46
with the EPCS AP MLD, if the corresponding per-STA profile is present and contains an
47
48 EDCA Parameters Set element or,
49 • the default EDCA parameter values found in Table 9-155 (Default EDCA Parameter Set
50 element parameter values if dot11OCBActivated is false or the STA is a non-sensor
51
STA) otherwise.
52
• update the dot11MUEDCATable to respective values that correspond to fields in the MU EDCA
53
54 Parameter Set element included in the per-STA profile, with the Link ID corresponding to the AP
55 with which the STA is associated, carried in the Priority Access Multi-Link element contained in
56 an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request or an EPCS Priority Access Enable Response frame
57
sent by an AP affiliated with the EPCS AP MLD, if the corresponding Per-STA Profile is present
58
and contains an MU EDCA Parameter Set element.
59
60 — While EPCS priority access is enabled, the STA affiliated with an EPCS non-AP MLD shall
61
• use the latest EDCA parameter set, included in the per-STA profile, with the Link ID correspond-
62
63 ing to the AP which the STA is associated with, carried in the Priority Access Multi-Link ele-
64 ment contained in an EPCS Priority Access Enable Request or an EPCS Priority Access Enable
65

38
39 MU EDCA parameters).

41 An AP affiliated with an EPCS AP MLD manages the EDCA parameter set and the MU EDCA parameter
42

43
as follows:

52

56 ried in a Beacon or Probe Response frame that lowers the priority for those STAs.

58
59 • an AP affiliated with an EPCS AP MLD with its EPCS priority access state set to the torn down
60
state for all its associated STAs announces the EDCA parameter set corresponding to the link in
61
Management frames (e.g., Beacon or Probe Response) that it transmits following the procedure
62
63 in 10.2.3.2 (HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)).

## 第 653 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 35.17 EHT SCS procedure
2
3
The SCS procedure is used by a non-AP MLD to request an AP MLD to classify incoming individually
4
addressed MSDUs based on parameters provided by the non-AP MLD and/or describe its traffic
5
6 characteristics to an AP MLD.
7
8 An EHT STA establishes SCS stream with an EHT AP, as defined in 11.25.2 (SCS procedures), subject to
9
the additional rules and restrictions defined in this subclause.
10
11
12 A non-AP EHT STA with dot11SCSActivated equal to true that supports transmission of SCS Request
13 frames containing SCS Descriptor element with a QoS Characteristics element shall set the SCS Traffic
14 Descriptor Support subfield value in the EHT Capabilities element that it transmits to 1. An EHT AP with
15
dot11SCSActivated equal to true that supports transmission of SCS Response frames containing SCS
16
Descriptor element with a QoS Characteristics element shall set the SCS Traffic Descriptor Support subfield
17
18 value in the EHT Capabilities element that it transmits to 1. All STAs affiliated with an MLD shall set the
19 SCS Traffic Description Support subfield of the EHT Capabilities element that they transmit to the same
20 value.
21
22
A non-AP EHT STA may transmit an SCS Request frame with SCS Descriptor element(s) containing a QoS
23
24 Characteristics element if the Request Type field in the frame is set to “Add” or “Change”. The QoS
25 Characteristics element describes the traffic characteristics of the requested SCS stream. A non-AP EHT
26 STA shall not transmit an SCS Request frame with SCS Descriptor element(s) containing a QoS
27
Characteristics element to an AP from which it has not received an EHT Capabilities element with the SCS
28
Traffic Description Support field equal to 1.
29
30
31 The MLDs maintain SCSIDs at MLD level, i.e., the SCSID used by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP
32 MLD in an SCS Request frame transmitted to an AP affiliated with an AP MLD is unique across all STAs
33
affiliated with the non-AP MLD.
34
35
36 All STAs affiliated with an MLD shall set the SCS field of the Extended Capabilities element that they
37 transmit to the same value. The SCSID is used by a non-AP MLD to request creation, modification, or
38 deletion of an SCS stream. The SCSID is used by an AP MLD to identify an SCS stream in SCS responses.
39
40
An SCS Request frame sent by a non-AP STA affiliated with a non-AP MLD to the AP of an AP MLD that
41
42 contains a QoS Characteristics element in which the Direction subfield is set to uplink or downlink or one
43 that does not contain a QoS Characteristics element is interpreted as a request for creation of an SCS stream
44 that applies at the MLD level.
45
46
If the SCS Descriptor element contains a QoS Characteristics element in which the Direction subfield is
47
48 equal to downlink, then the TCLAS Elements field shall be included in the SCS Descriptor element and the
49 TCLAS Processing Element field may be included in the SCS Descriptor element. The TCLAS Elements
50 and the TCLAS Processing Element fields, if present, describe the traffic classification the non-AP STA
51
requests the AP to apply to the corresponding stream.
52
53
54 An SCS Descriptor element contained in an SCS Request frame in which the QoS Characteristics
55 subelement is present and the Direction subfield in the QoS Characteristics element is equal to direct link or
56 uplink shall not contain the Intra-Access Category Priority Element, TCLAS Element, and TCLAS
57
Processing Element fields.
58
59
60 A value of REQUEST_DECLINED, REQUEST_TCLAS_NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_AP,
61 REJECTED_WITH_SUGGESTED_CHANGES, or
62 INSUFFICIENT_TCLAS_PROCESSING_RESOURCES shall be set in the corresponding SCS Status field
63
of the SCS status duple in the SCS Response frame when an EHT AP denies the SCS request for the
64
requested SCSID.
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 653
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 654 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 If the SCS Request frame with an SCS Description element containing a QoS Characteristics element is
2 rejected by an EHT AP by setting the Status field value to REJECTED_WITH_SUGGESTED_CHANGES,
3
the AP shall include an SCS Descriptor element containing a QoS Characteristics element in the SCS
4
Response frame signaling the suggested QoS characteristics parameters for this SCS stream. An AP shall
5
6 include an SCS Descriptor element containing a QoS Characteristics element in an SCS Response frame
7 with the Status field value set to SUCCESS or REJECTED_WITH_SUGGESTED_CHANGES only if the
8 SCS Descriptor element in the corresponding SCS Request frame contained a QoS Characteristics element.
9
10
The SCS Descriptor element that is included in an SCS Response frame shall not contain any Intra-Access
11
12 Category Priority element, TCLAS Elements field or TCLAS Processing Element field. The Request Type
13 field value in the corresponding SCS Descriptor element is reserved. The following fields in the QoS
14 Characteristics element included in the corresponding SCS Descriptor element in the SCS Response frame
15
may differ from the corresponding values in the requested SCS stream: Minimum Service Interval,
16
Maximum Service Interval, Service Start Time, and Medium Time.
17
18
19 A non-AP EHT STA with dot11EHTTXOPSharingTFOptionImplemented equal to true may send an SCS
20 request that contains a QoS Characteristics element whose Direction field is set to 2 (Direct Link) only if the
21
EHT AP sets the Triggered TXOP Sharing Mode 2 Support subfield in the EHT Capabilities element it
22
transmits to 1.
23
24
25 The QoS Characteristics element is a reference for the EHT AP’s scheduling. An EHT AP should schedule
26 transmission of downlink frames such that the delay bound and minimum data rate requested are met for the
27
downlink Data frames if the Direction subfield of the QoS Characteristics element indicates downlink. An
28
EHT AP should enable the transmission of uplink frames from the EHT STA with an interval that falls
29
30 between the requested minimum and maximum service intervals and the AP should meet the minimum data
31 rate requested if the Direction subfield of the QoS Characteristics element indicates uplink. An EHT AP
32 should enable the transmission of direct link frames from the EHT STA to another STA on the link specified
33
in the LinkID subfield of the Control Info field with an interval that falls between the requested minimum
34
and maximum service intervals.
35
36
37 The transmission of uplink Data frames should be enabled by using Basic Trigger frames or alternatively by
38 using MU-RTS TXS Trigger frames if both EHT STAs have
39
dot11EHTTXOPSharingTFOptionImplemented equal to true. The transmission of direct link frames should
40
be enabled by using MU-RTS TXS Trigger frames if both EHT STAs have set the Triggered TXOP Sharing
41
42 Mode 2 Support field in their transmitted EHT Capabilities elements to 1.
43
44 If the EHT STA is a TWT scheduled STA or TWT requesting STA (see 26.8 (TWT operation)) and there are
45
negotiated TWT SPs for the TID specified in the QoS Characteristics element with the EHT AP, the EHT
46
AP should ensure that the service interval aligns with negotiated TWT wake intervals.
47
48
49 If the EHT STA is an R-TWT scheduled STA (see 35.8 (Restricted TWT (R-TWT))) and there are
50 negotiated R-TWT SPs for the TID specified in the QoS Characteristics element then the EHT AP should
51
use these R-TWT SPs to serve traffic corresponding to the TID and specified direction in the QoS
52
Characteristics element. If negotiated R-TWT SPs for the TID specified in the QoS Characteristics element
53
54 are trigger-enabled R-TWT, then the EHT AP should ensure that the trigger frames are scheduled at the start
55 of the R-TWT SPs.
56
57
The EHT AP may discard a downlink data frame if the lifetime of the frame has exceeded the value
58
specified by the MSDU Lifetime field.
59
60
NOTE—A QoS Characteristics element provided by a non-AP EHT STA is used by a receiving EHT AP to facilitate the
61
creation of a schedule for contention based channel access (EDCA) or MU operation. How the AP uses the information
62
provided by the non-AP STA QoS Characteristics element that do not have corresponding normative requirements is
63
beyond the scope of the standard.
64
