30
31
36.3.20.4.4 Transmitter modulation accuracy (EVM) test
32
33
34 The transmit modulation accuracy test shall be performed by instrumentation capable of converting the
35 transmitted signals into a stream of complex samples at sampling rate greater than or equal to the bandwidth
36
of the signal being transmitted.
37
38
39 In this case, transmit modulation accuracy shall meet the required value in Table 36-65 (Allowed relative
40 constellation error versus constellation size and coding rate) using only the occupied data subcarriers. For
41 EHT TB PPDU transmission, two sets of EVM requirements are defined in Table 36-65 (Allowed relative
42
constellation error versus constellation size and coding rate) for different transmission power levels to assist
43
AP in better managing the interference among multiple STAs responding to a Trigger frame.
44
45
46 Local oscillator leakage that can potentially show up at the center frequency of the EHT PPDU tone plan and
47 within ±3 neighboring subcarriers shall be excluded from the computation of the transmitter modulation
48
accuracy test. The potential local oscillator leakage subcarriers for 20 MHz operating devices are the center
49
of primary 20 MHz of the EHT PPDU tone plan and ±3 subcarriers of it. The potential local oscillator
50
51 leakage subcarriers for 40 MHz operating devices are the center of the primary 40 MHz of the PPDU tone
52 plan and ±3 subcarriers. The potential local oscillator leakage subcarriers for 80 MHz operating devices are
53 the center of the primary 80 MHz of the PPDU tone plan and ±3 subcarriers of it. The potential local
54
oscillator leakage tones for 160 MHz operating devices are the center of the primary 160 MHz of the PPDU
55
tone plan and ±3 subcarriers of it. The potential local oscillator leakage tones for 320 MHz operating devices
56
57 are the center of the 320 MHz of the PPDU tone plan and ±3 subcarriers of it. For 40 MHz operating devices
58 that transmits 20 MHz, the potential local oscillator leakage subcarriers exist outside the PPDU bandwidth
59 and should not affect the transmitter modulation accuracy test. For 80 MHz operating devices that transmits
60
20 MHz or 40 MHz PPDU, the potential local oscillator leakage subcarriers exist outside the PPDU
61
bandwidth and should not affect the transmitter modulation accuracy test. For 160 MHz operating devices
62
63 that transmits 20 MHz or 40 MHz PPDU or 80 MHz PPDU, the potential local oscillator leakage subcarriers
64 exist outside the PPDU bandwidth and should not affect the transmitter modulation accuracy test. For
65
882 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 883 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 320 MHz operating devices that transmits 20 MHz or 40 MHz PPDU or 80 MHz PPDU or 160 MHz PPDU,
2 the potential local oscillator leakage subcarriers exist outside the PPDU bandwidth and should not affect the
3
transmitter modulation accuracy test.
4
5
6 The transmitter modulation accuracy test procedure for the occupied subcarriers of the PPDU is similar as in
7 steps of the transmit modulation accuracy test procedure defined in 27.3.19.4.4 (Transmitter modulation
8 accuracy (EVM) test) as follows.
9
a) Start of PPDU shall be detected.
10
11 b) Transition from L-STF to L-LTF shall be detected and fine timing shall be established.
12
c) Coarse and fine frequency offsets shall be estimated.
13
14 d) Symbols in a PPDU shall be derotated according to a single estimated frequency offset. Sampling
15
offset drift shall be also compensated.
16
17 e) For each EHT-LTF symbol, transform the symbol into subcarrier received values, estimate a single
18 phase from the pilot subcarriers, and derotate the subcarrier values according to the estimated phase.
19
f) Estimate the complex channel response coefficient for each of the subcarriers and each of the
20
21 transmit streams.
22 g) For each of the data OFDM symbols, transform the symbol into subcarrier received values, estimate
23
a single phase from the pilot subcarriers, and compensate the subcarrier values according to the
24
estimated phase, group the results from all of the receiver chains in each subcarrier to a vector, and
25
26 multiply the vector by a zero-forcing equalization matrix generated from the estimated channel.
27
h) For each data-carrying subcarrier in each spatial stream of RU under test, find the closest
28
constellation point and compute the Euclidean distance from it.
29
30 i) Compute the average across PPDUs of the RMS of all errors per PPDU as given by Equation (36-
31 102).

53 In case of a noncontiguous MRU, the transmit modulation accuracy test for the unoccupied subcarriers of

## 第 889 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 36.3.21 Receiver specification
2
3
36.3.21.1 General
4
5
6 For receiver minimum input sensitivity, adjacent channel rejection, nonadjacent channel rejection, receiver
7 maximum input level, and CCA sensitivity requirements described in this subclause, the input levels are
8 measured at the antenna connector and are referenced as the average power per receive antenna. The number
9
of spatial streams under test shall be equal to the number of utilized transmitting STA physical antenna
10
(output) ports and also equal to the number of utilized Device Under Test input ports. Each output port of the
11
12 transmitting STA shall be connected through a cable to one input port of the Device Under Test.
13
NOTE—Additional test requirements and/or test methods may be needed to meet regulatory requirements.
14
15
16 The requirements on receiver minimum input sensitivity in 36.3.21.2 (Receiver minimum input sensitivity),
17 adjacent channel rejection in 36.3.21.3 (Adjacent channel rejection) and nonadjacent channel rejection in
18 36.3.21.4 (Nonadjacent channel rejection) apply to PPDUs that meet all the following conditions:
19
— 0.8 μs GI is used.
20
21 — If the PPDU bandwidth is 20 MHz and the EHT-MCS is less than 10 or equal to 15, then BCC is
22
used. Otherwise, LDPC is used.
23
24 — The PPDU is an EHT MU PPDU without puncturing and a PPDU Type And Compression Mode
25 field in U-SIG field is equal to 1.
26
27
36.3.21.2 Receiver minimum input sensitivity
28
29
30 The PER shall be less than 10% for a PSDU with the rate-dependent input levels listed in Table 36-67
31 (Receiver minimum input level sensitivity). The PSDU length shall be 2048 octets for BPSK modulation
32 with DCM or 4096 octets for all other modulations.

36.3.21.3 Adjacent channel rejection
17
18
19 Adjacent channel rejection for W MHz (where W is 20, 40, 80, 160, or 320) shall be measured by setting the
20 desired signal’s strength 3 dB above the rate-dependent sensitivity specified in Table 36-67 (Receiver
21
minimum input level sensitivity) and raising the power of the interfering signal of W MHz bandwidth until
22
10% PER is caused for a PSDU length of 2048 octets for BPSK modulation with DCM or 4096 octets for all
23
24 other modulations. The difference in power between the signals in the interfering channel and the desired
25 channel is the corresponding adjacent channel rejection. The center frequency of the adjacent channel shall
26 be placed W MHz away from the center frequency of the desired signal.
27
28
The interfering signal in the adjacent channel shall be a signal compliant with the EHT PHY,
29
30 unsynchronized with the signal in the channel under test, and shall have a minimum duty cycle of 50%. The
31 corresponding rejection shall be no less than specified in Table 36-68 (Minimum required adjacent and
32 nonadjacent channel rejection levels).
33
34
35

15
16
36.3.21.4 Nonadjacent channel rejection
17
18
19 Nonadjacent channel rejection for W MHz channels (where W is 20, 40, 80, 160, or 320) shall be measured
20 by setting the desired signal’s strength 3 dB above the rate-dependent sensitivity specified in Table 36-67
21
(Receiver minimum input level sensitivity), and raising the power of the interfering signal of W MHz
22
bandwidth until a 10% PER occurs for a PSDU length of 2048 octets for BPSK modulation with DCM or
23
24 4096 octets for all other modulations. The difference in power between the signals in the interfering channel
25 and the desired channel is the corresponding nonadjacent channel rejection. The nonadjacent channel
26 rejection shall be met with any nonadjacent channels located at least 2(cid:117)W MHz away from the center
27
frequency of the desired signal.
28
29
30 The interfering signal in the nonadjacent channel shall be a signal compliant with the EHT PHY,
31 unsynchronized with the signal in the channel under test, and shall have a minimum duty cycle of 50%. The
32 corresponding rejection shall be no less than specified in Table 36-68 (Minimum required adjacent and
33
nonadjacent channel rejection levels).
34
35
36 The measurement of nonadjacent channel rejection for 160 MHz and 320 MHz operation in regulatory
37 domain is required only if such a frequency band plan is permitted in the regulatory domain.
38
39
36.3.21.5 Receiver maximum input level
40
41
42 The receiver shall provide a maximum PER of 10% at a PSDU length of 2048 octets for BPSK modulation
43 with DCM or 4096 octets for all other modulations, for a maximum input level of –30 dBm in the 5 GHz
44 and 6 GHz bands and –20 dBm in the 2.4 GHz band, measured at each physical antenna port for any
45
baseband EHT modulation.
46
47
48 36.3.21.6 CCA sensitivity
49
50 36.3.21.6.1 General
51
52
The thresholds in this subclause are compared with the signal level at each receiving antenna.
53
54
55 36.3.21.6.2 CCA sensitivity for operating classes requiring CCA-ED
56
57
For the operating classes requiring CCA-Energy Detect (CCA-ED), the PHY shall indicate a medium busy
58
condition if CCA-ED detects a channel busy condition. For improved spectrum sharing, CCA-ED is
59
60 required in some bands. The behavior class indicating CCA-ED is given in Table D-2 (Behavior limits). The
61 operating classes requiring the corresponding CCA-ED behavior class are given in E.1 (Country information
62 and operating classes). The PHY of a STA that is operating within an operating class that requires CCA-ED
63
shall operate with CCA-ED.
64
65
Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved. 891
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.
Authorized licensed use limited to: Georgia Institute of Technology. Downloaded on July 28,2023 at 09:06:59 UTC from IEEE Xplore. Restrictions apply.

## 第 892 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 CCA-ED shall detect a channel busy condition if the received signal strength exceeds the CCA-ED
2 threshold as given by dot11OFDMEDThreshold for the primary 20 MHz channel and each nonprimary
3
20 MHz subchannel (if present). The CCA-ED thresholds for the operating classes requiring CCA-ED are
4
subject to the criteria in D.2.5 (CCA-ED threshold).
5
6
7 For the EHT TB PPDU transmission, for each of 20 MHz subchannels that require CCA, CCA-ED shall
8 detect a channel busy condition if the received signal strength exceeds the CCA-ED threshold as given by
9
dot11OFDMEDThreshold. The CCA-ED thresholds for the operating classes requiring CCA-ED are subject
10
to the criteria in D.2.5 (CCA-ED threshold).
11
12
13 For transmissions that carry a frame that includes a BQR Control subfield (see 9.2.4.6a (Control subfield
14 variants of an A-Control subfield)), CCA-ED shall detect a channel busy condition if the received signal
15
strength exceeds the CCA-ED threshold as given by dot11OFDMEDThreshold for primary 20 MHz channel
16
and for each nonprimary 20 MHz channel (if present). The CCA-ED thresholds for the operating classes
17
18 requiring CCA-ED are subject to the criteria in D.2.5 (CCA-ED threshold).
19
NOTE—The requirement to detect a channel busy condition as stated in 36.3.21.6.3 (CCA sensitivity for the primary 20
20
MHz channel) and 36.3.21.6.4 (Per 20 MHz CCA sensitivity) is a mandatory energy detect requirement on all Clause 36
21
(Extremely high throughput (EHT) PHY specification) receivers. Support for CCA-ED is an additional requirement that
22
relates specifically to the sensitivities described in D.2.5 (CCA-ED threshold).
23
24
25 36.3.21.6.3 CCA sensitivity for the primary 20 MHz channel
26
27 An EHT STA with a W MHz operating channel width shall detect, with greater than 90% probability, the
28
start of a PPDU that occupies at least the primary 20 MHz channel in an otherwise idle W MHz operating
29
channel width, and issue a PHY-CCA.indication with the STATUS parameter set to BUSY within a period of
30
31 aCCATime (see 21.4.4 (VHT PHY)) if one of the following conditions is met:
32 — The start of a non-HT PPDU as defined in 17.3.10.6 (CCA requirements) when operating in the
33
5 GHz or 6 GHz band and 18.4.6 (CCA performance) when operating in the 2.4 GHz band.
34
35 — The start of an HT PPDU as defined in 19.3.19.5 (CCA sensitivity).
36
— The start of a non-HT duplicate, VHT, HE, or EHT PPDU for which the power measured within the
37
primary 20 MHz channel is at or above –82 dBm.
38
39
40
The channel-list parameter is present and set to {primary} if the operating channel width is greater than
41
20 MHz. The CCA signal shall be held busy (not issue a PHY-CCA.indication primitive with the STATUS
42
43 parameter set to IDLE) for the duration of the PPDU, unless it receives a CCARESET.request primitive
44 before the end of the PPDU for instance during spatial reuse operation as described in 35.10 (EHT Spatial
45
reuse operation).
46
47
48 The receiver shall issue a PHY-CCA.indication primitive with the STATUS parameter set to BUSY for any
49 signal that exceeds the threshold below in the primary 20 MHz channel within a period of aCCATime after
50 the signal arrives at the receiver’s antenna(s):
51
52 — a value specified by dot11MSDOFDMEDthreshold if any MediumSyncDelay timer at that STA has
53 not expired (see 35.3.16.8 (Medium access recovery procedure)).
54
— –62 dBm otherwise.
55
56 NOTE— –62 dBm is 20 dB above the minimum modulation and coding rate sensitivity (–82 + 20 = –62 dBm).
57
58
If the operating channel width is greater than 20 MHz, then the channel-list parameter is present and shall be
59
set to {primary}. Following the indication and while the threshold continues to be exceeded, the receiver
60
61 shall not issue a PHY-CCA.indication primitive with the STATUS parameter set to IDLE or with a change
62 in the channel-list parameter.
63
64
65
892 Copyright © 2023 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 893 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 36.3.21.6.4 Per 20 MHz CCA sensitivity
2
3
If the operating channel width is greater than 20 MHz and the PHY issues a PHY-CCA.indication primitive,
4
the PHY shall set the per20bitmap to indicate the busy/idle status of each 20 MHz subchannel. A 20 MHz
5
6 subchannel is busy if at least one of the following conditions is present:
7 — A signal is present on the 20 MHz subchannel at or above a threshold of –62 dBm at the receiver's
8
antenna(s). The PHY shall indicate that the 20 MHz subchannel is busy a period aCCATime after the
9
signal starts and shall continue to indicate the 20 MHz subchannel is busy while the threshold
10
11 continues to be exceeded.
12
— A non-HT, HT_MF, HT_GF, VHT, HE, or EHT PPDU for which the power measured within this
13
20 MHz subchannel is at or above max(–72 dBm, OBSS_PD ) at the receiver’s antenna(s). The
14 level
15 PHY shall indicate that the 20 MHz subchannel is busy with greater than 90% probability within a
16 period aCCAMidTime (see 36.3 (EHT PHY)).
17
18
NOTE—Following the receipt of a Trigger frame with the CS Required subfield in the Common Info field set to 1, the
19
EHT PHY is only required to detect a signal at the –62 dBm threshold since the other conditions require more time than
20
is available before the response is expected.
21
22
23 OBSS_PD is defined in 26.10.2.4 (Adjustment of OBSS PD and transmit power) and applied in the
level
24
equations to define the detection level in this subclause if an EHT STA has ignored a 40 MHz, 80 MHz,
25
160 MHz, or 320 MHz inter-BSS PPDU following the procedure in 26.10.2.2 (General operation with non-
26
27 SRG OBSS PD level) or 26.10.2.3 (General operation with SRG OBSS PD level). It is applied to any
28 secondary channels within the PPDU bandwidth of the inter-BSS PPDU and during the RXTIME of the
29
inter-BSS PPDU. Otherwise, OBSS_PD is not applied in the equations to define the detection level in
30 level
31 this subclause.
32

## 第 895 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 NOTE 1—For an EHT MU PPDU the A-MPDU is per user in the MAC sublayer and the EHT-STF and EHT-LTF, and
2 Data are per user in the PHY in Figure 36-75 (PHY transmit procedure for an EHT MU PPDU).
3
4 NOTE 2—The transmission of NON_HT, HT_MF, HT_GF, VHT, and HE formats is specified in 36.2.6 (Support for
5 non-HT, HT, VHT, and HE formats).
6
7
In all options, in order to transmit data, the MAC generates a PHY-TXSTART.request primitive, which
8
causes the PHY entity to respond with a PHY-TXSTART.confirm primitive and enter the transmit state.
9
10 Further, the PHY is set to operate at the appropriate frequency through station management via the PLME,
11 as specified in 36.4 (EHT PLME). Other transmit parameters, such as EHT-MCS, coding types, and transmit
12 power, are set via the PHY-SAP using the PHY-TXSTART.request(TXVECTOR) primitive, as described in
13
36.2.2 (TXVECTOR and RXVECTOR parameters). After transmitting a PPDU that carries a Trigger frame,
14
the MAC sublayer issues a PHY-TRIGGER.request with a TRIGVECTOR parameter that provides the PHY
15
16 entity with the information needed to demodulate the expected EHT TB PPDU response. The remainder of
17 the subclause applies to the first two paths.
18
19
The PHY indicates the state of the primary channel and other channels (if any) via the PHY-CCA.indication
20
primitive (see 36.3.21.6 (CCA sensitivity) and 8.3.5.12 (PHY-CCA.indication)). Transmission of the PPDU
21
22 shall be initiated by the PHY after receiving the PHY-TXSTART.request(TXVECTOR) primitive. The
23 TXVECTOR parameters for the PHY-TXSTART.request primitive are specified in Table 36-1 (TXVECTOR
24 and RXVECTOR parameters).
25
26
After the PHY preamble transmission is started, the PHY entity immediately initiates scrambling and
27
28 encoding of the SERVICE field and PSDU. The encoding method for the Data field is based on the
29 FEC_CODING, CH_BANDWIDTH, NUM_STS, MCS, RU_ALLOCATION, and STA_ID parameters of
30 the TXVECTOR, as described in 36.3.4 (EHT PPDU formats).
31
32
The data shall be exchanged between the MAC and the PHY through a series of PHY-DATA.request(DATA)
33
34 primitives issued by the MAC, and PHY-DATA.confirm primitives issued by the PHY. PHY padding bits are
35 appended to the PSDU to make the number of bits in the coded PSDU an integral multiple of the number of
36 coded bits per OFDM symbol.
37
38
Transmission can be prematurely terminated by the MAC through the PHY-TXEND.request primitive.
39
40 PSDU transmission is terminated by receiving a PHY-TXEND.request primitive. Each
41 PHY-TXEND.request primitive is acknowledged with a PHY-TXEND.confirm primitive from the PHY.
42
43
A packet extension and/or a signal extension may be present in the PPDU. The PHY-TXEND.confirm
44
primitive is generated at the latest of the actual ending time of the PPDU, the end of the packet extension if
45
46 present, and the end of the signal extension if present.
47
48 In the PHY, the GI with GI duration indicated in the GI_TYPE parameter of the TXVECTOR is inserted in
49
every data OFDM symbol as a countermeasure against delay spread.
50
51
52 Once the PPDU transmission is completed the PHY entity enters the receive state.
53

## 第 899 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 been configured with BSS identification information and STA identification information (i.e., BSS color
2 value and STA-ID) so that it can receive data intended for the STA in the specific BSS. Other receive
3
parameters, such as RSSI and indicated DATARATE, may be accessed via the PHY SAP.
4
5
6 Upon receiving the transmitted PHY preamble in a greater than or equal to 20 MHz BSS, the PHY measures
7 a receive signal strength. This activity is indicated by the PHY to the MAC via a PHY-CCA.indication
8 primitive. A PHY-CCA.indication(BUSY, channel-list) primitive is also issued as an initial indication of
9
reception of a signal as specified in 36.3.21.6 (CCA sensitivity). The channel-list parameter of the
10
PHY-CCA.indication primitive is absent when the operating channel width is 20 MHz. The channel-list
11
12 parameter is present when the operating channel width is 40 MHz, 80 MHz, 160 MHz, or 320 MHz.
13
14 The PHY shall not issue a PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive in response to a PPDU that does not overlap
15
the primary channel unless the PHY at an AP receives the EHT TB PPDU solicited by the AP. The PHY
16
shall issue a PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive for the EHT TB PPDU solicited by the AP.
17
18
19 The PHY includes the measured RSSI and RSSI_LEGACY values in the
20 PHY-RXSTART.indication(RXVECTOR) primitive issued to the MAC.
21
22
After the PHY-CCA.indication(BUSY, channel-list) primitive is issued, the PHY entity shall begin receiving
23
24 the training symbols and searching for L-SIG in order to set the maximum duration of the data stream. Then
25 the PHY will search for the preambles for non-HT, HT, VHT, HE, and EHT PPDUs, respectively. If the
26 constellation used in the first symbol after the first long training field is QBPSK, the PHY entity shall
27
continue to detect the received signal using the receive procedure for HT-GF depicted in Clause 19 (High
28
Throughput (HT) PHY specification). For detecting the EHT preamble, the PHY entity shall search for
29
30 RL-SIG and evaluate the LENGTH field. If RL-SIG is detected, the PHY entity should check the parity bit
31 and RATE fields in L-SIG and RL-SIG. If either the check of the parity bit is invalid or the RATE field is not
32 set to 6 Mb/s, a PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive is not issued. If the check of the parity bit is valid and
33
the RATE field indicates 6 Mb/s but the LENGTH field value in L-SIG is a not a multiple of three, a PHY-
34
RXSTART.indication primitive is not issued. If the EHT preamble is not detected, the PHY should continue
35
36 to detect the received signal using non-HT, HT, VHT, and HE receive procedure in Clause 17 (Orthogonal
37 frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) PHY specification), Clause 19 (High Throughput (HT) PHY
38 specification), Clause 21 (Very High Throughput (VHT) PHY specification), and Clause 27 (High
39
Efficiency (HE) PHY specification), respectively.
40
41
42 If a valid parity bit and the RATE with 6 Mb/s are indicated in L-SIG and RL-SIG and the LENGTH field
43 value in L-SIG and RL-SIG is a multiple of three, U-SIG field is present after RL-SIG. PHY entity shall
44 begin receiving the U-SIG field and identify the PPDU version based on the PHY Version Identifier field in
45
the U-SIG field. The PHY entity shall check the constellation of the second symbol of the U-SIG field. If the
46
constellation is QBPSK, the PHY entity shall receive the U-SIG field and the repeated U-SIG field (four
47
48 symbols in total following the RL-SIG). If the constellation is BPSK, the PHY entity shall receive the U-SIG
49 field (two symbols in total following the RL-SIG). Then the PHY entity shall check the CRC of the U-SIG
50 field (and the repeated U-SIG field if present).
51
— If the U-SIG field indicates a valid CRC, the PHY entity shall report the version independent fields
52
53 in the U-SIG field (e.g., TXOP, BSS color, and Bandwidth) to the MAC entity.
54
— If the U-SIG field indicates a valid CRC, and the PHY version identifier or the BSS color or the
55
UL/DL does not contain an intended value, or the constellation of the second symbol of the U-SIG
56
57 field is QBPSK, the PHY entity shall issue a PHY-RXSTART.indication(RXVECTOR) then issue a
58 PHY-RXEND.indication(Filtered).
59
— If the U-SIG field indicates a valid CRC and the U-SIG field indicates a Validate U-SIG indication,
60
the PHY shall issue the error condition PHY-RXEND.indication(FormatViolation) primitive and
61
62 maintain PHY-CCA.indication(BUSY, channellist) primitive for the predicted duration of the
63 transmitted PPDU derived from the LENGTH field in L-SIG as defined in Equation (36-108) unless
64 it receives a PHY-CCARESET.request primitive before the end of the PPDU for instance during
65

1 spatial reuse operation as described in 35.10 (EHT Spatial reuse operation). A Validate U-SIG
2 indication is defined as a Validate field in the U-SIG field being set to 0 or a field value of a field in
3
the U-SIG field being set to a Validate state.
4
5 — The PHY entity shall not process the Disregard field.
6
— If the U-SIG field indicates a valid CRC and the U-SIG field indicates a Disregard U-SIG indication,
7
the PHY entity shall continue processing the U-SIG. A Disregard U-SIG indication is defined as a
8
9 Disregard field in the U-SIG field being set to any value or a field value of a field in the U-SIG field
10 being set to a Disregard state.
11
— If the U-SIG field indicates an invalid CRC, the PHY shall issue the error condition
12
PHY-RXEND.indication(FormatViolation) primitive and maintain PHY-CCA.indication(BUSY,
13
14 channellist) primitive for the predicted duration of the transmitted PPDU derived from the LENGTH
15 field in L-SIG as defined in Equation (36-108) unless it receives a PHY-CCARESET.request
16 primitive before the end of the PPDU for instance during spatial reuse operation as described in
17
35.10 (EHT Spatial reuse operation).

20 RXTIME(cid:11)(cid:80)s(cid:12) = L ---- E ---- N ---- G ----- T --- H ------ + ----- 3 --(cid:117)4+20+SignalExtension (36-108)
21 3
22
23
where
24
25 LENGTH is the value of the LENGTH field in L-SIG.
26 SignalExtension is defined in Table 27-54 (HE PHY characteristics).
27
28
If the CRC checks in U-SIG field is valid, and PHY version identifier, the BSS color, and the UL/DL all
29
indicates an intended value, and a Validate U-SIG indication is not indicated, then the PHY entity will parse
30
31 the PPDU Type And Compression Mode subfield and the DL/UL subfield in the U-SIG field and identify
32 the EHT PPDU type.
33
34
If the received PPDU is EHT MU PPDU, the PHY entity shall begin receiving the EHT-SIG, EHT-STF, and
35
EHT-LTF for EHT MU PPDU as shown in Figure 36-78 (PHY receive procedure for an EHT MU PPDU).
36
37 The PHY entity shall check the CRC of the Common field of EHT-SIG.
38
— If the CRCs protecting the Common field of EHT-SIG are valid, for all supported modes,
39
unsupported modes and Validate indication, the PHY entity shall maintain PHY-
40
41 CCA.indication(BUSY, channellist) primitive for the predicted duration of the transmitted PPDU, as
42 defined by RXTIME in Equation (36-109), unless it receives a PHY-CCARESET.request primitive
43 before the end of the PPDU for instance during spatial reuse operation as described in 35.10 (EHT
44
Spatial reuse operation). A Validate EHT-SIG indication is defined as a field value of a subfield
45
either in the EHT-SIG common field or in the receiver’s own user field being set to a Validate state.
46
47 — If the UL/DL subfield of the U-SIG field is set to 0 and the CRCs protecting the Common field of the
48 EHT-SIG are valid, the PHY entity shall search for intended STA-ID in each User field. If an
49
intended STA-ID is detected in a user encoding block or in the common encoding block of EHT-SIG
50
(STA-ID can be present in the common encoding block of EHT-SIG only if the PPDU type and
51
52 compression mode and UL/DL indicate a DL non-OFDMA transmission) with valid CRC, and an
53 unsupported mode or a Validate EHT-SIG indication is not indicated, the PHY entity shall continue
54 receiving the EHT-STF right after the EHT-SIG.
55
56 — If the UL/DL subfield of the U-SIG field is set to 1 and the CRC protecting the common encoding
57 block of the EHT-SIG is valid and an unsupported mode or a Validate EHT-SIG indication is not
58 indicated, the PHY entity may continue receiving the EHT-STF right after the EHT-SIG without
59
checking the STA-ID subfield.
60
61 — If the UL/DL subfield of the U-SIG field is set to 0 and the CRCs protecting the Common field of the
62 EHT-SIG are valid and no intended STA-ID is detected in all the User fields, the PHY entity shall
63 issue a PHY-RXSTART.indication(RXVECTOR) then issue a PHY-RXEND.indication(Filtered).

1 — If the UL/DL subfield of the U-SIG field is set to 0 and the CRCs protecting the Common field of the

3

4

6 primitive.

15 CCA.indication(BUSY, channellist) primitive for the predicted duration of the transmitted PPDU
16 derived from the LENGTH field in L-SIG as defined in Equation (36-108) unless it receives a PHY-
17 CCARESET.request primitive before the end of the PPDU for instance during spatial reuse
18
operation as described in 35.10 (EHT Spatial reuse operation).

21 If the received PPDU is EHT TB PPDU, the PHY entity shall continue receiving the EHT-STF and EHT-
22 LTF for an EHT TB PPDU shown in Figure 36-79 (PHY receive procedure for an EHT TB PPDU). If a STA
23 receives an EHT TB PPDU and the TRIGVECTOR parameters are not present in its PHY entity, the STA
24
shall use Equation (36-108) to calculate the predicted duration of the EHT TB PPDU.
25
26
27 If signal loss occurs during reception prior to completion of the PSDU reception, the error condition
28 PHY-RXEND.indication(CarrierLost) shall be reported to the MAC. After waiting for the end of the PPDU
29 as determined by Equation (36-109), the PHY shall set the PHY-CCA.indication(IDLE) primitive and return
30
to the RX IDLE state.

where
36
37 T , N , and T are defined in Equation (36-97), Equation (36-95), and Equation (36-96),

38 respectively.

SignalExtension is defined in Table 27-54 (HE PHY characteristics).
40
41
42 Except in an EHT sounding NDP, a Data field follows the EHT-STF and EHT-LTF fields. The number of
43 symbols in the Data field and the packet extension duration are computed from Equation (36-95) and
44 Equation (36-96), respectively.
45
46
The received PSDU bits are assembled into octets, decoded, and present to the MAC using a series of
47
48 PHY-DATA.indication(DATA) primitive exchanges. Any final bits that cannot be assembled into a complete
49 octet are considered pad bits and discarded. After the reception of the final bit of the last PSDU octet, and
50 possible padding and tail bits, the PHY entity shall check whether packet extension and/or signal extension
51
is applied. If packet extension and/or signal extension is applied, the PHY entity shall wait until the packet
52
extension and/or signal extension expires before issuing a PHY-RXEND.indication (NoError, RXVECTOR)
53
54 returning to the RX IDLE state, as shown in Figure 36-80 (PHY receive state machine).

56 36.3.24 Channel numbering and channelization
57
58
36.3.24.1 General
59
60
61 The STA may operate in the 2.4 GHz band, 5 GHz band, or 6 GHz band. The set of valid operating channel
62 numbers by regulatory domain is defined in Annex E. Channel allocation for each band is defined as
63
follows:
64

## 第 902 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 — In 19.3.15.2 (Channel allocation in the 2.4 GHz band) for the 2.4 GHz band
2
— In 19.3.15.3 (Channel allocation in the 5 GHz band) for the 5 GHz band
3
4 — In 27.3.23.2 (Channel allocation in the 6 GHz band) for the 6 GHz band
5
6
36.3.24.2 Channelization for 320 MHz channel
7
8
9 The 320 MHz channel consists of any two adjacent 160 MHz channels in the 6 GHz band. Two type of
10 channelizations for 320 MHz channel are defined: 320 MHz-1 and 320 MHz-2. 320 MHz-1 is defined as
11 320 MHz channel with channel center frequency numbered 31, 95, and 159. 320 MHz-2 is defined as
12
320 MHz channel with channel center frequency numbered 63, 127, and 191.
13
14
15 36.3.25 Regulatory requirements
16
17 WLANs implemented in accordance with this standard are subject to equipment certification and operating
18
requirements established by regional and national regulatory administrations. The PHY specification
19
establishes minimum technical requirements for interoperability, based upon established regulations at the
20
21 time this standard was issued. These regulations are subject to revision or may be superseded. Requirements
22 that are subject to local geographic regulations are annotated within the PHY specification. Regulatory
23 requirements that do not affect interoperability are not addressed in this standard. Implementers are referred
24
to the regulatory sources in Annex D for further information. Operation in countries within defined
25
regulatory domains might be subject to additional or alternative national regulations.
26
27
28
36.4 EHT PLME
29
30
31 36.4.1 PLME_SAP sublayer management primitives
32
33

34
and the intralayer of higher level LMEs. These attributes are accessed via the PLME-GET, PLME-SET,
35

46
MIB attributes) contains two types: static and dynamic.

49
50 dot11EHTPPEThresholdsMappingsTable OBJECT-TYPE
51 SYNTAX SEQUENCE OF Dot11EHTPPEThresholdsMappingsEntry
52 MAX-ACCESS not-accessible
53 STATUS current
54 DESCRIPTION
55 "A conceptual table for EHT PPE thresholds mappings, which determines the
56 nominal packet padding value as a function of the two PPE thresholds,
57 PPET8 and PPETmax, for an EHT PPDU of a particular RU allocation size and
58 NSS value. The MIB supports the ability to share separate PPE thresholds
59 for each NSS/RU pair. The thresholds mappings table contains one entry for
60 each NSS/RU pair and contains two fields for each entry: PPET8 and
61 PPETmax."
62 REFERENCE "IEEE Std 802.11-<year>, 35.13 (Nominal packet padding values
63 selection rules)"
64 ::= { dot11smt 47 }
65

47 "The index of the RU value portion of the NSS/RU pair for which the values

34
Change Dot11StationConfigEntry as follows (not all lines shown):
35
36
-- **********************************************************************
37
-- * dot11Interworking TABLE
38
-- **********************************************************************
39
40
41 Dot11InterworkingEntry ::=
42 SEQUENCE {
43 dot11NonAPStationMacAddress MacAddress,
44 …
45 dot11NonAPStationAddtsResultCode INTEGER},
46 dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized TruthValue}
47
Insert the following after dot11NonAPStationAddtsResultCode:
48
49
50 dot11EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized OBJECT-TYPE
51 SYNTAX TruthValue
52 MAX-ACCESS read-write
53 STATUS current
54 DESCRIPTION
55 "This is a control variable.
56 It is written by the SME after the AP receives the permissions for the
57 non-AP STA to use the EPCS priority access from the SSPN interface.
58
59 This attribute, when true, indicates that the non-AP STA is permitted to
60 invoke and use the EPCS priority access capability. If this capability is
61 false, the non-AP STA is not permitted to invoke and use the EPCS priority
62 access capability."
63 DEFVAL { false }
64 ::= { dot11InterworkingEntry <Last assigned + 1> }

R.4.2.4 Non-AP STA Interworking Capability

42 Change the first paragraph as follows:
43
44
This parameter is derived from the non-AP STA’s Extended Capabilities element, which is included in
45
(Re)Association Request frames. This parameter is also derived from the EHT Capabilities element of the
46
47 non-AP STA when the non-AP STA supports the EPCS priority access. The AP SME obtains this
48 information from the MLME SAP, e.g., MLME-ASSOCIATE.indication primitive. This information needs
49 to be passed over the SSPN interface since the service authorization decisions can depend on the non-AP
50
STA capabilities.

53 Insert a new subclause at the end of subclause R.4.2 (SSPN interface parameters) as follows:
54
55
R.4.2.14 Authorized EPCS priority access type
56
57
58 This per-non-AP STA parameter indicates the priority type allocated to the non-AP STA as determined by
59 the SSPN. The AP uses this information to authorize requests for EPCS priority access.
60
61
The following is used:
62
63 — dot1EPCSPriorityAccessAuthorized is used to authorize a non-AP STA to enable EPCS priority
64 access

## 第 975 页

IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023
1 Annex Z
2
3
4 (informative)
5
6
7 Change the title of the Annex as follows:
8
9
10 HE-SIG-B and EHT-SIG content examples
11
12
13
14 Z.1 General
15
16
Change as follows:
17
18
19 This annex provides a number of examples to illustrate the content of HE-SIG-B content channels and EHT-
20
SIG content channels.
21
22
23 HE-SIG-B content channels are padded to the same length and to an OFDM symbol boundary as defined in
24 27.3.11.8.5 (Encoding and modulation). For illustration simplicity, the examples do not include the com-
25 plete padding bits but only pad to make the two HE-SIG-B content channels equal in length and an integer
26
number of 4 bits. All the padding bits in the examples are set to 0.
27
28
29 In the examples, the binary sequence of each HE-SIG-B field is in transmission order. For the entire content
30 of each HE-SIG-B content channel, the binary sequences are converted to hexadecimal listed in transmis-
31 sion order as pairs of hexadecimal digits, where the first bit transmitted is the LSB of the first transmitted
32
octet and is the LSB of the second hexadecimal digit.
33
34
35 EHT-SIG content channels are padded to the same length and to an OFDM symbol boundary as defined in
36 36.3.12.8.6 (Encoding and modulation). For illustration simplicity, the examples do not include the com-
37 plete padding bits but only pad to make different EHT-SIG content channels equal in length and an integer
38
number of 4 bits. All the padding bits in the examples are set to 0.
39
40
41 In the examples, the binary sequence of each EHT-SIG field is in transmission order. For the entire content
42 of each EHT-SIG content channel, the binary sequences are converted to hexadecimal listed in transmission
43 order as pairs of hexadecimal digits, where the first bit transmitted is the LSB of the first transmitted octet
44
and is the LSB of the second hexadecimal digit.
45
46
47 Change the title of the subclauses Z.2, Z.3, Z.4, and Z.5 as follows:
48
49
50
Z.2 HE-SIG-B example Example 1

52
53

55
56
57
58 Z.4 HE-SIG-B example Example 3
59
60
61
62 Z.5 HE-SIG-B example Example 4
63
64
65

37 The AP can perform a dynamic split of the User fields for the three MU-MIMO STAs on 484+242-tone
38 MRU 2, with zero, one, two, or three User fields assigned to EHT-SIG content channel 1 and three, two,
39 one, or zero User fields assigned to EHT-SIG content channel 2 correspondingly. In this example, there are
40
two User fields assigned to EHT-SIG content channel 1 and one User field assigned to EHT-SIG content
41
channel 2, to avoid a disparity in the number of User fields between content channels.

1 Annex AA
2
3
4 (informative)
5
6
7
Multiple BSSID configuration examples
8
9
10
11
12 AA.1 Introduction
13
14
15 Change as follows:
16
17
This annex provides examples showing the relationship between profile periodicity (indicated by the Full
18
Set Rx Periodicity field in the Multiple BSSID Configuration element) and the DTIM interval (DTIM Period
19
20 field in the Multiple BSSID-Index element) for a multiple BSSID set as described in 11.1.3.8.3 (Discovery
21 of a nontransmitted BSSID profile). The examples provide guidance on how an AP might organize the
22 advertisement of nontransmitted BSSID profiles in its Beacon frames if it cannot fit all the profiles in a sin-
23
gle Beacon frame (i.e., partial list of profiles) it is advertising. By having the DTIM interval for a nontrans-
24
mitted BSSID a multiple of the profile periodicity, the profile for that BSSID would always appear in its
25
26 DTIM beacon. This helps an associated non-AP STA save power as it is able to receive any updates to the
27 profile when it wakes to receive the DTIM beacon.
28
29
30 In addition, this annex also provides examples illustrating the relationship between a multiple BSSID set, a
31 co-hosted BSSID set, and multi-link operation.
32
33 Change the title of the subclause AA.2 as follows:
34
35
36
37 AA.2 Examples illustrating the relationship between profile periodicity and
38
DTIM interval
39
40
41
Move the following content from subclause AA.1 (Introduction) as the first paragraph of this
42
43 subclause and change as follows:
44
45
The examples provide guidance on how an AP might organize the advertisementinclusion of nontransmitted
46
BSSID profiles in its Beacon frames if it cannot fit all the profiles in a single Beacon frame (i.e., the AP
47
48 advertises partial list of profiles) it is advertising. By havingselecting the DTIM interval for a nontransmitted
49 BSSID as a multiple of the profile periodicity, the profile for that BSSID would always appear in its DTIM
50 beacon. This helps an associated non-AP STA to save power by not having to wake up from a doze state, to
51
listen to beacons other than the DTIM beacons and as it is able to receive any updates to theits associated
52
profile when it wakes to receive the DTIM beacon.

20
of affiliated APs from different multiple BSSID sets) shows an example where APs affiliated with an AP
21
MLD belong to a multiple BSSID set on their respective channel. Further, APs within the same AP MLD

8 ID is not shown in the figure since the AP MLD ID value is equal to the BSSID Index of the affiliated AP
9
operating on that link and therefore, the value can be different on each link (see 9.4.2.170.2 (Neighbor AP
10
Information field)).
